atomic_claim: Research, digital resources, and NLP systems for indigenous languages of the Americas are reviewed.
The main challenges and research questions for distant and low-resource languages in the Americas are presented.
A method detects annotation errors in manually and automatically annotated dependency parse trees using ensemble parsing, Bayesian inference, and active learning.
The method improves parsing accuracy on in-domain data.
The method improves parsing accuracy on out-of-domain data.
Plural mentions are introduced as a distinct category for coreference resolution and entity linking.
The SemEval 2018 character identification corpus is expanded with annotations for plural mentions.
A coreference resolution algorithm is presented that selectively creates clusters to handle both singular and plural mentions.
A deep learning-based entity linking model is proposed that jointly handles singular and plural mentions through multi-task learning.
Adjusted evaluation metrics are proposed for coreference resolution and entity linking to accommodate plural mentions.
The new coreference resolution and entity linking models outperform traditional models that handle only singular mentions.
State-of-the-art lexical simplification system performance is evaluated on individual English learners at different proficiency levels.
Benefits of using complex word identification models to personalize lexical simplification are measured.
A simple personalized complex word identification model based on graded vocabulary lists helps lexical simplification avoid unnecessary simplifications and produce more readable output.
A joint learning framework combines supervised query-document matching loss with unsupervised text reconstruction loss for representation learning in information retrieval.
The framework leverages labeled click-through data and abundant unlabeled data to learn task-specific and general semantic representations for retrieval.
Joint learning improves retrieval performance compared to strong baselines on TREC collections.
An attributed and predictive entity embedding method is introduced for fine-grained entity typing in knowledge bases, leveraging multiple sources of information comprehensively.
The proposed method outperforms eight state-of-the-art methods on two DBpedia datasets for fine-grained entity typing.
Two conceptually simple local neural models for constituent parsing are introduced, one making local decisions on constituent spans and another on CFG rules.
The best of the introduced models achieves highly competitive labeled bracketing performance on the Penn Treebank and Chinese Treebank 5.1.
Local neural models achieve state-of-the-art constituent parsing accuracy despite relying only on local output decisions.
WikiRef recommends highly relevant references for a target scientific Wikipedia page by borrowing from the reference sections of its wikilinks.
WikiRef includes a classifier that determines whether a wikilink is a potential source of references.
WikiRef’s reference recommendation outperforms the most competitive baseline on datasets of scientific Wikipedia pages in both Computer Science and Physics domains.
Interpretation of implicit conditions is formalized as classifying user utterances into related database fields while identifying evidence for the classification.
A new task of interpreting implicit conditions in database search dialogues is introduced.
An SVM-based model is implemented for the implicit condition interpretation task.
An RCNN-based model is implemented for the implicit condition interpretation task.
Evaluation on a simulated real estate dialogue corpus shows that the SVM-based model outperforms the RCNN-based model.
CASS incorporates a copy and alignment mechanism into a sequence-to-sequence model for solving math word problems.
Reinforcement learning directly optimizes solution accuracy when training the sequence-to-sequence model for math word problems.
The copy and alignment mechanism addresses the generation of spurious numbers and incorrect number positions in the model's output.
Reinforcement learning yields better performance than maximum likelihood estimation on math word problem solving.
Combining the neural model with a feature-based model outperforms previous state-of-the-art results on math word problem solving.
Knowledge-enriched word embedding (KEWE) encodes reading difficulty knowledge into word representations.
A knowledge graph of word-level difficulty is constructed from three perspectives.
Two word embedding models are designed to incorporate difficulty context from the knowledge graph via loss functions.
KEWE applied to readability assessment on English and Chinese datasets demonstrates improved performance.
TextEnt learns distributed representations of entities and documents directly from knowledge base descriptions.
TextEnt is trained to predict the entity described by a document and to map the document and its target entity close together in a continuous vector space.
TextEnt is trained on a large collection of Wikipedia documents.
TextEnt achieves state-of-the-art performance on fine-grained entity typing.
TextEnt achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiclass text classification.
Code and trained representations of TextEnt are released for academic research.
A generative adversarial network is used to transfer feature representations from a source language with rich annotations to a target language with scarce annotations for relation classification.
Feature imitation is achieved through competition between a relation classification network and a rival discriminator.
The feature adaptation approach improves cross-lingual relation classification performance beyond the previous state-of-the-art on the ACE 2005 multilingual corpus.
A neural transition-based model using copy, delete, and insert actions performs morphological transduction tasks such as inflection generation, lemmatization, and reinflection.
The model outperforms state-of-the-art systems on low-resource and medium-resource morphological transduction settings.
The model remains competitive with state-of-the-art systems in high-resource morphological transduction settings.
A generic copy action enables rapid generalization from few training examples.
Minimum risk training mitigates weaknesses of maximum likelihood estimation and neutralizes negative effects of using a separate character aligner in the training pipeline.
LSTM language models can correctly classify sentences as grammatical or ungrammatical for extraction violations involving extra arguments and subject-relative clause islands under certain conditions.
The grammaticality classification by LSTM language models is sensitive to linguistic processing factors.
LSTM language models fail to induce an abstract notion of grammaticality for the tested long-distance dependency domain.
An attention mechanism is employed for context-dependent dynamic feature selection in incremental transition-based parsers.
The model learns to focus on specific tokens to decide the next parsing action.
Multilingual experiments demonstrate the technique's effectiveness across many languages.
An experiment with an augmented test dataset shows that the technique helps understand model behavior on locally ambiguous points.
Domain independent models predict the year of authorship using only neologism usage patterns.
The models capture temporal locality of word usage over a span of 150 years.
The models generalize to multiple domains, including news, fiction, and non-fiction, with competitive performance.
Modeling only the distribution of neologism usage counts, without relying on specific word identities, achieves competitive performance with orders of magnitude fewer input features.
An implicit syntax feature extraction approach uses hidden-layer vectors from a neural syntax parser for RST discourse parsing.
A simple transition-based model serves as a baseline for neural RST parsing.
Adding a dynamic oracle enhances the transition-based baseline model for RST parsing.
The transition-based baseline model with dynamic oracle achieves competitive performance on a standard RST parsing dataset.
Integrating implicit syntax features into the transition-based model yields improvements surpassing those obtained with explicit Tree-RNN syntax integration.
An active-learning based framework reduces the amount of labeled data required to learn entity structure representations.
The framework can automatically generate programs that map entity mentions to their structures using human-comprehensible labels.
The framework consistently outperforms handwritten transformation programs and supervised learning models.
The framework improves performance on relation extraction tasks.
The framework improves performance on entity resolution tasks.
A hand-crafted approach uses a small bilingual dictionary with minimal manual verification to create distantly supervised data for low-resource cross-lingual event type detection.
Adversarial training of bilingual word representations is applied to event-type detection.
The hand-crafted distant supervision approach and adversarial bilingual word representation training achieve comparable performance on event-type detection.
A strategy for allocating a limited manual annotation budget maximizes performance gain in low-resource cross-lingual event type detection.
The method mines the most correlated word pairs between two discourse arguments to model pair-specific clues.
The mined word pairs are integrated as interactive attention into argument representations produced by a bidirectional LSTM.
A neural tensor network with a sparsity constraint is employed to capture deeper and more important pair patterns for implicit discourse relation recognition.
The TIASL framework demonstrates effective performance on the PDTB dataset for implicit discourse relation recognition.
A sketch-based two-step neural model generates structured SQL queries from natural language requests.
The first step uses a sequence-to-sequence model to predict an SQL sketch with placeholders for entities such as column names, table names, aliases, and variables.
The second step employs a dual-encoder sequence-to-sequence model that conditions on both the natural language query and the predicted sketch to produce the final SQL query.
The model achieves superior performance compared to prior approaches on the WikiSQL dataset.
The model achieves superior performance compared to prior approaches on the SENLIDB dataset.
Entailment vectors embed lexical entailment relations from WordNet into word embeddings.
A set-theoretic model is introduced to learn entailment vectors from word pairs with known lexical entailment relations.
Entailment vectors are incorporated into a decomposable attention model for textual entailment.
Using entailment vectors improves textual entailment performance relative to a baseline that uses only standard word2vec vectors.
The resulting textual entailment model achieves performance close to or above state of the art without using manually-crafted rules or extensive syntactic features.
A comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art domain adaptation techniques for neural machine translation is provided.
A neural framework generates summaries constrained to a vocabulary-defined linguistic preference of a target audience.
The framework tunes summary words during generation to account for the generation context.
The framework produces summaries that adhere to the target vocabulary while maintaining superior summary quality compared to a word embedding based lexical substitution algorithm.
The framework can generate understandable summaries using simpler words.
The framework can generate readable summaries using shorter words.
Community-based profiling features of Twitter users are incorporated into abuse detection.
The proposed methods outperform the current state of the art in abuse detection.
A qualitative analysis of model characteristics is performed.
Two novel German educational media text datasets targeting adults and children are analyzed with 400 automatically extracted linguistic complexity measures.
Both datasets exhibit broad linguistic adaptation to the target audience, and this adaptation generalizes across the datasets.
A binary classification model for German readability achieves high accuracy on both datasets.
The German readability model demonstrates robust cross-corpus performance, the first such model to do so.
New resources for German readability assessment are provided, including a corpus of German news broadcast subtitles (Tagesschau/Logo) and an expanded GEO/GEOlino corpus.
High order LSTM predicts multiple tags for the current token, including the current tag and several previous tags.
Multi-Order BiLSTM (MO-BiLSTM) combines low-order and high-order LSTMs.
MO-BiLSTM uses a pruning technique to maintain scalability for high-order models.
MO-BiLSTM achieves state-of-the-art performance on all-phrase chunking.
MO-BiLSTM achieves highly competitive performance on two named entity recognition datasets.
A review of existing Spanish corpora annotated with negation is provided.
Information about Spanish negation-annotated corpora is compiled and made available for researchers.
An analysis of annotation compatibility among Spanish negation corpora is performed.
The analysis determines that merging the existing Spanish negation corpora is not possible due to annotation incompatibility.
A convolutional neural network combined with an external sentiment lexicon encodes sentiment knowledge into pre-trained word vectors.
Encoding sentiment knowledge into word vectors yields higher sentiment analysis accuracy than benchmark methods.
PorSimplesSent provides 4,888 Portuguese sentence pairs for readability assessment, exceeding the size of comparable English corpora.
Three versions of PorSimplesSent are publicly released.
Four baseline models for sentence readability assessment on PorSimplesSent are established.
Three construction scenarios for the Portuguese sentence readability corpus are evaluated using a parallel simplification corpus with annotated operations.
A pairwise ranking method using 17 linguistic and psycholinguistic features accurately identifies the ranking of sentence pairs.
A hybrid system that merges supervised machine learning with hand-built rules computes animacy of referring expressions and co-reference chains.
The hybrid system achieves state-of-the-art performance for animacy classification of referring expressions.
The hybrid system achieves state-of-the-art performance for animacy classification of coreference chains.
A training and test dataset of 142 narrative texts with annotated referring expressions and co-reference chains is released.
Evaluation on an OntoNotes subset shows accurate animacy classification of coreference chains and referring expressions.
The system can detect animacy of unusual referents in folktales, such as characters that belong to traditionally inanimate classes.
A survey of the current state of research on diachronic word embeddings and semantic shift detection is provided.
Several axes for comparing methods that trace temporal semantic shifts with word embedding models are proposed.
Main challenges and prospective applications for the emerging subfield of diachronic word embeddings are outlined.
A multi-perspective framework jointly trains heterogeneous experts to aggregate context information for cloze-style reading comprehension.
Simple aggregation modules model individual perspectives and their outputs are combined by a one-timestep pointer network to produce answers.
An efficient sampling mechanism generates additional training examples by matching the distribution of candidates between labeled and unlabeled data.
The proposed method attains state-of-the-art performance on the CLOTH cloze-test dataset.
Fine-grained NER models are empirically compared for English and Japanese.
LSTM+CNN+CRF works well for English fine-grained NER but performs poorly for Japanese fine-grained NER.
Removing the CNN layer and utilizing dictionary and category embeddings improves Japanese fine-grained NER performance.
The method that removes the CNN layer and utilizes dictionary and category embeddings increases Japanese fine-grained NER F-score.
A dataset creation method alters an existing machine reading comprehension dataset to incorporate hard-to-detect not-answerable questions.
An automatic difficulty labeling method assigns difficulty levels to not-answerable questions by measuring similarity between a question and its target passage.
The resulting dataset with difficulty level annotations is validated for evaluating not-answerable question detection.
The dataset enables more precise evaluation of not-answerable question detection performance.
A graph-based semi-supervised learning framework learns lexicons for semantic parsing from large text corpora and lexical resources.
The framework constructs a graph using a phrase similarity model trained on large text corpora and lexical resources.
The framework applies a graph propagation algorithm to infer label distributions for unlabeled phrases from labeled phrases.
The framework improves semantic parsing performance on the WebQuestions benchmark compared to a base system without the learned lexicon.
The framework achieves competitive semantic parsing performance on the Free917 benchmark relative to state-of-the-art systems.
A systematic evaluation of word embedding models for bilingual terminology extraction from specialized comparable corpora is conducted.
Character-based embedding models outperform other embedding models in the quality of extracted bilingual lexicons.
An efficient method for combining embedding models trained on specialized and general-domain data sets is introduced, achieving higher performance than the best individual embedding model.

The evaluation determines which state-of-the-art embedding model, whether trained on specialized corpora or pre-trained on large general data sets, is most appropriate for bilingual terminology extraction.
A multilevel heuristic approach regulates rationale extraction for entity relation classification.
Rationale selection is regularized by a semi-supervised process that incorporates word, syntax, sentence, and corpus level features.
The approach improves classification performance on relation classification tasks.
The approach enhances the interpretability of extracted rationales compared to state-of-the-art methods.
Event graphs are generated from text based on dependencies to capture temporal and causal relations for reading comprehension questions.
Answer candidates are ranked by aligning generated event graphs.
Alignment of event graphs is constrained by graph-based reasoning to enforce temporal and causal agreement.
The method self-adaptively complements existing reading comprehension solutions and is automatically triggered only when applicable.
Adding the method as an add-on improves state-of-the-art reading comprehension performance on benchmarks such as RACE and MCTest.
An entailment-aware encoder jointly learns summarization generation and entailment recognition in a multi-task framework.
An entailment-aware decoder is trained using entailment Reward Augmented Maximum Likelihood (RAML).
Incorporating entailment knowledge improves both informativeness and correctness of abstractive sentence summarization relative to baseline models.
An interactive attention mechanism learns representations of context words, sentiment words, and aspect words within reviews.
Sentiment and aspect representations are incorporated into the decoder via an attention fusion network to generate aspect- and sentiment-aware review summaries.

The abstractive summarizer is jointly trained with a text categorization task to learn a category-specific encoder that locates salient aspect information and captures style variations across categories.
The proposed model outperforms strong competitors on a real-life review summarization dataset.
A novel resource-based evaluation scenario assesses the impact of lemmatization and POS typing on word embedding performance.
Lemmatization and POS typing have complementary qualitative and vocabulary-level effects on word embedding performance.
Combining lemmatization and POS typing yields superior word embedding performance compared to using either operation alone.
Performance improvement from lemmatization and POS typing is more pronounced for verbs.
Better conceptual alignment between word embeddings and lexical resources underlies the performance gains from lemmatization and POS typing.
A decision-making component determines whether to ask a follow-up question or to stop the conversation to make a guess in a task-oriented visual dialogue model.
Incorporating the decision-making component yields dialogues that are less repetitive.
Incorporating the decision-making component yields dialogues with fewer unnecessary questions, potentially increasing interaction efficiency and naturalness.
Relation networks are introduced to learn better source representations for neural machine translation.
Relation networks associate source words with each other to enhance the memorization capability of recurrent neural networks.
Source representations and their relations are incorporated into the attention component during decoding while keeping the encoder-decoder framework unchanged.
The method improves translation performance compared to conventional encoder-decoder models.
The method outperforms approaches that use supervised syntactic knowledge.
Operation Network models abstractive sentence compression as an editing procedure consisting of delete, copy, and generate operations.
Operation Network combines the advantages of delete-based and generate-based sentence compression models.
Operation Network deletes unnecessary words from the source sentence and then either generates new words from a large vocabulary or copies words directly from the source sentence.
Operation Network achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
A set of hypotheses concerning long tail phenomena, their interactions, and their impact on entity linking system performance is formulated.
Evaluation datasets for entity linking primarily capture head entities and only incidentally include tail data, encouraging overfitting to popular and non‑ambiguous cases.
The most difficult entity linking cases are infrequent candidates with ambiguous forms.
Recommended actions for better inclusion of tail cases in entity linking evaluation datasets are presented.
MEMD adds an auxiliary encoder and an auxiliary decoder to neural encoder-decoder models for short-text conversation.
MEMD provides training guidance without requiring extra data or altering the internal network structure.
MEMD improves the quality of generated conversational responses according to automatic metrics and human evaluations.
MEMD generates more diverse and smoother replies in short-text conversation generation.
Introduces a fine-grained Arabic dialect identification task covering 25 specific cities and Standard Arabic.
Builds multiple classification systems and explores a large space of features for Arabic dialect identification.
Achieves city-level dialect identification accuracy that surpasses the previous state-of-the-art technique for Arabic dialect identification.
Provides data analysis revealing similarity and differences across Arabic dialects.
REMAN is a publicly available corpus of literature texts annotated with emotion trigger spans and the roles of experiencers, targets, and causes.
Baseline results are provided for automatic prediction of emotion triggers and relational roles in REMAN.
Emotion lexicons are insufficient to capture the high variability of emotion expressions in literature.
Joint modeling of emotions with their experiencer, target, and cause roles improves performance across all subtasks.
High-precision patterns are created to identify self-reported diagnoses of nine mental health conditions without manual labeling.
SMHD dataset is introduced, comprising social media posts from users with one or multiple mental health conditions and matched control users.
Distinctions in users' language are examined using linguistic and psychological variables.
Text classification methods are explored to identify individuals with mental conditions from their language.
Domain adaptation for sentiment analysis is cast as an embedding projection task.
The model learns to project two mono-domain embedding spaces into a shared bi-domain space while jointly predicting sentiment.
The model achieves state-of-the-art sentiment classification results on multiple source-target domain pairs, including Amazon and SemEval datasets.
The model matches state-of-the-art performance on similar domains and outperforms existing approaches on highly divergent domains.
A transduction language for natural language treebank transformations is introduced.
The transduction language underlies a tree transduction tool that provides clean, precise, and concise descriptions of treebank transformations.
The tree transduction tool enables non-hard-coded implementation of treebank transformations.
A summary evaluation approach predicts summary content quality without requiring human model summaries.
Features reflecting coverage, diversity, informativeness, and coherence are derived by exploiting the compositional capabilities of corpus-based and lexical resource-based word embeddings.
A learning model trained on these features predicts summary content quality in the absence of gold model summaries.
The metric replicates human-assigned scores for summarization systems and summaries on query-focused and update summarization tasks from TAC 2008 and 2009.
The combination of derived features provides reliable estimates of summary content quality when model summaries are unavailable.
A hybrid neural model is introduced for deep question answering on history examination questions.
The model employs a cooperative gated neural network to retrieve answers.
The model uses extra labels provided by a neural Turing machine labeler to assist answer retrieval.
The neural Turing machine labeler works well with only a small training dataset.
The cooperative gated mechanism effectively captures semantic representations of lengthy answers.
The hybrid neural model achieves substantial performance gains over various neural model baselines on history exam question answering.
A forest-to-sequence neural machine translation model translates from a packed forest of source parse trees to target sentences.
The model represents source syntactic uncertainty by encoding exponentially many parse trees as a packed forest.
A neural transducer is learned to operate on the input forest and generate translations.
The forest-to-sequence model outperforms sequence-to-sequence and tree-to-sequence neural translation models on English–German, English–Chinese, and English–Farsi translation tasks.
LBSA incorporates sentiment lexicons into supervised attention for neural sentiment analysis.
LBSA enables a recurrent neural network to focus on sentiment content.
LBSA generates sentiment-informative representations.
LBSA provides better interpretability than general attention models.
LBSA reduces noise compared to general attention models.
LBSA outperforms previous methods on large-scale sentiment classification datasets.
A dataset of Twitter users who self-report their race or ethnicity through a survey is introduced.
Predictive models are developed to infer user-level race and ethnicity from Twitter text for the four largest racial and ethnic groups.
The predictive models achieve high AUC performance in race and ethnicity prediction.
The predictive models are made publicly available to the research community.
A sequence-to-sequence generation based data augmentation framework leverages semantic alternatives of an utterance to augment data for task-oriented dialogue language understanding.
The framework incorporates a diversity rank into utterance representations to encourage generation of diverse utterances.
Diversely augmented utterances improve the performance of the language understanding module in task-oriented dialogue systems.
The framework achieves significant performance gains on the Airline Travel Information System dataset when only a small training set is available.
The framework achieves significant performance gains on a newly created semantic frame annotation of the Stanford Multi-turn Multi-domain Dialogue Dataset when only a small training set is available.
Case studies confirm that the method generates diverse utterances.
RuSentiment is a dataset of 31,185 Russian social media posts annotated for sentiment analysis.
RuSentiment includes comprehensive annotation guidelines that can be extended to other languages.
An active learning-style strategy was used to pre-select 6,950 posts to diversify the dataset.
Baseline sentiment classification results are reported on RuSentiment.
High-quality Russian word embeddings trained on 3.2 billion VKontakte tokens are released.
Hierarchical User Aspect Rating Network (HUARN) jointly models user preference and overall ratings for document-level multi-aspect sentiment classification.
HUARN employs a hierarchical architecture that encodes word, sentence, and document level information.
HUARN incorporates user attention and aspect attention mechanisms to build sentence and document level representations.
HUARN combines document representation with user and overall rating information to predict aspect ratings of a review.
HUARN utilizes a multi-task framework that treats diverse aspects differently.
HUARN achieves state-of-the-art performance on two real-world datasets for document-level multi-aspect sentiment classification.
A comprehensive review of deep learning methods applied to answer selection is provided.
A convolutional neural network is applied to lyrics segmentation.
The convolutional neural network approach improves state-of-the-art performance in lyrics segmentation.
Novel features are evaluated for deeper macrostructure detection in song lyrics.
Subword-augmented embedding is used to enhance word embeddings for cloze reading comprehension.
Different augmentation strategies for subword-augmented embedding are explored to improve cloze-style reading comprehension models.
A reader model incorporates subword-level representations with a short list to handle rare words effectively.
The proposed subword-augmented approach yields superior performance and generalization compared to state-of-the-art baselines on multiple public datasets.
Interaction-Aware Topic Model (IATM) integrates network embedding and user attention for topic modeling of microblog conversations.
A conversation network linking users based on reposting and replying relationships is constructed to capture dynamic user behaviors.
Dynamic interactions and user attention are modeled to learn interaction-aware edge embeddings that incorporate social context.
Interaction-aware edge embeddings are incorporated into neural variational inference to generate more consistent topics.
Experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model.
CASCADE combines content-driven and context-driven modeling for sarcasm detection in online social media discussions.
CASCADE extracts contextual information from the discourse of a discussion thread.
CASCADE utilizes user embeddings that encode stylometric and personality features of users.
CASCADE integrates user embeddings with content-based feature extractors such as convolutional neural networks.
CASCADE achieves a significant boost in sarcasm classification performance on a large Reddit corpus.
NCEL integrates local contextual features and global coherence information for entity linking using a Graph Convolutional Network.
NCEL improves computational efficiency by performing graph convolution on subgraphs of adjacent entity mentions.
NCEL enhances robustness to data noise through an attention scheme.
NCEL is trained on Wikipedia hyperlinks to mitigate overfitting and domain bias.
NCEL achieves effective entity linking performance and generalization across five publicly available datasets.
Analyses of time complexity, key module impact, and qualitative results demonstrate NCEL's effectiveness and efficiency.
A pointer-copy mechanism based sequence-to-sequence model performs sentence simplification.
Multi-task learning with entailment and paraphrase generation auxiliary tasks enhances the entailment and paraphrasing capabilities of a sentence simplification model.
A multi-level layered soft sharing approach assigns higher-level layers to semantic auxiliary tasks and lower-level layers to lexico-syntactic auxiliary tasks in a sentence simplification model.
A multi-armed bandit based training procedure dynamically switches among auxiliary tasks during multi-task learning for sentence simplification.
The resulting model achieves superior automatic simplification metrics and human evaluation scores compared to competitive simplification systems.
Structure-infused copy mechanisms facilitate copying important words and relations from source sentences to summary sentences.
The approach combines source dependency structure with the copy mechanism of an abstractive sentence summarizer.
Incorporating source-side syntactic information improves the effectiveness of abstractive summarization.
The proposed approach compares favorably to state-of-the-art methods for abstractive summarization.
String transduction can be reduced to sequence labeling.
A technique is introduced to handle character deletions and insertions when reducing string transduction to sequence labeling.
The reduction technique is compatible with any sequence labeling algorithm.
The reduction technique is most effective for string transduction tasks that exhibit strong locality and lack long-range dependencies.
The reduction technique outperforms seq2seq models and achieves state-of-the-art results on spelling correction for social media, OCR correction, and morphological inflection.
An extractive headline generation method based on learning to rank extracts the most informative substring from each community question answering question as its headline.
The extractive headline generation method outperforms several baselines, including a widely used prefix-based method.
A framework for learning from rich prior knowledge defines a family of crowdsourcing models capable of combining annotations with differing structures such as document labels and word labels.
The model instance uses predicted expected values of measurement functions over annotations and data to unify annotation models.
The model outperforms prior crowdsourcing approaches.
The model enables active sample selection that jointly chooses annotator, data item, and annotation structure to reduce annotation effort.
A method paraphrases noun compounds by generating prepositional paraphrases using LSTM-based representations of the noun compound and its paraphrase.
The method creates representations of noun compounds and their corresponding prepositional paraphrases such that their similarity is high.
The learned representations are used to select the correct preposition for a noun compound.
Cross-lingual embedding architectures are adapted to treat noun compounds and prepositional paraphrases as parallel sequences.
Experiments show that the approach achieves strong performance on multiple manually annotated noun‑compound datasets.
Standard translation-based relation induction approaches perform poorly.
A probabilistic relation induction model based on translations uses Gaussian distributions to model translation variability and encode soft constraints on source and target words.

A probabilistic relation induction model based on Bayesian linear regression encodes a linear relationship between vector representations of related words, representing a weaker assumption than translation-based models.
The Structurally Constrained Recurrent Network (SCRN) model is reproduced.
Naive dropout, variational dropout, and weight tying are applied to regularize the SCRN model.
When regularized and optimized appropriately, the SCRN model matches LSTM performance on English language modeling.
When regularized and optimized appropriately, the SCRN model surpasses LSTM performance on non-English language modeling.
A model jointly learns named entity recognition and morphological disambiguation without requiring external morphological disambiguation tools.
The joint learning approach operates independently of the morphological annotation schemes used in different languages.
Experiments with three model architectures that combine NER and morphological disambiguation demonstrate that joint learning improves NER performance.
The jointly learned morphological disambiguator achieves performance competitive with existing disambiguation tools.
An autoencoding framework learns a meta-embedding space that can reconstruct multiple source word embeddings simultaneously.
Three variants of autoencoded word meta-embeddings are introduced, each designed to satisfy distinct meta-embedding requirements.
The autoencoded meta-embeddings achieve superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art meta-embedding methods across multiple benchmark tasks.
SimpleDBpediaQA is a benchmark dataset for simple question answering over knowledge graphs, derived by mapping SimpleQuestions entities and predicates from Freebase to DBpedia.
SimpleDBpediaQA includes strong baseline results using both neural and non-neural methods.
A uniform user embedding learning approach bridges knowledge between source and target social media for cross-media user profiling.
A cross-media user-word network captures relationships among users through textual information.
A modified cross-media user-user network captures relationships among users through social information.
Joint learning of a heterogeneous network composed of the user-word and user-user networks produces user embeddings for profiling.
User embeddings are used as input to classification or regression models for gender classification and age regression across media.
Empirical studies show the approach is effective for cross-media gender classification and cross-media age regression.
Provides the first direct comparison of previously published transfer learning approaches for entity recognition with differing source and target class labels.
Conducts experiments on seven new source/target corpus pairs, nearly doubling the number of corpus pairs studied in prior work.
Empirically determines conditions under which each published transfer learning approach performs well for entity recognition with novel classes.
Finds that simpler transfer learning approaches perform best when labeled target data is very limited.
Finds that neural transfer learning approaches perform better when more labeled target data is available.
Webis Clickbait Corpus 2017 comprises 38,517 Twitter tweets annotated for clickbait on a 4-point scale.
Tweets in the corpus were sampled from the top 27 most retweeted news publishers over a 150‑day period to reduce publisher and topic bias.
Each tweet in the corpus was annotated by five Amazon Mechanical Turk workers using a 4‑point scale.
The corpus was used to evaluate twelve clickbait detection systems submitted to the Clickbait Challenge 2017.
First reproduction studies of three groups of complementary methods for Target Dependent Sentiment Analysis are conducted.
First large-scale mass evaluation of Target Dependent Sentiment Analysis methods across six English datasets is performed.
Code, a model zoo, and Jupyter Notebooks for Target Dependent Sentiment Analysis are released on GitHub.
Replication or reproduction experiments should consider multiple datasets and release documented code to reduce barriers to repeatability and generalisability.
Vector-based multi-head attention pooling includes max pooling, mean pooling, and scalar self-attention as special cases.
Penalization terms are introduced to reduce redundancy in multi-head attention.
The proposed model achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple datasets for natural language inference, author profiling, and sentiment classification.
The generalized pooling approach can be easily implemented for problems beyond those evaluated.
An end-to-end system answers multiple-choice questions using semi-structured tables as knowledge.
The system first retrieves the tables most similar to a given question.
The system then measures relevance between the question and candidate table cells and selects the most related cell as the answer source.
The system achieves performance superior to prior state-of-the-art on the TabMCQ dataset.
A hierarchical attention network weighs the importance of various linguistic information and learns mutual attention between a document and linguistic information for stance detection.
The hierarchical attention neural model fully employs diverse linguistic information to construct document representations for stance detection.
The hierarchical attention neural model demonstrates effectiveness for stance detection on two benchmark datasets.
A comprehensive survey of deep neural network architectures for named entity recognition is provided.
Deep neural network architectures for named entity recognition are contrasted with feature-engineered and other supervised or semi-supervised approaches.
Neural networks achieve improvements over previous named entity recognition methods.
Incorporating lessons from feature-based named entity recognition systems can yield further improvements in neural named entity recognition performance.
A paraphrastic sentence fusion model jointly performs sentence fusion and paraphrasing using a skip-gram word embedding model at the sentence level.
The paraphrastic sentence fusion model improves information coverage and abstractiveness of generated sentences.
An optimal solution for the classical summary length limit problem is proposed.
The methods achieve significant improvements over state-of-the-art methods in multi-document abstractive summarization.
A reordering RNN that shares the input encoder with the decoder and is trained jointly with a multi-task loss predicts permutations of input words following the target language word order.
An Inversion Transduction Grammar (ITG) transition system is combined with an RNN to parse inputs into trees for reordering.
The ITG‑RNN reordering model achieves superior reordering accuracy without any feature engineering.
The ITG‑RNN reordering model attains comparable reordering accuracy using substantially less training data and decodes significantly faster than a strong attention‑based RNN baseline.
LCQMC is a large-scale Chinese question matching corpus that focuses on intent matching rather than paraphrase.
LCQMC was constructed by collecting question pairs with a search engine, filtering irrelevant pairs using Wasserstein distance, and manually checking the remaining pairs.
LCQMC contains over 260,000 question pairs organized into training, development, and test splits.
Baseline experiments on LCQMC demonstrate its high quality and provide solid baseline performance for sentence matching methods.
Human-based intrinsic evaluation found that PASS-generated texts were rated slightly lower than human-written texts on fluency and clarity.
Human-based intrinsic evaluation found that PASS-generated texts were rated slightly higher than human-written texts on correctness of information.
Tailoring of PASS-generated texts was accurately recognized in most cases by participants.
Participants struggled to correctly identify whether a text was written by a human or by PASS.
Investigation of whether tailoring affects perceived text quality yielded no significant results, possibly due to negative preconceptions about computer-generated texts.
Weak signals are defined for training deep neural networks to detect claim sentences.
The query is relaxed, which increases coverage of detected claim sentences.
The system generalizes from the weak signal and attains higher precision and coverage than previously reported methods.
The system is adapted to identify argumentative sentences in heterogeneous web texts, achieving performance comparable to a supervised baseline.
A multi-attention-based neural network (MANN) incorporating Highway Network and concatenated embedding features is designed for story ending prediction.
External knowledge resources are integrated into the multi-attention-based neural network for story ending prediction.
The extended multi-attention-based neural network with external knowledge surpasses previous state-of-the-art results on the story ending prediction task.
Hand analysis identifies specific traits of the multi-attention-based neural network that contribute to its superior performance on story ending prediction.
Hand analysis reveals the process by which external knowledge is obtained for the story ending prediction task.
Six poetic style classes ranging from parlando to lettristic decomposition are defined and related by gradual differentiation.
A model classifies spoken free verse poetry into the six poetic style classes using deep hierarchical attention networks that integrate text and audio.
The model reliably differentiates the six poetic style classes on a large corpus of German author-read post-modern poetry.
Analyses validate that the model’s decision-making process aligns with the hypothesized fluency continuum of poetic styles.
Analyses investigate the relative importance of textual and phonetic features for automatic prosodic classification of free verse poetry.
A character-based decoder that constructs word sequences from letter‑ngram vectors is introduced for historical handwriting recognition.
The decoder is integrated into a multilingual transductive transfer‑learning framework for recognizing historical handwritten documents.

A new dataset compiled from untapped resources covering the same domain and period as the target Italian Comedy registers, together with resources from other domains and languages, is created for training.
The system attains high character recognition performance on Italian Comedy data despite limited lexical overlap between training and target data.
The system attains high word recognition performance on Italian Comedy data despite limited lexical overlap between training and target data.
A method that exploits syntactic structure features to enhance humor recognition.
The method achieves improvements over humor theory-driven baselines.
Certain syntactic structure features consistently correlate with humor.
Experimental results show that humor can be regarded as a stylistic phenomenon independent of content.
Content-independent syntactic structures can identify humor and provide interpretability.
Sandwich Neural Network learns semantic and structure representations automatically without relying on parsers.
Adaptive Learning Sandwich Neural Network learns corpus-level weights for combining semantic and structure information.
Self-Attention Sandwich Neural Network uses an attention mechanism to assign instance-level weights to semantic and structure information.
Joint learning of local semantic and global structure representations yields competitive performance on multiple text classification tasks.
GenSense incorporates semantic relations between senses, relation strength, and semantic strength.
GenSense outperforms previous approaches on semantic relatedness tasks.
GenSense outperforms previous approaches on contextual word similarity tasks.
GenSense outperforms previous approaches on semantic difference tasks.
A hierarchical LSTM architecture models multiple sentences containing person names for police killing recognition.
Supervised attention mechanisms based on semantic word lists and dependency trees upweight important contextual words in police killing detection.
The combined hierarchical LSTM and supervised attention method achieves state-of-the-art performance on police killing detection.
A new F1-based evaluation metric for the Fake News Challenge stance-detection task is proposed, resulting in a different system ranking.
The original Fake News Challenge evaluation metric is shown to favor the majority class and overestimate the discriminative power of methods.
A feature-rich stacked LSTM model that matches the performance of the best systems on the Fake News Challenge stance-detection task and outperforms them on minority classes is introduced.
A new dataset for evaluating generalization of stance-detection methods is derived.
In-domain and cross-domain experiments using the new dataset assess the generalization ability of stance-detection methods.
A framework automatically generates responses to e-commerce product questions by ranking snippets drawn from reviews or existing question-answer pairs.
The framework includes a question-based response review ranking component that ranks review snippets without requiring labeled response reviews.
Distant supervision using existing question-answer pairs is employed to train the response ranking model.
The response ranking model leverages knowledge from question-answer pairs and the corresponding retrieved review lists.
Experiments on datasets from a real-world commercial e-commerce site demonstrate the framework's effectiveness.
A set of text classification datasets is constructed where training and test splits share no direct lexicon overlap but have similar language structure.
Various text classifiers exhibit a large performance drop on these lexicon-separated datasets, indicating reliance on keyword cues rather than compositional meaning.
Simple models equipped with proposed regularization techniques that discourage reliance on key lexicons achieve substantially higher classification accuracy on the lexicon-separated datasets.
Two new metrics that frame image description as a word recall task are introduced to quantify diversity of automatic image descriptions.
The new metrics enable evaluation of system performance on both the head and the long tail of the vocabulary.
Nine state-of-the-art image description systems are evaluated on the MS COCO dataset using the proposed diversity metrics.
Systems trained with maximum likelihood objectives produce less diverse output than systems trained with additional adversarial objectives, according to the proposed metrics.
Adversarially trained models increase the number of word types generated from the head of the vocabulary but not from the tail.
The compositional capacity of image description systems is assessed by measuring their ability to generate compound nouns and prepositional phrases of varying lengths.
A toolkit for measuring diversity and compositional capacity of image description systems is released.
A method for extracting tweets from social media platforms for corpus creation is presented.
A multilingual corpus for sentiment analysis and emoji prediction in Hindi, Bengali, and Telugu is created.
The corpus creation methodology is applicable to resource-scarce languages when speakers are active on social media platforms.
Multiple NLP tasks are performed and analyzed on the created corpus, providing observations about task performance.
A model for entity disambiguation combines local contextual information and global evidences using Limited Discrepancy Search.
The model starts from a complete solution generated by a local model and searches the space of possible corrections to improve the solution from a global perspective.
The search employs a heuristic function that prioritizes the least confident local decisions.
The search employs a pruning function that scores global solutions based on local fitness and global coherence among predicted entities.
Experiments on CoNLL 2003 and TAC 2010 benchmarks demonstrate the model's effectiveness.
CSE is a convolutional neural network model that generates general-purpose sentence embeddings.
CSE encodes sentences using both word-level and n-gram features, generalizing bag-of-words embedding models.
Sentence embeddings produced by CSE serve as effective initialization for downstream tasks.
CSE outperforms averaging word embeddings in transfer learning scenarios.
Initializing supervised learning models with pre‑trained CSE embeddings yields state‑of‑the‑art performance.
An evaluation methodology consisting of automatically constructed stress tests for natural language inference is introduced.
The stress tests enable examination of whether NLI systems make real inferential decisions.
Evaluation of six sentence-encoder models on the stress tests reveals their strengths and weaknesses with respect to challenging linguistic phenomena.
The evaluation identifies important directions for future research on NLI models.
Variant identification of verbal multiword expressions is modeled as a classification task.
Syntactic subtrees containing previously seen lemma combinations are extracted and classified using features relevant to morpho‑syntactic variation of verbal multiword expressions.
The proposed classification approach outperforms a baseline on a French corpus.
Triad-based neural network generates affinity scores between entity mentions for coreference resolution.
The network simultaneously processes three mentions to capture mutual dependency and logical constraints among them.
Using the generated affinity scores with standard hierarchical clustering yields state-of-the-art coreference results on the English portion of the CoNLL 2012 Shared Task.
The system relies on few handcrafted features and is easy to train and use.
It is the first neural network system to model mutual dependency of more than two mentions at the mention level.
The triad architecture can be extended to polyads of higher order to model dependencies among more than three mentions.
Three lightly-supervised representation learning methods originally designed for image processing are adapted to named entity classification.
A rigorous comparative analysis of the three methods is performed on two distinct named entity classification datasets.
The adapted representation learning methods achieve state-of-the-art performance on semi-supervised named entity classification.
One-shot learning methods outperform iterative representation learning approaches for named entity classification.
A mean teacher framework based method attains top performance and operates independently of the underlying task-specific model.
A method combines partial annotation learning with a reinforcement learning instance selector to address incomplete and noisy annotations in distant supervision for Chinese named entity recognition.
The reinforcement learning instance selector distinguishes positive sentences from auto-generated annotations in distant supervision for named entity recognition.
Partial annotation learning reduces the impact of unknown character labels in partially annotated training data for Chinese named entity recognition.
Two Chinese named entity recognition datasets for distinct domains are constructed using distant supervision.
The combined approach outperforms existing systems on the constructed Chinese named entity recognition datasets.
ROPs predict entities at each step of multi-hop knowledge graph paths using recurrent neural networks and vector representations of entities and relations.
ROPs model multi-hop paths of arbitrary length while updating entity and relation representations at each step.
ROPs handle different types of multi-hop knowledge graph reasoning within a unified framework.
ROPs achieve state-of-the-art performance on Knowledge Base Completion.
ROPs achieve state-of-the-art performance on Path Query Answering.
An online Bayesian change point detection algorithm detects topic changes from online LDA output.
Online LDA models shifts in topics in real time from news articles and social media streams.
Online LDA provides benefits over standard LDA for detecting changes in document streams.
Online change point detection algorithms provide benefits over offline algorithms for detecting significant real-life changes in document streams.

Graphene uses a two-layered transformation stage comprising a clausal disembedding layer and a phrasal disembedding layer with rhetorical relation identification to simplify complex sentences for Open Information Extraction.
Graphene extracts propositions as a two-layered hierarchy of core relational tuples and accompanying contextual information linked via rhetorical relations.
Graphene outperforms state-of-the-art Open Information Extraction systems in constructing correct n-ary predicate-argument structures.
Existing Open Information Extraction approaches benefit from the transformation process introduced by Graphene.
A model-free context-aware word composition model uses latent semantic information as global context for learning representations.
The model resolves word sense disambiguation and word composition within a unified framework.
The model yields consistent improvements over strong word representation and composition models across word, phrase, and sentence granularities.
A dynamic routing aggregation mechanism obtains a fixed-size encoding vector from sequence encoder outputs.
Two dynamic routing policies are designed to aggregate RNN/CNN outputs into a final encoding vector.
Dynamic routing refines messages based on the state of the final encoding vector.
The dynamic routing aggregation method outperforms other aggregation models on five text classification tasks.
LNEx uses gazetteer-derived statistical language models to evaluate observed n-grams in Twitter text as legitimate location name variants.
LNEx automatically filters and augments gazetteer location names to handle abbreviations, name contractions, and auxiliary content.
LNEx detects boundaries of multi-word location names in informal social media text.
LNEx outperforms ten state-of-the-art location name taggers on event-specific tweets, achieving a substantially higher average F-Score.
LNEx supports stream processing of location name extraction.
An alternative but equivalent formulation of the knowledge transfer mechanism in multilingual topic models is defined.
A multilingual topic model is created that requires only a dictionary for training, relaxing the need for parallel or comparable corpora.
The new model learns coherent multilingual topics from partially and fully incomparable corpora using limited dictionary resources.
A neural network model incorporates both the erroneous token and its context to generate a correction vector for Chinese word usage errors.
The model selects correction candidates by comparing the correction vector against a vocabulary and proposes the top five candidates.
Native speaker evaluation shows that the system can propose at least one acceptable correction within the top five candidates for the majority of cases.
This work is the first study on general-type Chinese word usage error correction.
The system enables non-native Chinese learners to self-revise their sentences.
A novel end-to-end neural model for large-scale parallel sentence extraction from Wikipedia is introduced.
The neural model operates in a language-independent manner.
The neural model is robust and highly scalable for parallel data harvesting.
The system collects parallel German-English, French-English, and Persian-English sentence pairs from Wikipedia.
Human evaluation demonstrates that the model collects high-quality parallel data.
A statistical framework is proposed to extend human evaluation results to other language pairs.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the German-English BUCC 2017 parallel sentence extraction task.
NMT models achieve lower character error rate than SMT models for historical spelling normalization.
NMT models are evaluated on historical spelling normalization for English, German, Hungarian, Icelandic, and Swedish.
Vanilla RNNs are competitive with GRUs and LSTMs for historical spelling normalization.
Subword-level NMT models with small subword vocabularies outperform character-level models for historical spelling normalization.
A hybrid method further improves historical spelling normalization performance.
A new neural network approach for Emotion Representation Mapping (ERM) is introduced.
The neural network approach outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods for ERM.
A refined evaluation methodology for ERM is presented.
The model yields results that are almost as reliable as human annotations, even in cross-lingual settings.
New emotion ratings are generated for 13 typologically diverse languages with near-gold quality.
A multi-stage action-based model performs morphological transformation and part-of-speech tagging using arbitrary units of input for character-level Korean morphological analysis.
A data-driven Bi-LSTM model achieves state-of-the-art word-level tagging accuracy on the Sejong Korean corpus among models that do not employ prior linguistic knowledge.
A data-driven Bi-LSTM model achieves state-of-the-art sentence-level tagging accuracy on the Sejong Korean corpus among models that do not employ prior linguistic knowledge.
A target-specific abstract-guided news document representation model is introduced.
The model uses a target-sensitive representation of the news abstract to weight sentences in the news content, selecting and combining the most informative sentences for market modeling.
Document representations derived from full news documents improve estimation of cumulative abnormal returns of companies compared to using titles and abstracts.
The model is especially effective when combining information from multiple document sources compared to sentence-level baselines.
Task-specific attention models are introduced for multilingual neural machine translation.
Task-specific attention models retain parameter sharing while allowing language-pair-specific attention specialization.
Target-specific attention yields consistent improvements in translation quality across all translation directions compared to fully shared-parameter models.
Target-specific attention improves translation quality in low-resource zero-shot translation directions lacking explicit parallel data.
A joint multi-task deep learning framework is used to detect and classify emotion categories across multiple genres.
The framework employs soft parameter sharing across genre-specific tasks.
Training on a multigenre emotion corpus enables prediction of emotions across various genres.
The joint multi-task model yields better performance across genres than single-genre training within the same neural network architecture.
The Grammar Matrix customization system is extended to handle clausal modifiers (adverbial clauses).
A typologically-driven analysis of clausal modifiers across languages is implemented in the Grammar Matrix.
Evaluation on test suites from five genetically and geographically diverse languages not used in development demonstrates high coverage and low overgeneration.
A recurrent neural network model incorporates a long-term aware attention structure for slot filling.
The model uses positional encoding that primarily considers the relative distance between words.
The model employs multi-task learning of a character-based language model and an intent detection task.
The model outperforms existing recurrent neural network models in discovering open-vocabulary slots without external resources.
The model achieves greater performance gains as the number of slots, including unknown words, increases in a multi-domain dataset.
The model attains superior intent detection performance compared to prior recurrent neural network models.
CALM is a coordination analyzer that improves conjunct identification beyond dependency parses.
CALM employs language model–based scoring and linguistic constraints to search hierarchical conjunct boundaries for nested coordination.
CALM splits conjunctive sentences at identified conjuncts to produce multiple simple sentences.
State-of-the-art Open IE systems experience substantial yield loss due to ineffective processing of conjunctive sentences.
CALMIE extracts information from the simple sentences produced by CALM, achieving higher extraction yield with a moderate precision increase compared to extraction from original sentences.
A detailed reproduction and ablation study of the Hierarchical Multiscale LSTM architecture is provided.
Simplifying certain aspects of the Hierarchical Multiscale LSTM architecture can improve its performance.
Linguistic units (segments) learned by various levels of the Hierarchical Multiscale LSTM are investigated.
The quality of learned linguistic units does not correlate with overall language modeling performance of the Hierarchical Multiscale LSTM.
iParaphrasing defines a task to extract visually grounded paraphrases that are different phrasal expressions describing the same visual concept in an image.
A novel neural network-based method with image attention is introduced to model similarity between visually grounded paraphrases.
The first experimental results on the iParaphrasing task are reported.
A large-scale data-driven empirical analysis of vulgar words in social media is performed.
Socio-cultural and pragmatic aspects of vulgarity are analyzed using tweets from users with known demographics.
Sentiment ratings for vulgar tweets are collected to examine the relationship between vulgar word usage and perceived sentiment.
Explicit modeling of vulgar words improves sentiment analysis performance.
A sentence weighting method for neural machine translation domain adaptation is proposed.
A domain similarity metric that evaluates relevance between a sentence and an entire domain dataset is introduced.
The computed sentence similarity is integrated into the training objective to weight sentences.
The sentence weighting method achieves significant improvement over strong baselines on both the IWSLT Chinese-English TED task and a task with synthetic training parallel data.
Sliced recurrent neural networks (SRNNs) enable parallelization of recurrent computation by slicing input sequences into subsequences.
SRNNs obtain high-level information through multiple layers while adding few extra parameters.
Standard recurrent neural networks are a special case of SRNNs when linear activation functions are used.
SRNNs train significantly faster than standard recurrent neural networks.
SRNNs achieve better performance than standard recurrent neural networks on large-scale sentiment analysis datasets.
A template-based approach combines syntactic features of a document for authorship attribution.
Parse-tree based features independent of document topic and reflecting authors' innate writing styles are introduced.
Using templates that include sub-trees of parse trees together with other syntactic features improves author attribution rates.
Dempster’s rule based combination of evidence from syntactic features outperforms alternative evidence-combination methods for authorship attribution.
The methodology maintains high attribution performance even when the true author is not included in the candidate author set.
A generic pluralizer for Bantu languages was developed that attains high accuracy in both coverage testing and random sampling.
The characteristic noun class tables for Bantu languages were redefined, expanding from 29 to 53 noun classes to address computational non-determinism.
Systematic pluralization analysis was conducted for seven Bantu languages spanning three Guthrie language zones.
Pluralization in isiZulu and Runyankore requires integrating syntactic and semantic information, indicating similar approaches across different language zones.
The generic pluralizer performs comparably to language-specific pluralizers for isiZulu and Runyankore.
A deconvolution-based global decoding model generates translations conditioned on structural predictions of the target-side context, freeing decoding from sequential order.
The deconvolution-based global decoding model achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art neural machine translation methods.
The deconvolution-based global decoding model demonstrates robustness to translating sentences of varying lengths.
The deconvolution-based global decoding model reduces repetition in translations by using target-side context instructions during decoding.
A methodology maps several publicly available dialogue act corpora to a subset of the ISO standard.
The mapped corpora form a large task-independent training corpus for dialogue act classification.
A domain-independent dialogue act tagger trained on this corpus can be applied to out-of-domain conversational data.
Training on multiple corpora enhances robustness of dialogue act tagging across different categories.
A system integrates fine-grained Question Classification with a Deep Learning model for Answer Selection.
The Question Classification taxonomy is modified to support Answer Selection.
A new Entity Identification component highlights entities to aid Answer Selection.
Question Classes serve as a strong signal for Deep Learning models in Answer Selection.
The system outperforms the previous state of the art on the TREC Answer Selection test set.
Information flow in multimodal processing is categorized with respect to cognitive models of human information processing.
Methods for combining multimodal representations are analyzed.
Benefits of multimodal grounding for various language processing tasks are examined.
A multi-task learning approach enables joint training of rumour verification and auxiliary tasks such as detection, tracking, and stance classification.
Multi-task learning improves the performance of rumour verification.
The relationship between dataset properties and the outcomes of multi-task learning models for rumour verification is examined.
Dependent gated reading bidirectional GRU network (DGR) models the relationship between document and query during encoding and decision making in cloze-style question answering.
DGR achieves highly competitive performance on the Children’s Book Test (CBT-NE and CBT-CN) benchmarks.
DGR achieves highly competitive performance on the Who DiD What (WDW) benchmark under both strict and relaxed evaluation settings.
Ablation and attention studies validate the effectiveness of the dependent gated reading mechanism in DGR.
A sequential pair-wise discriminator sharing weights with the encoder is used to learn sentence-level embeddings for paraphrase generation.
A loss function that penalizes large distances between true paraphrase embeddings and unrelated candidate embeddings is integrated with a sequential encoder-decoder network.
The learned sentence embeddings are evaluated on a sentiment analysis task.
The method achieves statistically significant improvements over state-of-the-art on paraphrase generation benchmarks.
The method achieves statistically significant improvements over state-of-the-art on sentiment analysis benchmarks.
A survey of automated fact checking research across natural language processing and related disciplines is provided.
Task formulations and methodologies for automated fact checking are unified across prior work.
Evidence is identified as an important distinguishing factor among automated fact checking task formulations and methods.
Experiments reveal that annotators diverge in language annotation tasks due to a range of ineliminable factors.
Focusing on reducing annotator disagreement can create generation goals that reward output farther from natural human-like language.
A new approach to using agreement metrics in natural language generation evaluation tasks is introduced.
A visually-grounded version of the Textual Entailment task, incorporating an image alongside premise and hypothesis, is proposed.
Blind and visually-augmented models of textual entailment are compared on a multimodal SNLI dataset.
Visual information is shown to be beneficial for textual entailment performance.
An error analysis demonstrates that current multimodal textual entailment models do not perform optimal grounding.
Multi-layer representation fusion (MLRF) fuses stacked layer representations in neural machine translation.
MLRF designs three fusion functions to learn a better representation from the stack.
MLRF improves translation quality over a strong Transformer baseline on IWSLT German-English and NIST Chinese-English tasks.
MLRF achieves a new state-of-the-art performance in German-English translation.
A novel semi-supervised approach utilizes large amounts of unlabelled data for disfluency detection.
A light-weight neural network extracts hidden features using only self-attention, without recurrent or convolutional layers.
Generative Adversarial Network training enforces a similar distribution between labelled and unlabelled data in the semi-supervised disfluency detection approach.
The semi-supervised approach achieves significant improvements over strong baselines for disfluency detection.
Parallel corpora for English and Ethiopian languages (Amharic, Tigrigna, Afan-Oromo, Wolaytta, and Ge’ez) are created.
Bi-directional statistical machine translation experiments are conducted using the created parallel corpora.
Bi-directional SMT systems achieve promising translation quality as measured by BLEU scores.
Morphological richness of Ethiopian languages has a great impact on SMT performance, especially when they are target languages.
A computational model estimates semantic similarity in the neural activation space.
The computational model achieves better performance than state-of-the-art word embeddings for semantic similarity estimation of very similar or very dissimilar words.
The computational model performs well on entailment and word categorization tasks.
Neural activation semantic models can contribute to computational models for certain natural language processing tasks while providing insights into human cognition.
A novel analogy between arrows and verbs is established.
Arrows are represented in terms of qualitatively different structural and semantic frames.
Arrow frames are resolved to specific interpretations using shallow world knowledge.
Arrow interpretation is investigated through linguistic, crowdsourcing, and machine-learning methodology.
Double Path Networks for Sequence to Sequence learning (DPN-S2S) integrates convolutional and self-attention pathways in both encoder and decoder.
The encoder of DPN-S2S maintains separate convolutional and self-attention representations through a double path architecture.
A gated attention fusion module in DPN-S2S automatically selects relevant encoded information during decoding.
DPN-S2S improves sequence-to-sequence learning performance compared to state-of-the-art systems.
Multi-label classification is framed as a sequence generation problem.
A sequence generation model with a novel decoder structure is applied to multi-label classification.
The proposed method captures correlations between labels.
The proposed method automatically selects the most informative words for predicting each label.
The proposed method outperforms previous work on multi-label classification.
SeVeN encodes relationships between words as vectors in a continuous vector space within a graph structure.
A pipeline that learns relation vectors by averaging word vectors and applying an autoencoder is proposed.
Encoding relational information in a dedicated vector space captures aspects of word meaning that complement those captured by word embeddings.
Clusters of relation vectors reveal relational similarities at a more abstract level than traditional word vector differences.
Semantic vector networks are effective for measuring word similarity.
Semantic vector networks are effective for neural text categorization.
A method identifies loanwords in monolingual corpora by generating semantically related candidates using cross-lingual word embeddings and a small bilingual lexicon.
A log-linear model combines shallow features such as pronunciation similarity and hybrid language model features to predict loanwords.
The method is applied to detect loanwords in Uyghur from Arabic, Chinese, Persian, and Russian.
The method outperforms baseline models for loanword identification across four donor languages.
Neural machine translation models that incorporate loanword identification results achieve superior out-of-vocabulary translation performance.
Prospective-Performance Network predicts the future reward of a partially-generated response during beam search.
Future reward is defined as the expectation that a partial response appears in the top-ranked responses of a larger-width beam search.
Integration of Prospective-Performance Network enables the decoder to promote high-potential results during beam search.
The method increases the quality and diversity of generated responses while maintaining inference efficiency.
A web-based framework enables efficient and scalable crowdsourcing of highlight annotations.
The framework enables comparison of automated highlighting systems' performance.
The framework provides more accurate and fine-grained evaluation of automated highlighting systems than existing performance metrics.
An end-to-end neural model unifies predicate disambiguation and argument labeling in a single step.
The model predicts semantic role labels for all word pairs using a biaffine scorer without relying on syntactic parse information.
The BiLSTM encoder is augmented with a non-linear transformation to differentiate predicates from arguments.
Semantic role labeling is modeled as a word pair classification task via a biaffine attentional mechanism.
The syntax-agnostic model achieves higher performance than syntax-aware SRL systems on the CoNLL-2008 and CoNLL-2009 benchmarks for English and Chinese.
This is the first syntax-agnostic SRL model that surpasses all known syntax-aware models.
A coherent Universal Dependencies approach to the core versus non-core distinction is proposed.
Inconsistencies in Universal Dependencies version 2 regarding the argument–adjunct dichotomy are demonstrated.
A relatively conservative modification of Universal Dependencies that resolves the identified inconsistencies is proposed.
Three general multi-task learning approaches are evaluated on eleven sequence tagging tasks.
Jointly learning all eleven sequence tagging tasks improves performance over independent or pairwise learning in roughly half of the cases.
Pairwise multi-task learning reveals which tasks can benefit other tasks and which tasks can be benefited when learned jointly.
Certain tasks are identified as consistently beneficial to other tasks, while other tasks are consistently harmed by other tasks.
One multi-task learning approach generates task embeddings that naturally cluster semantic tasks separately from syntactic tasks.
A web-based language evolution simulator predicts how a word in a source language evolves into a word form in a target language.
The simulator's method is language-agnostic and relies only on training word pairs without using external knowledge.
A representation learning algorithm creates a semantic space where clinically related documents are positioned close to each other.
Referral information is used to weakly supervise an LSTM network that learns the document semantic space.
Clustering of documents based on the learned similarity metric enables visualization of disease progression for individual patients.
The learned document representations support analysis of diseases at the population level.
