An approach detects a user's stance toward a topic by leveraging the user's stances toward other topics and the user's social media posts.
Factorization machines are used to model user preferences toward topics from social media data.
Users' social media posts provide useful signals for modeling topic preferences, enabling prediction of stances for silent users.
A method selects the contextually correct definition of an acronym without requiring per‑acronym training.
The method can be applied to many different acronyms using only a few instances per acronym.
The method compares the averaged context vector of an acronym’s expansion with a weighted average vector of the surrounding context words.
The method outperforms classical cosine similarity for unsupervised acronym disambiguation.
Word embeddings trained on a large scientific text corpus provide better performance for acronym disambiguation than embeddings trained on larger general‑domain corpora.
A dataset comprising tens of thousands of examples of thousands of ambiguous acronyms from scientific paper image captions with their correct definitions is constructed.
OIE performance is evaluated on scientific texts from ten disciplines.
Two state-of-the-art OIE systems are evaluated using a crowd-sourcing approach.
OIE systems perform significantly worse on scientific text than on encyclopedic text.
An error analysis of OIE on scientific text is provided.
A corpus of sentences and judgments for OIE on scientific text is released.
Multilingual addressee and response selection is defined as predicting an appropriate addressee and response for an input message in multiple languages.
Several knowledge transfer methods are presented that leverage high-resource language data to compensate for low-resource language data in conversational systems.
A new multilingual conversation dataset is created for evaluating multilingual addressee and response selection methods.
The presented knowledge transfer methods are shown to be effective for multilingual addressee and response selection.
A low-latency speech translation system adapted to lecture settings for several European language pairs is presented.
Neural machine translation is integrated into a speech translation framework with adjustments for runtime constraints and diverse input types.
One-shot learning is employed to add new topic-specific terms to the translation system.
Multilingual neural machine translation is used to increase the number of covered languages.
A goal-oriented dialog dataset containing code-mixed conversations in Hindi-English, Bengali-English, Gujarati-English, and Tamil-English is created.
Baseline performance of existing state-of-the-art models on the code-mixed dataset is established.
The dataset and baseline implementations are released for research purposes.
A model integrates tree learning and graph learning approaches to directly capture discussion structures for discourse act classification.
The model achieves higher accuracy and F1 score than the previous best model for classifying discussion discourse acts on Reddit.
An attention mechanism is employed to inspect interactions among the tree and graph learning components of the model.
DIDEC provides a corpus of spoken Dutch image descriptions paired with eye-tracking data for free viewing and description viewing tasks.
The corpus includes eye-tracking recordings captured while participants produce spoken descriptions of images.
An initial analysis of self-corrections in Dutch image descriptions is presented.
Eye-tracking data from the description viewing task is more coherent than eye-tracking data from the free-viewing task.
Variation in image descriptions (image specificity) is only moderately correlated across different languages.
Lingke is an information retrieval augmented chatbot that answers questions using product introduction documents.
Lingke employs a fine-grained pipeline to distill responses from unstructured documents.
Lingke uses attentive sequential context-response matching for multi-turn conversations.
HiDE provides fast access to hidden knowledge generated from all Medline abstracts.
HiDE enables real-time exploration of the full range of hidden connections generated by a literature based discovery system.
HiDE combines a graph-based approach for large-scale hidden knowledge generation with an inference algorithm that identifies the most promising information.
Weighted vector averaging outperforms context-sensitive models on most extrinsic supervised and unsupervised evaluation benchmarks.
Structural features encoded in RNN-based compositional models are useful for certain classification tasks.
An analysis of evaluation datasets identifies the aspects of meaning that the datasets measure.
The analysis identifies characteristics of various compositional models that explain their performance variance across benchmarks.
Various compositional models, ranging from bag-of-words representations to RNN-based models, are evaluated on several extrinsic supervised and unsupervised evaluation benchmarks.
Deep utterance aggregation aggregates previous utterances into a fine-grained context representation for response matching in multi-turn conversations.
Self-matching attention routes vital information within each utterance in the deep utterance aggregation model.
The model matches a response with each refined utterance and aggregates the matching scores attentively across turns.
The model achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on three multi-turn conversation benchmarks.
A new e-commerce dialogue corpus is introduced as a benchmark for multi-turn conversation response matching.
English Resource Grammar (ERG) is used to detect negation scope in sentences from pathology reports.
Incorporating ERG-based negation scope information into feature extraction improves classification of pathology reports for cancer laterality compared with NegEx.
Differences between NegEx and ERG negation detection results on pathology reports are analyzed.
CRST retrieves tweets containing claims for a given topic from Twitter in real time.
CRST includes a claim-oriented ranking module composed of an offline topic-independent learning-to-rank model and an online topic-dependent lexicon model.
The claim-oriented ranking module can be adapted to new topics without manual processes or external information.
CRST outperforms prior claim retrieval systems and argument mining systems.
The concept of a salient entity is introduced for deducing omitted entities in paragraphs.
Salient entities are computed by constructing a graph on identified entities without parsing and ranking it with standard graph measures.
Ranked salient entities are embedded in the context of sentences to deduce omitted entities for relation extraction.
Deducing omitted entities using salient entities improves relation extraction quality.
A detailed overview of approaches proposed for Open Information Extraction is presented.
Major challenges faced by Open Information Extraction systems are presented.
The evolution of suggested Open Information Extraction approaches over time is shown.
Specific issues addressed by Open Information Extraction approaches are depicted.
Commonly applied evaluation procedures for Open Information Extraction systems are critiqued.
Experiments assess the impact of punctuation marks on native language identification using multi-class classification, language-group classification, and cross-corpus evaluation.
Punctuation marks are shown to be persistent and robust indicators of an author's native language even at high proficiency in a non-native language.
Hidden Markov Model variants that use crowd stance and tweet timestamps as the only features can model true and false rumours.
Incorporating crowd stance as a feature improves automatic veracity classification of social media rumours.
Stance alone outperforms approaches that combine stance with content and user features for rumour veracity prediction.
Knowledge-aware Attentive Network (KAN) is a transfer learning framework that uses a knowledge base to enable cross-domain answer selection.
KAN includes a knowledge module that integrates knowledge-based representational learning into answer selection models.
Knowledge-based representations learned by KAN are shared between source and target domains, providing regularization and more general representations for new domains.
Empirical evaluation shows that KAN consistently outperforms strong competitors in cross-domain answer selection.
deepQuest provides a neural framework that supports quality estimation at word, sentence, and document granularity.
A lightweight sentence-level quality estimation method using basic encoders is introduced.
The lightweight method achieves faster processing compared to a state-of-the-art pre-trained approach.
The framework improves performance on document-level quality estimation tasks.
deepQuest is the first neural architecture designed for document-level quality estimation.
Applying quality estimation models to outputs of statistical and neural machine translation systems for European languages uncovers new challenges posed by neural MT.
A graph-based active learning model selects a small set of words for user rating in a personalized text retrieval system.
A complex word identification model trained on user-rated words is used to find texts containing a desired proportion of new, challenging, and familiar vocabulary.
The personalized text retrieval algorithm effectively identifies simpler texts for low-proficiency learners and more challenging texts for high-proficiency learners.

Scaled-up qualitative analysis of word vector neighborhoods quantifies interpretable characteristics of word embedding models, such as preference for synonyms or shared morphological forms as nearest neighbors.
Twenty-one interpretable characteristics of word embeddings are shown to correlate with performance on fourteen extrinsic and intrinsic task datasets.
The lack of correlation between certain embedding characteristics and task performance is explained.
The approach enables multi-faceted evaluation, parameter search, and a more principled, hypothesis-driven development of distributional semantic representations.
A gold-standard annotated corpus of adult-child dialogues with semantic role labels for both verbs and prepositions is created.
The corpus augments the Adam files in the Brown portion of the CHILDES corpora and extends the partial annotation from prior work.
Verb sense and argument labels in the corpus follow Propbank guidelines, while preposition sense labels follow Srikumar and Roth (2011) guidelines.
Inter-annotator agreement for the corpus is reported for sense, number of arguments, role label identity, and span.
The corpus is released in CHAT and XML formats for use in supervised semantic role labeling.
Bot#1337 is a skill-based conversational agent that uses a supervised dialog manager to select appropriate skills for response generation.
Bot#1337 implements skills for chit-chat, topic detection, text summarization, question answering, and question generation.
The supervised dialog manager design allows developers to focus on skill implementation rather than finite state machine based dialog management.
Bot#1337 achieved the top ranking in the 1st NIPS Conversational Intelligence Challenge 2017.
Three metrics are introduced for identifying topics that are highly correlated with metadata.
A substantial proportion of topics in models trained on two real-world collections exhibit high correlation with metadata regardless of model size.
Words that cause high topic-metadata correlation can be predicted.
Selective subsampling of predicted words reduces topic-metadata correlation, improves topic stability, and maintains or improves model quality.
A unified recursive neural network framework jointly models elementary discourse unit segmentation, tree structure construction, center labeling, and sense labeling for Chinese discourse parsing.
The framework enables end-to-end Chinese discourse parsing.
The parser achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Chinese Discourse Treebank dataset.
The released source code includes a pre-trained model, constituting the first open-source toolkit for Chinese discourse parsing.
The toolkit can be integrated into downstream applications without requiring external resources such as a syntactic parser.
LanguageNet disambiguates word senses using English–Chinese translation pairs from a parallel corpus.
LanguageNet learns vector space representations of words and trains a classifier to group words into sense clusters.
LanguageNet provides definitions, bilingual synonyms, and sense‑relevant example sentences for each identified sense.
LanguageNet assists second‑language learners in searching for different meanings and usages of a word.
Ve'rdd is an open-source online dictionary editing system for the endangered minority language Skolt Sami.
Ve'rdd enables community members to re-evaluate and edit grassroots dictionaries that have been edited by multiple amateur contributors.
Ve'rdd incorporates community editing activities into a state-of-the-art finite-state language description of Skolt Sami.
Ve'rdd integrates with existing Uralic language tools and infrastructures, providing a user-friendly interface that masks technical complexities.
A deep reinforcement learning method extracts short, readable, decisive rationales from input text.
A rationale-augmented classification model improves prediction accuracy for charge prediction.
Extracted rationales provide introspection explanations for model predictions, enhancing model transparency.
The system extracts rationales that are highly consistent with manual annotations.
The system achieves prediction accuracy comparable to attention models.
A system analyzes Arabic curricula and provides insights into their content.
The system allows exploration of word presence and surface forms in Arabic curricula.
The system provides contrasting statistics between curricula from different countries.
The system includes a facility to grade text according to a specified grade level.
The system gives feedback about the complexity or difficulty of words used in a text.
Syntax contributes positively to cross-genre authorship attribution but not to cross-topic or single-domain attribution.
Pure syntactic language models are ineffective alone for authorship attribution and require combination with other robust models.
A word-masking procedure that selectively masks nouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs is employed to assess their contribution to stylometric attribution.
Common nouns and proper nouns heavily influence authorship attribution on the IMDB1M review dataset, highlighting strong topic interference.

On the Guardian10 cross-domain dataset, certain common nouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs aid stylometric attribution, while proper nouns primarily reflect content and benefit from being fully masked.
Sensala is an open source framework that provides the logical meaning of natural language text.
Sensala's theoretical foundation is a lambda calculus extended with exception handling.

Sensala uses contexts, continuations, events, and dependent types to handle complex linguistic phenomena such as donkey anaphora, verb phrase anaphora, propositional anaphora, presuppositions, and implicatures.
A Chinese writing correction system for learning Chinese as a foreign language generates multiple correction suggestions for erroneous input sentences.
The system retrieves example Chinese sentences with English translations to help users understand correct grammar pattern usage.
The system is the first Chinese writing error correction system built on a neural machine translation framework.
Empirical evaluation validates the design choices of the Chinese writing correction system.
A system performs machine-driven annotations of legal documents to extract business constraints from unstructured regulatory text.
Natural language processing pipelines determine whether a regulation is relevant to a specific organization.
Natural language processing pipelines extract the set of requirements imposed by a regulation.
Natural language processing pipelines infer the regulatory intent of a law.
Cool English provides corrective feedback for essays.
Cool English applies a sequence-to-sequence model to grammatical error correction.
Cool English is trained on the EF-Cambridge Open Language Database (EFCAMDAT), a large learner corpus annotated with grammatical errors and corrections.
Cool English achieves competitive performance on publicly available test sets.
INCEpTION is an annotation platform for interactive and semantic annotation tasks.
INCEpTION incorporates machine learning capabilities that actively assist and guide annotators.
INCEpTION supports concept linking, fact linking, knowledge base population, and semantic frame annotation.
INCEpTION is a generic and modular platform targeting multiple research domains such as digital humanities, bioinformatics, and linguistics.
INCEpTION is publicly available as open-source software.
A slightly extended rule-based bridging resolution system is applied to newly released bridging-annotated corpora to assess generalisability.
The rule-based system shows performance degradation on out-of-domain corpora and low performance on certain in-domain corpora.
The performance issues are caused by two distinct phenomena—referential bridging and lexical bridging—being conflated under the bridging definition.
Filtering out gold or predicted coreferent anaphors before applying the bridging resolution system improves bridging resolution performance.
GR-GCN augments graph convolution networks by incorporating label dependencies in the output space.
GR-GCN achieves state-of-the-art performance on proprietary and external short text classification datasets.
GR-GCN demonstrates greater robustness to noise in textual features than baseline methods.
Writing Mentor is a free Google Docs add-on that provides self-regulated feedback to struggling writers.
Writing Mentor generates feedback using natural language processing methods and resources based on developmental writing features.
Writing Mentor’s feedback addresses writing sub-constructs including source use, claim and evidence, topic development, coherence, and English conventions.
Preliminary user analysis indicates that Writing Mentor is perceived as usable and potentially beneficial for writing improvement.
SetExpander expands a seed set of terms into a more complete set of terms belonging to the same semantic class.
SetExpander implements an iterative end-to-end workflow for term set expansion.
SetExpander enables users to select, expand, view, validate, re-expand, and store term sets, simplifying extraction of domain-specific fine-grained semantic classes.
SetExpander has been integrated into an automated recruitment system.
SetExpander has been integrated into an issues and defects resolution system.
A system that supports Japanese students in composing formal e‑mails to superiors was developed.
An investigation of the importance of formal e‑mail communication in Japan and the requirements for successful e‑mail composition was conducted.
The system was evaluated through a twofold assessment.
The evaluation indicated generally positive outcomes and identified additional ways to improve the system.
An end-to-end information retrieval system uses domain-specific custom language models to expand search terms.
The system processes multi-lingual, jargon-rich unstructured text while enforcing privacy compliance.
A novel statistical approach evaluates word embeddings, enabling monitoring of the models in production.
The system is deployed for risk management in the financial sector.
The approach is applicable to domains beyond finance.
A general multilingual model framework for NLU models enables faster bootstrapping of new language models and reduces development effort per language.
Evaluation of various deep learning architectures reveals their effect on multilingual NLU model performance.
Multilingual NLU models achieve comparable or superior performance to monolingual models on language-specific test data while requiring reduced feature engineering and maintenance effort.
Word prisms learn orthogonal transformations to linearly combine source word embeddings.
Word prisms provide efficient inference for task-specific meta-embeddings.
Word prisms improve performance on six extrinsic NLP tasks compared to other meta-embedding methods.
Annobot enables dataset annotation and creation through conversational interaction with a chatbot.
Annobot supports data labeling for binary, multi-class, multi-label classification, regression, machine translation, question answering, and text summarization tasks.
Annobot provides pre-annotation, active sampling, online learning, and real-time inter-annotator agreement features.
Annobot integrates with the Facebook Messenger platform.
A usability experiment demonstrates that Annobot offers advantages over existing labeling tools.
Deep visual features extracted from images in web snippets are used as a complementary view alongside textual features for search result clustering.
A multiobjective clustering technique (AMOSA) jointly optimizes a cluster quality measure and an agreement-disagreement index across the visual and textual views.
Variable-length solution representations and a wide range of permutation operators are introduced to automatically detect the number of clusters during the clustering process.
The method generates a set of alternative partitionings on the final Pareto front using the multi-view multiobjective technique.
Experimental evaluation on benchmark search result clustering datasets demonstrates that combining visual and textual views improves clustering performance.
A hybrid neural architecture quantifies model uncertainty using Bayesian weight approximation.
The hybrid architecture substantially increases inference speed at test time.
The hybrid architecture is applied to a clinical dialogue comprehension task.
The approach enables active learning by selecting underrepresented samples for more optimal model updates with new data.
A neural model scopes task-relevant sentences in large documents for conversational agents.
Applying the model as a preprocessing step improves precision of intent detection and entity extraction in an email-based meeting scheduling assistant without decreasing recall.
The scoping approach enables component-level analysis in large documents, such as signature block identification.

An online Readability Leveled Arabic Thesaurus interface provides possible lemmas, roots, English glosses, related Arabic words and phrases, and readability levels on a five-level scale for a given input word.
The interface integrates multiple existing Arabic resources and processing tools.
The system enables Arabic speakers and learners to benefit from advances in Arabic computational linguistics technologies.
An algorithm iteratively refines and improves both word-level and character-level embedding models.
The algorithm outperforms existing algorithms on multiple word similarity datasets.
The algorithm can be successfully applied to job title normalization in e-recruitment, addressing out-of-vocabulary words.
A method converts continuous word embeddings into binary representations while preserving semantic and relational knowledge.
The binary embedding method significantly reduces embedding file size, enabling deployment on devices with limited storage.
Different binary embedding approaches are designed to suit downstream tasks with varying contextual and semantic information requirements.
Experiments show that binary embeddings achieve comparable performance on downstream tasks despite substantial size reduction and minor changes in evaluation metrics.
A detailed analysis of the characteristics of enterprise text data is presented.
Unsupervised methods for cleaning enterprise text repositories after automatically segregating domain terms from true noise terms are proposed.
Identified noise terms in enterprise text are corrected in a contextual fashion.
Manual evaluation of error corrections is performed on several standard datasets, including hate‑speech detection datasets with deliberate distortion.
Classification accuracy is enhanced after applying the noise‑correction method.
WOMBAT provides unified access to word embeddings from code.
WOMBAT supports scalable access to large word embedding collections.
WOMBAT enables robust and reproducible preprocessing of word embeddings.
Code using WOMBAT for accessing word embeddings is cleaner, more readable, and easier to reuse than code using standard in-memory methods.
WOMBAT allows fast evaluation of large word embedding collections on a SemEval sentence similarity task on a standard notebook computer.
MARUPA leverages user paraphrasing feedback to create annotated training examples for failing utterances in dialog systems.
MARUPA generates new training data automatically without manual annotation effort.
Retraining a dialog system with MARUPA-generated data improves accuracy for long-tail utterances.
Retraining a dialog system with MARUPA-generated data improves coverage for long-tail utterances.
The approach is evaluated in a commercial dialog system across multiple domains and three languages.
Task-aware, end-to-end compression jointly compresses word embeddings while learning NLU tasks.
The compression techniques enable neural NLU models to be executed on resource-constrained devices.
The compression approach achieves extreme compression with minimal predictive performance loss.
The compression approach outperforms a range of baseline methods.
Analysis indicates that incorporating downstream task signals is important for effective compression with minimal degradation.
LTV provides a website and API that generate labeled topic classifications based on the Dewey Decimal Classification.
nnDDC is a largely language-independent neural network classifier for the Dewey Decimal Classification optimized using a wide range of linguistic features.
nnDDC is evaluated on up to 40 languages to demonstrate its language independence.
A topic model derived from nnDDC generates probability distributions over semantic units at sense, word, and text levels.
Each dimension of the vector representation produced by the topic model is uniquely labeled by a Dewey Decimal Classification class.
The neural network-based Classifier-Induced Semantic Space (nnCISS) is introduced.
The task of generating a test set with high semantic diversity for NLU evaluation in dialog systems is introduced.
An approach that extracts high-traffic utterance patterns and selects utterances from different regions of the embedding space is proposed to achieve high semantic diversity.

Three selection strategies—clustering of utterances in the embedding space, solving a maximum distance optimization problem, and heuristic sampling such as random uniform sampling and popularity—are evaluated for semantic diversity.
A greedy maximum sum of distance solver yields the highest semantic and lexical diversity among the evaluated strategies while maintaining comparable runtime.
AnlamVer is a Turkish semantic model evaluation dataset that provides separate similarity and relatedness scores for each word pair.
AnlamVer contains 500 Turkish word pairs annotated by 12 human subjects with distinct similarity and relatedness scores.
AnlamVer is designed to evaluate distributional semantic models across multiple word attributes, including frequency, morphology, concreteness, and relation types such as synonymy and antonymy.
AnlamVer balances word-pair frequencies to enable assessment of model robustness to out-of-vocabulary and rare words in Turkish.
Explicit modeling of cognitive appraisal dimensions of events is incorporated into emotion classification models.
High-quality appraisal dimension annotations of event descriptions improve classification of discrete emotion categories.
A corpus of appraisal-annotated emotion-associated event descriptions is released publicly.
A highly contextual model of event salience employs a rich event representation, integrates document-level information, and enables interactions between latent event encodings.
The model attains state-of-the-art performance on a standard event salience dataset.
A new evaluation metric for event salience addresses flaws in prior evaluation methodologies.
A pre-trained language model based active learning approach for sentence matching is introduced.
The approach uses linguistic criteria derived from a pre-trained language model to measure and select instances for annotation.
The approach achieves greater accuracy with fewer labeled training instances.
A method automatically detects misspellings from product images.
A large-scale corpus of product images with misspellings is curated.
A rich set of features for misspelling detection from product images is defined.
A novel model leverages importance weighting to account for within-class distributional variance in misspelling detection.
The method achieves a relative improvement in F1 score on both the curated corpus and an out-of-domain public dataset.
The method reduces reliance on large-scale custom dictionaries for misspelling detection.
A dataset of sentence simplification pairs with surrounding Wikipedia context is constructed.
A model learns distinct influences of preceding and following sentences and incorporates them into an enhanced transformer architecture for sentence simplification.
The model achieves higher SARI scores than baseline models, demonstrating that document context improves sentence simplification.
Ablation experiments show that using only preceding sentences or only following sentences as context each yields significant simplification improvements.
An end-to-end differentiable model integrates natural logic formalism with subsymbolic vector representations and neural components.
The model adapts module networks to implement natural logic operations.
A memory component is incorporated to capture contextual information within the model.
The framework can model monotonicity-based reasoning, outperforming baseline neural networks that lack an inductive bias for monotonicity.
The model remains robust when transferred from upward to downward inference.
Analyses demonstrate that the model's subcomponents improve intermediate aggregation performance.
A penalty-based loss re-estimation method regulates the attention learning process in implicit discourse relation classification.
The method integrates penalty coefficients into loss computation based on overstability of attention weight distributions.
Experiments on the Penn Discourse Treebank demonstrate that the loss re-estimation method improves performance of various attention mechanisms.
The loss re-estimation method achieves performance competitive with state-of-the-art methods on implicit discourse relation classification.
A two-phase prototypical network with prototype attention alignment and triplet loss dynamically recognizes novel relations with few support instances.
The two-phase prototypical network avoids catastrophic forgetting when learning novel relations.
Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model for incremental few-shot relation classification.
Knowledge Enhanced Distant Data Augmentation (KnowDis) provides a data augmentation framework for event causality detection.
KnowDis augments training data using lexical and causal commonsense knowledge via distant supervision.
KnowDis improves event causality detection performance compared to previous methods when using automatically labeled training data.
Scalable Universal Dependency treebank synthesis techniques exploit large unlabeled multilingual text via language representation models.
A data augmentation technique uses synthetic treebanks to improve production-grade dependency parsers.
Synthetic treebanks are generated by a state-of-the-art biaffine parser adapted with pretrained Transformer models such as Multilingual BERT.
The adapted biaffine parser achieves higher labeled attachment scores on multiple languages.
Production parsers trained with increasingly large synthetic treebanks attain higher labeled attachment scores than parsers trained only on originally annotated Universal Dependency treebanks.
Active DOP is a language-independent treebank annotation tool that supports rich annotations with discontinuous constituents and function tags.
Active DOP uses an exemplar-based parsing model that learns online from each newly annotated sentence during annotation.
Active DOP enables experimentation with active learning methods to accelerate annotation in a naturalistic setting.
Active DOP measures actual annotation costs and tracks specific user interactions during annotation.
Active DOP is suitable for annotation scenarios with only a limited seed treebank or a radically different domain.
A comparative study evaluates several model-agnostic noise-robust methods on three text classification datasets using three popular classification models.
Model-agnostic noise-robust methods achieve higher classification accuracy than baselines without requiring modifications to the network architecture.
This evaluation constitutes the first comprehensive assessment of model-agnostic loss methods for handling inherent label noise in large-scale text classification.
Hierarchical Attention Mechanism incorporates acoustic-model confidence scores when integrating N-best ASR hypotheses for spoken language understanding.
Multi-task learning framework integrates multiple ASR hypotheses and additional related tasks for domain and intent classification in spoken language understanding.

Multi-task learning with Hierarchical Attention Mechanism yields higher domain and intent classification performance than using a single best hypothesis or existing hypothesis integration or reranking methods.
Decoding hidden representations reveals that the model can recover correct transcriptions by combining fragmented information across hypotheses and identifying frequent ASR error patterns.
The model can rewrite user queries to improve understanding, demonstrating knowledge broadcasting through multi-task learning.
A BERT-based model performs integrated analysis of cohesion in Japanese texts.
The model outperforms prior work on zero anaphora resolution in Japanese.
The model outperforms prior work on coreference resolution in Japanese.
Coreference resolution is shown to differ in nature from other cohesion tasks and requires separate treatment.
The integrated analysis includes predicate-argument structures for nominal predicates and bridging anaphora relations in Japanese.
Schema Aware Semantic Reasoning Framework translates natural language queries into a sequence of tasks for an ontology reasoner.
Schema Aware Semantic Reasoning Framework is integrated with the ontology-based question-answering system ATHENA.
Experiments on four business intelligence query benchmarks show that the framework substantially improves accuracy for handling BI queries.
An atrous convolution based knowledge graph embedding method is introduced.
The method increases feature interactions by using atrous convolutions.
The method employs residual learning to mitigate information forgetting and vanishing/exploding gradient issues.
The method has a simpler structure and higher parameter efficiency than existing deep neural network based methods.
The method achieves better performance than state-of-the-art methods on most evaluation metrics across six benchmark datasets.
A reconstruction training approach learns global relationships among words for word embedding reconstruction.
The approach reduces the model size of pre-trained word embeddings by a large factor while preserving quality.
The approach improves the performance of subword-based reconstruction models on word similarity benchmarks.
DART is a lightweight annotation tool for labeling structured data with textual descriptions.
DART is an interactive application that reduces human effort when annotating large quantities of structured data such as tables or tree structures.
DART employs a backend sequence-to-sequence model to iteratively analyze annotated labels and improve sampling of unlabeled data.
A simulation experiment shows that DART reduces the total number of annotations needed by using active learning and automatically suggesting relevant labels.
State-of-the-art embedding-based entity alignment methods are evaluated under varying seed mapping sizes and biases in an industrial setting.
A new industrial benchmark for entity alignment, extracted from two heterogeneous medical knowledge graphs, is introduced and evaluated.
Experimental analysis identifies advantages and disadvantages of embedding-based entity alignment methods for industrial deployment.
A BERT-based dual embedding model encodes contextual words and learns dual embeddings for Chinese idioms.
The model matches each candidate idiom embedding with the hidden representation of the blank in the context.
The model matches each candidate idiom embedding with hidden representations of all context tokens via context pooling.
The model uses two separate idiom embeddings for the two matching operations.
The model achieves performance superior to existing state-of-the-art on a Chinese idiom cloze test dataset.
Ablation experiments show that both context pooling and dual embedding each contribute to the performance improvement.

Contrastive Zero-Shot Learning with Adversarial Attack (CZSL-Adv) uses a contrastive loss to map slot value contextual representations to corresponding slot description representations for cross-domain slot filling.
CZSL-Adv incorporates an adversarial attack training strategy to improve model robustness for slot filling.
CZSL-Adv achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines in zero-shot and few-shot slot filling settings.
A computational model incorporates local discourse context to classify argument components in multi‑party classroom discussions.
A computational model incorporates speaker context to classify argument components in multi‑party classroom discussions.
Both local discourse context and speaker context improve argument component classification performance, with the magnitude of improvement depending on context size and position.
A hierarchical classification framework extracts relations in a top-down manner for distant supervised relation extraction.
The framework uses a hierarchically-refined representation method to achieve hierarchy-specific representations.
The framework introduces a top-down classification strategy instead of training a set of local classifiers.
Experiments on the NYT dataset show that the approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods, particularly addressing the long-tail problem.
Fast word predictor performs efficient next-word prediction on mobile devices.
Fast word predictor achieves comparable prediction accuracy to standard neural networks while using substantially less memory.
Fast word predictor provides orders-of-magnitude faster inference on middle-end mobile devices.
Fast word predictor is integrated as a feature in a chat application serving over one hundred million users.
A neural model generates delexicalized paraphrases.
Training data is created by pairing each input with multiple reference paraphrases that represent weak semantic equivalence based on annotated slots and intents.
Convolutional neural networks are applied prior to pooling on slot values, and pointers are used to locate slots in the output.
Generated paraphrases achieve high quality, improving exact match on live utterances.
Automatically generated paraphrases improve performance of intent classification and named entity recognition tasks.
TrainX performs Named Entity Linking for medical experts using a combination of Flair entity recognition and fine-tuned BERT-based Bi-Encoders.
TrainX employs active sampling strategies to select informative samples for annotation by medical experts.
TrainX can link entities against large knowledge bases such as UMLS.
TrainX supports zero-shot linking of entities never seen during training.
Zero-shot linking in TrainX mitigates the problem of rare and expensive training data in the medical domain.
Symmetric Local Search Network (SLSN) performs simultaneous detection and matching of emotion and cause clauses via local search.
SLSN comprises two symmetric subnetworks, each containing a clause representation learner and a local pair searcher.
The local pair searcher is a cross-subnetwork component that extracts local emotion-cause pairs.
Experimental evaluation on the ECPE corpus shows that SLSN outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.
Relational paraphrase sentences are used to enrich diverse relation expressions for relation extraction.
A task-specific paraphrase dataset is automatically constructed from existing labeled data.
A novel joint learning framework captures information from relational paraphrases to improve relation extraction.
The approach improves relation extraction performance on a widely used dataset, outperforming a strong baseline.
A reinforcement learning reward is derived exclusively from the source input sentence.
Reinforcement learning adapts the translator by rewarding translated sentences that contain more accurate slot tags.

The translation adaptation proceeds in two stages: an initial adaptation using a small in-domain parallel SLU corpus followed by a second adaptation using a large monolingual source-language SLU corpus.
The adapted translator generates an English SLU corpus that achieves high slot filling F1 and domain classification accuracy for Chinese-to-English language transfer.
The proposed method outperforms naive translation in both domain classification accuracy and slot filling F1.
An empirical study evaluates BERT's encoding of selectional preferences by correlating model-assigned probabilities with plausibility scores from the SP-10K corpus across five syntactic relations.
BERT shows no strong overall correlation between its probabilities and selectional preference plausibility scores across syntactic relations.
Certain head words exhibit strong correlation between BERT probabilities and selectional preference plausibility scores.

Masking all tokens except the head word yields the highest positive correlations between BERT probabilities and selectional preference plausibility scores, indicating that predicate semantics influences argument selection.
XplaiNLI is an interactive visualization interface for explaining natural language inference decisions.
XplaiNLI computes natural language inference using multiple methods.
XplaiNLI uses identified heuristics and biases of NLI models to generate explanations for their decisions.
A general purpose relation extractor represents relation surface forms using Wikidata descriptions.
The relation extractor architecture exploits implicit knowledge of a language model via a question-answering approach.
The proposed zero-shot learning system is evaluated on the FewRel 1.0 dataset in English.
METNet is a mutual enhanced transformation network for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
METNet includes an aspect enhancement module that improves aspect representation learning by incorporating contextual semantic features.
METNet employs a hierarchical structure that iteratively enhances representations of aspect and context.
METNet achieves effective performance on SemEval 2014 datasets for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
METNet performs outstandingly in multi-aspect scenarios.
Dependency-based syntactic features are combined with classical machine learning classifiers for multilingual irony detection.
Word embeddings trained on parsed data are evaluated on gold standard irony detection datasets for English, Spanish, French, and Italian.
Dependency-based syntactic features are incorporated into the Multilingual BERT architecture for irony detection.
Fine-grained dependency-based syntactic information is shown to be informative for detecting irony across English, Spanish, French, and Italian.
TeRo defines temporal evolution of entity embeddings as a rotation from the initial time to the current time in a complex vector space.
For facts involving time intervals, TeRo represents each relation with a pair of dual complex embeddings to handle the beginning and the end of the relation.
TeRo can learn and infer various relation patterns over time, overcoming limitations of existing knowledge graph and temporal knowledge graph embedding models.
TeRo achieves superior link prediction performance compared to existing state-of-the-art models on three temporal knowledge graphs.
TeRo analyzes the effect of time granularity on link prediction over temporal knowledge graphs, a previously uninvestigated aspect.
Sentiment signal distributions of reviews and their corresponding summaries are complementary.
A hierarchically-refined review-centric attention model is introduced to guide interactions between review text and summary for sentiment analysis.
The review-centric attention model utilizes user-written summaries more effectively for review sentiment analysis.
The review-centric attention model outperforms existing methods when the summary is generated by an automatic summarization system.
ESC is a newly created causal dataset.
A new causal label method based on core words is proposed.
Multiple candidate causal label sequences are evaluated experimentally to determine an optimal labeling method.
Existing causality datasets are summarized and their practicability and extensibility are analyzed.
MIML incorporates semantic class concepts to guide both initialization and adaptation in meta-learning for few-shot relation classification.
MIML connects instance-based information with semantic-based information to achieve more effective initialization and faster adaptation.
MIML attains comparable or superior one-shot performance to humans on the FewRel few-shot relation classification benchmark.
A web demo showcases the main characteristics of the AutoGOAL framework.
AutoGOAL automatically finds the best solution strategy for a given computational task.
AutoGOAL can be applied seamlessly to natural language processing tasks as well as structured classification problems.
Experimental evaluation demonstrates AutoGOAL's performance on several machine learning problems, including two recent NLP challenges.
AutoGOAL is designed for automatic machine learning but is applicable to any scenario with multiple possible solution strategies.
Graph Enhanced Dual Attention network (GEDA) characterizes complex interactions between sentences and potential relation instances for document-level relation extraction.
GEDA refines sentence representations generated by sentence-to-relation attention using a heterogeneous graph convolutional network before applying relation-to-sentence attention.
A regularizer based on the natural duality of sentence-to-relation and relation-to-sentence attention is introduced, with its weights supervised by supporting evidence of relation instances.
The model achieves competitive performance on a large-scale dataset, particularly improving inter-sentence relation extraction.
Neural predictions of the model are interpretable and can be easily observed.
TPLinker is a one-stage joint extraction model that discovers overlapping relations sharing one or both entities while avoiding exposure bias.
TPLinker formulates joint extraction as a token pair linking problem.
TPLinker introduces a handshaking tagging scheme that aligns the boundary tokens of entity pairs under each relation type.
TPLinker performs significantly better on overlapping and multiple relation extraction and achieves state-of-the-art performance on two public datasets.
Vision-based representations derived from visual information do not utilize all semantic information present in the neural network layers.
Image-based meta-embeddings constructed from computer vision models incorporate information from all layers of the network.
Image-based meta-embeddings encode a richer set of semantic attributes and provide a more complete representation of human conceptual knowledge compared to prior representations.
PairGCN provides an end-to-end graph convolutional network for emotion-cause pair extraction.
PairGCN models pair-level contexts to capture dependency information among local neighborhood candidate pairs.
PairGCN groups contexts into three types and propagates each type with a distinct mechanism.
PairGCN demonstrates effectiveness on a benchmark Chinese emotion-cause pair extraction corpus.
A deep generative language modelling approach to native language identification fine-tunes a GPT-2 model separately for each native language.
The approach assigns a native language label to an unseen text by selecting the fine-tuned GPT-2 model that yields the lowest language modeling loss.
The approach outperforms traditional machine learning approaches for native language identification.
The approach achieves the best results on benchmark native language identification datasets.
Lin identifies tasks in textual communication using an unsupervised method that leverages dependency parsing and VerbNet.
Lin achieves comparable or higher accuracy than supervised models on domains with large training sets.
Lin maintains strong performance on unseen domains.
Cross-lingual word embeddings are employed to detect cognates among fourteen Indian languages.
Context from a knowledge graph is incorporated to generate improved feature representations for cognate detection.
The cognate detection mechanism is evaluated on a dataset covering twelve Indian languages.
Evaluation datasets are created for the Indian languages Konkani and Nepali.
Cognates extracted by the method enhance neural machine translation quality.
Code, newly constructed datasets, and cross-lingual models are released publicly.
A medical textual entailment framework incorporates a domain-specific knowledge graph via a dual-encoding mechanism to obtain semantic and global representations of input medical text.
The framework enables modeling of question focus and integration of real-world background knowledge for medical textual entailment.
The framework achieves performance gains over state-of-the-art language models on the MEDIQA-RQE benchmark.
Probing layers of multilingual BERT reveals phylogenetic and geographic language signals across one hundred languages.
Language distances computed from multilingual BERT representations are used to infer language trees that closely match reference family trees according to quartet tree distance.
Distance matrix regression analysis indicates that multilingual BERT-derived language distances are best explained by phylogenetic factors and least explained by structural factors.
A novel measure of diachronic meaning stability based on cross-lingual representation variability correlates with published ranked lists derived from linguistic approaches.
Multilingual BERT representations enable computation of language distances that reflect both phylogenetic and geographic relationships among languages.
A system automatically extracts and curates crime-related information from multi-source digitally published news articles collected over a multi-year period.
The system employs a deep convolution recurrent neural network to extract crime-related entities and events from news articles.
The methods are capable of detecting crimes beyond known categories and actively maintain an updated crime ontology.
Experiments on a collection of crime-reporting news articles produce a crime register that records details of crimes across geographies and time.
The crime register can be used for analytical and reporting purposes.
A semantically consistent and syntactically variational encoder-decoder framework generates paraphrases with semantic consistency and syntactic variation.
Adversarial learning is used to enforce that the syntactic latent variable is free of semantic information.
An additional discriminator is employed to improve word-level and sentence-level semantic consistency in generated paraphrases.
The framework can generate multiple paraphrases that are semantically consistent yet syntactically different.
The framework outperforms baseline models on n-gram matching and semantic similarity metrics.
DoLFIn represents latent features as an unordered set each with a probability weight between 0 and 1.
DoLFIn allows straightforward computation of the probability that an input component supports the model's decision.
DoLFIn provides interpretable solutions for text classification tasks.
DoLFIn outperforms classical CNN and BiLSTM text classifiers on SST2 and AG-news datasets.
DoLFIn avoids the typical trade-off between model usefulness and interpretability.
A multimodal ensemble combines a state-of-the-art image-based classifier, an object-detection-based classifier, and a fine-tuned language model applied to OCR-extracted advertisement texts.
The multimodal ensemble establishes a new state of the art for symbolism prediction in image advertisements.
Centering and projecting pre-trained word embeddings using principal component vectors is equivalent to applying a linear autoencoder that minimizes squared L2 reconstruction error.
Experimental results verify the theoretical equivalence between the principal component post-processing step and a linear autoencoder applied to pre-trained word embeddings.
Retaining the top principal components improves pre-trained word embeddings without requiring additional linguistic resources or labeled data.
EDGE framework automates distractor generation for multiple choice questions.
Reforming Question and Reforming Passage modules in EDGE use gate layers to ensure generated distractors are inherently incorrect.
Distractor Generator module in EDGE applies an attention mechanism to control the plausibility of generated distractors.
EDGE outperforms existing models and achieves a new state-of-the-art on a large-scale public dataset.
A character-based neural model performs joint Chinese word segmentation and part-of-speech tagging using multi-channel attention over character n-grams.
The multi-channel attention module categorizes n-gram features into groups based on frequency and length and assigns context-dependent importance weights to each group.
The proposed model outperforms strong baseline models and achieves state-of-the-art performance on five benchmark datasets for Chinese word segmentation and part-of-speech tagging.
A beam search method generates diverse corrections by rewriting only the parts of a sentence that need correction in grammatical error correction.
The beam search method adjusts the search token in the beam according to the probability that a prediction is copied from the source sentence.
The method produces more diverse corrections than existing methods without losing accuracy in grammatical error correction.
A writing assistance system suggests lexical substitutions to incorporate more academic vocabulary.
The system draws substitution candidates from an academic word list and ranks them using a masked language model.
Lexical formality analysis improves the quality of the suggestions compared to a baseline that relies only on the masked language model.
VTAGI network performs visual-textual alignment for graph inference in visual dialog.
VTAGI includes a Visual and Textual Alignment (VTA) module that represents an image with integrated visual regions and corresponding textual concepts.
VTAGI includes a Visual Graph Attended by Text (VGAT) module that treats visual features with semantic information as graph nodes and learns relationships among them.
VTAGI addresses the lack of structural inference in visual dialog.
VTAGI outperforms previous state-of-the-art models on the VisDial v1.0 dataset.
RIVA is a pre-trained multimodal model that incorporates relationship inference and visual attention for tweet data.
RIVA controls attention-based visual clues with a gating mechanism that assesses the role of the image relative to text semantics.
A teacher-student semi-supervised paradigm leverages a large unlabeled multimodal tweet corpus together with labeled data for text-image relation classification.
Text-related visual features significantly benefit visual-linguistic models for multimodal named entity recognition.
The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on multimodal named entity recognition datasets.
TableGPT generates text from tables in few-shot settings by transforming tables into natural language prompts for GPT-2.
TableGPT employs a multi-task learning framework that includes a table-structure reconstruction auxiliary task to preserve structural information.
TableGPT employs a content-matching auxiliary task that aligns generated text with the source table to improve text fidelity.
TableGPT achieves superior performance compared to existing systems on multiple few-shot table-to-text datasets across different domains.
InterGCN jointly learns aspect-focused and inter-aspect relations using graph convolutional networks for aspect sentiment analysis.
A heterogeneous graph is constructed for each instance that incorporates aspect-focused and inter-aspect contextual dependencies.
An aspect-focused graph is derived by refining a dependency graph to emphasize syntactic dependencies between contextual words and aspect-specific words.
The aspect-focused GCN captures key aspect and contextual words for sentiment feature learning.

The inter-aspect GCN models representations based on an inter-aspect graph built from relative dependencies between aspect words and other aspects, enabling interactive extraction of inter-aspect relations.
InterGCN achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods and substantially improves over BERT on four benchmark datasets.
The task of multi-word lexical simplification, which replaces sentence fragments with simpler multi-word alternatives, is introduced.
MWLS1, a corpus of 1462 English sentences with 7059 human-provided simplifications, is contributed.
Plainifier, an automatic solution based on a purpose-trained neural language model, is presented for multi-word lexical simplification.
Plainifier's performance is evaluated by comparing it to human and resource-based baselines.
An automatic annotation method identifies spans of six embedded languages (Latin, French, English, Italian, Spanish, Greek) in historical German books from the Deutsches Textarchiv.
Quantitative analysis reveals differences between code-switching patterns in historical German books and those in speech and social media corpora.
Classifiers predict code-switching occurrences in the Deutsches Textarchiv corpus using only features of the matrix language.
A logic-based natural language inference system integrates event semantics, degree semantics, and lexical knowledge.
The integrated system attains higher accuracy than prior logic-based and deep-learning NLI systems on linguistically challenging datasets.
The integration of event semantics and degree semantics enables handling of diverse linguistic phenomena without sacrificing the strengths of either framework.
The system is evaluated on multiple natural language inference datasets that contain linguistically challenging problems.
A topic description task is introduced to detect the global semantic context of an image stream for visual storytelling.
A multi-agent communication framework jointly learns a topic description generator and a story generator via an iterative updating mechanism.
The proposed approach generates visual stories of higher quality than state-of-the-art methods on the VIST dataset, as shown by quantitative results, ablations, and human evaluation.
A recurrent neural network model that integrates fingerprinting predicts users' future opinions on unseen articles.
A dataset of event-comment pairs from six news websites is collected for evaluating opinion prediction.
The recurrent neural network model with fingerprinting achieves substantial performance gains on the collected dataset.

A graph-based parser with a local loss is introduced to incorporate single-grained word segmentation data and dictionary example sentences as weak supervision for multi-grained Chinese word segmentation.
A high-quality multi-grained Chinese word segmentation dataset of over 9,000 sentences from newswire and web texts is manually annotated according to a new guideline.
The proposed weakly supervised model achieves higher F1 scores than the previous state-of-the-art multi-grained Chinese word segmentation model on both newswire and web test sets.

GAAMA is a production question answering model that combines Attention-over-Attention, diverse attention heads, hierarchical transfer learning, and synthetic data augmentation while remaining computationally inexpensive.
GAAMA is trained on the Natural Questions dataset, which requires reading full Wikipedia articles and exhibits reduced lexical overlap between questions and articles.
GAAMA achieves higher F1 performance than the industry-scale state-of-the-art system on Natural Questions.
GAAMA transfers zero-shot to unseen domains, achieving respectable performance on the BioASQ benchmark.
GAAMA transfers zero-shot to unseen domains, achieving respectable performance on the newly introduced CovidQA dataset.
CovidQA is a newly introduced dataset for question answering in the COVID domain.
Curiosity-driven Question Generation is introduced as a novel text generation task that generates questions seeking information not present in the input text.
A methodology is described to derive data for Curiosity-driven Question Generation from non-conversational question answering datasets.
Several automated metrics are investigated to measure properties of curiosity-driven questions.
Model pre-training and reinforcement learning approaches are experimented with for Curiosity-driven Question Generation.
A qualitative evaluation of generated curiosity-driven questions is reported.
KeyGames is an unsupervised keyphrase extraction framework that applies evolutionary game theory and a consistent labeling problem to classify candidates as keyphrase or non-keyphrase.
pke+ is a Python pipeline that standardizes candidate extraction and evaluation steps for keyphrase extraction, enabling systematic and comparable performance analysis.
KeyGames outperforms most state-of-the-art keyphrase extraction systems and generalizes better to documents of varying domains and lengths.
pke+ pre-processing improves the reported performance of several existing keyphrase extraction systems.
Collaborating Relation-augmented Attention (CoRA) is a neural network that addresses both wrong labeling and long-tail relation challenges in distant supervision relation extraction.
Relation-augmented attention network uses sentence-to-relation attention on sentence bags to mitigate the effect of wrong labeling.
Collaborating relation features shared among hierarchical relations are incorporated to enhance relation-augmenting and balance training data for long-tail relations.
An auxiliary training objective guides the relation-augmenting process to produce more accurate bag-level representations.
CoRA achieves higher precision, AUC, and Hits@K than previous state-of-the-art methods on the NYT benchmark dataset.
Analyses demonstrate that CoRA handles long-tail relations more effectively than competing approaches.
CoNAN is a Complementary Neighboring-based Attention Network for referring expression generation.
CoNAN explicitly utilizes visual differences between a target object and its highly-related neighboring objects.
CoNAN determines highly-related neighbors via an attentional ranking module.
The speaker module in CoNAN incorporates visual difference features as additional input for expression generation.
CoNAN generates referring expressions that outperform state-of-the-art models on the RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, and RefCOCOg datasets.
Using gold information for extracting attachment candidates and omitting comparison to a full syntactic parser yields an overly optimistic assessment of PP attachment performance.
A realistic evaluation of various PP attachment systems is presented that uses fully predicted information as system input.
Comparison against a strong neural parser demonstrates that a full parsing approach outperforms modeling PP attachment disambiguation as a separate task.
Existing approaches to unsupervised dependency parsing are surveyed.
Two major classes of approaches to unsupervised dependency parsing are identified.
Recent trends in unsupervised dependency parsing are discussed.
Deep rectification-modulation network (RMN) transforms temporal sentence localization into a multi-step reasoning process by repeating rectification and modulation.
Rectification module corrects implicit attention misalignment that focuses on wrong positions during cross-modal interaction.
Modulation module captures frame-to-frame relations with sentence information to better correlate and compose video contents over time.
Cascading multiple rectification-modulation layers progressively refines video and query interactions for more precise localization.
RMN achieves state-of-the-art performance on three public temporal sentence localization datasets.
Extensive ablation studies provide comprehensive analysis of the contributions of the rectification and modulation modules.
The AMR-ConceptNet-Pruned (ACP) graph combines Abstract Meaning Representation graphs of questions with ConceptNet knowledge and prunes the integrated graph.
The ACP graph is used to interpret reasoning paths for CommonsenseQA questions.
The ACP graph is used to predict answers on the CommonsenseQA task.
ACP-based models achieve higher performance than baseline methods on CommonsenseQA.

A text style transfer system separates sentence reconstruction and style control into distinct modules, employing a Transformer-based autoencoder for reconstruction and an adaptive style embedding in the style module.
The adaptive style embedding allows continuous adjustment of style strength in generated sentences by modifying the embedding expression.
The modular design simplifies the model architecture relative to prior approaches that jointly perform reconstruction and style control.
The system attains superior style transfer performance and content preservation compared to previous unsupervised text style transfer methods.
Reference texts labeled with attribute identifiers are leveraged to enrich inductive biases for review generation.
Review generation is reformulated as a text-to-text generation task that incorporates selected reference texts.
A reference selection method is introduced for choosing references from a large candidate pool without requiring textual context.
Model complexity for review generation is improved through the proposed framework.
The proposed models achieve higher performance than previous approaches on both automatic and human evaluation metrics.
A controlled generation task is defined that expands a sequence of natural-language facts into a longer narrative.
Human-based evaluation metrics are introduced for the fact-to-story generation task.
A method for deriving a large training dataset for the fact-to-story generation task is introduced.
Auto-regressive unidirectional language models such as GPT2 achieve higher fluency but have difficulty adhering to requested facts in the fact-to-story task.
A plan-and-cloze model based on fine-tuned XLNet achieves competitive fluency while adhering to requested content in the fact-to-story task.
Directional graph convolutional networks jointly perform aspect extraction and sentiment analysis while encoding syntactic dependency information.
Directional graph convolutional networks achieve state-of-the-art performance on three benchmark datasets for joint aspect extraction and sentiment analysis.
Neural models that normalize text by considering similarities of word strings and sounds are proposed.
Variants of the neural text normalization model that consider only word string similarity or only sound similarity are introduced.
Leveraging word string similarity enables handling of misspellings and abbreviations in text normalization.
Leveraging sound similarity enables handling of phonetic substitutions and emphasized characters in text normalization.
The proposed models achieve higher F1 scores than a baseline model without similarity information.
A multi-perspective strategy enhances diversity of automatically generated music comments.
The proposed model generates diverse music comments based on different musical perspectives.
Experiments on two music comment datasets demonstrate that the model outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
A neural network-based approach automatically generates online advertisements from webpage text sources.
The approach is trained on open data collected from multiple heterogeneous subdomains, eliminating the need for manual labeling.
An ensemble method that combines multiple instances of a seq2seq model is introduced to handle subdomain heterogeneity.
The ensemble approach identifies existing subdomains and leverages them for advertisement generation.
Experimental evaluation on a Russian-language dataset demonstrates that the approach improves the quality of generated advertisements.
A variational autoencoder-based model for semi-supervised dependency parsing relaxes the tree constraint in both the encoder and the decoder.
The model learns fully arc-factored representations for dependency parsing.
Relaxing the tree constraint allows the model to circumvent challenges associated with tree-structured encoding and decoding.
Evaluation on multiple languages shows the model achieves higher parsing accuracy than previous semi-supervised dependency parsing approaches.
Evaluation on multiple languages shows the model parses faster than previous semi-supervised dependency parsing approaches.
Biased TextRank ranks text spans according to both their importance for language processing tasks and their relevance to an input focus.
Biased TextRank modifies the random restart probabilities in TextRank based on the relevance of graph nodes to the task focus.
Biased TextRank enables focused content extraction for text.
Biased TextRank is applied to focused summarization.
Biased TextRank is applied to explanation extraction.
Biased TextRank achieves improved performance on two datasets relative to prior methods.
Biased TextRank is unsupervised and easy to implement.
Biased TextRank is substantially faster and lighter than current state-of-the-art NLP methods for similar tasks.
Sequence-to-Sequence with Shared Latent Space (S2S-SLS) is introduced for formality style transfer.
S2S-SLS employs two auxiliary losses and joint training of bi-directional transfer and auto-encoding.
S2S-SLS with RNN or Transformer architectures consistently outperforms baselines, especially when data are limited.
Informal and formal sentences closely resemble each other, unlike translation tasks where languages have different vocabularies and grammars.
A hierarchical video description method first generates simple subject‑verb‑object sentences and then produces a coherent paragraph using the simple sentences as indirect input.
A video dataset annotated with captions at two levels of linguistic complexity is introduced.
A two‑stage network trained on the hierarchical linguistic representation generates paragraph descriptions that are more complex than those produced by existing one‑stage approaches.
A robust training method for machine reading comprehension models trains on soft labels.
The method incorporates label smoothing, word overlapping, and distribution prediction.
Applying the method to ALBERT yields state-of-the-art performance on NewsQA and QUOREF.
Reinforcement learning is used to directly optimize question generation-specific objectives instead of log-likelihood training.
Three reward functions are designed to target fluency, relevance, and answerability of generated questions.
Optimizing question-specific rewards improves automatic evaluation metrics for question generation.
Rewards that correlate well with human judgement, such as relevance, lead to real improvement in question quality.
Optimizing the answerability reward introduces bias that degrades question quality.
A dynamic topic tracker learns a global hidden representation for topics in knowledge base-to-text generation.
The dynamic topic tracker recognizes the corresponding topic at each generation step.
Recognized topic information is used to guide the generation process, alleviating the off-topic problem in knowledge base-to-text generation.
The dynamic topic tracker enhances sentence generation performance and significantly mitigates the off-topic problem.
A hierarchical loss correction strategy avoids fitting noise and enhances clean supervision signals in weakly supervised machine reading comprehension.
The loss correction strategy uses an unsupervisedly fitted Gaussian mixture model to compute weight factors for correcting the loss distribution.
The loss correction strategy employs a hard bootstrapping loss to modify the loss function.
In early training stages, noisy samples produce significantly larger loss values than clean samples.
The proposed loss correction methods improve model performance on weakly supervised machine reading comprehension datasets.
CHIME is a cross-passage hierarchical memory network for generative question answering.
CHIME extends XLNet with an auxiliary memory module comprising a context memory that collects cross-passage evidence and an answer memory that continuously refines generated answers.
CHIME produces more syntactically well-formed answers and higher precision than state-of-the-art baselines on the AmazonQA review dataset.
The memory module in CHIME introduces interpretability, as shown by qualitative analysis.
A Self-Review Mechanism infuses sequential information into a conditional masked translation model.

The Self-Review Mechanism inserts a left-to-right mask into the decoder of the conditional masked translation model and autoregressively reviews each generated word to decide replacement or retention.
The resulting model requires substantially less training computation than a typical conditional masked translation model.
The resulting model outperforms several state-of-the-art non-autoregressive translation models.
Via knowledge distillation, the resulting model surpasses a standard left-to-right Transformer model while achieving faster decoding.
A collection of transcription corrections and grammatical error annotations for the CrowdED Corpus of spoken English monologues on business topics is provided.
1108 crowdworker submissions of corrected transcriptions are shown to be usable for automatic transcription post-editing and grammatical error correction for speech.
Grammatical error detection models that concatenate pre-trained and contextual word representations as input achieve the highest performance on the CrowdED Corpus.
Adding additional features, auxiliary objectives, or extra training data from written error-annotated corpora does not improve grammatical error detection performance on the CrowdED Corpus.
GenWiki is a large-scale, general-domain dataset for knowledge graph-to-text generation containing 1.3 million text and graph examples.
GenWiki includes a human-annotated test set.
GenWiki provides a new benchmark for unsupervised text generation from knowledge graphs.
A multitask parsing model jointly performs syntactic parsing for projective dependency trees under CATiB and UD annotation schemes.
Partially created trees for one annotation are used as features for the other annotation in the model's scoring function.
The multitask parsing model reduces parsing errors on CATiB and UD compared to a strong baseline.
Error reduction is primarily attributable to sharing tree representations between tasks rather than sharing BiLSTM layers.
Teacher forcing causes exposure bias that makes sequence-to-sequence inflection models struggle to recover in unseen environments.
A training algorithm modification that more closely mimics test conditions reduces exposure bias and enables better generalization of sequence-to-sequence inflection models to unseen environments.
Hypernym prediction is formulated as a sequence generation task over WordNet taxonomy paths.
Training encoder‑decoder models to generate taxonomy paths improves direct hypernym prediction.
The hypo2path model achieves state‑of‑the‑art performance on hypernym prediction.
Neural Sentiment Forecasting (NSF) predicts the polarity of the next utterance in a dialog.
NSF incorporates a neural simulation component that generates a simulated next utterance from the preceding context.
NSF incorporates a sequence influence component that learns both pairwise and sequence-wise influence for sentiment prediction.
Empirical studies demonstrate that sentiment forecasting is an important task for dialog systems.
Empirical evaluation shows that NSF outperforms several strong baselines on sentiment forecasting.
A logic-guided semantic representation learning model for zero-shot relation classification is introduced.
The model builds connections between seen and unseen relations using implicit and explicit semantic representations derived from knowledge graph embeddings and logic rules.
The model generalizes to unseen relation types.
The model achieves improvements on zero-shot relation classification.
LaAP-Net predicts answers and corresponding bounding box evidence for text visual question answering.
Context-enriched OCR representation (COR) enables multimodal fusion to support answer localization.
LaAP-Net achieves superior performance on three benchmark datasets for text visual question answering.
GlobalGraph assigns each node a global type label and models long-distance node relations through global type label similarity.
GlobalGraph learns similarity scores between nodes and the question and models long-distance node relations through the sum of two nodes' scores.
GlobalGraph is a novel framework for knowledge base question answering that mitigates oversmoothing in deep graph neural networks.
Experiments demonstrate that GlobalGraph achieves superior results on two widely used multi-hop KBQA datasets.
Interactive key-value Memory-augmented Attention (IMAP) is introduced for image paragraph captioning.
IMAP tracks attention history and the decoder state update chain to reduce repetitive and incomplete image descriptions.
IMAP employs an adaptive attention mechanism that allows many-to-many alignment between image regions and caption words.
Experiments demonstrate that IMAP is effective on the Stanford image paragraph captioning benchmark.
Weighted domain-invariant representation learning (WDIRL) is a novel framework for cross-domain sentiment analysis that incorporates weighting to address label distribution changes across domains.
Existing domain-invariant representation learning (DIRL) models can be transferred to the WDIRL framework with minimal effort.
Empirical studies on extensive cross-domain sentiment analysis tasks demonstrate that WDIRL is effective.
Applying standard DIRL may degrade domain adaptation performance when label distributions differ across domains.
A new approach for script learning integrates external event knowledge from the ASER knowledge base via a knowledge retrieval stage and a knowledge integration stage.
