Three methods are proposed to integrate external event knowledge into the script learning model.
The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Multi-Choice Narrative Cloze (MCNC) task.
A modality enriched neural network detects metaphors.
Metaphor detection is framed as a modality shift.
Word sensorimotor scores are concatenated with word vectors as input to an attention-based Bi-LSTM.
Leveraging modality norms improves metaphor detection.
The model achieves higher F1 performance than prior methods on the VUA corpus.
Semantic Transformation bridges dense and sparse text representation spaces.
Semantic Transformation enables NLP research to shift from dense to sparse representations or to jointly use both spaces.
Semantic Transformation is effective for text classification tasks.
Semantic Transformation is effective for natural language inference tasks.
Feedback-weighted learning based on importance sampling uses binary user feedback to improve an initial supervised conversational question answering system after deployment.
Feedback-weighted learning improves over the initial supervised system, achieving performance close to a fully-supervised system in in-domain experiments on QuAC.
Feedback-weighted learning matches the performance of a fully-supervised system in out-of-domain experiments on DoQA.
Simulated experiments derive binary user feedback from gold annotations for document classification and conversational question answering datasets.
ForceReader is a BERT-based interactive machine reading comprehension model that incorporates Attention Separation Representation.
Attention Separation Representation addresses attention deconcentration caused by BERT’s combined input method.
ForceReader employs a Multi-mode Reading and Interactive Reasoning strategy aligned with the logical nature of reading comprehension tasks.
Conditional Background Attention is used in ForceReader to provide overall context semantics after attention separation.
ForceReader achieves a significant improvement over BERT on reading comprehension tasks.
Detailed visual analyses of attention in ForceReader lead to proposed strategies for explaining attention behavior.
Definition Frames are a matrix distributed representation extracted from definitions, where each dimension is semantically interpretable.
Definition Frames dimensions correspond to Qualia structure relations that uniquely define a term.
Definition Frames achieve competitive performance with other distributional semantic approaches on word similarity tasks.
A self-explainable model for NLP text classification constructs explanations concurrently with classification predictions.
The model extracts a rationale from the input text and uses it to predict a concept of interest as the final prediction.
The model provides three types of explanations: rationale extraction, a feature importance measure, and concept clustering.
The model can be compressed without applying complicated compression techniques.
The explainability approach is experimentally demonstrated on several well-known text classification datasets.
A heterogeneous approach for Chinese grammatical error correction combines a neural machine translation model, a sequence editing model, and a spell checker.
The heterogeneous approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on Chinese grammatical error correction.
The heterogeneous approach achieves state-of-the-art performance without relying on data augmentation or GEC-specific architecture changes.
Experiments evaluate all possible configurations of model composition order and number of correction rounds in the heterogeneous system.
An adaptation of the ERRANT scorer enables scoring of Chinese sentences for detailed analysis of each component's contribution to grammatical error correction.
A data augmentation method that combines crowdsourced paraphrase variations with similar questions from a knowledge community website is introduced for spoken language understanding.
The augmentation method generates a large number of synthetic training samples from a small seed set.
The augmentation method improves spoken language understanding accuracy.
Leveraging two sources of wisdom of crowds is effective for enhancing spoken language understanding systems.
Language Branch Machine Reading Comprehension (LBMRC) is introduced as an augmentation approach that groups passages in a single language with questions in all target languages for cross-lingual MRC.
A multilingual distillation approach is devised to amalgamate knowledge from multiple language-branch MRC models into a single model covering all target languages.
Combining LBMRC with multilingual distillation produces a model that is more robust to translation noise and improves cross-lingual machine reading comprehension ability.
The single multilingual model generated by the combined approach can be applied to all target languages, reducing training, inference, and maintenance costs relative to maintaining multiple models.
Extensive experiments on two cross-lingual MRC benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.
Two new metrics assess downstream reliability of pre-trained word embeddings.
Downstream reliability of pre-trained word embeddings depends on the evaluation metric, the handling of out-of-vocabulary words, and whether the embeddings are fine-tuned.
Attention weight distribution statistics and probing tasks are used to investigate word-level representations in Chinese BERT.
Chinese BERT captures word information.
Word-level features in Chinese BERT are primarily located in the middle representation layers.
Downstream tasks differ in their reliance on word features in Chinese BERT, with part-of-speech tagging and chunking relying most and natural language inference relying least.
Contrastive examples are generated from correct data-to-text sentences by applying rule-based replacements of frequent crucial terms such as "gain" or "drop".
Training data-to-text models with contrastive examples improves lexical choice without degrading fluency.
The performance of data-to-text models trained with contrastive examples varies with the choice of loss function, affecting different evaluation metrics.
Contrastive examples produced by specific rule sets further improve performance on the market comment generation task.
Human evaluation indicates that using contrastive examples enhances the quality of generated market comments.
A method identifies nominal coordination boundaries without training on labeled data.
The method uses pre-trained word embeddings to measure word similarities and detects coordination spans based on shared syntactic and semantic similarity of conjuncts.
The method yields good results in identifying coordinated noun phrases in the GENIA corpus.
The method achieves performance comparable to a recent supervised method for coordination of simple noun phrases.
Two anomalies in automatically generated questions for unsupervised question answering are identified.
A mitigation approach for the identified anomalies of synthetic questions in unsupervised question answering is proposed.
The mitigation approach improves unsupervised question answering performance across multiple question answering tasks.
Molweni is a machine reading comprehension dataset with discourse structure built over multiparty dialog.
Molweni contains 10,000 dialogs and 88,303 utterances sourced from the Ubuntu Chat Corpus.
Molweni includes 30,066 annotated questions, covering both answerable and unanswerable cases.
Molweni provides discourse dependency annotations in a modified Segmented Discourse Representation Theory style, comprising 78,245 discourse relations for multiparty dialog discourse parsing.
Experiments demonstrate that Molweni is challenging for current MRC models, as BERT-wwm experiences a substantial performance drop on Molweni compared to its SQuAD 2.0 results.
Factorizing arbitrary classification problems into a generic binary classification problem embeds the task notion into the transformer model.
The approach outperforms prior methods in few-shot transfer learning on small training data.
The approach can predict new classes without any training examples.
A multi-level alignment pretraining method is introduced for multilingual semantic parsing within a unified architecture.
Adversarial training is used to align the representation spaces of different languages.
Sentence-level and word-level parallel corpora are employed as supervision to align semantics across languages.
Multi-level alignment and semantic parsing tasks are jointly trained.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on a publicly available multilingual semantic parsing dataset ATIS and a newly constructed dataset.

A bracketing-based encoding represents any 2-planar dependency tree over a sentence of length n as a sequence of n labels, providing almost total coverage of crossing arcs in sequence labeling parsing.
Existing bracketing encodings for parsing as labeling can only handle a very mild extension of projective trees.
The method balances brackets and encodes arcs belonging to each of two planes, enabling near-complete coverage of non-projective arcs in sequence labeling parsing.
The linearizations improve labeled attachment score accuracy over the original bracketing encoding in highly non-projective treebanks while maintaining similar parsing speed.
The linearizations are especially suitable when part-of-speech tags are not used as input parameters to the models.
Text-to-SQL is formulated as a question answering problem using a unified machine reading comprehension model.
A BERT-based machine reading comprehension model is employed, leveraging intermediate training on other MRC datasets.
The unified MRC approach achieves competitive performance on the WikiSQL benchmark.
Several methods for taxonomy enrichment in resource-poor settings applicable to many languages are presented.
Novel English and Russian datasets for training and evaluating taxonomy enrichment systems are created.
A technique for creating taxonomy enrichment datasets for other languages is described.
Chinese lexical fusion recognition is introduced as a new task related to coreference recognition.
An end-to-end model jointly handles mentions and coreference relationships for Chinese lexical fusion recognition.
The model incorporates contextualized BERT representations and augments them with sememe knowledge from HowNet using graph attention networks.
A manually annotated benchmark dataset for Chinese lexical fusion recognition is created.
Experiments demonstrate that the end-to-end model is effective and competitive for Chinese lexical fusion recognition.
Detailed analysis provides comprehensive understanding of Chinese lexical fusion recognition and the proposed model.
Hierarchical planning is applied to generate building instructions at multiple abstraction levels in Minecraft.
Crowdsourcing evaluation shows that users are sensitive to the abstraction level of building instructions.
A balanced abstraction strategy that combines low-level and high-level object descriptions yields more favorable user responses than strategies lacking such balance.
A morphologically aware probability model jointly models lexeme translation and inflectional morphology for bilingual lexicon induction.
The model achieves higher accuracy than state-of-the-art bilingual lexicon induction methods in both supervised and weakly supervised settings.
Issues in bilingual lexicon induction caused by ignoring inflectional morphology are identified.
Three suggestions for improving bilingual lexicon induction are proposed.
Multitask learning with answer-aware supporting fact prediction guides the question generator.
A question-aware reward function within a reinforcement learning framework maximizes utilization of supporting facts in question generation.
The approach outperforms single-hop neural question generation models on automatic evaluation metrics and human evaluation metrics for quality and coverage.
The approach is evaluated on the HotPotQA multi-hop question answering dataset.
A simple language-independent system based on a combination of filters identifies occurrences of previously seen verbal multiword expressions.
The system achieves the highest averaged F-score across eleven languages among participants of a recent shared task.
The system attains the highest performance for both seen and unseen verbal multiword expressions.
Focusing on the identification of seen verbal multiword expressions is presented as a strategy to improve overall verbal multiword expression identification.
A survey of the current state of research on bridging reference resolution is provided.
Future research directions for bridging reference resolution are discussed.
Three new methods are introduced for supplying neural word inflection models with additional source forms to strengthen analogy formation.
Providing a Transformer sequence-to-sequence model with additional analogy sources in the input consistently enhances inflection performance.
The introduced methods achieve state-of-the-art inflection results across many languages, with particular gains in low-resource settings.
A method for combining the analogy-motivated approach with data hallucination or augmentation is proposed.
Combining the analogy-motivated approach with data hallucination or augmentation yields complementary improvements, especially when training data is extremely limited.
VICTR captures rich visual semantic information of objects from text descriptions for text-to-image multimodal tasks.
VICTR generates text representations that integrate textual and visual semantic information by training objects, attributes, and relations from scene graphs with Graph Convolutional Networks.
VICTR aggregates word-level and sentence-level embeddings to produce visual contextual word and sentence representations.
VICTR can be attached to existing text-to-image generation models and improves their quantitative and qualitative performance.
A neural model uses multi-task learning with coreference resolution to improve bridging reference resolution.
The model relies on a small set of corpus-independent features, enabling application across different bridging corpora.
The architecture achieves state-of-the-art performance on full bridging resolution for multiple corpora.
Evaluations show that the model works equally well on diverse bridging corpora.
Two strategies for copying low-frequency or out-of-vocabulary subwords from input source code are introduced for function name generation.
A model using the proposed copying strategies attains higher modified F1 and accuracy than a conventional method on Java-small and Java-large datasets.
HeterEvent graph network models heterogeneous relations in event chains using word and event nodes connected by word-word, word-event, and event-event edges.
HeterEvent graph network includes a message passing process that enables information interaction among homogeneous and heterogeneous nodes.
HeterEvent graph network explicitly models discontinuous event segments by identifying specific paths between corresponding nodes in the graph.
The ensemble model HeterEvent[W+E] achieves superior performance on one-step and multi-step inference tasks compared to existing baselines.
An unsupervised method learns compositional representations of multi-word expressions using a recurrent neural network composition function trained with a Skip-Gram objective.
The method can leverage large unlabeled text corpora for training.
The method can operate with provided multi-word expression boundaries or with boundaries predicted by a domain-agnostic part-of-speech pattern.
The method achieves state-of-the-art performance on coarse-grained semantic classification of the Tratz dataset.
The unsupervised version attains performance comparable to a supervised version and surpasses it in certain configurations.
A novel end-to-end robust framework for cross-lingual document retrieval is introduced.
The framework defines a smooth cosine similarity as a relevance measure between queries and documents.
The framework employs a Smooth Ordinal Search Loss as its objective function.
A theoretical generalization error bound is proved for the proposed framework.
Experiments demonstrate significant improvements over existing document search models on cross-lingual retrieval across multiple languages.
Subtree swapping with strong grammaticality constraints generates artificial dependency-annotated sentences for data augmentation.
The data augmentation method improves dependency parsing performance compared to previous proposals when using the same basic inputs.
Sampling sentences from resource-rich corpora under a low-resource distribution mimics low-resource languages for conducting low-resource experiments.
YodaLib generates Mad Libs style humorous stories by automatically selecting and ranking candidate words and sentences according to demographic background.
A dataset of Mad Libs stories filled by workers and annotated for humor on Amazon Mechanical Turk is compiled.
BERT is employed to predict location-biased word fillings for incomplete sentences.
BERT is fine-tuned to classify whether a sentence contains location-specific humor.
YodaLib achieves higher performance than a prior semi-automated method for demographic-aware humor generation.
YodaLib exceeds human annotators in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations of humor generation.
Adaptive knowledge distillation dynamically adjusts the contribution of multiple teacher models during distillation for low-resource neural machine translation.
An ensemble of teacher models can be distilled into a single student model for low-resource neural machine translation.

The adaptive knowledge distillation approach yields improvements over strong baselines in low-resource neural machine translation experiments transferring from six high-resource language pairs to five low-resource language pairs.
An isometry feature mapping based refinement method corrects pre-trained word embeddings by preserving the global geometry of all words.
A local tangent space based refinement method aligns original and refined embedding spaces to improve word representations without performing weighted locally linear combination twice.
The proposed refinement methods achieve superior performance over state-of-the-art baselines on standard semantic relatedness and similarity tasks.
A rule-based pipeline automatically identifies and labels personal information in learner essays across fifteen categories.
The pipeline replaces each identified personal item with an alternative item of the same type drawn from curated name and location resources.
Evaluation on manually anonymized essays shows that the system can identify the majority of personal information and achieves high inter-annotator agreement.
EBERT extracts robust latent representations for noisy ASR text classification.
EBERT processes all transformer tokens using a novel attentive embracement layer combined with multi-head attention.
The attentive embracement layer selects important features from clean data during training.
EBERT achieves higher classification performance on noisy ASR inputs than a baseline BERT fine‑tuning approach.
Evaluation on the Chatbot and Snips intent classification corpora demonstrates EBERT's performance gains under ASR errors.
Dual-decoder Transformer jointly performs automatic speech recognition and multilingual speech translation.
Dual-decoder Transformer uses a dual-attention mechanism that lets each decoder attend to information from the other decoder.
Parallel and cross dual-decoder Transformers are two variants that differ in the level of dependency between the decoders.
Dual-decoder Transformer achieves state-of-the-art translation performance in multilingual settings on the MuST-C dataset.
Parallel dual-decoder Transformer shows no trade-off between automatic speech recognition and speech translation performance compared to a vanilla multi-task architecture.
A rule-based converter maps STREUSLE annotations to UCCA representations.
A supervised delexicalized parser maps STREUSLE annotations to UCCA representations using only STREUSLE features.
Both conversion methods achieve accuracy comparable to fully supervised UCCA parsers.
The conversion results indicate that UCCA annotations are partially redundant with STREUSLE annotations.
The comparative analysis identifies several important areas where UCCA and STREUSLE diverge.
A transcription workflow that integrates spoken term detection and human-in-the-loop is introduced.
The workflow is evaluated through a pilot experiment in an almost zero-resource scenario involving two endangered languages.
Transcribing a small set of isolated words can bootstrap transcription of a larger speech collection when insufficient data exist to train a robust ASR system.
A miscellaneous-context-based method converts sentences into knowledge embeddings in the form of directed graphs.
Conceptual graphs are employed to compactly frame miscellaneous textual information.
The graph representation method accommodates slot-filling challenges in typical question answering.
The graph representation method explicitly captures neighboring connections of reference concept nodes at the sentence level.
A task-agnostic semantics-measured module projects edges of sentence-level graphs into a semantic relevance space relative to corresponding concept nodes.
Combining the graph representation with the semantics-measured module achieves high answer prediction accuracy for QA-type relation extraction.
The combined approach provides human-comprehensible graphical interpretations for each well-formed sample.
The approach represents the first interpretable process for learning vocabulary representations.
A modular learning approach decomposes stance change prediction into separate modules that model user beliefs, arguments, and their interaction.
The modular learning approach outperforms an end-to-end BERT model on stance change prediction using the same inputs.
An evaluation of popular automatic speech recognition services on incremental speech recognition using metrics designed for conversational AI standards.
An evaluation of speaker diarization capabilities of popular automatic speech recognition services for multi‑party interaction.
Microsoft provides the most effective incremental speech recognition system that preserves disfluent material.
IBM provides the most effective incremental speaker diarization system and an incremental speech recognition system that is most robust to speech overlaps.
Google offers a balanced performance between incremental speech recognition and speaker diarization, but none of the evaluated services reliably handle natural spontaneous conversations in real time.
Systematic probing experiments assess lexical semantics theories for cognitive plausibility.
Systematic probing experiments assess lexical semantics theories for technological usefulness.
Feature-based lexical semantics approach is superior to inference-graph and cooccurrence-vector approaches in representing lexical semantics.
Trivial adjustments of the scale factor in skip connections cause gradient explosion or vanishing that correlates with network depth.
Layer normalization mitigates gradient explosion or vanishing caused by scale adjustments in skip connections and consistently improves performance over plain skip connections.

Recursively applying skip connections with layer normalization to adaptively adjust input scale substantially improves performance and generalizes across machine translation and image classification tasks.
Iterative Emotion Interaction Network explicitly models emotion interaction using iteratively predicted emotion labels.
Iterative Emotion Interaction Network retains the performance advantages of explicit emotion interaction modeling without requiring gold emotion labels at inference.
Iterative Emotion Interaction Network achieves state-of-the-art performance on two emotion recognition in conversations datasets.
Word sense clustering differentiates homonyms and merges homophones in Japanese.
Contextualized BERT embeddings are used to cluster tokens into distinct sense groups.
Distinct sense groups are employed to normalize synonymous instances to a single representative form.
Normalization of synonymous instances benefits language model performance.
Normalization of synonymous instances benefits transliteration.
The task of affective language forecasting, which predicts future changes in language based on past language changes, is introduced.

Fundamental autoregressive characteristics of affective language forecasting, including necessary history size, static versus dynamic sequence length, and varying time-step resolutions, are established.
A dual-sequence GRU model with decayed hidden states is proposed for modeling sequences of language-based emotion over time.
The dual-sequence GRU model with decayed hidden states outperforms moving-average baselines in affective language forecasting.

An anonymized Twitter dataset of 1,900 users with weekly and daily scores for six emotions and two linguistic attributes, along with task setup and evaluation code, is released for affective language forecasting.
The survey categorizes neural natural language interface to database approaches into encoder and decoder improvements.
The survey highlights schema linking, schema encoding, and grammar-based decoders as the three most important directions for neural natural language interfaces to databases.
The survey provides an overview of the most important natural language interface to database datasets.
The survey compares top-performing neural models for natural language interfaces to databases.
The survey offers insight into recent non-deep learning solutions for natural language interfaces to databases.

An empirical comparison of three transformer-based models (BERT, RoBERTa, XLNet) and two non-transformer-based models (CRF, BiLSTM-CNN-CRF) on fine-grained named entity recognition across multiple domains is presented.
Transformer-based models incrementally outperform the studied non-transformer-based models on fine-grained named entity recognition in most domains.
The choice of domain significantly influences fine-grained named entity recognition performance independent of data size or model type.
Heterogeneously Retrofitted Spectral Word Embedding heterogeneously twists the similarity matrix of word pairs using lexical constraints.
Heterogeneously Retrofitted Spectral Word Embedding generates word vectors by spectral decomposition of the twisted similarity matrix, providing a linear algebraic analytic form.
Heterogeneously Retrofitted Spectral Word Embedding achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art retrofitting methods such as AR.
The embedding’s linear algebraic relationship with the similarity matrix enables analysis of the contribution of each model component.
The method is very efficient to execute.
Tensor decomposition is used to process non-binary constituency trees without binarisation.
A composition function based on the canonical tensor decomposition exploits the rich structure of non-binary constituency trees.
Weight sharing constraints on the factor matrices limit the number of parameters in the tensor-based composition function.
A Tree-LSTM model incorporates the tensor-based composition function for processing non-binary constituency trees and is experimentally evaluated on multiple NLP tasks.
EmNet jointly learns sentence-level emotions and constructs context-adapted emotion lexicons.
Context-adapted emotion lexicons enable handling words with multiple emotions based on different contexts.
EmNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on a benchmark Twitter dataset for emotion classification.
EmNet is validated on both LSTM and BERT architectures, demonstrating its effectiveness for emotion identification in tweets.
A topical constraint conditions response decoding on topic word embeddings to align response topics with the dialog context.
A semantic constraint regularizes the decoding objective with a weighted semantic distance computed via optimal transport between response and context representations.
The combined constraints enable generation of more topic-relevant and content‑rich responses than conventional neural generative models, as shown by automatic metrics and human judgments.
An intent discovery framework mines conversational logs to generate labeled datasets for training intent models.
ITER-DBSCAN is a density-based clustering algorithm that extends DBSCAN to handle unbalanced data.
Empirical evaluation across multiple conversation and intent datasets demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed framework and clustering algorithm.
USL-H is a configurable evaluation metric for open-domain dialogue systems.
USL-H composes metrics of understandability, sensibleness, and likability in a hierarchical manner.
USL-H achieves strong correlation with human judgments of dialogue quality.
USL-H maintains configurability across different quality aspects and underlying metrics.
Meaning-form correlation is analyzed on artificial toy languages, English dictionary definitions, and English literature sentences.
Synonymy and ungrounded stop-words are shown to influence meaning-form correlation measurements.
Simple mitigation methods for synonymy and stop-word effects produce widely varying outcomes across different datasets.
Data and code for meaning-form correlation analysis are publicly released.
An all-span training objective for DIORA reconstructs words inside each span using its contextual representation.
The all-span training objective improves unsupervised constituency parsing accuracy on English and Japanese compared to the original DIORA.
MHAL is a deep neural network architecture that explicitly wires lower and higher linguistic components.
MHAL uses a multi-head attention mechanism to tie representations between single words and full sentences.
MHAL learns to simultaneously solve classification tasks at different levels of granularity by transferring knowledge between hierarchies.
MHAL systematically outperforms equivalent models that are not incentivized toward developing compositional representations on broad text classification tasks.
MHAL enables sentence information to flow to individual words, allowing zero-shot sequence labeling without any word-level supervision.
Dynamic curriculum learning (DCL) reorders training samples dynamically based on loss decline and model competence.
DCL eases training by highlighting easy samples that the current model has sufficient competence to learn.
DCL outperforms strong baselines on multiple low-resource machine translation benchmarks and across different data sizes.
CEREC is a large-scale corpus for entity resolution in email conversations comprising thousands of email threads and tens of thousands of entity coreference chains.
CEREC was constructed using a two-step annotation process that minimizes manual effort.
Experiments evaluate the impact of different features and the performance of four baseline methods on the corpus.
The best baseline achieves the highest reported F1 for mention identification and coreference resolution on the corpus, indicating substantial room for improvement.
An in-depth qualitative and quantitative error analysis of the baselines is provided to understand their limitations.
Several probes are designed to assess BERT's access to constructional information.
Extensive experiments are conducted with the probes to evaluate BERT's constructional knowledge.
BERT is shown to have access to a significant amount of constructional information.
Constructional information is redundantly encoded in lexico-semantic representations.
The results provide insights into the linguistic information learned by deep learning language models.
An approach filters back-translated data by assigning higher weight to high-quality pseudo parallel sentence pairs during unsupervised neural machine translation training.
The approach computes a weight for each pseudo parallel sentence pair using a sentence-wise round-trip BLEU score that is normalized batch-wise.
The approach is compared with current state-of-the-art methods for unsupervised neural machine translation.
A user study was conducted in which novice translators revised Chinese translations of English patents using bilingual concordancing.
Concordancing with an in-domain bilingual corpus improves translation quality of technical terms more than concordancing with a general-domain bilingual corpus.
Bilingual corpora can serve as pedagogical tools for translation training in the technical domain.
CoLAKE jointly learns contextualized representations for language and knowledge using an extended masked language modeling objective.
CoLAKE extracts the knowledge context of an entity from large-scale knowledge bases instead of relying solely on static entity embeddings.
CoLAKE integrates language and knowledge contexts in a unified data structure called a word‑knowledge graph.
CoLAKE is pre‑trained on large‑scale word‑knowledge graphs with a modified Transformer encoder.

CoLAKE outperforms prior models on most knowledge‑driven, knowledge‑probing, and language‑understanding tasks and achieves high performance on a synthetic word‑knowledge graph completion task, demonstrating the benefit of simultaneous contextualization of language and knowledge.
Incorporating HTML tags alongside text improves named entity recognition performance compared to using text alone.
The performance improvement from using HTML tags holds across datasets with varying entity types, HTML density, and construction quality, demonstrating flexibility and adaptability of the method.
A comparative analysis of sequence-to-sequence modeling for machine translation and dialogue response generation is conducted.
Task differences between machine translation and dialogue response generation lead to major differences in the behaviors of their sequence-to-sequence systems.
Observations from the comparative study provide insights for developing better translation and dialogue generation models.
Contextual embeddings achieve high performance on abstract document matching.
TF-IDF baselines outperform contextual embeddings on granular textual similarity tasks.
A simple method that integrates TF-IDF with contextual embeddings is introduced.
Integrating TF-IDF with contextual embeddings yields relative improvements on granular textual similarity tasks.
PEDNet is a persona-enhanced dual alternating learning network for conversational response generation.
PEDNet includes a Context-Dominate Network that selects an appropriate persona and ensures contextual relevance of generated responses.
PEDNet includes a Persona-Dominate Network that enhances utilization of persona information by weakening interference from specific content in the conversation context.
PEDNet trains the Context-Dominate and Persona-Dominate networks alternately using a multi-task training approach.
PEDNet generates more personalized responses than baseline methods on the Persona-chat dialogue dataset.
Distill and Replay (DnR) continually learns natural language understanding tasks with a single model by distilling knowledge and replaying experience from previous tasks.
Distill and Replay (DnR) can be constructed on top of various language model architectures.
Distill and Replay (DnR) outperforms previous state-of-the-art models in continual learning of tasks of the same type from different domains.
Distill and Replay (DnR) outperforms previous state-of-the-art models in continual learning of tasks of different types.
Distill and Replay (DnR) can incrementally compress model size while still outperforming most baselines.
A benchmark dataset for evaluating fairness in dialogue systems is constructed.
Quantitative measures for assessing fairness in dialogue models are proposed.
Popular dialogue models exhibit significant prejudice towards different genders and races.
Two simple debiasing methods for dialogue systems are proposed.
The proposed debiasing methods reduce bias in dialogue systems.
The benchmark dataset and implementation are released to support fairness research in dialogue systems.
A framework expresses typological statements with real-valued logics that capture empirical truth degrees on multilingual treebanks.
The framework supports arbitrarily complex propositional logic formulae.
Experiments on Universal Dependencies treebanks re-evaluate established typological formulae across the available treebanks.
Experiments evaluate new candidate universal formulae generated by a search algorithm on Universal Dependencies treebanks.
Topic-word Guided Dialogue Graph Attention (TGDGA) network models dialogue as an interaction graph based on topic word information.
Masked graph self-attention mechanism integrates cross-sentence information flows and emphasizes related utterances in dialogue summarization.
Topic word features are incorporated to assist the decoding process in abstractive dialogue summarization.
The proposed method achieves superior performance compared to most baselines on the SAMSum Corpus and Automobile Master Corpus.
MEISD provides a large-scale balanced multimodal multi-label dataset for emotion, intensity, and sentiment annotation in dialogues.
MEISD includes textual, audio, and visual features extracted from TV series.
A baseline experimental setup for emotion and sentiment recognition in conversations is established using MEISD.
A Hierarchically Disentangled Model for text generation with named entities is introduced.
Training is divided into multiple branches, each producing a model with overlapping subsets of vocabulary.
A cooking‑recipe dataset comprising 158,473 recipes with entity annotations and processed action graphs is curated.
Entity extraction combines Named Entity Recognition with an unsupervised method using dependency parsing and POS tagging, followed by dataset cleaning.
The model is evaluated on graph‑to‑text generation and ingredients‑to‑recipe generation, demonstrating performance relative to previous state‑of‑the‑art baselines.
Inverse N-gram loss (INF) incorporates contextual fluency and diversity in a single formula.
INF loss dynamically adjusts its weight using the inverse frequency of token n-grams applied to Softmax Cross-Entropy loss, promoting rare tokens while retaining fluency.
Training a Transformer with INF loss on English and Japanese Twitter single-turn dialogues yields higher automatic evaluation scores than models trained with Softmax Cross-Entropy loss or ITF loss.
Training a Transformer with INF loss on English and Japanese Twitter single-turn dialogues achieves higher human evaluation scores for coherence and richness compared to baseline loss functions.
A discourse parser that incorporates recent contextual language models achieves highly accurate predictions of discourse structure and nuclearity.
The parser achieves state-of-the-art performance on RST-DT and Instr-DT for predicting discourse structure and nuclearity.
Pretraining the parser on the large-scale silver-standard discourse treebank MEGA-DT improves its performance.
Layer-wise multi-view learning treats each encoder layer’s output as a redundant view of the input sentence for neural machine translation.
An intermediate encoder layer is incorporated as an auxiliary view alongside the topmost encoder layer.
A partially shared decoder processes the primary and auxiliary views while maintaining independent predictions.
Consistency regularization based on KL divergence encourages the two views to learn from each other.
The approach yields stable improvements over multiple strong baselines on five translation tasks without altering network architecture or inference speed.
A dialogue breakdown detection model jointly incorporates a pretrained cross-lingual language model and a co-attention network.
The model leverages word embeddings trained on one hundred languages to generate contextualized representations.
Co-attention captures interaction between the latest utterance and conversation history to determine whether the latest utterance causes a dialogue breakdown.
The model outperforms previous approaches on all evaluation metrics in both the Japanese and English tracks of the Dialogue Breakdown Detection Challenge 4.
KAITML uses a dual-level graph attention mechanism to incorporate commonsense knowledge into utterance representations.
KAITML employs an Incremental Transformer to encode multi-turn conversational context.
KAITML introduces multi-task learning to reduce confusion between neutral and non-neutral utterances in emotion recognition.
KAITML achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art models on five benchmark emotion recognition datasets.
TG-ReDial is a conversational recommender system dataset that incorporates topic threads to enforce natural semantic transitions toward the recommendation scenario.
TG-ReDial is created using a semi-automatic process that makes human annotation more reasonable and controllable.
The task of topic-guided conversational recommendation is defined based on TG-ReDial.
An effective approach for topic-guided conversational recommendation is proposed.
The proposed approach achieves effective performance on topic prediction, item recommendation, and response generation sub-tasks.
A mapping from left-to-right transition-based parsing algorithms to sequence labeling encodings of syntactic trees is defined.
The mapping establishes a theoretical relation between transition-based parsing and sequence-labeling parsing.
The mapping provides a method to derive new fast and simple sequence labeling encodings from existing transition-based parsers for various formalisms.
Sequence labeling versions of four dependency parsing algorithms are implemented based on the mapping.
The implemented sequence labeling parsers are learnable and achieve performance comparable to existing sequence labeling encodings.

SumAggGIN performs graph inference in two stages: a heterogeneous Summarization Graph that identifies topic-related emotional phrases and an Aggregation Graph that models local dependencies between neighboring utterances.
The heterogeneous Summarization Graph captures global semantic connections by recognizing topic-related emotional phrases across a conversation.
The Aggregation Graph captures short-term emotional effects by reasoning over neighboring utterances containing emotional phrases.
The combined global-to-local graph inference architecture achieves superior performance on three conversational emotion recognition benchmark datasets compared to prior state-of-the-art methods.
A novel coreference-aware semantic parser generates incremental surprisal measures for sentences.
Incremental surprisal measures from the coreference-aware parser explain human response times in a naturalistic self-paced reading experiment.
Coreference information guides human expectations about upcoming words during natural reading.
Coreference effects on memory retrieval can exist independently of coreference effects on expectations.
GAQCorpus is a large-scale English multi-domain corpus annotated with theory-based argument quality scores.
First computational approaches to theory-based argument quality assessment are introduced as strong baselines.
Large-scale argument quality annotation is demonstrated to be feasible.
Exploiting relations between argumentation dimensions improves performance of theory-based assessment.
Synergies between theory-based prediction and practical argument quality assessment are explored.
Locally Autoencoding Parser (LAP) encodes input using continuous latent variables in a sequential manner for semi-supervised graph-based projective dependency parsing.
Globally Autoencoding Parser (GAP) encodes input into dependency trees as latent variables with exact inference for semi-supervised graph-based projective dependency parsing.
Both LAP and GAP use a deep neural network‑enhanced encoder to incorporate contextual information and a generative decoder to reconstruct the input.
LAP and GAP share parameters and adopt a unified architecture with distinct loss functions for labeled and unlabeled data.
Experiments show that the autoencoding models can exploit unlabeled data to improve parsing performance when labeled data are limited.
The autoencoding models outperform a previously proposed semi-supervised dependency parsing model.
A novel content-equivalent Japanese-English news corpus for training neural machine translation systems is introduced.
A domain-adaptation method using multiple tags is extended to train neural machine translation models with clean and noisy corpora.
The novel Japanese-English news corpus improves translation quality of neural machine translation systems.
The multi-tag domain-adaptation method achieves greater effectiveness than existing domain-adaptation methods when learning from multiple types of corpora.
A supervised visual attention mechanism for multimodal neural machine translation is trained using manual word–image region alignments.
The supervised visual attention mechanism captures word–image region relationships more precisely than conventional unsupervised visual attention.
Incorporating the supervised visual attention mechanism improves the performance of a Transformer-based multimodal neural machine translation model.
Combining the supervised visual attention mechanism with a supervised cross-lingual attention mechanism yields further performance improvements in multimodal neural machine translation.

AutoGOAL automatically builds machine learning pipelines that combine techniques and algorithms from different frameworks, including shallow classifiers, natural language processing tools, and neural networks.
The heterogeneous AutoML optimization problem is defined as the search for the best sequence of algorithms that transforms specific input data into the desired output.
AutoGOAL is experimentally evaluated on diverse machine learning problems and shown to be competitive with other AutoML alternatives on standard benchmarks.
AutoGOAL can be applied to novel natural language processing tasks where existing AutoML alternatives cannot be directly deployed.
AutoGOAL includes built-in compatibility with a large number of popular machine learning frameworks.
Instance-level parser selection (ILPS) is introduced as a novel cross-lingual transfer paradigm that predicts the best parser for each individual Universal POS sequence in a target language.
A Transformer-based supervised regression model is trained to predict parser accuracies for individual Universal POS sequences.
ILPS outperforms strong single-best parser selection baselines based on POS n-gram distribution similarity and language vector similarity on most low-resource test languages.
Aggregating ILPS predictions to select a parser at the treebank level yields a selector that surpasses the same baselines on most low-resource test languages.
The Heroes dubbing corpus is manually annotated with labels distinguishing on-screen and off-screen dubbing.
On-screen dubbing results in lower machine translation quality compared to off-screen dubbing.
Synchronisation constraints significantly reduce translation quality for off-screen dubbing.
Distinguishing between on-screen and off-screen dubbing is necessary for developing effective dubbing-customised machine translation strategies.
Cognitive models of users' communicative strategies are integrated into a reinforcement learning dialogue planning approach.
The integrated approach enables interactive systems to provide targeted, effective feedback about the system's understanding.
A prototype system is built that collaborates on reference tasks distinguishing arbitrarily varying color patches from similar distractors.
Experiments with crowd workers show that the approach yields context-sensitive clarification strategies that focus on key missing information.
The approach elicits correct answers that the system understands and increases overall dialogue success.
A neural network that incorporates inner-word and out-word features is introduced for Mongolian morphological segmentation.
The inner-word encoder uses self-attention mechanisms to capture inner-word features of the target word.
The out-word encoder employs a two-layer BiLSTM network to extract contextual out-word features from the sentence.
The decoder applies a multi-head double attention layer to fuse inner-word and out-word features and generate the segmentation output.
Experimental evaluation shows that the proposed network outperforms baseline methods and that its sub-modules contribute positively to performance.
Emotions are partially lost during neural machine translation.
A simple re-ranking approach informed by an emotion classifier can almost completely reverse emotion loss in translation by leveraging n-best list diversity.
The re-ranking approach can be applied to change emotions, enabling emotion style transfer in translation.
Qualitative analysis identifies recurring linguistic changes, such as modality changes, through which emotions are toned down or amplified in translation.
A neural Thai word segmenter uses syllable embeddings to capture linguistic constraints.
A neural Thai word segmenter employs dilated CNN filters to capture the environment of each character.
A machine-learning-based Thai orthographical syllable segmenter is introduced to generate syllable embeddings used as features for word segmentation.
The word segmentation system achieves higher speed and accuracy than previous state-of-the-art systems on both in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
A neural machine translation model for the Bribri language is presented.
An extremely low-resource Bribri-Spanish parallel dataset is released.
The model demonstrates successful learning of Bribri grammar.
The study analyzes translation of linguistic structures uncommon in Indo-European languages, including positional verbs, ergative markers, numerical classifiers, and complex demonstrative systems.
Iterative back-translation using Spanish sentences to generate synthetic Bribri sentences is employed to augment the dataset.
Back-translation augmentation improves translation quality when the synthetic Spanish sentences belong to the same domain as existing data.
Distributional semantics can effectively model the two elementary dimensions of aspectual class: states versus events, and telic versus atelic events.
Verb's local context provides the strongest indication of its aspectual class.
Closed-class words serve as stronger discriminating contexts for aspectual class than content words.
The presented approach achieves higher performance than prior methods on three aspectual classification datasets.
A new dataset of human‑human conversations annotated with lexical aspects is introduced.
Experiments reveal a correlation between telicity and both genre and discourse goals.
A probabilistic method synthesizes new word forms to complete multilingual translation dictionaries for unseen translations.
An approximate Maximum Mutual Information decoding objective improves performance on many-to-one and one-to-one word-level translation tasks.
Training in a many-to-many setting enables leveraging information from related languages to predict words in multiple target languages.
Ensembling with mixture-of-experts and incorporating related languages improves whole-word accuracy compared to a single-source baseline.
Decoding with the MMI objective yields better results when multilingual data is unavailable.
A new stemming dataset for Kinyarwanda verbal forms was collected through crowd-sourcing.
Morphological disambiguation of Kinyarwanda verbal forms is learned from the stemming dataset.
A feed-forward neural network classifier with engineered features is used for Kinyarwanda verbal form disambiguation.
The disambiguation method achieves high non-contextualized accuracy.
Inflectional properties of stems and morpheme association rules are the most discriminative features for disambiguation.
Representational Similarity Analysis is introduced as a method for probing linguistic dependencies in contextualized embeddings.
BERT’s contextualized embeddings encode linguistic dependencies such as verb subjects, pronoun antecedents, and sentence head words.
BERT encodes these linguistic dependencies more strongly than less linguistically-salient control features.
The RSA-based probing approach can adjudicate between hypotheses about which contextual aspects are encoded in language representations.
Combining soft contextualized input augmentation with label output smoothing yields cumulative improvements in neural machine translation performance.
Varying prior distributions, smoothing strength hyperparameters, and token selection procedures provides effective recipes for integrating input and output smoothing.
Extensive experiments on both small and large translation datasets demonstrate the benefits of combined input and output smoothing, including when back-translation is employed.
Analysis reveals reasons why input and output smoothing methods should be preferred for neural machine translation.
A Heterogeneous User History graph convolution network incorporates users' historical answers grouped by dialogue act labels to recognize dialogue act labels of utterances.
A History Selection process denoises user history within the Heterogeneous User History graph.
A Similarity Re-weighting process denoises user history within the Heterogeneous User History graph.
An Edge Re-weighting process denoises user history within the Heterogeneous User History graph.
The model achieves superior performance on the MSDialog and MRDA benchmark datasets compared to prior state-of-the-art methods.
A domain transfer based data augmentation method revises synthetic query translation candidates into search-aware examples.

The domain transfer model is built on an advanced Transformer architecture that incorporates layer coordination and mixed attention to accelerate refinement and leverages parameters from a pre-trained cross-lingual language model.
Large manually verified French-to-English and Spanish-to-English query translation test sets are introduced.
The proposed model outperforms strong baselines and existing domain transfer methods in both translation quality and retrieval accuracy.
[MASK]-based augmentation adapts contextual data augmentation for Japanese zero anaphora resolution.
Linguistically-controlled masking adapts contextual data augmentation for Japanese zero anaphora resolution.
The proposed methods reduce the computational cost of data augmentation for Japanese zero anaphora resolution.
The proposed methods increase the accuracy of Japanese zero anaphora resolution.
The proposed methods improve the quality of augmented training data compared to conventional contextual data augmentation.
A method embeds trees in a vector space using Tensor-Product Representations that enables retrieval of the original tree structure and nodes from the embedding.
A non-exact inversion technique provides an error bound when sufficient randomness is present in the representation scheme.
Cryptographic Role Embedding uses cryptographic hashing to represent an unbounded number of tree positions without memoizing position vectors.
Experiments on parse tree data demonstrate that a 30,000-dimensional Cryptographic Role Embedding achieves invertibility with error below one percent.
The proposed embedding requires far fewer dimensions than prior methods that would need astronomically many dimensions for comparable invertibility.
MG-PriFair is a multimodal neural framework that generates personalized reviews with privacy and fairness awareness.
A novel differentially private embedding model is introduced to train privacy-guaranteed embeddings.
An evaluation approach for sentiment fairness in the food-review domain is introduced.
A novel multimodal review dataset for review generation is released.
MG-PriFair can generate plausibly long reviews while limiting the amount of user data exploited and employing minimally sentiment-biased word embeddings.
MG-PriFair is the first review generation model to incorporate user privacy and sentiment fairness.
A fast Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes inference method jointly infers topics and visualization.
The inference method is black-box and accommodates model changes with minimal mathematical rederivation.
The method achieves efficient and effective performance on real-world large datasets compared with existing baselines.
A mixture-of-experts model learns multiple facet-specific embeddings for each entity.
Individual entity embeddings are learned using a variant of the GloVe model.
A gating network leverages pre-trained word vectors to encourage semantic coherence of each facet embedding.
An intermediate self-supervised learning task for sentence encoders improves machine translation quality estimation at sentence and word levels.
The translation language model training objective of the cross-lingual language model is modified to orient the pre-trained model toward quality estimation.
The proposed method operates without requiring annotated quality estimation data.
The method is complementary to quality estimation approaches that use pre-trained sentence encoders and domain adaptation.
Intermediate learning yields quality estimation performance that matches state-of-the-art models without combining multiple approaches.
The method outperforms other approaches that rely on pre-trained sentence encoders.
GEO model generates equations without using hand-crafted features.
GEO model incorporates an Operation Feature Feed Forward (OP3F) layer to retain encoder-level knowledge.
GEO model includes an auxiliary task of operation group difference prediction.
GEO model includes an auxiliary task of implicit pair prediction.
GEO model outperforms existing state-of-the-art models on two math word problem datasets.
GEO model achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art models on a third math word problem dataset.
Pure character-based NMT models for Finnish→English distribute word-level information across the entire character sequence rather than a single character.
Characters at different positions play distinct roles in learning linguistic knowledge in character-based NMT.
Deeper character-based NMT models require more layers to encode word senses, which explains their superiority over subword-based models.
Sparse word-level attention is explored to enforce character hidden states to capture full word-level information.
Using word-level attention with a single head reduces translation quality in character-based NMT.
An unsupervised algorithm extracts trivia facts from a single Wikipedia article by leveraging its hierarchical structure via top‑down processing.
The algorithm operates with substantially lower computation time than methods that consider Wikipedia categories.
The algorithm provides a domain‑independent approach for extracting trivia facts.
Human evaluation shows that the algorithm mines higher‑quality trivia facts than existing methods across entity domains.
A manual evaluation method for machine translation output marks problematic parts of the translation instead of assigning scores or classifying errors.
Annotations produced by this method can be used to analyze errors, challenging linguistic phenomena, and inter-annotator disagreements.
The method is applicable to any genre, domain, and language pair and can be guided by various quality criteria.
The method can be applied to generated text beyond machine translation.
A method fine-tunes pre-trained language models in an unsupervised manner for text clustering.
The method jointly learns text representations and cluster assignments via a clustering-oriented loss.
The method outperforms baseline methods and achieves state-of-the-art performance on three text clustering datasets.
A model detects rumors by representing Twitter conversation structure as a graph.
Representing conversation structure as a graph improves rumor detection accuracy.
The model outperforms several baseline models, including a state-of-the-art model, on two rumor datasets.
A large-scale taxonomy of empathetic response intents is produced based on manual labeling of 500 response intents from an empathetic dialogue dataset.

Lexical and machine learning methods are applied to automatically analyze speaker and listener utterances across an entire empathetic dialogue dataset using identified response intents and 32 emotion categories.
Information visualization techniques are used to summarize emotional dialogue exchange patterns and their temporal progression in empathetic conversations.
Novel empathy patterns in human-human open-domain conversations are identified through analysis of response intents and emotion categories.
Translation distributions in neural machine translation reproduce various statistics of the data well.
Beam search decoding deviates from the statistics reproduced by translation distributions.
Some known pathologies and biases of neural machine translation are caused by MAP decoding rather than the model's statistical assumptions or maximum likelihood training.
The most likely translations under neural machine translation models accumulate very little probability mass, making the mode essentially arbitrary.
Approximate minimum Bayes risk decoding yields competitive translation results, confirming that neural machine translation models capture important aspects of translation in expectation.
Decision rules that consider the full translation distribution are advocated over MAP decoding.
A human evaluation collects fluency and adequacy scores and categorizes error types for recent AMR-to-English generation systems.
Human judgments provide more nuanced comparisons of AMR-to-English generation systems than automatic metrics.
Automatic metrics are mostly successful in ranking AMR-to-English generation systems overall.
Analysis of human evaluation results identifies common error types made by AMR-to-English generation systems.
A task of distinguishing foreground and background events in news articles, including identification of the temporal position of background events relative to the foreground period, is defined.
A featurized model that leverages discourse information classifies background versus foreground events and predicts the temporal position of background events.
The model achieves strong performance on a news article dataset for distinguishing background versus foreground events and for predicting temporal position.
An implementation and annotated dataset for the foreground/background event distinction task are released.
KD-MKB learns knowledge base embeddings by mutually and jointly distilling knowledge within a dynamic teacher-student setting.
Knowledge distillation between knowledge bases through entity and relation inference is observed.
Cooperative learning outperforms traditional distillation and sequential distillation baselines.
A task of selecting a diverse and non-redundant answer set for community question answering is introduced.
Determinantal point processes are used to select answer sets based on answer importance and similarity computed with BERT.
A dataset of community question answering from a Japanese site is constructed for evaluating diverse answer set extraction.
The DPP-based method outperforms baseline methods for diverse answer set selection on the constructed dataset.
An approach automatically identifies words that can serve as labels for few-shot text classification given small amounts of training data.
Automatically identified label-to-word mappings achieve performance comparable to hand-crafted mappings on multiple tasks.

A multi-task learning framework enables training of a universal incremental dialogue processing model that jointly performs disfluency detection, language modelling, part-of-speech tagging, and utterance segmentation in a deep recurrent architecture.
The four tasks provide positive inductive biases to each other, with each task's optimal contribution depending on the severity of its noise.
The multi-task model outperforms comparable single-task models.
The multi-task model delivers competitive performance for incremental dialogue processing.
The multi-task model is beneficial for conversational agents used in psychiatric treatment.
AraBench is an evaluation suite for dialectal Arabic-to-English machine translation.
AraBench aggregates existing dialectal Arabic-English resources and constructs novel test sets.
AraBench defines four coarse, fifteen fine-grained, and twenty-five city-level dialect categories spanning genres such as media, chat, religion, and travel.
Strong baselines for dialectal Arabic-English translation are established using fine-tuning, back-translation, and data augmentation training settings.
A dialectal Arabic-English translation system is released publicly for research purposes.
A news dataset annotated with sentence-level emotion labels (positive, negative, neutral) is constructed.
An emotion label identification model that combines BERT and BiLSTM-CRF is proposed.
The proposed BERT‑BiLSTM‑CRF model is evaluated on the constructed news emotion dataset.
Preferentially annotating low‑confidence news articles in a human‑in‑the‑loop framework efficiently improves classification model performance.
HeterTFV is a graph-based reasoning approach that combines linguistic and symbolic information for table-based fact verification.
HeterTFV constructs a program graph to encode LISP-like logical forms and capture the semantic compositionality of programs.
HeterTFV builds a heterogeneous graph that incorporates linguistic information and symbolic information via program nodes.
HeterTFV performs graph-based reasoning over multiple node types to effectively combine linguistic and symbolic information.
HeterTFV demonstrates effectiveness on the TABFACT benchmark dataset.
Emergent communication pretraining via referential games is introduced for neural networks.
Emergent communication pretraining substantially improves few-shot machine translation performance.
An extrinsic evaluation protocol is introduced to assess emergent languages by relating their similarity to natural languages with downstream translation gains.
The influence of communication success and maximum sequence length on downstream translation performance is measured.
A customized adapter layer and annealing strategies for the MAP inference regularizer during fine-tuning are introduced to facilitate knowledge transfer and prevent catastrophic forgetting.
The method yields large BLEU improvements in few-shot machine translation with as few as 500 training instances across multiple language pairs.
SaSAKE extracts keyphrases from research papers using a syntax and semantics aware approach.
SaSAKE uses a transformer architecture that stacks sentence encoders to incorporate sequential information.
SaSAKE uses graph encoders to incorporate syntactic and semantic dependency graph information.
Incorporating syntactic and semantic dependency graphs alleviates long-range dependency problems and improves identification of multi-word keyphrase boundaries.
SaSAKE achieves state-of-the-art performance on three benchmark keyphrase extraction datasets.
An evaluation study compares offline and online neural machine translation architectures for convolutional Pervasive Attention and attention‑based Transformer models.
A human evaluation assesses the impact of online decoding constraints on translation quality for English‑German and German‑English language pairs.
German‑English translation is identified as particularly sensitive to latency constraints in online decoding.
The evaluation reveals distinct strengths and shortcomings of each model when shifting from offline to online decoding.
A tree transformer model is introduced for rumor detection that leverages user interactions encoded in conversation trees.
The model employs post-level self-attention to aggregate intra-subtree and inter-subtree stances.
The model consistently improves rumor detection performance on the TWITTER and PHEME datasets.
LAVA employs the latent space of a variational auto-encoder as the action space for reinforcement learning in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
LAVA shapes the latent variable distribution by pre-training with a response auto-encoding auxiliary task to obtain an informed prior.
LAVA shapes the latent variable distribution by multitask learning with a response auto-encoding auxiliary task.
Response auto-encoding is used as an auxiliary task that captures generative factors of dialogue responses without requiring additional data or labels.
The resulting latent representations are more characterized by distinct actions and support end-to-end dialogue policy optimization.
The method achieves state-of-the-art success rates for end-to-end dialogue policy optimization.
CosMo is a conditional seq2seq-based mixture model.
CosMo generates context-dependent clauses that form a dynamic knowledge graph on-the-fly for commonsense reasoning.
CosMo enables dynamic and diverse content generation for commonsense reasoning.
CosMo outperforms state-of-the-art models on zero-shot commonsense question answering.
Domain-general recurrent neural networks without explicit linguistic inductive biases learn number agreement in unnatural sentence structures that are not found in natural languages.
An analysis reveals that the network's weights store number agreement knowledge with substantial overlap between natural and unnatural structures.
The network exhibits effective singular versus plural understanding for individual sentences but lacks a unified concept of number agreement across the full range of inputs.
The overlap of weights handling natural and unnatural structures underscores the non-human-like nature of the learned knowledge.
Webis-EditorialSum-2020 provides 1330 curated summaries for 266 news editorials.
A tailored annotation scheme evaluates summary quality based on thesis-indicativeness, persuasiveness, reasonableness, conciseness, and self-containment.
An in-depth analysis of the Webis-EditorialSum-2020 corpus is presented.
Two extractive summarization models are evaluated on the Webis-EditorialSum-2020 corpus.
Existing systems for simple factoid question answering are evaluated for robustness across multiple datasets.
Shifting training and test datasets and training on a union of simple question answering datasets is used to assess model generalization.
Performance on the SimpleQuestions dataset does not indicate success on more general simple question answering.
Combining character-granularity and word-granularity features enables Chinese sentence intention matching.
Soft alignment attention enhances local information of sentences at corresponding granularity levels.
Capturing sentence features from multiple perspectives and correlation information between different granularity levels improves representation for intention matching.
The approach achieves performance comparable to or better than BERT-based models on the BQ and LCQMC datasets.
A new interpretation and application of Item Response Theory detects annotators' bias.
The bias identification method compares annotators' bias and characterizes annotation disagreement.
The bias identification method spots outlier annotators.
The bias identification method improves annotation guidelines.
The bias identification method complements Inter Annotator Agreement values for understanding annotation disagreement.
A model creates representations of social media users from their language and uses them to detect fake news.
Language-based user representations are beneficial for fake news detection.
Linguistic features of fake news spreaders are largely domain independent and consistent across two English datasets.
The relationship between language use and social graph connections is leveraged to assess the presence of an echo chamber effect.
Encoding the preceding narrative context of a target sentence with a BERT-based text encoder substantially increases textual emotion classification accuracy.
A multi-task learning setup that jointly classifies basic emotion categories and their positive/negative polarity outperforms a loss that directly encodes the wheel structure of Plutchik’s emotions.
Altering the training loss to incorporate a priori knowledge of emotion categories via multi-task learning improves emotion classification performance.
Global Context-enhanced Graph Convolutional Networks (GCGCN) represent entities as nodes and entity-pair contexts as edges to capture global context for document-level relation extraction.
Context-aware Attention Guided Graph Convolution (CAGGC) processes partially connected graphs to incorporate global context.
Multi-head Attention Guided Graph Convolution (MAGGC) processes fully connected graphs to incorporate global context.
Pre-training GCGCN on a large-scale distantly supervised dataset with curriculum learning followed by fine-tuning on a human-annotated dataset improves document-level relation extraction performance.
GCGCN achieves state-of-the-art performance on the DocRED benchmark.
A large-scale corpus of e-mail conversations with domain-agnostic and two-level dialogue act annotations is released, containing over 6,000 messages and 35,000 sentences from more than 2,000 threads.
The corpus adopts the ISO standard 24617-2 as the dialogue act annotation scheme.
BERT outperforms other neural network models, including previous state-of-the-art models, on dialogue act recognition for this corpus, though it does not reach human performance.
Two-level granularity dialogue act tags enable a dialogue act recognition model to learn efficiently via multi-task learning.
A model trained on this corpus generalizes to other domains of asynchronous conversation, indicating domain independence of the dialogue act annotations.
ADDQG uses an answer-aware initialization module with a gated connection layer to introduce document and answer information to the decoder.
ADDQG employs a semantic-rich fusion attention mechanism that integrates answer representations with document representations during decoding.
ADDQG applies reinforcement learning with syntactic and semantic metrics as reward to enhance training.
ADDQG leverages the target answer as guidance to generate answer-focused questions.
ADDQG outperforms state-of-the-art models in both automatic and human evaluations on the HotpotQA dataset.
Hy-NLI learns to classify NLI sentence pairs as linguistically challenging or not.
Hy-NLI uses deep learning models such as BERT and XLNet for linguistically less complex NLI cases.
Hy-NLI employs a symbolic engine to handle linguistically hard NLI cases.
Hy-NLI achieves state-of-the-art performance on both mainstream and adversarial NLI datasets.
A corpus analysis is performed to develop a representation of the knowledge and reasoning used to interpret indirect speech acts.
Contextual features are computationalized to model their influence on a speaker’s beliefs.
The influence of speaker beliefs on intended meaning and surface form of an utterance is modeled.
Domain-general patterns of reasoning involved in indirect speech act interpretation are axiomatized.
A proof-of-concept architecture for indirect speech act interpretation is implemented using Answer Set Programming.
Bayes-enhanced Lifelong Attention Network (BLAN) learns attention knowledge using generative parameters of naive Bayes.
BLAN stores knowledge from each sentiment classification task in a knowledge base and constructs lifelong attentions for future tasks.
Lifelong attentions built by BLAN enhance the network's attention to improve learning of new sentiment classification tasks.
BLAN demonstrates effectiveness for sentiment classification on Amazon product review datasets across multiple tasks.
A network embedding method models consistency across different views of networks.
The method creates a second view from the input network that captures relations between nodes based on node content.
The method incorporates a multiview adversarial regularization module to enforce consistency between latent representations of the two views.
The method achieves favorable performance compared to state-of-the-art algorithms on link prediction and node clustering tasks.
The method attains promising results on 30-day unplanned ICU readmission prediction.
Token embeddings learned by neural sentence encoders contain significant information about the exact linear context of each token, as demonstrated using a neighboring word identity prediction task.
Standard probing tasks can be learned without additional linguistic structure when token embeddings encode linear context information.
A framework is introduced to scrutinize analysis efforts in probing studies.
With current models and baselines, conclusions that representations contain linguistic structure are not well-founded.

Restricting classifier expressiveness or using strong baselines helps to better estimate the complexity of learning in probing but does not provide a foundation for speculating about the nature of encoded linguistic structure.
Political ideology biases in training data propagate through several widely-used NLP models.
Propagation of political ideology biases can deteriorate retrieval accuracy.
A text representation can be learned that is invariant to political ideology while still judging topic relevance.
An English urgency dataset of crisis-related tweets with urgency status annotations is released.
Evaluation datasets for Sinhala and Odia low-resource languages are released.
Zero-shot transfer from English to Sinhala and Odia is achieved using cross-lingual classifiers.
RoBERTa-based classifiers achieve the highest performance on English urgency detection among the evaluated models.
LASER-feature classifiers achieve the best zero-shot transfer performance to Sinhala, while XLM-R-feature classifiers achieve the best zero-shot transfer performance to Odia.
PheMT is a dataset for evaluating the robustness of Japanese-English machine translation systems against specific linguistic phenomena in user-generated content.
Experiments with PheMT reveal that both in-house models and widely used off-the-shelf systems are greatly disturbed by the presence of certain linguistic phenomena.
A model generates e-commerce product summaries guided by encoded product attribute tables.
The model constrains attribute words to appear in summaries only through copying from the source attribute table.
A Chinese e-commerce product summarization dataset is constructed.
The model improves the faithfulness of generated e-commerce product summaries.
TransQuest provides a simple quality estimation framework based on cross-lingual transformers.
Two neural architectures are implemented and evaluated within the TransQuest framework.
The TransQuest methods achieve state-of-the-art results on WMT datasets, outperforming current open-source quality estimation frameworks.
The TransQuest framework enables effective transfer learning for low-resource languages, yielding competitive results.
The cross-lingual transformer-based framework allows quality estimation across language pairs without retraining.
Automatic glossing models use a modern multi-source neural architecture that incorporates easy-to-collect translations.
Cross-lingual transfer is applied to improve automatic interlinear glossing for under-resourced languages.
A simple output length control mechanism is employed to refine automatic glossing model outputs.
