The proposed models achieve higher overall accuracy, lemma recall, and tag recall than a recent state-of-the-art baseline in three low-resource scenarios.
LAD is a corpus of 7.7 million Arabizi tweets.
SALAD is a manually annotated subset of LAD for sentiment analysis.
BAERT is an Arabizi language model created by pre‑training a BERT architecture on LAD.
BAERT outperforms multilingual BERT on Arabizi sentiment analysis.
Native-like expression identification is defined as a task that contrasts texts written by native speakers with those written by proficient second language speakers.

A neural network-based classifier of sentence-level nativeness is combined with an explainability method that measures the approximate contribution of a given expression to the classifier’s prediction.
A special label neutral is introduced and the classification task is reformulated as complementary-label learning.
Crowdsourcing-based evaluation demonstrates that the method uncovers linguistically interesting usages distinctive of native speech.
ManyEnt is a benchmark for entity typing models in few-shot scenarios.
ManyEnt provides a fine-grained variant with 256 entity types.
ManyEnt provides a coarse-grained variant with 53 entity types.
Both variants of ManyEnt are derived from the Wikidata knowledge graph using a semi-automatic process.
Two BERT-based baselines are evaluated on ManyEnt, achieving strong performance in a 10-way 1-shot setting.
A framework learns embeddings for meta-textual categories.
The framework optimizes a pairwise ranking objective using combined textual and meta-textual embeddings to improve matching.
The framework yields considerable gains in cross-lingual retrieval on Wikipedia across multiple language pairs.
The framework yields considerable gains in prior-art patent retrieval for a language pair.
The mode of combining textual and meta-textual information critically affects model improvement.
Global backward reading processes discourse units from the end to the beginning of a document to exploit the left-branching bias of Chinese discourse structure.
Local reverse reading reverses the order of paragraphs within a discourse unit to enhance differentiation of coherence between adjacent discourse units.
A model incorporating global backward reading and local reverse reading outperforms strong baselines on the Chinese MCDTB dataset for paragraph-level discourse parsing.
A novel, large, diverse corpus of spontaneous linguistic productions annotated with speakers’ gender is compiled.
A large-scale empirical study of gender differences in idiomatic language usage is performed.
Idiomatic choices reflect gender-specific lexical and semantic preferences in general language.
Men’s and women’s idiomatic usages express higher emotion than their literal language.
Men’s and women’s idiomatic usages exhibit detectable differences in dominance compared to similar distinctions in their literal utterances.
Contextual analysis of idiomatic expressions reveals considerable differences reflecting subtle divergences in usage environments shaped by cross-gender communication styles and semantic biases.
Contextual Alignment Enhanced Cross Graph Attention Network (CAECGAT) is introduced for cross-lingual entity alignment.
CAECGAT jointly learns embeddings for different knowledge graphs by propagating cross‑KG information through pre‑aligned seed alignments.
Extensive experiments on three benchmark cross‑lingual entity alignment datasets demonstrate remarkable performance gains over state‑of‑the‑art methods.
TWEETSUM is an event-oriented dataset for social summarization that contains tweets from twelve real-world hot events.
TWEETSUM provides four expert summaries for each event and includes an evaluation of annotation quality.
TWEETSUM incorporates additional social signals such as user relations, hashtags, and user profiles, and supplies a user relation network for each event.
Baseline performance of several typical extractive summarization methods is reported on TWEETSUM.

Iterative Dimension Reduction bridges the gap between actual initialization performance and the minimum initialization performance required for self-learning in unsupervised bilingual dictionary induction.
Iterative Dimension Reduction maintains performance on similar language pairs while improving accuracy on distant language pairs in unsupervised bilingual dictionary induction.
Iterative Dimension Reduction enables successful unsupervised bilingual dictionary induction for distant language pairs where prior methods failed.
A natural extension of the Orthogonal Procrustes Analysis algorithm uses multiple orthogonal translation matrices to model the mapping between word embeddings of two languages.
An algorithm is derived to learn multiple orthogonal translation matrices for word translation.
The multiple-matrix approach yields better performance on bilingual word translation tasks than a single-matrix baseline.
The multiple-matrix approach yields better performance on cross-lingual word similarity tasks than a single-matrix baseline.
Multiple orthogonal translation matrices can model multiple senses of a word.
A novel methodology produces plausible counterfactual explanations for deep transformers in financial text classification.
The methodology applies adversarial training regularization to language models in the FinTech domain.
The methodology improves model accuracy compared to state-of-the-art and human performance in financial text classification.
The methodology generates counterfactual explanations that are judged more plausible by human evaluators.
A new annotated dataset of patronizing and condescending language towards vulnerable communities is introduced.
The dataset supports the development of NLP models that identify and categorize patronizing and condescending language.
Standard NLP models struggle to identify patronizing and condescending language.
Among standard models, BERT achieves the highest performance for identifying patronizing and condescending language.
A classification approach augments article content representations with argumentation features to distinguish news stories from opinion pieces.
Argumentation features outperform previously used linguistic features for separating news stories and opinion articles.
Argumentation features improve performance over fine-tuned transformer-based models on data from publishers unseen during training.
An end-to-end approach for conversational recommendation dynamically manages and reasons over users’ past preferences and current requests using a structured cumulative user memory knowledge graph.

The Memory Graph (MG)–Conversational Recommendation parallel corpus MGConvRex comprises over 7,000 human-to-human role‑playing dialogs grounded on a large‑scale user memory and provides disjoint training and testing user sets for zero‑shot recommendation reasoning.
A simple, expandable formulation is introduced for constructing and updating the Memory Graph from unstructured utterances.
An end-to-end graph‑based reasoning model updates the Memory Graph from dialog utterances and predicts optimal dialog policies based on the updated graph.
The model’s predictions inherit the graph structure, enabling natural explanations of recommended policies.
The proposed model attains competitive performance on both offline metrics and online simulation evaluations.
BERT-based NER models are trained and evaluated for Chinese biomedical patent data.
A Chinese biomedical patents NER dataset is constructed for evaluating NER methods.
The trained NER solution can detect meaningful biomedical entities and novel gene-gene interactions using limited labeled data, training time, and computing power.
Pointer-LSTM selects important words for target prediction using a pointer network.
Pointer-LSTM maintains a coherent input process for LSTM modules while skipping words.
Pointer-LSTM enables changing the skip rate during inference.
Pointer-LSTM is faster than the standard LSTM architecture.
Pointer-LSTM achieves higher accuracy than Leap-LSTM at high skip rates.
Pointer-LSTM maintains robust accuracy when the skip rate is changed during inference.
Cross-lingual lemmatization approaches are evaluated for low-resource Indian languages.
Six Indian languages from two language families are used as test languages for lemmatization evaluation.
Linguistic insights into the performance of each evaluated lemmatization model are provided.
A method predicts the minimal age at which a text can be understood.
The method analyzes each sentence with a recurrent neural network and aggregates the sentence-level information to produce a text-level age prediction.
Multiple approaches are proposed and compared to baseline models at both sentence and text levels.
An LSTM-based model achieves state-of-the-art performance for minimal age prediction at sentence and text levels.
A survey maps the research trajectory of automatic personality detection from psychology approaches through psycholinguistics to recent NLP approaches on large social media datasets.
The survey identifies gains and losses associated with the shift toward large-scale NLP methods for automatic personality detection.
The survey delineates realistic expectations for automatic personality detection from text.
A 23-class fine-grained multi-label sexism classification task is defined, representing more categories than prior work.
A semi-supervised multi-task learning approach leverages supporting tasks without manual labeling cost for fine-grained multi-label sexism classification.
Unlabeled sexism reports are incorporated via unsupervised learning to construct the multi-task setup.
Objective functions are designed to explicitly exploit label correlations in the training data.
The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance on a recently released dataset for multi-label sexism classification across multiple evaluation metrics.
SADID evaluation datasets constitute benchmark datasets for Arabic and its dialects in low-resource spoken language machine translation.
Analysis of the datasets reveals their resulting properties.
Bootstrapping existing parallel sentences combined with multilingual training improves MT models without external data, achieving strong baselines.
A dynamic convolution network (DCN) is used as an utterance encoder to capture dialogue contexts for joint dialogue act recognition and sentiment classification.
A context-aware dynamic convolution network (CDCN) is introduced to better leverage local contexts when dynamically generating kernels.

Bi-channel versions (BDCN and BCDCN) are developed under multi-task learning to jointly perform dialogue act recognition and sentiment classification, with each channel learning task-specific feature representations and latent interaction.
Incorporating the DiaBERT language model enhances performance on joint dialogue act recognition and sentiment classification.
The proposed frameworks achieve state-of-the-art performance on two benchmark datasets, highlighting the importance of modeling local contexts.
Intermediate task transfer learning on SciBERT provides a better starting point for fine-tuning on scientific keyphrase identification and classification than BERT.
SciBERT with intermediate task transfer learning achieves competitive performance on scientific keyphrase identification and classification relative to prior works and strong baselines.
BERT with intermediate task transfer learning does not improve performance on scientific keyphrase identification and classification, likely due to catastrophic forgetting.
Sequence tagging intermediate tasks, especially syntactic structure learning tasks such as POS tagging, are most effective for scientific keyphrase identification and classification.

BERT and SciBERT are evaluated with intermediate task transfer learning across a large set of data‑rich intermediate‑target task combinations for scientific keyphrase identification and classification.
Multimodal selective gate network leverages reciprocal relationships between textual and multi-level visual features to select event highlights during encoding.
Modality regularization encourages generated summaries to capture image-embedded highlights more accurately.
Multimodal selective gate can be integrated with both text-based decoders and multimodal-based decoders.
Multimodal selective gate network improves multimodal sentence summarization performance compared to baseline models.
Multimodal selective gate network selects important information from the input sentence.
A model for unrestricted resolution of split-antecedent anaphors is introduced.
The model builds on a baseline enhanced with BERT embeddings.
Incorporating auxiliary corpora annotated by crowd workers mitigates sparsity and improves split-antecedent anaphora resolution.
Transfer learning with element-of bridging references and single-antecedent coreference as auxiliary tasks enhances performance on split-antecedent anaphora.
Combining three auxiliary corpora yields the best performance for split-antecedent anaphora resolution.
Non-dialog data from unrelated NLP tasks can be used to train dialog state tracking models.
Out-of-task training mitigates data sparsity in dialog state tracking.
A cross-lingual neural reranking model matches UMLS concepts to Wikipedia pages.
The cross-lingual neural reranking model substantially improves recall@1 over word- and character-level BM25.
Ranked Wikipedia pages are released for 700,000 UMLS concepts.
WikiUMLS, a dataset for training and evaluation of alignment models between UMLS and Wikipedia, is released.
The released alignment resources enable manual alignment of UMLS and Wikipedia with minimal effort.
A first-of-its-kind dataset of 7,601 Gab posts annotated for abuse presence, severity, and target is introduced.
AbuseAnalyzer is a system that detects abuse presence, predicts abuse target, and predicts abuse severity in Gab posts.
AbuseAnalyzer achieves strong performance on abuse presence detection, target prediction, and severity prediction.
A geometry-inspired attack generates natural language adversarial examples by iteratively approximating the decision boundary of deep neural networks.
The geometry-inspired attack fools natural language models with high success rates while modifying only a few words.
Human evaluation indicates that adversarial examples generated by the geometry-inspired attack are difficult for humans to recognize.
Adversarial training improves model robustness against the geometry-inspired attack.
A sophisticated framework for building verb conjugators for highly inflectional polysynthetic languages was created.
The largest available corpus of sentences in a polysynthetic language (Inuktut) aligned with English sentences was released.
Machine translation systems were trained on the Inuktut-English corpus and evaluated.
Free online automatic speech recognition services were provided to ease transcription of recordings in Indigenous languages.
Software for text prediction and read-along audiobooks for Indigenous languages was developed.
RoBERT is a Romanian-only pre-trained BERT model.
RoBERT is evaluated on seven Romanian-specific NLP tasks covering sentiment analysis, dialect and cross-dialect topic identification, and diacritics restoration.
RoBERT outperforms multilingual models on all evaluated Romanian-specific NLP tasks.
RoBERT outperforms another monolingual BERT implementation on all evaluated Romanian-specific NLP tasks.
CharacterBERT replaces the wordpiece tokenization in BERT with a Character-CNN module that constructs word representations from characters.
CharacterBERT achieves higher performance than BERT on a variety of medical domain tasks.
CharacterBERT generates robust, word-level, open-vocabulary representations.
XHate-999 is a multi-domain, multilingual evaluation dataset for abusive language detection.
XHate-999 aligns test instances across six typologically diverse languages, enabling disentanglement of domain transfer and language transfer effects in abusive language detection.
Baseline experiments with state-of-the-art monolingual and multilingual transformer models establish strong results on XHate-999.
Domain and language adaptation via intermediate masked language modeling on abusive corpora in the target language substantially improves zero-shot abusive language detection in the target language.
Attention Word Embedding (AWE) integrates an attention mechanism into the continuous bag-of-words (CBOW) word2vec model.
AWE-S extends AWE by incorporating subword information.
AWE and AWE-S outperform state-of-the-art word embedding models on word similarity benchmarks and when used to initialize NLP models.
A multi-prototype embedding network jointly extracts entity pairs and their relations in few-shot relational triple extraction.
A hybrid prototypical learning mechanism bridges text and knowledge for entities and relations, injecting implicit correlations between them.
Prototype-aware regularization learns more representative prototypes for few-shot relational triple extraction.
The method improves performance of few-shot relational triple extraction.
Regularized Attentive Capsule Network (RA-CapNet) identifies highly overlapped relations in informal sentences.
Multi-head attention is embedded into a capsule network as low-level capsules, using the subtraction of two entities as a relation query to select salient features regardless of their positions.
Disagreement regularization explicitly encourages diversity among multiple attention heads and low-level capsules to discriminate overlapped relation features.
RA-CapNet achieves significant improvements in relation extraction on widely used benchmark datasets.
Automated scoring of mindreading ability in middle childhood and early adolescence is introduced.
MIND-CA corpus comprises 11,311 question-answer pairs from 1,066 children aged 7 to 14.
Machine learning experiments are performed for automated scoring of mindreading ability.
Extensive quantitative and qualitative evaluation of the automated scoring system is conducted.
State-of-the-art NLP solutions are shown to be applicable to automated scoring of mindreading in middle childhood and early adolescence.
Character is defined as an animate entity that is important to the plot.
The definition can be reliably annotated with substantial inter-annotator agreement.
An annotated dataset of narrative texts containing character and non-character co-reference chains is released.
A supervised classifier using a simple set of easily computable features can effectively identify characters.
Error analysis indicates that character identification performance is primarily affected by co-reference quality and chain length.
Automatic pull quote selection is introduced as a task for selecting article spans that catch reader attention.
Baseline approaches for automatic pull quote selection are established, spanning handcrafted features, a neural mixture-of-experts model, and cross-task models.
Analysis of individual features and embedding dimensions in pull quote selection models uncovers unexpected properties of pull quotes.
Human evaluation confirms that automatic pull quote selection is a distinct task and that the selection models are suitable.
Aspect-based document similarity is modeled as a pairwise document classification task that incorporates aspect information.
Two datasets comprising research paper pairs from the ACL Anthology and CORD-19 are constructed for evaluating aspect-based document similarity.
SciBERT achieves the highest performance among evaluated models for aspect-based document similarity.
Qualitative analysis shows that aspect-based document similarity provides more fine-grained recommendations than traditional similarity measures.

NYTWIT is a dataset of over 2,500 novel English words published in the New York Times between November 2017 and March 2019, manually annotated with novelty classes such as lexical derivation, dialectal variation, blending, and compounding.
Baseline experiments on NYTWIT show that both uncontextual and contextual prediction of novelty class remain challenging for state-of-the-art NLP systems.
An incremental coherence model captures lexical relations between sentences.
The incremental coherence model is evaluated against simple neural models that rely on pretrained language models in two downstream tasks.
Evaluations indicate that interpreting texts incrementally may be useful for designing more advanced models.
A new nuclear type for multi-nuclear relations in RST parsing is introduced.
A new transition action that constructs multi-branch trees for RST parsing is introduced.
Refined dependency features extracted from a Bi-Affine model are added to the feature set for transition-based RST parsing.
A joint Reduce-SN action that combines reduce-shift and nuclear type assignment is found to be more suitable than a separate reduce-then-assign nuclear type approach for RST parsing.
The multi-branch tree structure achieves state-of-the-art performance on all 18 coarse RST relations.
GBERT and GELECTRA are German language models based on BERT and ELECTRA architectures.
GBERT and GELECTRA achieve state-of-the-art performance on document classification and named entity recognition tasks for both base and large model sizes.
Increasing the amount of training data improves the performance of German language models.
Applying Whole Word Masking during pretraining improves the performance of German language models.
Benchmarking shows that GBERT and GELECTRA outperform existing German language models.
A framework extracts sentence-level adherence information (e.g., full, partial, none) from electronic health records.
The framework extracts span-level adherence details such as adherence type, reasons, and outcomes from electronic health records.
A publicly available corpus of 3,000 de-identified sentences annotated for adherence information is released.
State-of-the-art transformer models are evaluated for automating sentence-level and span-level adherence extraction.
Physicians' language for documenting adherence information is analyzed.
Language model transformers' outputs correlate positively with human evaluation scores for open-domain dialogues.
Language model transformers can serve as automatic evaluators of open-domain dialogue quality.
Analysis reveals insights into the behavior and inner workings of language model transformers in conversational contexts.
DT-QDC is a Chinese question dataset that includes difficulty labels and enriched attributes such as keywords, chapter, and question type.
DT-QDC is the largest Chinese question dataset currently available.
MTMS-BERT is a model that judges question difficulty from multiple views.
ORMS-BERT is a model that judges question difficulty from multiple views.
MTMS-BERT and ORMS-BERT achieve superior difficulty‑judgment performance compared to baseline models on the DT-QDC dataset.
A hierarchical encoder-tagger model (HET) generates summaries by identifying important utterances in medical conversations.
High-quality summaries can be generated by extracting problem statements and treatment recommendations from medical conversations.
HET outperforms strong baselines and prior models on a medical conversation summarization dataset.
Adding conversation-related features to HET further improves system performance.
A set of 24 domain-specific sentiment lexicons is induced from domain-specific data.
Initial linear models are used to generate word intensity scores for lexicon induction.
Deep models based on word vector representations are trained to overcome seed data scarcity in lexicon induction.
Substantial differences in word polarities are observed across domains.
The induced domain-specific sentiment lexicons improve performance on review classification tasks.
The induced lexicons enable effective cross-lingual word sentiment prediction.
HabNet is a hierarchical bi-directional self-attention network framework for paper review rating prediction and recommendation.
HabNet employs three hierarchical encoders—sentence encoder, intra-review encoder, and inter-review encoder—to derive contextual representations at successive levels.
HabNet identifies useful predictors for making final acceptance decisions.
HabNet discovers inconsistencies between numerical review ratings and textual sentiment expressed by reviewers.
Two new metrics for evaluating review rating prediction models under data imbalance are introduced.
HabNet outperforms existing methods on review rating prediction in experiments on PeerRead and OpenReview datasets.
A new benchmark corpus derived from ACLED data for fine-grained event classification is introduced.
BERT transformer achieves the highest micro and macro F1 performance among evaluated models on the new benchmark.
TF-IDF character n-gram based SVM provides a competitive alternative to BERT for fine-grained event classification.
The evaluation highlights trade-offs in robustness and sensitivity to training data size across the benchmark models.
A decision support system determines depressive triage level from tweets by emulating the PHQ-9 questionnaire.
A BERT-based multitask learning framework identifies depressive symptoms in tweets using figurative usage detection as an auxiliary task.
Co-task aware attention enables soft parameter sharing across BERT layers and tasks to select optimal information.
Modeling figurative usage improves robustness and reliability for distinguishing depression symptoms in tweets.
A BERT-based classification model for location mention recognition is evaluated on crisis-related tweets using multiple training data regimes.
Combining in-domain and out-of-domain data from news articles and tweets is examined as a training strategy for location mention recognition.
Training on geographically-nearby disaster events improves performance compared to training on distant events for location mention recognition.
Twitter-based training data yields higher performance than general-purpose data for location mention recognition.
Crisis-related Twitter data yields higher performance than general-purpose Twitter data for location mention recognition.
A constituency lattice structure derived from constituency tree constituents is constructed to leverage syntactic information for aspect term extraction.
Two encoding approaches, one based on BiLSTM-CRF and another on BERT, are introduced to encode the constituency lattice for aspect term extraction.
The proposed constituency lattice encoding models achieve superior performance on two benchmark datasets for aspect term extraction.
An English-German parallel asymmetric corpus for detecting unfair clauses in privacy policies and terms of service is introduced.
Language-agnostic sentence-level annotation projection methods are compared for cross-lingual text classification.
A combination of word embeddings and dynamic time warping achieves the best performance among the evaluated projection methods.
TIMBERT provides an end-to-end model for mapping sentences to toponym-labeled sentences in the medical domain.
A family of BERT-based toponym identification models is introduced for the medical domain.
TIMBERT outperforms the previous state-of-the-art on the SemEval 2019 task 12 test set.
The approach automates place name detection in the medical domain to support epidemiological study of virus spread.
Tf-Idf data selection improves the quality of bilingual lexicons extracted from specialized comparable corpora.
Cross entropy data selection improves the quality of bilingual lexicons extracted from specialized comparable corpora and substantially reduces computation time.
A BERT-based data selection method is introduced for bilingual lexicon induction from specialized comparable corpora.
All evaluated data selection techniques yield large improvements in the quality of extracted bilingual lexicons.
McR2 enables relational reasoning over answer candidates using fused representations of document, query, and candidates.
The encoding architecture of McR2 integrates bidirectional attention flow, self-attention, and document-gated query reading to efficiently encode inputs.
A novel relational reasoning network in McR2 performs comparison and inference across answer candidates.
McR2 achieves superior performance relative to baseline models on four benchmark datasets derived from Children’s Book Test and Who DiD What.
An ablation study demonstrates that the key components of McR2 contribute to its effectiveness.
Emix constructs virtual training examples by interpolating word embeddings and hidden layer representations.
Emix achieves improvements over prior interpolation-based regularizers and data augmentation techniques.
Emix exhibits greater robustness to model sparsification compared to prior methods.
A retrieval-based model learns neural attentive text representations to answer legal questions at the article level.
The model uses convolutional neural networks to extract important information from questions and legal articles.
The model applies attention mechanisms to represent questions and articles and to align them in a matching process.
The model achieves higher recall and NDCG than state-of-the-art retrieval-based methods for legal question answering.
A model simulates languages and their features over time in a realistic geographic environment.
The model incorporates language phylogeny and migration mechanisms with parameters calibrated to reproduce known language family sizes and geographic dispersions.
Languages in the model are assigned feature values that can change randomly or under the influence of nearby languages according to predetermined probabilities.
The effects of model settings on resulting geographic and genealogical patterns are evaluated using homogeneity measures defined in the literature.
The model is flexible and realistic enough to be employed for answering a wide range of related research questions.
RuSemShift is a large-scale manually annotated test set for semantic change modeling in Russian covering two long-term time period pairs: pre‑Soviet to Soviet, and Soviet to post‑Soviet.
The annotation of RuSemShift follows the DURel framework and uses sentence contexts extracted from the Russian National Corpus.
Several distributional approaches achieve promising performance on RuSemShift.
Intrinsic computational assessment predicts fifteen argument quality dimensions using only textual features.
Systematic experiments with eight feature types on an existing argument corpus achieve moderate but significant learning success for most quality dimensions.
Rhetorical quality is identified as the most challenging dimension for computational assessment.
Subjectivity features provide strong predictive power for argument quality assessment despite corpus length bias.
Length bias in the corpus impedes full validity of the computational assessment.
Human assessors exhibit greater disagreement among themselves than with the computational approach.
A short comprehensive survey collects and organizes crowdsourcing problems reported by workers in interviews.
A new English corpus of workers' forum discussions is compiled for analysis.
Topic modeling is used to mine workers' complaints from the corpus.
Literature review shows that problems in task evaluation, such as unfair rejections, late payments, and unjustified blockings, are prevalent across crowdsourcing process phases.

Empirical analysis of forum discussions indicates that poor task design issues, including malfunctioning environments, inaccurate workload estimation, and privacy violations, are the most concerning to workers.
A model learns distributed sentence representations by applying bi-directional 3D convolutions to a three-dimensional sentence tensor constructed from visual representations of words or characters.
Multiple 3D convolutions of varying lengths are used as bi-gram, tri-gram, quad-gram, and five-gram detectors jointly.
Forward and backward convolutions learn distributional semantic knowledge from the sentence tensor, analogous to a Bi-LSTM.
Feature maps from forward and backward convolutions are concatenated to produce the final sentence embedding.
The architecture consists of a single convolutional layer, enabling easy and fast training.
Evaluations on several downstream NLP tasks demonstrate that the resulting sentence embeddings achieve excellent performance.
LangID models trained on up to 1,629 languages achieve comparable quality on held-out test sets to previously reported models.
Human-judged LangID accuracy for web-crawl text corpora is low for many lower-resource languages, indicating a need for more robust evaluation.
Wordlist-based tunable-precision filters are introduced and curated wordlists are released for approximately 500 languages.
Transformer-based semi-supervised LangID models are introduced, substantially increasing median dataset precision.
The proposed techniques enable creation of an initial dataset containing at least 100,000 relatively clean sentences for each of over 500 languages.
STMC-Transformer improves gloss-to-text translation performance on the PHOENIX-Weather 2014T dataset.
STMC-Transformer improves video-to-text translation performance on the PHOENIX-Weather 2014T dataset.
STMC-Transformer improves translation performance on the ASLG-PC12 corpus.
Video-to-text translation with STMC-Transformer surpasses translation of ground-truth glosses.
Ground-truth gloss translation does not constitute an upper bound for sign language translation performance.
Glosses are an inefficient representation of sign language for translation.
A domain expert-controlled, replicable procedure constructs concept-modeling ground truths for evaluating word and paragraph embeddings in concept-focused textual domains.
The procedure is demonstrated by constructing the expert ground truth QuiNE-GT for the QUINE philosophy corpus.
QuiNE-GT serves as a ground truth for evaluating embeddings on the concept of naturalized epistemology in the QUINE corpus.
The methodology for constructing expert concept-modeling ground truths is explicitly conceptualised and systematised for the first time.
Expert-controlled concept-modeling ground truths enable proper evaluation of word embedding techniques and increase their trustworthiness in specialized domains.
A parsing algorithm for Tree-Wrapping Grammars combines neural supertagging with A* parsing.
Tree-Wrapping Grammars can be extracted from constituency treebanks.
An English Tree-Wrapping Grammar is extracted from the Role and Reference Grammar treebanks.
Initial parsing experiments with the extracted English Tree-Wrapping Grammar are reported.
A large-scale English dataset for aspect-level sentiment analysis in the automotive domain is released.
A large-scale Korean dataset for aspect-level sentiment analysis in the automotive domain is released.
The released datasets contain over 58,000 comment-aspect pairs, making them larger than existing datasets for this task.
Korean is introduced as a new language for aspect-level sentiment analysis in the automotive domain.
Baseline performances of recent models are provided on the released datasets.
A graph attention network that leverages dependency tree structure provides syntactic awareness for aspect-level sentiment classification.
BERT subword representations are incorporated into dependency tree graphs, enabling more accurate word representations through graph attention.
Combining syntactic graph attention with BERT pre-trained knowledge enhances modeling of interactions between context and aspect words in aspect-level sentiment classification.
Empirical evaluation shows that the proposed syntax-aware graph attention network is effective for aspect-level sentiment classification.
DocBank provides a benchmark dataset of 500,000 document pages with fine-grained token-level annotations for document layout analysis.
DocBank is constructed using weak supervision from LaTeX source files of arXiv documents.
DocBank includes manually split train, development, and test sets for evaluation.
Models trained on DocBank accurately recognize layout information across a variety of documents.
DocBank enables fair comparison of models from different modalities and supports investigation of multimodal approaches for document layout analysis.
Paraphrase detection combined with expert knowledge can be used to automatically create dialog corpora.
The automatic dialog corpus creation approach can generate doctor‑patient interaction data.
A generation model trained on automatically created dialog data consistently outperforms a hybrid classification/retrieval model.
Automatic dialog corpus creation enables semi‑automatic production of large quantities of training data.
Automatic dialog corpus creation provides a natural way to guide learning.
Automatic dialog corpus creation introduces a novel method for assessing the quality of human‑machine interactions.
Personalized word embeddings are introduced.
Personalized word embeddings improve language modeling performance compared to generic embeddings.
Words belonging to specific psycholinguistic categories exhibit greater variation in personalized representations across users.
Combining generic and personalized word embeddings yields superior language modeling performance relative to using either type alone.
A language model that incorporates personalized word embeddings can be used for authorship attribution.
Probing tasks are generalized to the visual-semantic case for embeddings of image-caption pairs.
Three concrete probing tasks are defined within the general visual-semantic probing framework.
Classifiers are trained to probe for the defined visual-semantic properties.
State-of-the-art visual-semantic embeddings are evaluated using the proposed probing tasks.
Visual-semantic embeddings achieve higher accuracy than corresponding unimodal embeddings on the probing tasks, indicating that text and image dimensions complement each other.
A systematic study investigates the use of cross-sentence information for named entity recognition with BERT across five languages.
Adding cross-sentence context as additional sentences to BERT input systematically increases named entity recognition performance.
Contextual Majority Voting combines predictions from multiple sentence contexts to further increase named entity recognition performance.
The proposed approach improves state-of-the-art named entity recognition results on English, Dutch, and Finnish.
The proposed approach achieves the best reported BERT-based named entity recognition results on German.
The proposed approach attains performance comparable to other BERT-based approaches on Spanish.
MoralScore is a weakly-supervised framework that automatically measures moral intensity from text using only moral polarity labels.
MoralScore captures latent moral information from both words and sentence-level semantics.
A set of evaluation metrics for moral intensity measurement is introduced.
MoralScore achieves strong performance on both automatic and human evaluations.
A span-based joint extraction framework with attention-based semantic representations is introduced.
Attentions are used to calculate span-specific and contextual semantic representations.
Four attention variants are investigated for generating contextual semantic representations.
The model outperforms previous systems on entity and relation extraction tasks.
State-of-the-art results are achieved on ACE2005, CoNLL2004, and ADE datasets.
A set of adversarial answer generation methods of varying sophistication is introduced for benchmark content scoring datasets.
Simple adversarial answer generation methods cause a substantial decrease in automatic content scoring performance.
Random word shuffling of real answers leads to adversarial answers that are frequently accepted by a state-of-the-art scoring system.
Adversarial training improves the robustness of content scoring systems against adversarial answers.
Adversarial training does not completely eliminate the vulnerability of content scoring systems to adversarial answers.
Multimodal Topic-enriched Auxiliary Learning (MTAL) captures topic information from texts and images for depression detection.
A modality-agnostic topic model mines topical clues from both discrete textual signals and continuous visual signals.
Topic modeling for texts and images is employed as auxiliary tasks to improve depression detection performance.
Empirical evaluation shows that incorporating multimodal topic information improves depression detection compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
ConTextING integrates document-wise contextual embeddings from BERT into graph neural networks for inductive text classification.
ConTextING employs a joint training mechanism that simultaneously learns from document embeddings and contextual word interactions.
ConTextING outperforms pure inductive graph neural networks and BERT-style models on inductive text classification.
Using a sub-word graph improves performance in ConTextING.
Joint training with separated classifiers improves performance in ConTextING.
A source invariance network learns invariant information from parallel source inputs in multi-source neural machine translation.
The source invariance network can be integrated with multi-encoder RNN and transformer based multi-source NMT models to improve translation results.
Experiments on two multi-source translation tasks show that the source invariance network improves translation quality.
The source invariance network captures implicit invariance between different source modalities.

Aspect-category based sentiment analysis is reformulated as a category-sentiment hierarchy prediction problem with a hierarchical output structure that first identifies multiple aspect categories and then predicts sentiment for each identified category.

Hierarchical Graph Convolutional Network (Hier-GCN) models inner-relations among aspect categories with a lower-level GCN and inter-relations between aspect categories and sentiments with a higher-level GCN.
The hierarchical output structure outperforms existing output structures for aspect-category based sentiment analysis.
Hier-GCN consistently achieves the best results on four benchmark datasets for aspect-category based sentiment analysis.
A dataset comprising a large collection of uncurated complex geolocation descriptions and a manually curated test set is released for complex geoparsing.
An automated pipeline that combines Wikipedia and OpenStreetMap is used to generate complex geolocation description data.
A new geoparsing evaluation framework with a scoring methodology is introduced for assessing parsing of complex geographical descriptions.
Baseline geoparsing methods are provided and evaluated using the new evaluation framework.

A set of probing tasks covering distinct sentence-level linguistic features from multiple annotation levels is used to assess linguistic knowledge in a neural language model before and after fine-tuning.
BERT encodes a wide range of linguistic characteristics.
Fine-tuning BERT on specific downstream tasks reduces the amount of encoded linguistic information.
Greater retention of readable linguistic information in BERT correlates with higher accuracy in predicting sentence labels.
VKGDR represents a corpus as a graph of entities and their relations (a virtual knowledge graph) for document retrieval.
VKGDR computes relevance between queries and documents based on the virtual knowledge graph representation.
Unsupervised VKGDR outperforms baseline methods in zero-shot domain-specific document retrieval.
Unsupervised VKGDR outperforms a fully-supervised bi-encoder in zero-shot domain-specific document retrieval.
The virtual knowledge graph construction method yields better retrieval performance than previous approaches.
Group-Transformer reduces both computational cost and parameter size of Transformers.
Group-Transformer splits the feature space into multiple groups.
Group-Transformer factorizes calculation paths to reduce computations for group interaction.
Group-Transformer demonstrates effectiveness and efficiency on the enwik8 and text8 benchmark tasks.
Group-Transformer is the first Transformer design that employs a group strategy used in efficient CNN architectures.
A single binary classifier trained with class identifier prefixes can learn all few-shot classes jointly in the Amazon Review Sentiment Classification dataset.
The prefixing approach outperforms most published results on the ARSC few-shot classification task.
Including domain information in the task definition yields only modest improvement in model accuracy for ARSC few-shot classification.
Zero-shot classification without further fine-tuning achieves performance equivalent to few-shot classification on the ARSC dataset.
The ARSC few-shot task classes, defined by the intersection of domain and rating, are very similar to each other, indicating the dataset is unsuitable for studying few-shot text classification.
Six semantic structures for common questions in knowledge graph question answering are defined.
Structure-BERT predicts the semantic structure of a natural language question.
Predicted semantic structures are used to filter out noisy candidate query graphs.
A BERT-based ranking model ranks the remaining candidate query graphs.
The combined method achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on the MetaQA and WebQuestionsSP benchmarks.
A self-supervised intermediate fine-tuning strategy based on PubMed abstracts improves biomedical language model performance on patient case description tasks.
The strategy fine-tunes biomedical language models to predict masked medical concepts from case report abstracts.
Selecting sufficiently salient medical concepts for masking is crucial for the success of the fine-tuning strategy.
The fine-tuning strategy achieves state-of-the-art results on two benchmarks for interpreting patient case descriptions.
A quantitative measure of systematic offensive stereotyping (SOS) bias in word embeddings is introduced.
The SOS bias metric is validated on the most commonly used word embeddings.
The relationship between the SOS bias metric and hate speech detection performance of word embeddings is investigated.
SOS bias is present in almost all examined word embeddings.
The SOS bias metric correlates positively with statistics from published surveys on online extremism.
The SOS bias metric provides information distinct from established social bias metrics.
SOS bias does not explain the performance of hate speech detection models based on different word embeddings.
State-of-the-art NLP models are applied to unstructured earnings call transcripts to identify clusters of digital strategy patterns.
A Transformer-based architecture is used for text classification of earnings call transcripts, enabling improved understanding of conversation context.
Clustering analysis is applied to classified earnings call data to investigate digital strategy patterns.
Fortune 500 companies are found to employ four distinct digital strategies: product-led, customer experience-led, service-led, and efficiency-led.
An empirical baseline for measuring digital strategy from earnings calls is provided.
An imagination module based on Regularized Auto-Encoders learns context-aware and category-aware latent embeddings without relying on category labels at inference time.
The imagination module improves gameplay accuracy in the CompGuessWhat?! zero-shot scenario.
The imagination module improves Oracle accuracy in the GuessWhat?! benchmark when no gold categories are available at inference time.
The imagination module improves Guesser accuracy in the GuessWhat?! benchmark when no gold categories are available at inference time.
The imagination module boosts reasoning about object properties and attributes.
An extensive analysis quantifies bias introduced by decoding techniques in open-domain language generation across the full decoding space.
Individual components of decoder algorithms are shown to impact bias metrics such as toxicity and sentiment.
A trade-off between bias scores and human-annotated generation quality is evaluated throughout the decoder space.
Inspecting the entire decoding spectrum is demonstrated to be useful for assessing inference-time bias.
Sentimental Words Aware Fusion Network incorporates sentimental words knowledge into multimodal fusion representation learning.
The shallow fusion component uses a crossmodal coattention mechanism to obtain bidirectional context information between each pair of modalities, producing fused shallow representations.
The aggregation component employs a multitask sentimental words classification objective to guide deep fusion of three modalities and generate a sentimental words aware fusion representation.
Introducing sentimental words prediction as a multitask improves the quality of multimodal fusion representations.
A simple approach enables pre-trained models to learn rare biomedical words during prompt-based fine-tuning.
The approach improves performance on biomedical natural language inference tasks in few-shot prompt settings without adding parameters or training steps.
Mintaka provides a complex, natural, multilingual dataset for end-to-end question answering.
Mintaka contains 20,000 English question-answer pairs annotated with Wikidata entities and translated into eight languages, resulting in 180,000 samples.
Mintaka includes eight types of complex questions, such as superlative, intersection, and multi-hop questions, that were naturally elicited from crowd workers.
Baseline models achieve low hits@1 performance on Mintaka, indicating substantial room for improvement of existing models.
R-VGAE predicts concept relations within a graph of concept and resource nodes.
Prerequisite chain learning is framed as an unsupervised task without labeled concept pairs.
The unsupervised approach achieves higher prerequisite relation prediction accuracy and F1 than graph-based semi-supervised methods and other baselines.
R-VGAE is the first graph-based model to use deep learning representations for unsupervised prerequisite learning.
The corpus of NLP lecture slides is expanded to 1,717 files with manual concept pair annotations over 322 topics.
Hierarchical Representation-based Dynamic Reasoning Network constructs hierarchical representations for biomedical evidences to learn semantics within and among evidences.
Hierarchical Representation-based Dynamic Reasoning Network performs dynamic reasoning based on hierarchical representations of evidences to solve complex biomedical problems.
Hierarchical Representation-based Dynamic Reasoning Network outperforms existing state-of-the-art models on three mainstream biomedical question answering datasets.
Ablation study demonstrates that each component of Hierarchical Representation-based Dynamic Reasoning Network contributes to its performance.
A hybrid system using automated training-data augmentation is proposed for Text-to-SQL.
The system combines rule-based and deep learning components that interact to understand crucial information in a query and produce correct SQL.
The system achieves double-digit percentage improvement on databases that are not part of the Spider corpus.
Sentence Cloze-style Machine Reading Comprehension (SC-MRC) is introduced as a task that requires inserting the correct candidate sentence into passages with blanks.
CMRC 2019 is a Chinese dataset for evaluating SC-MRC, comprising a large number of blanks drawn from narrative stories.
CMRC 2019 includes fabricated candidate sentences that are similar to the correct ones to increase task difficulty.
Baseline systems based on pre-trained language models are implemented for the SC-MRC task.
State-of-the-art pre-trained models perform substantially worse than humans on the CMRC 2019 dataset.
The CMRC 2019 dataset and associated baseline systems are publicly released.
Active sampling dynamically selects data to continue pretraining BERT, yielding representations with minimal demographic information.
Active sampling improves fairness of downstream predictions for educational text classification across gender and first-language groups.
Active sampling improves accuracy of downstream predictions for educational text classification.
Active sampling can be applied to any pretrained language model and any demographic attributes.
A series of neural models, including both pretraining-based and non-pretraining-based variants, are evaluated for ranking the relevance of question‑answer pairs on the WikiHowQA dataset.
Extensive automated experiments and manual analysis show that incorporating answer summaries does not improve answer selection performance on WikiHowQA.
Popular pretrained language models exhibit significant implicit bias against people with disabilities.
Models trained on large corpora favor ableist language.
A norm-grounding knowledge model provides grounded social norms for complex narrative situations.
A norm-supported ethical judgment model based on neural module networks alleviates dilemma situations and enhances norm-level explainability in narrative ethical judgment.
Complementing complex narrative situations with grounded social norms improves ethical judgment.
The norm-supported ethical judgment model achieves state-of-the-art performance on two narrative judgment benchmarks.
DPTDR applies deep prompt tuning to dense passage retrieval.
DPTDR incorporates retrieval-oriented intermediate pretraining as a strategy for deep prompt tuning based retrievers.
DPTDR incorporates unified negative mining as a strategy for deep prompt tuning based retrievers.
DPTDR outperforms previous state-of-the-art models on MS-MARCO and Natural Questions.
Ablation studies show that retrieval-oriented intermediate pretraining and unified negative mining each improve DPTDR performance.
DPTDR reduces deployment cost by enabling reuse of a single backbone model across multiple retrieval tasks.
SCAN reduces spurious correlations in answer selection models by removing feature dependencies and language biases.
SCAN includes a feature decorrelation module that learns instance-specific weights during training to eliminate feature dependencies without prior knowledge.
SCAN includes a feature debiasing module that applies contrastive learning to mitigate negative language biases.
SCAN improves the robustness of answer selection models relative to strong baselines.
Adaptive Threshold Selective Self-Attention dynamically selects the most relevant characters for each query in self-attention.
Adaptive Threshold Selective Self-Attention automatically generates an attention score threshold per query to filter out low-scoring characters.
Adaptive Threshold Selective Self-Attention enhances the Transformer architecture for Chinese named entity recognition.
Adaptive Threshold Selective Self-Attention improves performance on benchmark Chinese NER datasets, achieving state-of-the-art results.
KiPT formulates event detection as a condition generation task.
KiPT constructs knowledge-injected prompts using external knowledge bases.
KiPT leverages a prompt tuning strategy to optimize the prompts.
KiPT outperforms strong baselines in few-shot event detection scenarios.
The model performs NIL prediction for funding entity mentions absent from the knowledge base.
The model combines transformer-based mention detection with a bi-encoder for entity linking.
The model achieves superior performance compared to strong existing baselines on funding entity linking tasks.
A data splitting strategy partitions social media posts into multimodal and unimodal subsets for information extraction.
A data discriminator trained with reinforcement learning assigns posts to the multimodal or unimodal set.
Outputs of modality-specific information extraction models are combined to produce final extraction results.
The proposed method achieves improved performance on two multimodal information extraction tasks.
IMCI integrates intra-document and inter-document context to encode evidence sentences for fact extraction and verification.
Intra-document context is defined as the document title and all other sentences from the same document.
Inter-document context is defined as all other evidence sentences that may originate from different documents.
IMCI attains state-of-the-art results on the FEVER 1.0 shared task.
Ablation experiments show that incorporating multi-view contextual information improves both fact extraction and verification performance.
HMCGR is a hybrid model that combines generation and classification for spatial relation extraction.
The generation component of HMCGR generates spatial relations with null roles.
The classification component of HMCGR extracts spatial relations with non-null roles.
A reflexivity evaluation mechanism based on the reflexivity principle is applied to improve extraction accuracy.
HMCGR achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines on the SpaceEval dataset.
Co-Reasoning Network (CORN) introduces a bidirectional multi-level connection structure based on Co-Attention Transformer that links each layer of a text encoder and a graph encoder.
The bidirectional connection structure enables deep interactive fusion of textual and graph node representations.
A QA-aware node based knowledge graph subgraph construction method aggregates question and answer entity nodes to guide subgraph expansion and reduce noise.
CORN achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CommonsenseQA and OpenBookQA benchmarks.
Gesture representations are extracted from monologue video data.
Neural sequential models are trained on gesture representations to assess information transmission of non-verbal signals.
Gestures exhibit entropy rate constancy, analogous to words, as predicted by Information Theory.
Speakers use simple gestures to convey information that enhances verbal communication, indicating rational organization of non-verbal information.
A method jointly embeds semantics from natural language descriptions and structural information for knowledge graph completion.
The method fine-tunes pre-trained language models with a probabilistic structured loss that reconstructs graph structures while the forward pass captures semantic information.
The method attains state-of-the-art performance on multiple knowledge graph completion benchmarks.
The method substantially improves performance in low-resource regimes by leveraging semantic descriptions.
Dynamic Relevance Graph Network creates graph edges dynamically based on relevance scores between nodes.
Dynamic Relevance Graph Network operates on knowledge-graph subgraphs defined by question and answer entities.
Dynamic Relevance Graph Network enables recovery of missing knowledge-graph edges needed for reasoning.
Dynamic Relevance Graph Network improves handling of negative questions by considering relevance between the question node and graph entities.
Dynamic Relevance Graph Network achieves competitive performance on CommonsenseQA and OpenbookQA benchmarks.
KMGRE directly models mention-level relations for document-level relation extraction.
KMGRE includes a mention-level relation extractor and a key instance classifier.
The mention-level relation extractor and key instance classifier are iteratively optimized with an EM-based algorithm.
A new method is introduced to solve the multi-label problem in optimizing the mention-level relation extractor.
KMGRE outperforms previous state-of-the-art models on two public document-level relation extraction datasets.
Five desiderata for modeling pronouns in language technology are defined.
A survey validates the five pronoun modeling desiderata.
Existing and novel pronoun modeling approaches are evaluated qualitatively against the desiderata.
A discrimination-free pronoun modeling approach yields measurable improvements on an established benchmark dataset.
A framework extracts question-worthy phrases from a passage for conversational question-answer generation.
The framework generates questions that incorporate previous conversational context.
The framework revises extracted answers after question generation to ensure exact match with the paired questions.
Answer revision improves the quality of synthetic conversational QA data.
The framework can be used for domain adaptation of conversational question answering.
OneEE performs event extraction in a single stage by recognizing word-word relations with parallel grid tagging.
OneEE incorporates an adaptive event fusion module to generate event-aware representations.
OneEE incorporates a distance-aware predictor that integrates relative distance information for word-word relation recognition.
OneEE achieves state-of-the-art performance on the overlapped and nested event extraction benchmarks FewFC, Genia11, and Genia13.
OneEE provides faster inference speed than baseline methods and enables further speed improvements through parallel inference.
ConceptNet embeddings outperform GloVe, fastText, and BERT embeddings for detecting conceptual clusters and switches in verbal fluency sequences.
Category-specific thresholds per category are more effective than a single cross-category threshold for analyzing clustering and switching in embedding sub-spaces.
GloVe, fastText, ConceptNet, and BERT representations are evaluated for suitability in detecting clusters and switches in verbal fluency tasks.
A multi-format transfer learning model with variational information bottleneck enables event argument extraction across datasets with different annotation formats.
The model incorporates a shared-specific prompt framework that learns both format-shared and format-specific knowledge for event argument extraction.
Variational information bottleneck is integrated to refine shared representations and eliminate irrelevant noise in event argument extraction.
The model achieves new state-of-the-art performance on three benchmark event argument extraction datasets.
Event sequence prediction is reformulated as a sequence generation problem.
A three-stage sequence-to-sequence architecture comprising augmentation, retrieval, and generation is introduced to incorporate external event knowledge for event sequence prediction.
The three-stage model outperforms existing statistical induction methods on an event sequence prediction dataset.
GMT-KBQA is a generation-based knowledge base question answering method that employs multi-task learning.
GMT-KBQA retrieves candidate entities and relations using dense retrieval.
GMT-KBQA jointly learns entity disambiguation, relation classification, and logical form generation.
GMT-KBQA attains state-of-the-art performance on the ComplexWebQuestions and WebQuestionsSP datasets.
Auxiliary tasks improve GMT-KBQA and give it strong generalization capability.
Event Optimal Transport generates temporally-ordered event sequences.
The model treats events as modeling units and explicitly extracts event semantics.
The model evaluates predicted event sequences by directly matching events using an event-level optimal transport objective.
The model achieves higher accuracy than previous models on all evaluation datasets.
The model achieves higher macro F1 than previous models on all evaluation datasets.
LKA is a self-labeled keypoint alignment model for table-based fact verification.
LKA includes a dual-view alignment module that aligns statement and table views to discriminate salient words through multiple interactions.
The dual-view alignment module employs a regular alignment network and an adversarial alignment network that cooperatively characterize alignment discrepancy.
LKA incorporates a mixture-of-experts block that integrates interacted information to support alignment and final classification.

LKA utilizes a contrastive learning loss to learn precise representations of structure-involved words, encouraging proximity of words sharing the same table attribute and distance from unrelated attributes.
LKA outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on three widely-studied table-based fact verification datasets.
LKA captures interpretable evidence words in table-based fact verification.
Prompt-based Conservation Learning (PCL) acquires new knowledge for multi-hop question answering while conserving knowledge from single-hop question answering, thereby mitigating forgetting.
PCL trains a model on single-hop question answering tasks, freezes the trained model, and expands it with additional sub-networks for multi-hop question answering.
PCL learns soft prompts for the added sub-networks to enable type-specific reasoning required by multi-hop questions.
PCL attains competitive performance on multi-hop question answering and retains strong performance on single-hop sub-questions, demonstrating effective mitigation of knowledge loss.
DiverseQA constructs an unsupervised QA dataset with diverse answer types by extending extracted named entities into longer sentence constituents as answer spans.
DiverseQA applies answer-type dependent data augmentation via adversarial training at the embedding level.
DiverseQA incorporates a denoising filter to mitigate noise in the constructed QA data.
DiverseQA outperforms previous unsupervised QA models on multiple benchmark datasets.
DiverseQA achieves strong performance in few-shot learning settings.
A re-ranking approach based on Determinantal Point Processes with BERT kernels is introduced for multi-answer retrieval.
The approach jointly considers query-passage relevance and passage-passage correlation to retrieve passages that are both query-relevant and diverse.
The re-ranking technique outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the AmbigQA dataset.
BERT-Flow-VAE is a weakly-supervised multi-label text classification model that reduces the need for full supervision.
BERT-Flow-VAE produces BERT sentence embeddings and calibrates them using a flow model.

BERT-Flow-VAE generates an initial topic-document matrix by averaging the outputs of a seeded sparse topic model and a textual entailment model that require only surface topic names and a few seed words per topic.
BERT-Flow-VAE adopts a variational autoencoder framework to reconstruct embeddings under the guidance of the topic-document matrix.
BERT-Flow-VAE uses the encoder means as predictions for multi-label text classification.
BERT-Flow-VAE outperforms baseline weakly-supervised multi-label text classification models on multiple datasets.
BERT-Flow-VAE achieves performance comparable to a fully-supervised multi-label text classification model.
Twelve contemporary language models are evaluated on Italian anaphoric zero pronoun coreference using five behavioral experiments.
XGLM models of 2.9B, 4.5B, and 7.5B parameters reproduce human coreference behavior across all five experiments.
Other language models reproduce subsets of the human coreference experimental results.
Human expectations about coreference can be derived from exposure to language alone.
Specific features of language models are identified that enable better reflection of human coreference behavior.
ArT focuses on key parts of the context and generates highly related knowledge through associative generation.
ArT includes a reverse thinking mechanism that enhances bidirectional inference between cause and effect for causal reasoning.
ArT performs commonsense question answering without requiring labeled data or external knowledge bases.
ArT outperforms prior unsupervised models on the COPA, SocialIQA, and SCT benchmarks across various pre-trained language model backbone sizes.
Weakly Supervised Formula Learner drives formula exploration using weak supervision from final answers, enabling autonomous learning of optimal formulas for mathematical reasoning.
Weakly Supervised Formula Learner achieves higher performance than baselines that use complete but imperfect formula annotations on the MathQA dataset.
Weakly Supervised Formula Learner achieves higher performance than other weakly supervised learning methods on the Math23K dataset.
MetaSLRCL is a meta learning framework for few-shot text classification that incorporates self-adaptive learning rates per task and neural network layer.
MetaSLRCL includes a task-oriented curriculum learning mechanism that trains the meta learner on tasks of increasing difficulty to improve generalization.
Experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of MetaSLRCL for few-shot text classification.
DABERT enhances BERT's ability to capture fine-grained differences in sentence pairs.
Dual Attention module measures soft word matches by introducing a dual channel alignment mechanism that models affinity and difference attention.
Adaptive Fusion module uses attention to learn aggregation of difference and affinity features and generates a vector describing matching details of sentence pairs.
DABERT demonstrates effectiveness on semantic matching benchmarks and robustness test datasets.
The machine reading comprehension framework is applied to semantic role labeling by treating predicate disambiguation as a multiple-choice reading comprehension task.
The selected predicate sense is used to construct queries for a second reading comprehension model that performs argument labeling.
A subset of possible semantic roles is selected to improve computational efficiency.
The framework leverages both predicate semantics and semantic role semantics for argument labeling.
The framework achieves state-of-the-art or comparable performance on semantic role labeling benchmarks.
UCCA includes annotated corpora in English, German, and French, with ongoing annotation in Russian and Hebrew.
A tutorial provides a detailed introduction to UCCA annotation guidelines, design philosophy, and available resources.
UCCA is compared to other meaning representation frameworks.
Findings from three recent shared tasks on UCCA parsing (SemEval and CoNLL) are surveyed.
Recent applications and extensions of UCCA demonstrate its value for natural language processing across multiple languages and domains.
Holistic graph network processes discourse-level and word-level context for logical reasoning in machine reading comprehension.
Hierarchical interaction mechanism models node-level and type-level relations as bridges in the reasoning process.
Holistic graph network is effective and generalizes across logical reasoning question answering and natural language inference datasets.
Holistic graph network can understand complex logical relations.
CRECL is a continual relation extraction framework that combines a classification network with a prototypical contrastive network for incremental-class learning.
The prototypical contrastive network in CRECL contrasts each instance with prototypes of candidate relations stored in a memory module.
The contrastive learning scheme in CRECL makes data distributions of all tasks more distinguishable, thereby alleviating catastrophic forgetting.
CRECL achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines on two public continual relation extraction datasets.
CRECL’s contrastive learning component improves continual relation extraction performance.
Regression analysis under improved experimental conditions reveals a genuine negative effect of frequency on lexical semantic shift in Romance cognates.
Regression analysis under improved experimental conditions reveals a genuine positive effect of polysemy on lexical semantic shift in Romance cognates.
Morphologically complex etyma are more resistant to lexical semantic shift in Romance cognates.
Cognates that have been in use over a longer timespan exhibit greater lexical semantic shift in Romance languages.
Cosine distance between cognate word embeddings quantifies the degree of lexical semantic shift.
ERGO builds a complete event relational graph for document-level event causality identification without using prior knowledge or external tools.
ERGO formulates document-level event causality identification as a node classification problem over event-pair nodes.
ERGO captures causation transitivity among event pairs via a graph transformer.
ERGO incorporates a criss-cross constraint and an adaptive focal loss to mitigate class imbalance in event causality identification.
ERGO achieves substantially higher performance than previous state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets.
A dataset of 9,165 German peer-review texts with helpfulness, quality, critical aspect ratings and demographic attributes is introduced.
Word Embedding Association Test (WEAT) analysis is conducted on the collected corpus in relation to its clustered labels.
WEAT analysis is performed on the most common pre-trained German language models (T5, BERT, GPT-2) and on GloVe embeddings.
WEAT analysis is performed on the same German language models after fine-tuning them on the peer-review dataset.
Pre-trained German language models exhibit substantial conceptual, racial, and gender bias according to WEAT.
Fine-tuning the German language models on the peer-review data leads to significant changes in bias along conceptual and racial dimensions.
The collected German peer-review corpus shows limited bias in co-occurrence analysis and in GloVe embeddings.
Parallel sentences extracted from existing French health comparable corpora constitute a new corpus for French biomedical text simplification.
A French translation of the WikiLarge corpus is employed as a resource for French biomedical text simplification.
Neural models are trained on French biomedical parallel corpora using different ratios of general and specialized sentences.
A small amount of specialized data significantly improves French biomedical text simplification.
Adapted Cross-Transformers with Global Sparse Attention (ACT-S) captures interaction of bilingual information for Chinese named entity recognition.
A model composed of multiple ACT-S modules integrates English information into Chinese representations for named entity recognition.
The model learns inter-features between bilingual languages and intra-features within Chinese for named entity recognition.
English text used for enhancement is automatically generated by machine translation, eliminating manual effort.
The bilingual enhancement model demonstrates effective and robust performance on four benchmark Chinese named entity recognition datasets.
Recovering Intended-Feature Subspace with Knowledge-Free (RISK) mitigates bias by operating in feature space without requiring bias attribute knowledge.
RISK treats shortcut features caused by biases as redundant and removes them by projecting onto a low-dimensional subspace of intended features.
An extremely low-dimensional subspace of intended features can robustly represent highly biased NLU datasets.
RISK consistently improves out-of-distribution generalization of NLU models.
RISK achieves state-of-the-art performance on out-of-distribution evaluation of NLU tasks.
RSGT extracts relational structure features suitable for both inter-sentence and intra-sentence temporal relations.
RSGT constructs a syntactic-and-semantic-based graph to represent relational structures for temporal relation extraction.
RSGT employs a graph neural network to learn representations of the syntactic-and-semantic-based graph.
RSGT uses an auxiliary temporal neighbor prediction task to fine-tune the encoder and obtain comprehensive node representations.
RSGT applies a conflict detection and correction algorithm to adjust incorrectly predicted temporal relation labels.
RSGT achieves superior performance on the MATRES and TB-Dense temporal relation extraction datasets.
DualCopyNet introduces a sequence labeling layer to identify seed words in the source text.
DualCopyNet employs dual copy mechanisms to generate keyphrases by copying identified seed words.
DualCopyNet outperforms baseline models on keyphrase generation tasks.
MECI is a multilingual dataset for event causality identification that includes English, Danish, Spanish, Turkish, and Urdu with consistent annotation guidelines.
MECI enables cross-lingual transfer learning research for event causality identification.
Experiments demonstrate that MECI is of high quality and provides ample research challenges.
A simple GNN model combined with a temporal information matching mechanism performs entity alignment between temporal knowledge graphs.
The proposed model achieves better performance while requiring less time and fewer parameters compared to existing time-aware attention mechanisms.
An unsupervised method generates alignment seeds using temporal information of temporal knowledge graphs.
The supervised method outperforms previous entity alignment methods on public datasets.
The unsupervised method attains competitive performance relative to supervised approaches.
Random Fourier Features and weighted re-sampling are used to decorrelate feature dependencies, mitigating spurious correlations in natural language understanding models.
A mutual-information-based purification step forces natural language understanding models to learn features that are more relevant to the task.
The combined decorrelation and purification approach achieves superior performance compared to prior debiasing methods on two well-studied natural language understanding tasks.
HRQE represents entities as pure quaternions.
HRQE uses unit quaternions to rotate head entities through the Hamilton product, modeling relations as 3D rotations.
HRQE employs scale parameters to constrain the modulus of entities, producing hierarchical distributions.
HRQE is the first model to simultaneously encode symmetry, antisymmetry, inversion, composition, multiple relation patterns, and learn semantic hierarchies.
HRQE demonstrates effectiveness comparable to state-of-the-art methods on four established knowledge graph completion benchmarks.
STAD classifies auto-annotated instances into confident and uncertain groups based on teacher model probabilities.
STAD identifies ambiguous but useful instances from uncertain instances and partitions relation labels into candidate-label and negative-label sets for each ambiguous instance.
STAD applies a set-negative training method to the negative-label sets of ambiguous instances.
STAD applies a positive training method to confident instances.
STAD uses a joint-training method that combines all data to build the final relation extraction system.
STAD achieves significant and consistent improvements over competitive self-training systems on low-resource relation extraction benchmarks.
Linguistic features are used to extract method sentence candidates from biomedical text.
A silver standard biomedical corpus of method sentences is created by labeling method entities with a rule-based method that employs Stanza dependency parsing.
Two machine learning algorithms are trained on the silver standard corpus to automatically extract method entities from biomedical text.
Machine learning models can automatically extract method entities with reasonable accuracy without requiring a gold standard dataset.
FILR incorporates a multi-dimensional information fusion module (MDIF) that extracts global document information for biomedical relation extraction.
FILR incorporates a multi-granularity reasoning module (MGLR) that performs inference using both entity-pair and mention-pair reasoning.
FILR achieves state-of-the-art performance on two widely used biomedical corpora (CDR and GDA).
Dialogue-based explanation is introduced as a paradigm to enhance sentence semantics for event detection.
Three simple dialogue generation methods are proposed to produce dialogue-based explanations of events.
