A hybrid attention mechanism is employed to combine dialogue-generated explanations with original event descriptions for event detection.
Experiments on two sentence-level event detection datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the dialogue-based explanation paradigm.
Dependency-aware prototype learning (DAPL) performs few-shot relation classification.
Dependency-aware prototype learning incorporates dependency trees and shortest dependency paths as structural information to complement contextualized sentence representations.
Dependency-aware prototype learning uses dependency-aware embeddings as attention inputs to learn attentive sentence representations.
Dependency-aware prototype learning includes a gate-controlled update mechanism that updates dependency-aware representations based on each network layer's output.
Dependency-aware prototype learning achieves substantially better performance than strong baselines on the FewRel dataset.
COMMA is a cognitive framework that models relationships among motivations, emotions, and actions in language-based human activities.
Three NLP tasks—emotion understanding, motivation understanding, and conditioned action generation—are defined to evaluate modeling of motivations, emotions, and actions.
The Hail dataset, constructed by automatically extracting samples from Story Commonsense, provides a challenging benchmark for the defined tasks.
Modeling the relationship among motivations, emotions, and actions is shown to be effective for NLP applications.
Models inspired by COMMA reveal the essential relationship among motivations, emotions, and actions more effectively than existing methods.
A holistic structure parsing algorithm models all nested named entities in a sentence as a single structure and discloses them in one pass.
Point-wise Mutual Information and frequency features derived from corpus-level statistics are incorporated to enhance named entity recognition.
The holistic structure parsing model attains performance comparable to state-of-the-art on widely-used nested NER benchmarks.
Corpus-aware features substantially improve domain adaptation for named entity recognition.
A keyword-based label smoothing method automatically generates soft labels from hard labels by exploiting relevance between labels and text instances.
The generated soft labels can be incorporated into existing models as auxiliary training targets without adding extra parameters.
Soft labels generated by the method contain rich knowledge of text features.
Incorporating the soft labels improves the performance of models on text classification tasks under both balanced and unbalanced settings.
Incorporating the soft labels improves the performance of models on large-scale text retrieval tasks under both balanced and unbalanced settings.
LINGUIST generates annotated data for intent classification and slot tagging via instruction fine-tuning of a multilingual sequence-to-sequence model.
LINGUIST achieves higher intent classification recall and slot tagging F1 than state-of-the-art methods in a 10-shot novel intent setting on the SNIPS dataset.
LINGUIST outperforms machine translation with slot alignment on slot tagging F1 in a zero-shot cross-lingual setting across multiple languages while matching intent classification performance.
LINGUIST yields significant improvements over a baseline that combines back-translation, paraphrasing, and slot catalog resampling on a large-scale internal multilingual dataset.
LINGUIST is the first demonstration of instruction fine-tuning of a large-scale sequence-to-sequence model to control the generation of multilingual intent- and slot-labeled data.
Correlated samples are retrieved with a sparse BM25 retriever from large-scale in-domain unlabeled data.
Entity type calibration is performed training-free via majority voting to simulate human reasoning.
A transformer-based multi-instance cross-encoder models correlated samples to capture correlation features during NER training.
Enhancing NER models with correlated samples improves performance on domain-specific texts such as Chinese addresses and e-commerce titles.
Empirical evaluation on Chinese address and e-commerce title datasets demonstrates the efficacy of the proposed methods.
BGM is a BERT-based graph convolutional network model for distantly supervised relation extraction.
BGM includes an instance embedding module that uses a BERT pretrained language model to extract key information from each instance.
BGM includes a bag representation module that constructs a bag graph and applies a convolution operation to obtain a bag representation.
BGM achieves considerable improvement on the NYT10 and GDS benchmark datasets.
SCL-RAI applies span-based contrastive learning to decrease distances between span representations with the same label and increase distances between those with different labels.
Span-based contrastive learning in SCL-RAI relieves ambiguity among entities and improves model robustness to unlabeled entities in named entity recognition.
Retrieval-augmented inference in SCL-RAI mitigates decision boundary shifting in named entity recognition.
SCL-RAI outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods on two real-world named entity recognition datasets.
CLIO is a role-interactive multi-event head attention network for document-level event extraction.
CLIO maps different events to multiple subspaces (multi-event heads) to handle multiple events in a document.
Within each event subspace, CLIO draws the semantic representation of each role closer to its corresponding arguments to determine event existence.
CLIO includes an event representation enhancing strategy that regularizes the pre-trained embedding space to be more isotropic.
CLIO achieves consistent improvements over previous methods on two widely used document-level event extraction datasets.
A weak supervision annotation framework selects representative samples for efficient manual annotation.
The framework auto-annotates remaining samples using a self-trained sentence encoder.
The framework includes a function for identifying inconsistent annotation errors.
The framework is applicable to any data annotation task.
The framework yields better classification results for real applications.
A context-aware normalisation approach for historical text uses neural machine translation and transfer learning.
A multidialect normaliser reranks the top five normalisation candidates with a word-level n-gram language model.
Incorporating dialectal information into training improves accuracy on historical multidialect datasets of German, Spanish, Portuguese, and Slovene.
Context-aware reranking provides additional accuracy improvement over a baseline normalisation system.
The method achieves higher accuracy than previously reported studies on three of the evaluated datasets.
Pair-Aware and Entity-Enhanced (PAEE) model performs document-level relation extraction.
Pair-Aware Representation module predicts potential relational entity pairs to restrict extraction to a subset of pairs.
Entity-Enhanced Representation module assembles directional entity pairs to obtain a holistic understanding of the entire document.
PAEE model achieves state-of-the-art performance on four benchmark datasets for document-level relation extraction.
A multi-party personalized dialogue dataset is constructed.
PersonaTKG is a graph convolution network model with an addressee selecting mechanism that integrates personas, dialogue utterances, and external text knowledge in a unified graph.
PersonaTKG outperforms baseline models on multi-party dialogue generation.
PersonaTKG improves persona consistency in generated responses.
Event Causality Extraction extracts cause-effect event pairs with their structured event information from plain texts.
A dual grid tagging scheme captures intra- and inter-event argument correlations for Event Causality Extraction.
An event type-enhanced model architecture implements the dual grid tagging scheme for Event Causality Extraction.
The proposed method demonstrates effective performance on Event Causality Extraction.
The Multiple LSTM-CRF (MLC) architecture provides a simple yet powerful approach for nested NER by training independent sequence labeling models for each entity type.
The MLC architecture achieves performance that matches or exceeds more sophisticated methods on three nested NER corpora.
Incorporating pre-trained language models into the MLC architecture yields state-of-the-art results on the Chilean Waiting List corpus.
An open-source library for computing task-specific metrics for nested NER was released.
The newly introduced metrics better assess a model's ability to detect nested entities than metrics used in prior work.
A data selection strategy generates diverse and semantic synthetic questions for healthcare question summarization in low-resource settings.
The data selection strategy uses guided semantic-overlap and diversity-based objective functions to select informative and diverse synthetic samples for data augmentation.
The data selection strategy achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark healthcare question summarization datasets.
Human evaluation indicates that the generated summarized questions are diverse, fluent, and informative.
A mechanistic model of the intention reading process is introduced.
The mechanistic model is integrated with pattern finding capacities.
An agent-based simulation is presented in which an agent learns a grammar for asking and answering questions about a scene.
The agent reconstructs queries corresponding to observed questions using only the answer and the scene.
The learned grammar enables mapping between natural language questions and queries without ever having observed the queries.
MedDistant19 provides a more accurate benchmark for broad-coverage distantly supervised biomedical relation extraction.
MedDistant19 is constructed by aligning MEDLINE abstracts with the SNOMED Clinical Terms knowledge base.
MedDistant19 addresses training-test relationship overlap and data construction inconsistencies present in prior benchmarks.
Existing broad-coverage distantly supervised biomedical relation extraction benchmarks exhibit substantial training-test relationship overlap.
Existing benchmarks contain inconsistencies in their data construction processes.
Experiments validate that findings from general-domain relation extraction also hold for biomedical relation extraction.
CofCED is a neural network for explainable fake news detection that operates on raw reports.
CofCED uses a hierarchical encoder to represent web text.
CofCED employs two cascaded selectors that choose the most explainable sentences for verdicts in a coarse-to-fine manner from top-K reports.
CofCED outperforms state-of-the-art detection baselines.
CofCED generates high-quality explanations from diverse evaluation perspectives.
Two explainable fake news datasets are constructed and released publicly.
Semantic-Preserving Adversarial Code Embeddings (SPACE) generate worst-case semantic-preserving attacks for source code comprehension models.
SPACE forces pre-trained language models to predict correct labels under worst-case semantic-preserving attacks.
SPACE improves robustness of pre-trained language models against state-of-the-art adversarial attacks.
SPACE boosts performance of pre-trained language models on code comprehension tasks.
A neural model for automated analytic essay scoring integrates a multidimensional item response theory model.
Integrating a multidimensional item response theory model improves interpretability of automated essay scoring predictions while maintaining scoring accuracy.
A model generates medical suggestions and accompanying natural-language explanations with narrative and causal structure for health consulting.
A discourse-aware mechanism with multi-task learning aligns generated explanations with medical suggestions.
The model achieves promising performance in both quantitative metrics and human evaluation.
A corpus of tweets containing both text and images is introduced.
Human judgments about whether users are located in the places they mention depend on whether annotators have access to the text, the image, or both, indicating the problem is multimodal.
A neural architecture that combines text and image modalities achieves better performance than unimodal baselines for determining location alignment.
An error analysis reveals when and why each modality (text or image) is beneficial for determining whether users are located in the places they mention.
A generative system for real-time in-battle commentary in mobile MOBA games is introduced.
An effective transform method that converts match statistics and utterances into a consistent encoding space is designed.
Experimental evaluation on large-scale real-world match data validates the system.
REIF is a model-agnostic instance sampling method for distant supervision relation extraction that uses influence functions to identify favorable and unfavorable instances.
REIF includes a fast influence sampling algorithm that reduces the computational complexity of instance selection from O(mn) to O(1).
REIF performs dynamic instance sampling based on the identified favorable instances.
REIF achieves superior performance compared to baselines with more complex architectures when training with sampled favorable instances.
REIF provides interpretable instance selection for distant supervision relation extraction.
PREC is a plug-and-play pre-trainer that learns user and news encoders through multi-task pre-training.
PREC-derived user and item representations boost click-through-rate prediction performance of conventional lightweight models.
PREC enables efficient online inference for news recommendation while maintaining compatibility with conventional models.
PREC's effectiveness is demonstrated through offline evaluation on public datasets and online A/B testing in an industrial application.
DAN+ introduces a multi-domain corpus and annotation guidelines for Danish nested named entities and lexical normalization.
Three modeling strategies for two-layer named entity recognition are empirically evaluated.
Transfer from German to Danish is compared with in-language annotation, evaluating language-specific and multilingual BERT models.
Lexical normalization and in-language BERT improve NER on least canonical data, while BERT-based models are sensitive to domain shifts.
Multi-task learning is identified as a robust strategy for two-layer NER, comparable to multi-label decoding.
Out-of-domain evaluation remains challenging and performance on news data quickly plateaus, highlighting the importance of cross-domain assessment of cross-lingual transfer.
A termhood score predicts the health relatedness of phrases and sentences.
The termhood score achieves high precision and substantial recall on a web dataset of cause-effect statements.
The termhood score outperforms state-of-the-art medical entity linkers and matches BERT-based approaches while being much faster.
Webis Medical CauseNet 2022 is a new resource containing millions of health-related cause-effect statements with cause and effect labeled.
MCLEA is a multi-modal contrastive learning based entity alignment model that obtains joint representations for multi-modal entity alignment.
MCLEA considers task-oriented modality and models inter-modal relationships for each entity representation.
MCLEA learns multiple individual representations from multiple modalities and then applies contrastive learning to jointly model intra-modal and inter-modal interactions.
MCLEA outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on public datasets in both supervised and unsupervised settings.
State-of-the-art GEC systems (T5 and GECToR) achieve higher performance than humans on the CoNLL-2014 benchmark according to the standard F0.5 evaluation metric.
Error analysis shows that current GEC systems fail to correct certain error classes.
SET-DST uses a schema encoder to comprehend dataset schemas for dialogue state tracking.
SET-DST enables transfer of dialogue state tracking models to new domains by encoding new schemas.
SET-DST achieves higher joint accuracy on the MultiWOZ 2.1 benchmark.
Unsupervised domain adaptation combined with multi-victim ensemble is used to perform imitation attacks on black-box NLP APIs.
Imitation attacks using this method can surpass the performance of the original black-box models on transferred domains.
Experiments on benchmark datasets and real-world APIs validate that imitators outperform the original black-box models.
Intent conditioning is introduced on a non-autoregressive semantic parser decoder.
The parser decouples top-level intent prediction from the rest of the parse.
A hybrid teacher-forcing approach is introduced to avoid training and inference mismatch.
The parser maintains O(1) decoding time complexity while generating more diverse top‑k outputs.
The parser improves top‑3 exact match for conversational semantic parsing.
The parser speeds up beam search inference on CPU compared to autoregressive models.
A semi-supervised learning approach is introduced for multimodal disaster tweet classification.
The approach leverages unlabeled social media data from disaster eyewitnesses and affected individuals to improve neural model performance.
The approach achieves higher F1 scores than fully supervised baselines on multimodal disaster tweet classification, especially in low-data regimes.
DP-Rewrite is an open-source framework for differentially private text rewriting that is modular, extensible, and highly customizable.
DP-Rewrite integrates multiple downstream datasets, models, pre-training procedures, and evaluation metrics to support flexible validation of private text rewriting research.
Experiments using DP-Rewrite on the ADePT system reveal a privacy leak in ADePT's pre-training approach.
A workflow detects previously fact-checked claims by generating abstractive summaries as queries for a general-purpose retrieval system.
An abstractive text summarization dataset of noisy Twitter claims paired with gold summaries is curated.
Retrieval performance improves when using out-of-the-box summarization models to generate queries instead of verbatim queries.
Retrieval performance further improves when fine-tuning summarization models on the curated dataset compared to using out-of-the-box models.
The proposed approach achieves higher recall@5 and mean reciprocal rank than baseline retrieval using verbatim queries.
A lightweight framework solves both biomedical event extraction and event verbalization with a unified text-to-text approach.
A new event graph linearization technique is introduced for converting event graphs into linear text sequences.
Highly comprehensive event‑text paired datasets covering a wide range of biomedical event types are released.
Baseline transformer models are evaluated on multiple biomedical text‑mining benchmarks and natural‑language generation metrics.
Extractive models achieve state‑of‑the‑art performance relative to single‑task competitors.
The framework demonstrates promising capabilities for controlled generation of coherent natural‑language utterances from structured event data.
A new task of tweet topic classification is defined.
Two datasets for tweet topic classification are released.
Training and testing data from recent time periods are provided for evaluating tweet classification models.
Current general- and domain-specific language models are quantitatively evaluated on the tweet topic classification task.
The evaluation yields insights into the challenges and nature of tweet topic classification.
CLOWER uses contrastive learning to integrate word-level and character-level representations.
CLOWER implicitly encodes coarse-grained word information into fine-grained character representations via contrastive learning on multi-grained information.
CLOWER can be incorporated into existing fine-grained pre-trained language models without modifying production pipelines.
CLOWER achieves superior performance over several state-of-the-art baselines on a range of downstream tasks.
Doc-GCN integrates heterogeneous syntactic, semantic, density, and appearance features via separate graphs for document layout analysis.
Graph convolutional networks are applied to each feature graph and node-level pooling is used to integrate the aspects.
Integrated features from all aspects are concatenated and classified with a 2-layer MLP for document layout component classification.
Doc-GCN attains state-of-the-art performance on PubLayNet, FUNSD, and DocBank datasets.
StoryWords 1.0 is a word embedding resource trained on a large corpus of influential children’s books spanning a century.
The gender portrayal in a century of influential children’s books reproduces traditional gender norms observed in society.
Female characters are more frequently associated with appearance than with competence relative to male characters in the analyzed children’s books.
Female characters are more frequently associated with family roles than with business roles relative to male characters in the analyzed children’s books.
Non-binary or gender-fluid individuals are rarely mentioned in the analyzed children’s books.
Training one variational auto-encoder per class serves as a data augmentation method for sentence classification.
The one‑VAE‑per‑class augmentation method systematically outperforms other generative data augmentation techniques across multiple dataset sizes and four binary classification tasks.
A corpus of dialogical explanations comprising 65 transcribed English dialogues from the Wired video series 5 Levels is introduced.
The corpus includes manual annotations of topic, dialogue act, and explanation move for each of the 1550 dialogue turns by five independent professionals.
Linguistic patterns of explainers and explainees are analyzed within the corpus.
Differences in linguistic patterns across explainee proficiency levels are explored.
BERT-based baseline experiments demonstrate that incorporating sequence information improves prediction of topics, dialogue acts, and explanation moves.

A data programming framework jointly constructs labeled data for language generation and understanding tasks by allowing annotators to modify automatically inferred alignment rule sets between sequence labels and text.
A dually-regularized denoising mechanism optimizes NLU and NLG models.
The framework generates high-quality data that approaches the quality of fully human-labeled data while using only a small number of labeled samples.
The framework outperforms existing annotation frameworks and semi-supervised approaches on two benchmarks.
A personalized dialogue generator detects implicit user persona from dialogue history without external knowledge.
The generator models user potential persona representation using perception and fader latent variables via conditional variational inference.
Posterior-discriminated regularization is introduced to enhance the training procedure of the dialogue generator.
Empirical evaluation shows the generator is more attentive to user persona and yields considerable improvements in automatic metrics and human evaluations compared to state-of-the-art methods.
A small clean dataset provides a more convincing evaluation of distantly supervised relation extraction models.
A small clean dataset enables building more robust denoising models for distantly supervised relation extraction.
A clean instance selection criterion based on influence functions uses sample-level evidence to identify good instances.
A teacher-student mechanism controls purity of intermediate results during bootstrapping of the clean set.
The model-agnostic approach achieves strong performance on denoising both real (NYT) and synthetic noisy datasets.

A pipeline first summarizes long, user-written medical questions using a supervised summarization loss, then performs two-step retrieval by matching the summary to an FAQ from a trusted medical knowledge base and retrieving relevant sentences from the associated answer document.
Three novel self-supervised semantically-guided losses are designed for question matching and answer relevance without requiring labeled data.
The system retrieves more relevant answers than strong retrieval-based question answering baselines.
The system achieves faster answer retrieval compared to strong retrieval-based baselines.
The self-supervised losses improve summarizer performance as measured by ROUGE and human evaluation metrics.
Z-score, min-max, all-but-the-top, and unit length normalization are applied to contextualized word representations.
Post-processing of contextualized embeddings unwraps vital information for lexical tasks such as word similarity and analogy.
Post-processing of contextualized embeddings unwraps vital information for sequence classification tasks.
Z-score normalization is identified as an essential step when using contextualized representations in applications.
ACEA is a multi-trait dataset of 1220 argumentative Chinese essays annotated for essay organization, topic, logic, and language.
HMTS is a hierarchical multi-task trait scorer that evaluates essay quality by modeling four traits.
HMTS incorporates an inter-sequence attention mechanism to enhance information interaction between different tasks.
HMTS uses trait-specific features for each scoring task in automatic essay scoring.
HMTS effectively scores essays on multiple traits, outperforming several strong baseline models.
A task is defined to generate a list of patient problems from daily progress notes.
A corpus of progress notes from the MIMIC-III database is constructed for the problem summarization task.
Data augmentation and domain-adaptive pre-training methods are applied to T5 and BART to increase exposure to medical vocabulary.
Domain-adaptive pre-training of T5 achieves significant performance gains over a rule-based system and general-domain pre-trained language models for problem list summarization.
Evaluation of the summarization task uses ROUGE, BERTScore, cosine similarity on sentence embeddings, and F-score on medical concepts.
MACRONYM is a manually annotated dataset of 27,200 sentences covering six languages and the legal and scientific domains for acronym extraction.
The dataset enables evaluation of multilingual and multi-domain acronym extraction.
Experiments demonstrate that acronym extraction faces unique challenges across different languages and learning settings.
A two-stage adaptation framework is introduced for frame detection via prompt learning.
During pre-training domain adaptation, two tasks based on pivots and prompts are used to learn a transferable encoder, verbalizer, and prompts.
In downstream scenario generalization, the learned encoder, verbalizer, and prompts are applied to new issues and label sets.
The framework achieves superior performance compared to baselines in both full-resource and low-resource conditions.
The framework enables effective frame detection across varying contexts and frame typologies.
TERMinator processes scientific texts to extract entities and semantic relations.
A dataset annotated for entity recognition and relation extraction in scientific texts is introduced.
Language models pre-trained on the target language do not always achieve the best performance for term recognition.
Incorporating heuristic approaches improves overall quality of term recognition and relation extraction tasks.
The TERMinator tool and its annotated corpus are publicly released for use by other researchers.

A parameter-efficient Mixture-of-Experts architecture reconstructs expert-layer parameter matrices with matrix product operator decomposition, sharing a central tensor among experts while using auxiliary tensors for expert-specific information.
A gradient mask strategy is introduced to address unbalanced optimization in the MPO-based Mixture-of-Experts architecture.
Experiments on T5 and GPT-2 demonstrate that the MPO-based Mixture-of-Experts architecture achieves superior performance with substantially fewer parameters than Switch Transformers.
DITFEND transfers coarse-grained domain-level knowledge by training a general model on data from all domains using a meta-learning approach.
DITFEND transfers fine-grained instance-level knowledge by training a language model on a target domain to evaluate the transferability of each source-domain instance and re-weight its contribution.
Experiments on two real-world datasets, including offline and online evaluations, demonstrate that DITFEND achieves superior effectiveness for fake news detection on specific target domains.
Webis-CausalQA-22 is a benchmark corpus of 1.1 million causal questions with answers.
A novel typology for distinguishing different types of causal questions is derived from data-driven manual analysis of questions from ten QA datasets.
High-precision lexical rules are used to extract causal questions of each type from existing QA datasets to create the corpus.
UnifiedQA is evaluated as an initial baseline on the benchmark, achieving a ROUGE-L performance.
Ecological diversity measures (richness, normalized richness, and two variations of Hill’s evenness) are used to evaluate the variety and balance of multiword expression annotations.

The behavior of these diversity measures on increasingly smaller samples of gold multiword expression annotations is analyzed to assess their suitability for evaluating multiword expression annotations.
Validated ecological diversity measures are applied to multiword expression annotations produced by systems in fourteen languages during the PARSEME shared task and to the corresponding gold corpora.
The impact of lemmatization errors on the evaluation of Turkish multiword expression annotations is investigated.
A comprehensive analysis of attention score calculation methods for attention alignment in vision-and-language tasks is performed.
The analysis evaluates how attention scores represent the significance of visual regions and textual tokens for global assessment.
Conditions under which attention score calculation mechanisms are more or less interpretable are identified.
The impact of different attention score calculations on model performance is assessed for visual question answering, text-to-image generation, and text-and-image matching.
Insights into the importance of each attention alignment score calculation when applied during training of vision-and-language models are provided.
LayerConnect inserts a lightweight linear connector between adjacent Transformer layers.
A hypernetwork composed of an interpolator and a down-sampler generates the parameters of each LayerConnect connector.
LayerConnect improves inference efficiency of pre-trained language models.
LayerConnect reduces the number of trainable parameters to a small fraction of the original model size while keeping performance degradation modest on the GLUE benchmark.
A cross-sentence Chinese text correction dataset for native speakers is introduced.
The dataset comprises 1,500 manually annotated texts, 30,811 sentences, and over 1,000,000 Chinese characters.
The dataset includes four error types: spelling errors, redundant words, missing words, and word ordering errors.
State-of-the-art text correction models achieve substantially lower performance than humans on the dataset.
The dataset addresses the lack of cross-sentence correction resources for native Chinese speakers.
MedLane is a dataset for automated clinical language simplification.
DECLARE is a model that follows the human annotation procedure for clinical language simplification.
Three specific evaluation metrics are proposed for fair evaluation of clinical language simplification.
DECLARE achieves state-of-the-art performance compared with eight strong baselines.
The MedLane dataset is useful for developing and evaluating automated clinical language simplification approaches.
GRASP employs a prompt-based fine-tuning approach for dialogue relation extraction.
GRASP captures relational semantic clues using an argument-aware prompt marker strategy.
GRASP captures relational semantic clues using a relational clue detection task.
GRASP achieves state-of-the-art performance on a dialogue relation extraction dataset without adding extra layers.
G3M integrates a novel dialog encoder and two gated mechanism modules within a multi-task learning framework for dialog routing.
The gated mechanism modules provide hierarchical information filtering that is non-invasive to existing dialog systems.
G3M leverages mined data-to-task and task-to-task knowledge across diverse dialog datasets.
G3M achieves state-of-the-art performance on two real-world dialog routing datasets.
G3M reduces RMSE and increases F1 compared to prior methods.
CogBERT induces fine-grained cognitive features from cognitive data and incorporates them into BERT by adaptively adjusting feature weights for different NLP tasks.
Cognition-guided pre-trained models consistently outperform basic pre-trained models on multiple NLP tasks.
Cognitive features contribute variably across NLP tasks, providing a fine-grained explanation of why cognitive data is helpful for NLP.
Different transformer layers encode different types of cognitive features, with word-level cognitive features encoded in lower layers and semantic-level cognitive features encoded in higher layers.
Attention visualizations demonstrate that CogBERT aligns with human gaze patterns and improves natural language comprehension ability.
A benchmark for continual few-shot intent detection comprising nine tasks spanning multiple domains with imbalanced class distributions.
Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder (PLE) is introduced for continual few-shot intent detection.
Pseudo Samples Replay is introduced as an auxiliary strategy for Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder.
Teacher Knowledge Transfer is introduced as an auxiliary strategy for Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder.
Dynamic Weighting Replay is introduced as an auxiliary strategy for Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder.
Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder with its auxiliary strategies prevents catastrophic forgetting during continual learning of intent detection.
Prefix-guided Lightweight Encoder with its auxiliary strategies promotes positive knowledge transfer across tasks in continual few-shot intent detection.
Identifying triggering posts as a predecessor task facilitates rumor verification.
A tree-structured annotation interface is created and the PHEME dataset is extended with message-level labels.
A graph-based model that considers direction and interaction of information flow implements role-aware rumor resolution.
Triggers play a critical role in verifying rumors and share similar linguistic patterns across unrelated events.
The progressive scheme improves rumor resolution performance.
KoCHET is a Korean cultural heritage corpus for named entity recognition, relation extraction, and entity typing tasks.
KoCHET contains 112,362 named entity recognition examples, 38,765 relation extraction examples, and 113,198 entity typing examples covering all entity types related to Korean cultural heritage.
Modified redistribution of KoCHET is permitted for both domestic and foreign researchers.
Experimental evaluation demonstrates the practical usability of KoCHET for cultural heritage tasks.
Statistical and linguistic analysis of KoCHET yields practical insights into the corpus.
A new dataset of tweets for detecting biographically relevant utterances is introduced.
State-of-the-art classifiers are evaluated on the biographically relevant utterance detection task, establishing strong baseline performance.
A linguistic analysis compares the predictiveness of various high-level features for biographically relevant utterance detection.
Biographically relevant utterance detection is demonstrated to be distinct from aspectual classification and sentiment analysis.
ParaZh-22M is a Chinese parabank comprising 22 million sentence pairs constructed from one-to-one bilingual sentence translation data and machine translation.

Paraphrasing using ParaZh-22M yields consistent and significant improvements over strong baselines across a wide range of Chinese NLP tasks, including CLUE benchmarks and low-resource machine translation.
A novel multi-task learning architecture incorporates contextual information for Sanskrit Compound Type Identification.
The architecture enriches syntactic information using morphological tagging and dependency parsing as auxiliary tasks.
The architecture achieves higher accuracy and F1-score than the previous state-of-the-art on benchmark datasets for Sanskrit Compound Type Identification.
Multilingual experiments show that the architecture is effective for compound type identification in English and Marathi.
BERT embeddings derived from stance-annotated datasets exhibit small but statistically significant differences between opposing stances.
Detection of stance traces in contextualized word representations is framed as a lexical semantic change problem.
CR-GIS introduces an interest-level star graph that models goal-aware implicit user interest sequences in conversational recommendation systems.
CR-GIS introduces a hierarchical Star Transformer that guides multi-turn utterance generation using the interest-level star graph.
CR-GIS employs a parallel star framework that combines the interest-level star graph and hierarchical Star Transformer for conversational recommendation.
Experiments demonstrate that CR-GIS yields more accurate recommended items than prior methods.
Experiments demonstrate that CR-GIS produces more fluent and coherent dialog utterances than prior methods.
Comprehensive probing experiments using XLM-R evaluate temporal relation classification between events in four languages.
Contextualized embeddings provide an advantage over static embeddings for temporal relation classification.
Sentence-level embeddings have a detrimental effect on temporal relation classification performance.
XLM-R achieves competitive results compared to state-of-the-art systems on temporal relation classification.
Probes reveal that XLM-R lacks sufficient encoded information to fully address temporal relation classification.
A wide range of linguistic phenomena are forgotten during 50 epochs of fine-tuning of BERT.
Preserved linguistic knowledge during fine-tuning correlates with performance on a linguistically-based downstream task.
A task predicting the evolution of written language competence of native language learners is employed to evaluate linguistic knowledge in fine-tuned models.
Fine-tuned neural language model accuracy across 50 epochs can be predicted using assessed linguistic competence.
Model linguistic competence exhibits a strong relationship with ability to solve a linguistically-based downstream task.
Neuro-Symbolic Visual Dialog (NSVD) combines deep learning with symbolic program execution for multi-round visually-grounded reasoning.
A stricter evaluation scheme is introduced that uses predicted answers for the full dialog history when calculating accuracy.
NSVD outperforms purely-connectionist methods on long-distance co-reference resolution in visual dialog.
NSVD outperforms purely-connectionist methods on maintaining question-answering performance across dialog rounds.

NSVD achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on CLEVR-Dialog while using a fraction of the training data and demonstrates higher robustness to incomplete dialog histories and better generalisation to longer dialogs, unseen question types, and new scenes.
NLG-Metricverse is an end-to-end open-source library for evaluating natural language generation in Python.
NLG-Metricverse provides a unified environment that aggregates a collection of NLG evaluation metrics.
NLG-Metricverse supports heterogeneous automatic metrics with n-arity management.
NLG-Metricverse enables meta-evaluation of individual metric performance, metric-to-metric correlations, and metric-to-human correlations.
NLG-Metricverse offers graphical visualizations to aid interpretation of metric scores.
NLG-Metricverse includes formal categorization and documentation to facilitate understanding of NLG metrics.
CILex utilizes contextual sentence embeddings together with additional context-capturing methods to perform lexical substitution.
CILex ensures that generated substitutes are semantically consistent with the target word while preserving the overall meaning of the sentence.
CILex attains state-of-the-art performance on the LS07 and CoInCo lexical substitution benchmarks.
LipKey is a large-scale news corpus containing human-written abstractive summaries, absent keyphrases, and titles.
LipKey is the largest news corpus that includes abstractive summaries, absent keyphrases, and titles.
Multi-task training jointly uses summaries, absent keyphrases, and titles as structured inputs for document summarization.
Including absent keyphrases and titles as additional context to the source document improves transformer-based summarization models.
OntoLex-FrAC defines a vocabulary for representing frequency, attestations, and corpus-based information in lexical resources.
OntoLex-FrAC complements OntoLex-Lemon by adding vocabulary needed to represent major types of information found in or automatically derived from corpora.
The first comprehensive description of the OntoLex-FrAC module is provided.
CGIM is a cycle guided interactive learning model for consistency identification in task-oriented dialogue.
Guided multi-head attention modules link each pair of subtasks to explicitly model interaction across two related tasks.
A cycle interactive mechanism enables information exchange among the three correlated subtasks via a cyclic interaction manner.
CGIM achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CI-ToD benchmark.
CGIM remains robust to variations in the initial task flow order.
DiaBiz.Kom provides a corpus of Polish telephone conversation transcripts annotated with communicative functions according to the ISO 24617-2 standard.
DiaBiz.Kom is the first Polish dialogue act corpus prepared according to the ISO 24617-2 standard.
DiaBiz.Kom is specified and evaluated as a resource for developing dialog analysis systems and advanced chatbot modules.
KG-S2S is a Seq2Seq generative framework that unifies representation of knowledge graph facts into flat text to handle multiple graph structures.
KG-S2S improves input representations of entities and relations and its inference algorithm to mitigate structure information loss from flat text.
KG-S2S achieves state-of-the-art performance on five knowledge graph completion benchmarks, outperforming competitive baselines.
KG-S2S’s ability is analyzed across different relation types and non-entity generations.
Tafsir Dataset provides span-based named entity annotations, sentence-based topic annotations, and span-based subtopic annotations for over 51,000 sentences of Classical Arabic literature.
Tafsir Dataset constitutes the first large-scale multi-task benchmark for Classical Arabic named entity recognition and topic modeling.
A novel script compression method combined with a transformer-based model achieves state-of-the-art performance on Classical Arabic named entity recognition.
Empirical evaluation shows that classical Arabic topic modeling is very challenging.
Tafsir Dataset is publicly released as an open-source resource.
K-MHaS is a multi-label dataset for Korean hate speech detection in online news comments.
K-MHaS contains 109k utterances and provides up to four labels per instance, handling subjectivity and intersectionality.
Strong baselines are benchmarked on K-MHaS.
KR-BERT with a sub-character tokenizer outperforms other baselines on K-MHaS by recognizing decomposed characters for each hate speech class.
A fast method predicts vectors for out-of-vocabulary terms using surrounding terms and the hidden context layer of word2vec.
The method serves as a strong baseline that surpasses several existing techniques on benchmark vector prediction tasks.
The method retains competitive performance in downstream tasks even when it underperforms other methods, with a very small margin.
The method is inexpensive to compute and requires no additional training stage.
Incorporating the method into existing techniques yields a new state-of-the-art on the word vector prediction problem.
A few-shot learning method reformulates classification and regression tasks as token-replaced detection problems using pre-trained token-replaced detection models.
The method constructs natural language prompts by inserting task-specific templates and label description words into the input.
The method predicts the label description word that is least replaced by the token-replaced detection model to determine the task output.

Systematic evaluation on a diverse set of datasets shows that this token-replaced detection based few-shot learner outperforms few-shot learners based on pre-trained masked language models in both one-sentence and two-sentence learning settings.
A method combines pre-trained sentence encoders with an alignment component that represents each sentence as a list of predicate-argument spans derived from the encoders.
The method decomposes sentence-level meaning comparison into span alignment for paraphrase identification tasks.
The alignment component improves paraphrase identification performance across various sentence encoders.
The alignment component enhances interpretability of sentence-level meaning comparisons.
The approach increases sensitivity to structural differences and improves discrimination of non-paraphrases with high lexical overlap.
A transition-based method parses complex questions into Question Decomposition Meaning Representation by constructing a computational graph node-by-node.
A decider predicts a sequence of actions and encodes intermediate states with specially designed encoders.
A searcher selects the optimal graph based on decider predictions to mitigate error propagation.
The transition-based method achieves higher parsing accuracy than several strong baselines.
The transition-based method yields transparent and human‑readable intermediate results, improving interpretability.
Dynamic nonlinear mixup generates synthetic samples using a nonlinear interpolation strategy that adapts to each input-label pair.
Distance-based sample selection computes cosine similarity between inputs to select multiple samples for each target sample in mixup.
Dynamic nonlinear mixup enlarges the space of synthetic samples relative to linear mixup.
Dynamic nonlinear mixup achieves superior performance compared to prior mixup methods on multiple public NLP datasets.
A framework incorporates augmented dialogue contexts into the learning objective and uses contrastive learning to obtain perturbation-invariant dialogue context representations.
ConMix adds token-level perturbations by stochastically mixing tokens from other contexts in the batch.
The framework improves ranking performance of BERT-based conversational models compared to baseline architectures.
ConMix outperforms previous data augmentation approaches and yields dialogue representations that are more robust to common inference perturbations.
CSL is a large-scale Chinese scientific literature dataset containing titles, abstracts, keywords, and academic fields of 396k papers.
CSL is the first scientific document dataset in Chinese.
A benchmark based on CSL evaluates model performance on scientific domain tasks including summarization, keyword generation, and text classification.
Analysis of existing text-to-text models on the benchmark reveals challenges for Chinese scientific NLP tasks.
A machine learning model for quantifier scope disambiguation incorporates relational knowledge conveyed by prepositions.
A scope-disambiguated corpus annotated with prepositional senses using the SNACS scheme is used for quantifier scope disambiguation experiments.
Manshadi and Allen’s method applied to the corpus enables inspection of the information gain provided by prepositions for quantifier scope disambiguation.
Statistical analysis shows that prepositional senses have a strong positive impact on the learnability of automatic quantifier scope disambiguation systems.
A model combines pre-trained language models with a fixed set of clinically approved suicidal cues.
The model is trained using a two-stage fine-tuning process.
The model achieves high ROC-AUC and strong F2-score for suicide risk detection, outperforming strong baselines.
The model can detect suicide risk early in the conversation, enabling real-time detection.
The model performs well across genders and age groups.
QSTS compares two questions by characterizing their target intent using question class, named entities, and semantic similarity.
QSTS addresses shortcomings of n-gram overlap based measures and achieves superior performance on publicly-available question generation datasets.
SimQG is a newly collected dataset containing questions generated by state-of-the-art question generation models with human relevance judgments for passage context and reference questions.
QSTS can score questions with respect to passage contexts, enabling reference-free evaluation of question generation performance.
QSTS is the first metric that enables reference-free measurement of question generation performance.
An automated method assesses digital language support for all languages recognized by ISO 639.
The assessment scrapes the names of supported languages from the websites of 143 digital tools representing a full range of ways digital technology can support languages.
Mokken scale analysis is used to produce an explainable model that quantifies digital language support.
The explainable model enables monitoring of digital language support on a global scale.
PLANE is a benchmark for testing fine-grained compositional entailment using adjective-noun phrases.
In-context learning and transfer learning are insufficient to solve PLANE.
A language model trained on PLANE generalizes well to out-of-distribution sets.
The required knowledge for compositional entailment can be stored in subword token representations.
A new automated evaluation method for open-domain dialogs uses follow-up likelihood.
The method measures the probability that a language model will continue a conversation with a fixed set of follow-up prompts.
The method achieves higher correlation with human evaluations than twelve existing evaluation methods.
A retrofitting method updates vectors of emotion‑bearing words to incorporate emotion information.
The retrofitted embeddings improve inter‑cluster and intra‑cluster distances for emotion‑based word clusters according to cluster quality metrics.
Classification models for sentiment analysis and sarcasm detection trained with the retrofitted embeddings achieve higher performance than models using the original embeddings.
The performance advantage of the retrofitted embeddings is larger when training data is limited.
KoBEST is a benchmark comprising five Korean-language downstream tasks.
Professional Korean linguists designed the KoBEST tasks to require advanced Korean linguistic knowledge.
The KoBEST dataset is fully human-annotated and thoroughly reviewed to ensure high data quality.
Baseline models and human performance results are provided for KoBEST.
KoBEST enables objective evaluation of Korean language models on diverse tasks.
Causal analysis is applied to identify mediators and confounding bias in dialogue response generation.
Mediator prediction preserves relevant information and is auto-regressively incorporated into the response generation process.
A dynamic topic graph guided conditional variational auto-encoder (TGG-CVAE) complements the semantic space and reduces confounding bias in generated responses.
Extensive experiments show that TGG-CVAE generates relevant and informative responses.
TGG-CVAE outperforms state-of-the-art models according to automatic metrics and human evaluations.
A joint paraphrasing task of creole translation and text normalization for Singlish messages is introduced.
The task is formulated across three linguistic dimensions: lexical level normalization, syntactic level editing, and semantic level rewriting.
An annotated dataset of Singlish-to-Standard English messages is created.
A perturbation-resilient sequence-to-sequence model is evaluated on the joint paraphrasing task.
The model improves performance of downstream tasks such as stance detection.
A hierarchical framework classifies predefined intents at a high level and performs slot filling guided by intent at a low level for zero-shot cross-lingual task-oriented dialogue.
The framework incorporates sentence-level alignment across languages to improve intent detection.
The framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on a public task-oriented dialogue dataset.
Training multilingual models with alignment objectives using parallel text explicitly aligns word representations across languages.
Two alignment strategies are explored for aligning multilingual embeddings.
Alignment objectives improve performance on Hindi-English sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and question answering tasks.
A comprehensive review of SHAP-based interpretability approaches for natural language processing is provided.
Instructive examples illustrating SHAP-based explanation methods for natural language processing are presented.
Recommendations regarding the applicability of SHAP-based explanation methods to natural language processing use cases are offered.
A zero-shot learning approach to script parsing tags texts with scenario-specific event and participant types without requiring domain-specific annotations.
The approach learns representations of potential event and participant mentions by promoting cluster consistency using annotated data.
The approach clusters event and participant candidates from unannotated texts belonging to an unseen scenario.
The approach outperforms a previous CRF model that required scenario-specific supervision.
The approach maintains strong performance when evaluated on a different corpus.
An automatic dialogue evaluation framework jointly performs goal segmentation and goal success prediction.
The framework extends RoBERTa-IQ with multi-task learning heads for goal segmentation and success prediction.
The extended model attains accuracy comparable to single-pass human annotation on a three-pass gold annotation benchmark.
A taxonomy classifies previously studied language model consistencies into sub-categories based on behavioural consistency.
A benchmark evaluates language models on multiple test cases distinguished by various consistency types and diverse downstream tasks.
Experiments on the benchmark reveal that modern pre-trained language models fail to perform well across all test cases and display high inconsistency.
The results demonstrate that a unified benchmark covering multiple consistency types and tasks is necessary for precise evaluation of language model consistency.
WikiHan consolidates Wiktionary transcriptions of Chinese varieties into a unified IPA and TSV format for comparative linguistics and Chinese NLP.
WikiHan includes entries for eight Chinese varieties and Middle Chinese.
WikiHan enables protoform reconstruction using an encoder-decoder cross-attention architecture, achieving notable accuracy and low phoneme and feature error rates.
ViNLI is an open-domain, high-quality corpus for evaluating Vietnamese natural language inference models.
ViNLI was constructed with strict quality control and language-specific guidelines for expressing entailment and contradiction in Vietnamese.
Experiments with state-of-the-art deep neural networks and pre-trained models on ViNLI reveal a large accuracy gap to human performance, demonstrating the corpus’s difficulty.
Wizard of Tasks provides the first conversational dataset of task assistance dialogues in cooking and home improvement domains.
Wizard of Tasks contains hundreds of conversations with tens of thousands of utterances collected using an asynchronous Wizard-of-Oz setup based on WholeFoods recipes and WikiHow articles.
Detailed analysis of Wizard of Tasks demonstrates its value and challenge for conversational task assistants in intent classification and abstractive question answering.
A model trained on Wizard of Tasks achieves high performance on intent classification.
A model trained on Wizard of Tasks achieves low performance on abstractive question answering, indicating the difficulty of low-resource AQA.
Ordinal log-loss is a simple log-based loss function for ordinal text classification.
Ordinal log-loss outperforms previously introduced state-of-the-art loss functions on four benchmark text classification datasets.
Noun-MWPs are math word problems whose answer is a non-numerical noun extracted from the problem text.
A novel method enables existing math word problem solvers to handle Noun-MWPs.
Applying the novel method to the Expression-Pointer Transformer yields the N-EPT model.
N-EPT solves Noun-MWPs more effectively than other models.
N-EPT also solves conventional math word problems.
MockingBERT adds resilience to misspellings in transformer-based NLP models retroactively without re-training the original model.
MockingBERT maintains language understanding performance on inputs without misspellings with minimal loss.
An efficient approximate method for generating adversarial misspellings reduces the cost of evaluating model resilience to adversarial attacks.
Flat argument spans are modeled as latent subtrees, reducing semantic role labeling to a tree parsing task.
A span-constrained TreeCRF is introduced to make tree structures span-aware.
The span-constrained TreeCRF is extended to a second-order version.
The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance among syntax-agnostic approaches on the CoNLL05 and CoNLL12 benchmarks.
BMNMT is a byte-based multilingual neural machine translation system that alleviates the representation bottleneck in multilingual NMT.
Random byte mapping combined with ensemble prediction enhances robustness of the byte-based multilingual NMT system.
BMNMT improves translation performance for endangered languages relative to subword- and word-based baselines.
BMNMT consistently outperforms subword/word-based baselines across twelve language pairs.
A manually annotated corpus of 10,000 tweets containing public reports of five COVID-19 event types is released.
Slot-filling questions are designed for each COVID-19 event type and 28 fine-grained slots are annotated.
The corpus enables fine-tuning of BERT-based classifiers to automatically extract publicly reported COVID-19 events.
A knowledge base constructed from two years of Twitter data contains over 4.2 million COVID-19 events.
The knowledge base can answer complex queries about COVID-19 events with high precision.
The proposed methodology can be quickly applied to develop knowledge bases for new domains in emerging crises such as natural disasters or future disease outbreaks.
Standard regularisation strategies such as weight decay and dropout are ineffective when training with noisy labels.
Pearson correlation is sensitive to outliers, enabling detection of noisy labels.
A simple noisy label detection method prevents error propagation from the input layer.
Experiments demonstrate that the noisy label detection method regularises noisy labels.
Experiments demonstrate that the noisy label detection method improves generalisation performance across multiple tasks and domains.
A weak supervision approach is used to predict the difficulty of technical interview questions.
A dataset of interview questions with difficulty scores for deep learning is created.
State-of-the-art models for question difficulty prediction are evaluated when trained using weak supervision.
Weak supervision is shown to be promising for the task of predicting difficulty of technical interview questions.
AnonData is a large multilingual NER dataset covering three domains across eleven languages and includes multilingual and code-mixing subsets.
AnonData is designed to represent contemporary NER challenges such as low-context short text, syntactically complex entities, and long-tail entity distributions.
AnonData was compiled from public resources using heuristic-based sentence sampling, template extraction and slotting, and machine translation.
A baseline XLM‑RoBERTa model achieves moderate performance on AnonData, demonstrating the dataset’s difficulty.
A gazetteer‑enhanced NER model (GEMNET) achieves substantially higher performance on AnonData, highlighting the challenge posed by the dataset and the benefit of gazetteer information.
Experiments test BERT's ability to infer Dowty proto-role semantic relations for verbal arguments.
A linear mapping is learned from BERT verb embeddings to an interpretable semantic property space derived from a linguistic dataset.
Semantic properties inferred from typed BERT embeddings are more linguistically plausible than those derived from static embeddings.
Semantic properties inferred from contextual BERT embeddings are evaluated against the original dataset.
Contextual BERT embeddings are assessed for their ability to model semantic properties of agents in verbs participating in the causative alternation.
Embedding-Push, Attention-Pull, and Robust targets transfer English embeddings to virtual multilingual embeddings without semantic loss.
The method improves zero-shot cross-lingual text classification performance compared to prior approaches.
The method achieves better multilingual alignment.
Zero-shot cross-lingual transfer learning can be performed without parallel corpora or bilingual dictionaries using the proposed method.
A large action space hinders reinforcement learning effectiveness in neural machine translation.
Reducing the vocabulary size improves reinforcement learning effectiveness for neural machine translation.

Reducing the dimensionality of the action space without changing the vocabulary improves reinforcement learning performance for neural machine translation as measured by BLEU, semantic similarity, and human evaluation.

Initializing the network’s final fully connected layer with a generalized layer over similar actions yields substantial improvement in reinforcement learning performance for neural machine translation.
Intersection agreement, an augmented Cohen’s Kappa, and precision, recall, and F1 are evaluated as metrics for inter-coder agreement on multi-label annotations.
A bootstrapping method is introduced to estimate chance agreement for each metric, producing an adjusted agreement coefficient that is more interpretable.
Simulated datasets are used to demonstrate how different agreement measures affect estimated inter-coder agreement.
A discourse relation annotation case study is used to illustrate the behavior of the evaluated agreement measures.

The proportion of double labels and the entropy of the label distribution are shown to affect agreement measures, and the bootstrapped adjusted agreement improves comparability of agreement scores across multi-label datasets.
A multilingual resource containing Wikipedia pages in 31 languages categorized into 219 fine-grained named entity categories is released.
Japanese Wikipedia pages were automatically categorized into the Extended Named Entity scheme with near‑perfect accuracy.
A shared task for Wikipedia categorization in 30 languages was organized, producing a multilingual resource of 32.5 million pages.
Ensemble learning combined system outputs to achieve higher average F‑measure than any single system.
The resource creation scheme is termed Resource by Collaborative Contribution (RbCC) and was used to construct structuring tasks for attribute extraction and link prediction in the SHINRA project.

A meta-learning approach is introduced for Persian natural language inference that leverages task information from Persian question answering and cross-lingual information from English natural language inference.
A task augmentation strategy for generating additional high-quality tasks is investigated for Persian natural language inference.
The proposed meta-learning model consistently outperforms a baseline approach on evaluations across four languages and an auxiliary task.
The impact of initializing parameters via zero-shot evaluation and CCA similarity on model performance is analyzed.
A method maps a syntactic dependency tree to a formal meaning representation based on Discourse Representation Theory using a series of graph transformations.
The method leverages off-the-shelf Universal Dependency parsers to produce semantic parses.
The resulting UD semantic parser achieves good performance for English, German, Italian, and Dutch and surpasses a neural semantic parser for lower-resourced languages.
The UD semantic parser does not hallucinate output.
The UD semantic parser is easy to port to other languages and provides complete transparency.
MPEE unifies horizontal and vertical early exiting to accelerate inference of transformer-based pretrained language models.
The vertical component of MPEE uses recycling early exiting classifier memory and weighted self-distillation to improve early exiting classifier performance.

The horizontal component of MPEE uses recycling class attention memory to emphasize informative tokens and truncates less informative tokens via weighted fusion, isolating them from subsequent computation.
MPEE achieves higher inference acceleration while maintaining competent performance compared to existing competitive methods.
Token and Head Adaptive Transformer compresses and accelerates BERT-based models via simple fine-tuning.
Progressive token and head pruning scheme eliminates redundant tokens and attention heads in later layers.
Multi-objective evolutionary search with a FLOPs constraint discovers joint token and head pruning strategies that maximize accuracy and efficiency under various computational budgets.
Large portions of tokens and attention heads can be pruned while achieving superior performance compared to baseline BERT-based models and Length Adaptive Transformers across downstream NLP tasks.
MobileBERT trained with joint token and head pruning achieves higher GLUE performance than Length Adaptive Transformer and the original model.
OpticE is a relation-adaptive embedding model for link prediction derived from optical interference and coherence theory.
OpticE enables entity semantics to vary automatically across different relations.
OpticE introduces a negative sampling method that combines multimapping properties with self-adversarial learning.
OpticE achieves competitive performance with existing link prediction methods on practical knowledge graph benchmarks.
WordNet-QU extends the WordNet lexical database with Quechua lexical entries.
WordNet-QU comprises a manually curated collection of digital lexical resources for Quechua.
WordNet-QU aligns Quechua synsets with Spanish using a synset alignment algorithm.
WordNet-QU contains 28,582 unique synset IDs distributed across Southern, Central, Northern, and Amazonian Quechua varieties.
Coref-MDG captures inter-document relationships based on commonsense and similarity and intra-document co-referential structures of knowledge segments within grounding documents.
CorefDiffs linearizes the static Coref-MDG into a conversational sequence logic.
CorefDiffs performs knowledge selection by incorporating contextual graph structures and knowledge difference sequences.
CorefDiffs outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods on three public document‑grounded dialog benchmarks.
Effective modeling of co‑reference and knowledge difference is critical for transitions in document‑grounded conversations.
SelF-Eval is a self-supervised fine-grained dialogue evaluation framework that models the correlation between turn quality and overall dialogue quality.
An automatic data construction method assigns fine-grained scores to arbitrary dialogue data.
SelF-Eval is trained with a multi-level contrastive learning schema that distinguishes different score levels.
SelF-Eval achieves higher consistency with human evaluations and outperforms state-of-the-art models on multiple benchmarks.
A detailed analysis of the experimental results is provided.
Syllable-based tokenization is applied to open-vocabulary language modeling in 21 languages using rule-based syllabification for six languages and hyphenation as a proxy for the remaining languages.
Syllable-based language models achieve comparable perplexity to subword models while outperforming character-level and other subword representations.

Syllable-based inputs improve neural machine translation performance for the Spanish–Shipibo-Konibo low-resource pair, surpassing unsupervised subword and morphological segmentation methods in both pairwise and multilingual translation systems when translating into Shipibo-Konibo.
SAKDP is a two-stage framework for knowledge-grounded dialogue generation that uses section-aware encoding of linearized commonsense knowledge.
PriorRanking is a ranking network that estimates the relevance of retrieved knowledge facts and clusters them into three priority sections.
Section-aware strategies encode linearized knowledge so that the pre-trained language model processes only high-priority facts, improving efficiency while maintaining performance.
Automatic and human evaluations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance.
A survey of literature and resources assesses the state of language technology for healthcare across world languages.
A rapid chatbot building exercise is conducted for multiple Asian and African languages with varying resource availability.
The assessment finds that language technology for healthcare is in a poor state even for languages with large speaker bases such as Sinhala and Hausa.
The assessment identifies gaps that can inform prioritization of research and investment strategies in language technology for healthcare.

A hierarchical multi-task learning framework with a coarse-to-fine paradigm is introduced, featuring a shared bottom level for all tasks, a mid-level divided among task groups, and a task-specific top level.
Task correlation is analyzed through data property, manual design, and model-based relevance perspectives to group similar tasks.
The hierarchical framework enables learning of basic language properties from all tasks, improves performance on relevant tasks, and reduces negative impact from irrelevant tasks.
The method achieves superior performance on thirteen benchmark datasets across five natural language understanding tasks.
The Bahasa Rojak Crawled Corpus (BRCC) is the first Bahasa Rojak corpus constructed for pre-training language models, created via data augmentation.
Mixed XLM is a new pre-trained model that automatically tags the language of each input token to process code-mixed input.
SentiBahasaRojak is a new Bahasa Rojak sentiment analysis dataset with substantial inter-annotator agreement.
Mixed XLM pre-trained on BRCC is evaluated on the SentiBahasaRojak dataset for social media code-mixing scenarios.
Pro-KD defines a progressive knowledge distillation technique that follows the teacher's training footprints to provide a smoother training path for the student.
Pro-KD mitigates the capacity-gap problem in knowledge distillation.
Pro-KD mitigates the checkpoint-search problem in knowledge distillation.
Pro-KD yields superior results compared to state-of-the-art techniques on image classification, natural language understanding, and question answering tasks.
CitRet is a hybrid model for cited text span retrieval that leverages semantic and syntactic structural characteristics of scientific documents.
CitRet augments SBERT-based contextual embeddings with pre-trained non-contextual Word2Vec embeddings to calculate semantic textual similarity.
CitRet achieves effective performance using only 1040 documents for finetuning.
CitRet outperforms previous state-of-the-art on the CLSciSumm shared tasks.
A new black-box NLP adversarial attack transfers adversarial examples from a similar domain to a target domain to degrade target model performance.
Learn2Weight predicts weight adjustments for a target model as a defensive strategy against similar-domain adversarial examples.
Learn2Weight outperforms standard black-box defense methods such as adversarial training and defense distillation in defending against similar-domain adversarial attacks.
Knowledge Distillation using Optimal Transport (KNOT) distills semantic knowledge from multiple teacher networks to a student network without accessing teacher parameters or training data.
KNOT trains a student model by minimizing the optimal transport cost between the student’s label probability distribution and the weighted sum of teacher predictions.
Semantic Distance (SD) is a metric that measures semantic closeness between predicted and ground-truth label distributions.
KNOT achieves higher Semantic Distance performance than baseline methods across three NLP tasks.
KNOT attains comparable standard accuracy and F1 to entropy-based distillation.
A concatenated French and Martinican Creole training baseline achieves optimal parsing performance for Martinican Creole despite extremely limited training data.
POS tag embeddings improve parsing of Martinican Creole compared to lexical embeddings, though they exhibit negative transfer effects.
Transfer learning and multi-task learning models are evaluated for developing a Martinican Creole parser using a French treebank.
Various input feature configurations and strategies for handling severe treebank size imbalance are examined for Martinican Creole parsing.
Attention network architectures that augment clinical text with support sets of diagnostic codes improve diagnosis prediction.
Augmentation strategies that incorporate diagnostic code support sets from prior admissions or emerging during differential diagnosis enhance robustness of diagnosis prediction.
Support sets improve prediction of less common diagnosis codes.
Support sets substantially improve prediction of pregnancy- and gynecology-related diagnoses.
The proposed method outperforms the PubMedBERT baseline for diagnosis prediction.
A set of semantically well-formed and nonsensical English sentences for testing subject‑verb agreement across syntactic complexities is generated.
Human subject‑verb agreement performance on the generated items is measured through an online crowdsourcing experiment.
BERT‑base subject‑verb agreement performance on the generated items is evaluated.
Both humans and BERT‑base make more subject‑verb agreement errors on nonsensical items than on meaningful items, especially when an attractor noun phrase intervenes between the subject and the verb.
The increase in subject‑verb agreement errors caused by lack of meaning is larger for BERT‑base than for humans, indicating higher lexical sensitivity of BERT‑base on this task.
A classifier identifies machine-written sentences and reveals that their linguistic features differ significantly from those of human-written sentences.
Distilled importance scores from the classifier are used to reweight synthetic sentences during training of a sentence embedding model.
The reweighted synthetic-data-trained sentence embedding model generalizes well to real-world datasets and outperforms baseline models on sentence similarity tasks.
Multi-level Community-awareness Graph Neural Network (MC-GNN) jointly models local and global relationships between words and their linguistic roles across multiple communities.
MC-GNN replaces the self-attention layer in the encoder of a transformer-based neural machine translation model.
MC-GNN alleviates the over-smoothing problem caused by sparse tree-shaped semantic graphs.
MC-GNN improves translation quality on four language-pair datasets.
MC-GNN reduces time complexity for translating very long sentences.
TR-HGAN detects fake news on social media using a hierarchical graph attention architecture.
TR-HGAN includes a topology imbalance smoothing strategy that assigns a topology weight to each node in a news propagation graph.
TR-HGAN includes a hierarchical-level attention mechanism that adaptively weights relationships to assess their authenticity.
TR-HGAN outperforms existing state-of-the-art fake news detection methods on real-world datasets.
A bidirectionally-adaptive transfer learning method splits the parent encoder's six transformer layers into two groups with distinct adaptation goals for low-resource neural machine translation.
The first group learns to encode low-resource languages in a bilingual shareable latent space using a generative adversarial network and masked language modeling.
The second group is directly transferred from the high-resource parent model and fine‑tuned together with the first group for low-resource translation.
Experiments on the SETIMES benchmark demonstrate substantial improvements in low-resource neural machine translation for Albanian, Macedonian, Croatian, and Romanian.
GandR retrieves exemplars whose outputs are similar to a preliminary prediction.
GandR generates a preliminary prediction using input-based retrieval of exemplars.
GandR generates a final prediction using exemplars retrieved based on output similarity to the preliminary prediction.
GandR achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple low-resource semantic parsing tasks.
SaAML integrates an adversarial network architecture into a meta-learning system for few-shot text classification.
SaAML exploits a temporal convolutional network to encourage discriminative representation learning.
SaAML incorporates an attention mechanism to promote comprehensive feature expression.
SaAML achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on four benchmark datasets for 1-shot and 5-shot text classification.
GraDA synthesizes factual data samples from knowledge graphs for commonsense reasoning datasets.
GraDA includes a graph-to-text model that conditionally generates questions from graph entities and relations.
GraDA includes a generator trained with a GAN loss that generates distractors for synthetic questions.
GraDA improves performance on SocialIQA, CODAH, HellaSwag, and CommonsenseQA.
GraDA works well for generative tasks such as ProtoQA.
Training with GraDA improves robustness to semantic adversaries.
Human evaluation indicates that synthetic datasets produced by GraDA are factual and answerable.
A method distills language-agnostic meaning embeddings from multilingual sentence encoders for unsupervised quality estimation of machine translation.
Adversarial training is used to eliminate language-specific information from meaning embeddings.
The method yields higher correlation with human evaluations in unsupervised quality estimation of machine translation.
FeatureBART incorporates syntactic features such as lemma, part-of-speech, and dependency labels into a span prediction based pre-training framework.
FeatureBART uses relevance mechanisms to incorporate features, which perform better than simple concatenation.
Feature-based models pretrained with FeatureBART achieve large improvements in low-resource bilingual neural machine translation compared to models without features.
Feature-based models pretrained with FeatureBART achieve modest improvements in low-resource multilingual neural machine translation compared to models without features.
ET5 is an end-to-end framework for conversational machine reading comprehension that shares parameters across decision making, span extraction, and question rephrasing.
