ET5 leverages a shared parameter mechanism to incorporate fine-grained entailment reasoning information across CMRC sub-tasks.
ET5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ShARC benchmark.

Language embedding embodiment incorporates language embeddings at multiple critical points in the information flow of a multilingual neural machine translation model to amplify translation direction signals.
Language-aware multi-head attention represents each language with a matrix that is divided into multiple heads, enabling learning of language representations in multiple subspaces.
Language-aware multi-head attention improves both supervised and zero-shot translation performance and reduces off-target translation in massively multilingual neural machine translation.
Matrix-based language representations learned by the proposed methods capture rich linguistic typology features as demonstrated by typology prediction experiments.
Pre-training produces flatter loss landscapes than random initialization, indicating improved generalization in resource-rich neural machine translation.
Pre-training produces smoother lexical probability distributions than random initialization, indicating increased negative diversity in resource-rich neural machine translation.
A model fusion algorithm aligns neurons between pre-trained and randomly initialized models using optimal transport.
The model fusion algorithm improves translation accuracy, generalization, and negative diversity on resource-rich translation benchmarks.
CILDA is a learning-based data augmentation technique tailored for knowledge distillation.
CILDA uses intermediate layer representations of the main task to improve the quality of augmented samples.
CILDA employs intermediate layer matching with a contrastive loss to enhance masked adversarial data augmentation.
CILDA outperforms existing state-of-the-art knowledge distillation approaches on the GLUE benchmark.
CILDA outperforms existing state-of-the-art knowledge distillation approaches in out-of-domain evaluation.
A semi-supervised multimodal model is adapted to learn gesture embeddings from aligned speech and gesture data in TED talks.
Gesture embeddings predict the native language of speakers.
Gesture embeddings improve language prediction performance.
Probing of gesture embeddings reveals that they encode psycholinguistic category information.
Function words drive the expressiveness of gestures.
WILDQA is a video understanding dataset composed of videos recorded in outdoor settings.
Video Evidence Selection is introduced as a task that identifies visual support for a given question and answer.
Baseline model evaluations reveal that WILDQA presents new challenges for vision and language research.
ngram-OAXE extends the OAXE loss by permitting reordering only between n‑gram phrases while enforcing strict word order within each phrase.
ngram-OAXE improves translation of n‑gram phrases in non‑autoregressive machine translation.
ngram-OAXE yields more fluent translations by better modeling sentence structure.
ngram-OAXE demonstrates effectiveness and universality across non‑autoregressive translation benchmarks, language pairs, and data scales.
A data uncertainty constraint leverages multiple semantically-equivalent questions to learn robust feature representations for text-to-SQL parsing.
The data uncertainty constraint reduces sensitivity of learned representations and improves parser robustness.
A model uncertainty constraint enforces consistency of output representations across perturbed encoding networks to refine query representations.
The model uncertainty constraint enhances generalizability and stability of neural text-to-SQL parsers.
Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets demonstrate that the combined method achieves state-of-the-art performance for text-to-SQL parsing.
Partial information decomposition provides a mathematically precise characterization of morphological systems.
Highly fusional languages are characterized by high levels of synergistic information.
A semantic-based pre-training framework leverages Abstract Meaning Representation to capture core semantic information in dialogues.
The framework extends standard pre-training with three tasks that learn core semantic units, semantic relations, and overall semantic representation from AMR graphs.
The model achieves superior performance on understanding both chit-chat and task-oriented dialogues compared to prior models.
It is the first work to use deep semantic representation for dialogue pre-training.
Dynamic position encoding generates target-aware position embeddings that refine each word’s positional information.
Dynamic position encoding learns source token positions end-to-end instead of using fixed predefined embeddings.
Dynamic position encoding improves neural machine translation quality on English-to-German, English-to-French, and English-to-Italian datasets relative to the standard Transformer.
BiBL enables bidirectional information transition between Text-to-AMR parsing and AMR-to-Text generation via a single-stage multitasking strategy.
BiBL achieves higher performance than strong previous seq2seq refinements on benchmark datasets without using extra training data.
BiBL provides a data-efficient approach that reduces training complexity compared to methods that rely on large extra data or complex structure modifications.
Cross-lingual feature extraction (CFE) learns cross-lingual features from monolingual corpora for low-resource unsupervised bilingual lexicon induction.
Integrating cross-lingual representations with pre-trained word embeddings enables fully unsupervised initialization for unsupervised bilingual lexicon induction.
The CFE method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on low-resource language pairs such as English–Vietnamese, English–Thai, English–Chinese, and English–Japanese.
Learned cross-lingual features enhance the representational ability and robustness of existing embedding models, as demonstrated by an ablation study.
Categorization is introduced to source contextualized representations in neural machine translation.
Categorization reduces sparsity and overfitting by finding prototypes of token representations over the training set and integrating their embeddings into the source encoding.
The method reduces compositional generalization error rates on the CoGnition dataset.
The method consistently outperforms the Transformer baseline on a range of general machine translation datasets.
Speaker-context interaction joint encoding (SCIJE) uses interaction features between different speakers for discourse parsing on multi-party dialogues.
Same speaker prediction (SSP) is a second-stage pre-training task that enhances conversational context representations by predicting whether two utterances are from the same speaker.
The speaker-aware model achieves state-of-the-art performance on two standard benchmark datasets for multi-party discourse parsing.
A weakly supervised model solves math word problems using only the final answer as supervision.
The model learns to generate equations from the problem description and final answer, and uses the generated equations to train a supervised math word problem solver.
Various weakly supervised techniques for generating equations directly from problem descriptions and answers are proposed and compared.
The weakly supervised approach achieves higher accuracy than previous weakly supervised methods on the Math23K and AllArith datasets.
New datasets of roughly 10,000 math word problems each in English and Hindi are curated and released for training weakly supervised models.
A semi-supervised extension of the model provides further performance improvements and additional insights.

SPACE-2 is a tree-structured pre-trained conversation model that learns dialog representations from limited labeled dialogs and large-scale unlabeled dialogs via semi-supervised contrastive pre-training.
A general semantic tree structure (STS) is defined to unify inconsistent annotation schemas across different dialog datasets.
A novel multi-view score function is introduced to increase relevance of dialogs sharing similar STSs while pushing away completely different dialogs during supervised contrastive pre-training.
A basic self-supervised contrastive loss is added to refine learned representations using unlabeled dialogs.
SPACE-2 achieves new state-of-the-art performance on the DialoGLUE benchmark covering seven datasets and four dialog understanding tasks.
Greedy_Baseline, Greedy_Advanced, Beam Search, and Threading are introduced as methods for automatically converting forum posts and comments into multi-turn dialogues.
The conversion methods are generalizable to any discussion forum.
The best conversion method achieves higher appropriateness than the baseline method.
Applying the best method to posts from ten subreddits yields a corpus of ten thousand multi-turn dialogues.
The generated Reddit dialogue corpus is more engaging but slightly less natural than dialogues in existing datasets such as Blended Skill Talk, Daily Dialogue, and Topical Chat.
Generating the Reddit dialogue corpus requires substantially less human labor and monetary cost compared to existing dialogue datasets.
A large multi-turn dialogue corpus from Reddit is created for the first time.
An Iterative Scheduled Data-switch Training Framework is introduced for robust neural machine translation.
The framework employs two training stages that iteratively switch between authentic and adversarial examples.
An improved curriculum learning method with a sampling strategy is introduced to schedule noise injection.
Empirical evaluation shows that the proposed model achieves higher robustness than competitive baselines on multiple translation benchmarks.
A preliminary study demonstrates that requiring a model to translate authentic and adversarial examples simultaneously is suboptimal for robust neural machine translation.
HeadMask masks attention heads in Transformer-based neural machine translation models to alleviate head inequality.
HeadMask is implemented using two distinct masking strategies.
HeadMask yields translation improvements across multiple language pairs.
Empirical analyses demonstrate that HeadMask reduces model dependence on specific attention heads and mitigates head imbalance.
Compare-Aggregate, a classical sequence match method, serves as a competitive few-shot one-class learner when combined with meta-learning.
Compare-Aggregate with meta-learning outperforms transformer-based models on few-shot one-class tasks.
The classical sequence match approach requires substantially less training cost than transformer-based models for few-shot one-class learning.
Meta-learning induces high-correlation dimensions in transformer model features, which are related to the number of layers and heads.
The few-shot one-class problem can be effectively addressed by framing it as a sequence matching task.
Meta Self-Paced Domain Adaptation (MSP-DA) trains a neural network-based self-paced learning procedure within a meta-learning framework in an end-to-end manner.
MSP-DA derives the meta train-test dataset split based on the self-pacing difficulties of source domain examples using a domain-shift variation of the cluster assumption.
MSP-DA simultaneously leverages self-training and self-tuning of domain-specific hyperparameters during learning.
MSP-DA improves performance on target domains relative to state-of-the-art unsupervised domain adaptation approaches.
Analyses of MSP-DA reveal how individual source domains affect the learned hyperparameters.
A scoring model automatically learns a model-based reward for dual reinforcement learning in semantic parsing.
A curriculum learning based training strategy stabilizes the training process of dual reinforcement learning for semantic parsing.
A Chinese propositional logic and first-order logic dataset is constructed for semantic parsing.
The proposed method outperforms existing methods on multiple semantic parsing datasets.
Propositional and first-order logic expressions generated by the model improve the performance of existing natural language inference models.
Adaptive Meta-learner via Gradient Similarity (AMGS) is a novel method for few-shot text classification.
AMGS alleviates overfitting by acquiring potential semantic representations of samples through a self-supervised auxiliary task in the inner loop.
AMGS leverages gradient similarity to add constraints on gradients obtained by the base-learner in the outer loop.
A systematic analysis of the influence of regularization on the AMGS framework is provided.
AMGS consistently improves few-shot text classification performance compared with state-of-the-art optimization-based meta-learning approaches.
Joint Relationship Attention Network (JRAN) learns visual relationships among region features and visual-semantic relationships between region features and semantic features.
JRAN dynamically trades off between visual and visual-semantic relationship representations during generation.
JRAN employs a relationship-based attention mechanism that adaptively focuses on the relationship representation when predicting each word.
JRAN achieves state-of-the-art performance on image-text generation benchmarks such as MSCOCO and Flickr30k.
JRAN improves BLEU4 and CIDEr scores on the Flickr30k dataset.
The re-ranking approach leverages visual-semantic measures to select captions that maximally capture visual information in an image.
The re-ranking approach applies the Belief Revision framework to adjust likelihood scores of top-n captions based on semantic relatedness to visual context.
The re-ranker improves image-captioning system performance without requiring additional training or fine-tuning.
A two-step rationale-centric data augmentation method improves the generalization of few-shot NER models to cross-domain settings.
The data augmentation method is model-agnostic.
The method outperforms previous state-of-the-art approaches, including counterfactual data augmentation and prompt-tuning, on cross-domain NER tasks.
Four state-of-the-art image-domain noise learning methods are adapted for text classification.
A benchmark for noise learning in text classification includes seven methods, four datasets, and five noise modes.
The dataset Golden-Chnsenticorp (G-Chnsenticorp) is created without intrinsic label noise to enable accurate comparison of noise learning methods.
Intrinsic label noise is present in commonly-used text classification datasets such as TREC, Ag-News, and Chnsenticorp.
This benchmark is the first noise learning benchmark for text classification.
A structure-aware encoder pre-trained on auto-parsed data by predicting word dependencies and fine-tuned on gold dependency trees improves graph-based dependency parsing.
The pre-training approach predicts word dependencies instead of context words along dependency paths, differing from typical pre-training methods.
The approach is effective and robust across different dependency standards, model architectures, and parsers, outperforming strong baselines.
Only a small amount of auto-parsed sentences (approximately two thousand) is sufficient to obtain improvement when pre-training a vanilla BERT-large based parser without adding extra parameters.
Incremental structure expanding (ISE) frames scene graph modification as a graph expansion task that incrementally expands a source graph without altering unchanged structure.
A model that iteratively predicts nodes and edges is proposed to infer accurate and harmonious expansion decisions progressively.
A challenging dataset containing more complex queries and larger scene graphs than existing datasets is constructed.
The proposed approach outperforms previous state-of-the-art models on four benchmarks.
A morpheme-based scheme for Korean dependency parsing is proposed and integrated into Universal Dependencies.
Automatic conversion scripts between the original Universal Dependencies format and the morpheme-based format are developed.
The proposed morpheme-based format improves Korean dependency parsing performance for statistical and neural models such as UDPipe and Stanza.
A morpheme-based word embedding for Korean is constructed to support parsing with the proposed format.
Detailed error analysis of parsing results using the morpheme-based format is presented.

A-TIP introduces a unified text infilling component with modified attention mechanisms and intra- and inter-blank positional encoding to perceive the number of blanks and the infilling length for each blank.
A-TIP includes a plug-and-play discriminator that guides generation toward improved attribute relevance while preserving text fluency.
A-TIP achieves state-of-the-art performance on three open-source text infilling datasets.
A position offset label prediction subtask is added to encoder-decoder architectures for grammatical error correction.
An auxiliary task predicts position offset labels of tokens, integrating insertion, deletion, and substitution operations into a unified framework.
A new copy mechanism (P-copy) based on predicted position offset labels replaces the standard copy module.
The POL-Pc framework improves the performance of baseline grammatical error correction models across Chinese, English, and Japanese datasets.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on a Chinese grammatical error correction dataset.
InforMin-CL is an information minimization based contrastive learning model for unsupervised sentence embedding learning.

InforMin-CL retains useful information and discards redundant information in sentence embeddings by jointly maximizing mutual information and minimizing information entropy between positive instances.
InforMin-CL achieves information minimization through a combination of contrast and reconstruction objectives.
The reconstruction objective in InforMin-CL reconstitutes a positive instance from another positive instance to minimize information entropy between positive instances.
InforMin-CL attains state-of-the-art performance on a broad set of downstream tasks, including supervised and unsupervised semantic textual similarity evaluations.
Label Switching uses pseudo data to guide graph-to-graph annotation conversion.
Graph2Graph Linear Transformation inherits parameters to guide graph-to-graph annotation conversion.
Both conversion approaches require no manually designed features.
Both conversion approaches handle conversion between graph-structured annotations.
Both conversion approaches achieve higher conversion scores than strong baselines.
Graphs produced by these conversion approaches improve target parser performance compared to graphs produced by baselines.
CoHS-CQG is a two-stage conversational question generation framework that incorporates a context and history selection module.
The context and history selection module selects the top-p sentences and history turns based on relevance scores to shorten the input.
CoHS-CQG achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CoQA benchmark in both answer-aware and answer-unaware settings.
A method generates sentences with a coordinate structure by embedding a mask with a coordinating conjunction in two ways and forcing masked language models to fill the blanks with identical text.
Decoding methods for BERT and T5 are introduced that enforce synchronized predictions for different masks.
A training framework is developed that selects synthetic examples for the supervised coordination disambiguation task.
The generated coordination instances improve performance on the coordination disambiguation task in low-resource settings.
A triplet contrastive loss fine-tunes a pre-trained multilingual model on triplets consisting of an image and two texts in different languages with the same meaning.
Fine-tuning with the triplet contrastive loss improves the connection between images and non-English texts in multilingual multi-modal representation learning.
The fine-tuned model achieves performance gains in image-text retrieval, zero-shot image classification, and sentence embedding tasks.
A detailed empirical case study of out-of-vocabulary words in modern Bulgarian text is presented.
A multi-faceted distributional analysis of word-formation processes underlying Bulgarian out-of-vocabulary words is conducted.
A manual and automatic taxonomic analysis of types and multidimensional properties of Bulgarian out-of-vocabulary words is performed.
The analyses provide insights that can aid compositional translation, tagging, parsing, and language modeling of Bulgarian out-of-vocabulary words.
A string editing based model for Chinese grammatical error diagnosis requires less training data by using a unified workflow to handle various error types.
The model separates error detection and correction into distinct stages, reducing the tag vocabulary size for detection.
During detection, the model outputs only error types, which significantly reduces the tag vocabulary compared with other string editing based models.
The correction of certain grammatical errors is reformulated as a masked character inference task, leveraging abundant training data and mature solutions.
The model achieves superior performance relative to other Chinese grammatical error diagnosis models on NLPTEA-CGED datasets.
A data augmentation method for neural machine translation enforces stronger semantic consistency within and across languages using a Conditional Masked Language Model.
The Conditional Masked Language Model is bi-directional and conditions on left and right context as well as the translation label.
Conditioning on both source and target sentences during word substitution enables enforcement of semantic consistency in augmented data.
Soft word substitution replaces a word with a probabilistic distribution over the vocabulary to increase diversity in data augmentation.
The combined augmentation approach yields more realistic augmented data and achieves state-of-the-art translation quality compared to recent strong baselines.
A textual entailment model can be used for clause nominalization.
Fine-tuning a textual entailment model on clause nominalization achieves the best performance among evaluated methods.
The fine-tuned textual entailment model outperforms unsupervised approaches that rely on language model scores from a state-of-the-art neural language model.
Dual capsule attention mask network employs mutual learning between coarse-grained and fine-grained feature branches for visual question answering.
Stackable dual capsule attention module fuses features and locates evidence in visual question answering.
Separate branches process coarse-grained and fine-grained features to provide complementary information for visual question answering.
Combined dual-branch architecture yields state-of-the-art performance on the VQA-v2 dataset.
The proposed method enhances interpretability of visual question answering predictions.
Six orthogonal and comprehensive human evaluation criteria for automatic story generation are defined.
HANNA provides 1,056 annotated stories generated by ten different automatic story generation systems.
Correlations between 72 automatic metrics and the six human criteria are quantitatively evaluated using HANNA.
Current automatic metrics for story generation exhibit notable weaknesses as revealed by the analysis.
Practical recommendations for evaluating automatic story generation are formulated based on the analysis.
An overview of graph neural network approaches for automatic text summarization is provided.
The functionality of graph neural networks in the context of automatic text summarization is detailed.
A comprehensive overview of models that utilize graph neural networks for automatic text summarization is presented.
A technique captures the argumentative structure of legal documents by integrating argument role labeling into the summarization process.
The technique improves abstractive summarization performance for legal documents compared to strong baselines.
A homogeneous graph of sentence-level nodes combined with heterogeneous graph neural networks captures both inter- and intra-sentence semantic information for extractive long document summarization.
The method achieves state-of-the-art results on long-document summarization benchmarks PubMed and ArXiv.
A 2D view of dialogue based on a time‑speaker perspective is introduced to separate dialogue into time and speaker streams.
A two‑stream model called ATM combines the time and speaker streams for dialogue summarization.
ATM surpasses existing models on multiple dialogue summarization datasets.
ATM outperforms state‑of‑the‑art models on the QMSum dataset.
ATM improves summary faithfulness and receives higher human evaluation scores.
ATM demonstrates generalization ability to machine reading comprehension tasks, indicating applicability to other dialogue‑based tasks.
Visual Prompt Tuning generates image embeddings for category names as visual prompts to incorporate visual information in few-shot text classification with vision-language pre-trained models.
Visual Prompt Tuning adds visual prompts to the alignment of input samples and category names in the prompt-tuning paradigm.
Visual Prompt Tuning is the first method to deploy vision-language pre-trained models for few-shot text classification.
Visual Prompt Tuning yields significant improvements in zero-shot and few-shot text classification settings.
Visual Prompt Tuning surpasses recent prompt-tuning methods on several public text classification datasets.
Type-dependent prompt cycleQAG is a cyclic question-answer generation framework for multi-hop question generation.
Cycle consistency loss based on negative cross entropy is used to generate syntactically diverse questions.
Question generation and question answering are learned jointly in a cyclic manner.
The proposed method outperforms baseline models on a multi-hop question generation dataset according to ROUGE and human evaluation metrics.
QUAK is a synthetic Korean-English quality estimation dataset generated fully automatically.
QUAK consists of three sub-datasets (QUAK-M, QUAK-P, QUAK-H) created using three strategies that are free from language constraints.
The synthetic generation process enables scaling QUAK-P and QUAK-H to millions of examples and QUAK-M to several millions of examples without human effort.
Word-level quality estimation results are quantitatively analyzed in various ways with statistical analysis.
Increasing the size of the synthetic QE datasets yields meaningful performance improvements in quality estimation.
DivSumm is a dataset of dialect-diverse tweets with human-written extractive and abstractive summaries.
Human-written reference summaries exhibit fairly balanced dialect diversity.
Cluster-based pre-processing improves the overall quality of system-generated summaries while preserving dialect diversity.
The paper analyzes dialect diversity in both human-written reference summaries and system-generated summaries.
UPER uses prompting templates with a pre-trained language model to compute document and keyword perplexity for assessing semantic salience in multi-document summarization.
UPER functions as an unsupervised plug-in that enhances existing document salience metrics for multi-document summarization.
UPER improves abstract generation performance in multi-document summarization across multiple datasets, data settings, and abstractive backbone models.
A large-scale investigation of various summary coherence modelling methods is conducted on an even playing field.
Intra-system correlation and bias matrices are introduced as analysis measures that identify biases in coherence measures and provide robustness against system-level confounders.
Currently available automatic coherence measures are unable to assign reliable coherence scores to system summaries across all evaluation metrics.

Large-scale language models fine-tuned on self-supervised tasks show promising performance for assigning coherence scores to summaries when fine-tuning accounts for generalization across different summary lengths.
SICK uses an external knowledge model to generate commonsense inferences as additional context for abstractive dialogue summarization.
SICK selects the most probable commonsense inference with a similarity-based selection method.
SICK++ adds commonsense inference generation as a supervised auxiliary task in a multi-task learning setting for dialogue summarization.
Injecting commonsense knowledge into the summarization framework yields more informative and consistent summaries compared to existing methods.
Seven methods for adapting pre-trained vision-and-language models to text-only input are systematically compared.
The comparison involves three pre-trained vision-and-language models.
Evaluations on GLUE and Visual Property Norms demonstrate that adaptation method choice critically affects zero-shot text-only performance of vision-and-language models.
Vision-and-language models exhibit reduced sensitivity to adaptation method choice on non-zero-shot text-only tasks.
Adaptation methods yield heterogeneous performance across different vision-and-language models.
Unimodal language model counterparts match the performance of vision-and-language models on text-only tasks irrespective of adaptation method.
Multi-label unknown intent detection is defined as detecting unknown intents in utterances that may contain multiple intents.
AIK predicts the number of intents in an utterance and determines whether all intents are known to detect unknown intents.
AIK achieves state-of-the-art performance on multi-label unknown intent detection benchmarks.
AIK reduces the false positive rate for unknown intent detection compared to prior baselines.
Structure-Aware Cross-Attention (SACA) re-encodes input graph representations conditioned on the generated decoder context at each decoding step in a structure-aware manner.
Dynamic Graph Pruning (DGP) dynamically drops irrelevant nodes during the decoding process.
The combination of SACA and DGP achieves state-of-the-art results on the LDC2020T02 and ENT-DESC graph-to-text datasets.
The proposed method incurs only a minor increase in computational cost compared to prior encoder-decoder models.
GAP integrates graph-aware elements into pre-trained language models for knowledge graph-to-text generation.
GAP introduces a mask structure that captures neighborhood information in the input graph.
GAP introduces a type encoder that biases graph-attention weights according to the connection type.
GAP achieves performance surpassing state-of-the-art models on KG-to-text benchmarks without additional pre-training tasks and with fewer parameters.
GAP provides a modular framework that allows interchangeable graph-aware components and enables interpretation of KG-to-text models using topological and type information.
A two-step image captioning method that first predicts content-word skeletons and then generates captions from the skeletons denoises alt-text-derived training data.
Sub-selecting content words as skeletons improves the quality of captions generated from noisy alt-text datasets.
Predicted English skeletons can be leveraged to generate captions in French, Italian, German, Spanish, and Hindi.
Skeleton-based caption generation provides control over caption length, content, and gender expression, enabling human-in-the-loop interpretable semi-automatic corrections.
CoLo is a contrastive learning based re-ranking framework for one-stage summarization.
CoLo models a contrastive objective that enables summarization models to generate summaries directly according to summary-level scores without additional modules or parameters.
CoLo improves extractive and abstractive performance of one-stage summarization systems on the CNN/DailyMail benchmark while preserving parameter efficiency and inference efficiency.
CoLo reduces training computational cost compared to state-of-the-art multi-stage summarization systems.
CoLo achieves several-fold inference speed-up relative to state-of-the-art multi-stage summarization systems while maintaining comparable results.
Noise-injected consistency training regularizes model predictions for extractive summarization.
Entropy-constrained pseudo labeling obtains high-confidence labels from unlabeled predictions by comparing the entropy of supervised and unsupervised predictions.
Combining consistency training and pseudo labeling enforces low-density separation between classes in extractive summarization.
The combined framework improves performance of supervised learning on extractive summarization with limited labeled data.
JPG is a framework that jointly trains radiology report generation, feature alignment, and disease prediction tasks.
Feature alignment in JPG learns visual features that are correlated with textual descriptions.
Disease prediction in JPG records diseases as keywords to support report generation.
Joint training in JPG creates a feedback loop where improved reports provide harder samples for feature alignment and disease prediction, enhancing visual and textual representations.
JPG outperforms existing state-of-the-art image captioning and medical report generation methods on the IU X-RAY dataset.
A parallel corpus of offensive Reddit comments and their style-transferred inoffensive counterparts annotated by expert sociolinguists is released.
Discourse-aware style-transfer models that incorporate inferential links between a comment and the text it replies to are introduced for offensive Reddit text.
Two methods for integrating discourse relations with pretrained transformer models are proposed.
The discourse-aware models reduce offensiveness in Reddit text while preserving the original meaning.
The discourse-aware models achieve better meaning preservation than discourse-agnostic state-of-the-art models according to automatic metrics and human evaluation.
A reinforced multi-task network performs polite response generation as a primary task and sentiment analysis as a secondary task using a Transformer encoder-decoder.
User sentiment is automatically identified and neutral responses are transformed into polite responses that conform to the sentiment and conversational history.
Sentiment-annotated conversations from Twitter are used as training data for the model.
The approach attains superior performance compared to baseline models in polite response generation.
Distribution uncertainty caused by mismatch between training and test distributions leads to overconfident softmax scores for out-of-domain samples.
A Bayesian out-of-domain detection framework calibrates distribution uncertainty using Monte-Carlo Dropout.
The Bayesian out-of-domain detection framework can be integrated with existing softmax-based baselines.
The Bayesian out-of-domain detection framework improves out-of-domain detection performance while adding minimal inference overhead.
Analyses demonstrate that Bayesian learning enhances out-of-domain detection effectiveness.
Prefix-Controlled Generator (PCG) uses a task-specific prefix to adapt table structures to the input format of pre-trained language models for few-shot table-to-text generation.
Prefix-Controlled Generator (PCG) generates an input-specific prefix that controls the factual content and word order of the generated text.
Prefix-Controlled Generator (PCG) demonstrates effectiveness through automatic and human evaluations on multiple domains of the Wikibio dataset.
A domain adaptation approach automatically extracts source-specific terms using a domain classifier.
The approach penalizes source-specific terms based on feature-attribution scores for hate-speech classes.
The approach yields consistent improvements in cross-domain hate-speech detection performance.
A heterogeneous graph is constructed to simultaneously model inter-aspect relationships and aspect-context relationships for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Composition-based Heterogeneous Graph Multi-channel Attention Network (CHGMAN) encodes the constructed heterogeneous graph for multi-aspect multi-sentiment classification.
Experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of CHGMAN for multi-aspect multi-sentiment classification.
MulZDG is a multilingual learning framework for zero-shot dialogue generation that transfers knowledge from an English corpus to a non-English corpus without any target-language training samples.
MulZDG constructs multilingual code-switching dialogue datasets by translating randomly selected utterances from monolingual English datasets.
MulZDG trains a unified multilingual dialogue model on code-switching datasets, enabling implicit semantic alignment between languages.
MulZDG serves as a multilingual data augmentation method that improves performance of the resource-rich source language.
Experiments demonstrate that MulZDG achieves competitive zero-shot performance comparable to training with sufficient examples and substantially improves source-language performance.
Three speaker-aware supervised contrastive learning tasks—Token-level SCL, Turn-level SCL, and Global-level SCL—are introduced.
Probing tests and manual analysis reveal that pre-trained models fail to identify different speakers in dialogues, leading to factual errors.
The speaker-aware supervised contrastive learning tasks achieve significant performance improvement on two mainstream dialogue summarization datasets.
Human evaluations indicate that pre-trained models equipped with speaker-aware supervised contrastive learning generate summaries with better factual consistency.
KEF exploits adjective-noun pairs extracted from images to enhance visual attention for target-oriented multimodal sentiment classification.
KEF improves sentiment prediction capability for target-oriented multimodal sentiment classification.
KEF achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on two public datasets.
Answer localness modeling with a Gaussian bias enables focus on answer-surrounded context in question generation.
Syntactic mask attention makes the syntactic structure of the input passage accessible during question generation.
Text structure is modeled as answer position and syntactic dependency for question generation.
The answer localness and syntactic mask attention modules improve performance over the ProphetNet pre-trained model on the SQuAD dataset.
Combining answer localness modeling and syntactic mask attention yields performance comparable to state-of-the-art pre-trained models on the SQuAD dataset.
TSAM models speaker’s emotional influences in conversational history for causal emotion entailment.
TSAM comprises an Emotion Attention Network and a Speaker Attention Network that incorporate emotion and speaker information in parallel.
TSAM includes an interaction module that exchanges information between the emotion and speaker streams via a mutual BiAffine transformation.
TSAM classifies multiple utterances synchronously to capture correlations between utterances in a global view.
TSAM achieves state-of-the-art performance on causal emotion entailment benchmarks.
A modularized health coaching dialogue system combines simplified NLU and NLG frameworks with mechanism-conditioned empathetic response generation.
The system generates more empathetic responses than prior health coaching dialogue systems.
The system generates more fluent and coherent responses than prior health coaching dialogue systems.
The system outperforms state-of-the-art models on NLU tasks while requiring less annotation.
PSAT is a dataset of 112 college admissions instructions that have been professionally simplified and verified by admissions experts.
PSAT includes manual alignments of 1,883 original-simplified sentence pairs.
PSAT constitutes a first-of-its-kind corpus for evaluating and fine-tuning text simplification systems in a high-stakes genre distinct from existing simplification resources.
C-DNPG extends multi-head self-attention with a granularity head that automatically infers the hierarchical structure of a sentence by estimating the granularity level of each input token.
C-DNPG introduces two novel attention masks, granularity resonance and granularity scope, to efficiently encode granularity information into attention.
C-DNPG outperforms baseline models on paraphrase generation benchmarks such as Quora question pairs and Twitter URLs.
Qualitative analysis demonstrates that C-DNPG captures fine-grained levels of granularity in generated paraphrases.
Context-tuning is a continuous prompting approach that derives contextualized prompts from input text to elicit knowledge from pretrained language models for natural language generation.
Continuous inverse prompting models an inverse generation process from output to input to increase relevance of generated text to inputs.
Context-tuning fine-tunes a tiny fraction of pretrained language model parameters while maintaining good performance.
HeterGraphLongSum is a heterogeneous graph neural network that incorporates passage nodes alongside word and sentence nodes for extractive long document summarization.
HeterGraphLongSum enriches sentence representations by aggregating information from passage nodes in the graph.
HeterGraphLongSum achieves state-of-the-art performance on long document summarization benchmarks (Pubmed and Arxiv) without using pre-trained language models.
Visual information guided zero-shot paraphrase generation (ViPG) uses images as pivots for back-translation.
ViPG jointly trains an image captioning model and a paraphrasing model, leveraging the captioning model to guide paraphrasing model training.
Using images as pivots enables generation of paraphrases with good relevancy, fluency, and diversity.
ViPG operates without requiring a parallel paraphrase corpus, relying only on paired image-caption data.
A simplified stance reasoning process that relaxes annotation granularity from token-level to sentence-level is introduced for stance detection.
Sub-tasks of the simplified stance reasoning process are implemented by maximizing mutual information between texts and opinioned targets.
A series of new test sets for evaluating stance detection models from various aspects are collected and constructed.
The proposed model outperforms previous task-agnostic debiasing methods on most of the new test sets while maintaining comparable performance to existing stance detection models.
RILGNet models aspect correlation in aspect-based sentiment analysis using a recurrent mechanism that iteratively improves joint aspect representations.
RILGNet employs inverse learning guidance to select aspect features by considering aspect correlation, providing more useful information for polarity determination.
RILGNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on SemEval 2014 datasets in multi-aspect sentiment analysis scenarios.
A survey of state-of-the-art multimodal disinformation detection covering text, images, speech, video, social media network structure, and temporal information.
Existing multimodal disinformation detection studies typically examine factuality and harmfulness in isolation.
MuCDN separates multi-party conversations into detached threads to understand conversational context for emotion recognition.
MuCDN employs two detachment methods for context modeling and speaker-specific modeling within detached threads.
MuCDN bridges the detached thread representations through a mutual module.
MuCDN achieves superior performance compared to baseline models on two emotion recognition datasets.
POS Guided Softmax explicitly models the probability of the next part-of-speech tag and the probability of the next token conditioned on the target part-of-speech.
POS Guided Sampling enriches part-of-speech diversity during decoding to address low diversity in neural text generation.
POS Guided Softmax and Sampling generate text with higher diversity while maintaining comparable quality relative to existing state-of-the-art methods.
A real-world code-switched offensive content dataset for testing three language pairs (en-fr, en-es, en-de) is released.
A synthetic code-switched offensive content dataset for training is released.
An algorithm for generating synthetic code-switched offensive content is introduced.
A process for gathering human-generated code-switched offensive content data is described.
A keyword classification baseline and a multilingual transformer-based classification model are evaluated on the released datasets.
SHEMuD provides parallel English utterances for Hindi humor data and includes sentiment and emotion annotations for each utterance.
A multitask framework is introduced that performs humor detection as the primary task and sentiment and emotion identification as auxiliary tasks.
The framework incorporates a Context Transformer that captures deep contextual relationships among input utterances.
The framework incorporates a Sentiment and Emotion aware Embedding (SE-Embedding) that integrates emotion and sentiment information with humor context.
Multitask learning with sentiment and emotion auxiliary tasks improves humor detection performance compared to single-task learning in both monolingual and multilingual settings.
SITSM selects in-context examples using emotion and situational information.
EMOSITSM selects in-context examples using emotion and situational information.
DIFF-EPITOME is an automatic evaluation metric that reflects the human tendency to express empathy.
DIFF-EPITOME effectively measures the degree of human empathy.
GPT-3 generates empathetic dialogues via prompt-based in-context learning with competitive performance relative to Blender 90M.
SOE generates long texts by first outlining summaries for different segments and then elaborating each bullet point into the corresponding segment.
The reconstruction strategy extracts segment summaries in an unsupervised manner by selecting the most informative part of each segment.
Summaries in SOE provide high-level guidance for text generation, avoiding reliance on local word predictions.
Conditional dependencies between summaries capture high-level discourse dependencies that are preserved during summary expansion.
Representing contexts as concise summaries enables SOE to consider significantly more context during generation.
Experiments demonstrate that SOE produces higher-quality long texts and converges faster than prior methods.
CoCGAN incorporates contrastive learning into adversarial category text generation to model flexible data-to-class and data-to-data relations.
The discriminator in CoCGAN optimizes a contrastive learning objective to capture both flexible data-to-class relations and data-to-data relations among training samples.
The generator in CoCGAN produces realistic category texts that aim to confuse the discriminator.
CoCGAN achieves significant improvements over baseline category text generation models on synthetic and real datasets.
HI-ASA models two-way interactions between aspect extraction and sentiment classification via hierarchical interactions.
The shallow-level interaction in HI-ASA uses a cross-stitch mechanism to selectively combine task-specific features.
The deep-level interaction in HI-ASA applies a mutual information technique to mutually constrain learning between the two tasks in the output layer.
HI-ASA achieves superior performance compared to baselines on three real-world datasets for joint span-based aspect-sentiment analysis.
GRETEL incorporates a graph contrastive topic model with a pre-trained language model to leverage both global and local contextual semantics for long document extractive summarization.
The graph contrastive topic model integrates a hierarchical transformer encoder and graph contrastive learning to fuse semantic information from the global document context and the gold summary.
GRETEL encourages extraction of salient sentences that are topically related to the gold summary rather than redundant sentences covering sub-optimal topics.
GRETEL achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on general-domain and biomedical long document extractive summarization datasets.
A multimodal approach detects humor in TV show utterances.
The approach predicts humor intensity by estimating audience laughter duration.
Prerecorded laughter is used as annotation, with laughter length serving as a proxy for humor intensity.
Evaluation on unseen episodes demonstrates accurate humor detection and accurate prediction of laughter duration.
A dynamic feature network is designed to fully exploit pre-trained features for efficient multi-source unsupervised sentiment domain adaptation.
An asymmetric mutual learning strategy is proposed to robustly estimate pseudo-labels of a target domain using knowledge from multiple source models.
The proposed method achieves superior performance compared to recent state-of-the-art approaches on multiple sentiment benchmarks.
Extensive ablation studies verify the effectiveness of each proposed module.
Prompt formulation significantly affects the performance of NLI-based zero-shot emotion classifiers across different corpora.
Experiments on multiple emotion datasets from tweets, events, and blogs using three NLI models reveal corpus-dependent prompt sensitivity.
Combining multiple prompts into an ensemble yields a classifier that is more robust across corpora than any individual prompt.
The prompt ensemble achieves performance comparable to the best single prompt for each corpus.
The encoder separates tokens into framing and topic groups to capture structural information of arguments.
The encoder incorporates paragraph-level position information to model high-level structure.
The encoder demonstrates effectiveness for literature discourse analysis on both a self-constructed corpus and a public corpus.
A moderation tool is introduced to assist moderators in promoting mutual understanding in the online climate change debate.
Classifiers are trained to label incoming posts with the arguments they entail, emphasizing minority arguments.
Active learning is applied to augment training data with rare arguments.
Lexical patterns that distinguish each argument from others are extracted.
Climate change arguments form clearly separable clusters in embedding space.
Argument classes are characterized by unique lexical patterns that provide quick insight into key concepts.
Augmenting training data is necessary for classifiers to adequately recognize rare arguments.
A dataset of professional reviews annotated with overall sentiment towards each volitional entity is introduced.
Experiments evaluate the contribution of document-level, sentence-level, and target-level sentiment analysis to Entity-Level Sentiment Analysis.
No single existing proxy task achieves satisfactory performance for Entity-Level Sentiment Analysis.
Sentiment is expressed towards targets that have a sentiment-relevant relation to a volitional entity, not only when the entity is directly mentioned.
The identified sentiment-relevant relations extend beyond anaphoric coreference resolution.
LD2G is a learnable dependency-based double graph architecture for aspect-based sentiment classification.
Multi-task domain adaptive pretraining combines Biaffine Attention and Masked Language Modeling to incorporate structural, relational, and linguistic features in sentiment text.
A dependency-enhanced double graph-based MPNN fuses structure and relation features for aspect-based sentiment classification.
The LD2G model outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on four benchmark aspect-based sentiment classification datasets.
First approaches for recognizing alternative lexicalizations of discourse relations are presented.
A pattern-based approach for recognizing alternative lexicalizations of discourse relations is evaluated.
A sequence labeling model for recognizing alternative lexicalizations of discourse relations is evaluated.
An experiment on pre-classification of candidate sentences for alternative lexicalization recognition is conducted.
An initial qualitative analysis of error cases made by pattern-based and sequence labeling models for alternative lexicalization recognition is provided.
A multitask framework jointly optimizes metaphor identification, component identification, and metaphor generation for Chinese nominal metaphors.
The metaphor identification module performs self-training to discover novel metaphors from a large unlabeled corpus.
The component identification module conditions generation on identified metaphor components to improve coherence.
An annotated corpus of Chinese sentences with diverse metaphorical patterns is constructed to train the identification and component modules.
The framework generates diverse Chinese nominal metaphors with good readability and high novelty.
Human evaluation indicates that the framework achieves higher consistency and creativity than baseline models.

Sentiment Interpretable Logic Tensor Network (SILTN) provides a neurosymbolic formalism that enables learning and reasoning with a differentiable first-order logic language for aspect-term sentiment analysis.
Two-stage syntax knowledge distillation (TSynKD) is a learning strategy that improves the accuracy of SILTN.
SILTN delivers high interpretability for aspect-term sentiment analysis.
SILTN delivers computational efficiency for aspect-term sentiment analysis.
Experiments on widely used aspect-term sentiment analysis datasets demonstrate that TSynKD improves SILTN's accuracy.
Multi-Grained Consistency Regularization (MGCR) leverages massive unlabeled data for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
Two granularity-specific filters are designed to remove noisy data for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
MGCR achieves superior performance compared with previous state-of-the-art methods on four target-oriented opinion words extraction benchmark datasets.
In-depth analysis shows that the granularity-specific filters effectively improve model performance.
Simple heuristics are introduced for generating contrastive test cases from the ImpPres dataset.
Transformer-based NLI models are evaluated on the generated contrastive test cases.
Analysis of model predictions on ImpPres sub-datasets reveals reliance on structural and lexical cues rather than pragmatic reasoning.
UGEN formulates joint multiple intent detection and slot filling as a question-answering problem.
UGEN uses five types of instructional prompt templates that each contain a question, candidate options, and the original utterance.
Instructional prompts guide UGEN to understand intents, slots, and their implicit correlations.
UGEN achieves state-of-the-art performance on two multi-intent benchmark datasets in the full-data setting.
UGEN outperforms baselines by a large margin in 5-shot and 10-shot few-shot scenarios, demonstrating robustness.
A linguistic analysis of segment-level errors in Otter’s automatic captioning system is performed for native and non-native English speakers of diverse language backgrounds.
Language-specific error profiles for vowels and consonants are derived based on linguistic theory.
Certain categories of ASR errors are predictable from the phonological structure of a speaker’s native language.
A probing task for evaluating topic-comment structure in Japanese language models is introduced.
A crowdsourced dataset of human topicalization judgments for Japanese is released.
Neural language models exhibit less context-dependent behavior in topicalization judgments than humans.
Extractive summaries are produced for a dataset of German-language newspaper commentaries.
An in-depth analysis of the connection between summary sentences and RST-based features is performed.
Insights from the analysis are transferred to various automated summarisation models.
RST features, particularly nuclearity and sentence-level relations, are informative for extractive summarisation.
HGraph-CL is a hierarchical graph contrastive learning framework for multimodal sentiment analysis.
HGraph-CL builds a unimodal graph for each modality to capture modality-specific sentiment implications.
HGraph-CL applies graph contrastive learning with unimodal graph augmentations to explore intra-modal relations.
HGraph-CL constructs a multimodal graph from unimodal graphs to capture inter-modal sentiment relations.
HGraph-CL applies graph contrastive learning with multimodal augmentation graphs to learn inter-modal relations.
HGraph-CL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on two benchmark multimodal sentiment analysis datasets.
A corrected version of a subset of the FactBank dataset is released.
Multiple training paradigms, data smoothing techniques, and polarity classifiers are evaluated on FactBank.
F-measure is presented as an important alternative evaluation metric for factuality.
New state-of-the-art results are achieved for four corpora including FactBank.
An error analysis is conducted on FactBank combined with two similar corpora.
A Locator model predicts Positions-to-Poison dynamically from context in text-based backdoor attacks.
Four strategies leveraging dynamically predicted Positions-to-Poison are introduced to enhance backdoor attack performance.
The Locator model yields a smaller test accuracy gap on clean data compared to prior methods.
The Locator model yields a higher attack success rate on poisoned inputs compared to prior methods.
Human evaluation indicates that the dynamically predicted Positions-to-Poison are important for classification.
An unsupervised multi-scale context-sensitive text-to-speech model for audiobooks is introduced.
A multi-scale hierarchical context encoder predicts both global-scale and local-scale context style embeddings from a wider text context in a hierarchical manner.
A multi-scale reference encoder extracts global and local style embeddings from reference speech to guide speaking style prediction.
A bi-reference attention mechanism aligns local-scale reference style embedding sequences and local-scale context style embedding sequences with phoneme embedding sequences.
The proposed method achieves superior naturalness and expressiveness compared to baselines on a real-world multi-speaker Mandarin novel audio dataset.
A multimodal mental health conversational corpus annotated at the utterance level with emotional reasoning and related emotion is introduced.
A novel task of detecting emotional reasoning and accompanying emotions in conversations is defined.
A multimodal multitask framework that includes a novel multimodal feature fusion technique and a contextuality learning module is developed for emotion detection and emotional reasoning detection.

The multimodal multitask framework leverages multimodal information and commonsense reasoning to achieve higher accuracy and F1 scores on emotion detection and emotional reasoning detection than existing state-of-the-art models.
Experiments determine the effects of context on human judgments in shallow discourse parsing.
Experiments determine the effect of quantifying uncertainty with annotator confidence ratings on model accuracy and calibration in shallow discourse parsing.
Including annotator accuracy and confidence improves model accuracy for implicit discourse relation prediction.
Incorporating confidence into the model’s temperature function yields models with better-calibrated confidence measures.
Qualitative analysis reveals insights into human and model behavior on shallow discourse parsing datasets.
A transfer learning methodology that leverages knowledge from confluent tasks is applied to argument mining.
A systematic study evaluates a wide range of knowledge sources for transferring to argument mining.
New state-of-the-art performance is achieved on argument component identification, component classification, and relation determination.
A lean approach that avoids extensive feature and model engineering provides novel empirical insights into argument mining.
E-VarM combines multi-level semantics from all hidden layers to mask out task-irrelevant words.
E-VarM uses contrastive learning to readjust distances between representations.
Empirical evaluation on ten benchmark text classification datasets shows that E-VarM simultaneously improves interpretability and accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods.
Large-scale pseudo-problems for basic-event contingent reasoning are automatically generated using an existing data construction method.
Training with the generated pseudo-problems enhances performance on commonsense contingent reasoning.
Training with the generated pseudo-problems enhances performance on discourse relation analysis, the Japanese Winograd Schema Challenge, and JCommonsenseQA via transfer learning.
Transfer learning demonstrates that contingent knowledge acquired from pseudo-problems generalizes to related tasks.
A stance detection model that incorporates sentiment information and commonsense knowledge improves transferability in zero-shot settings.
The model includes a graph autoencoder module to acquire commonsense knowledge.
The stance detection component integrates sentiment and commonsense features.
The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the VAST zero-shot and few-shot benchmark.
Ablation studies demonstrate that each module (graph autoencoder, sentiment integration, commonsense integration) contributes significantly to performance.
Analysis of the relations between sentiment, commonsense, and stance supports the effectiveness of using sentiment and commonsense for stance detection.
Dialo-AP is an end-to-end argument parser that constructs argument graphs from dialogues.
Dialo-AP formulates argument mining as dependency parsing of elementary and argumentative discourse units.
Dialo-AP is trained using extensive pre-training and curriculum learning on nine diverse corpora.
Dialo-AP generates argument graphs from dialogues by performing all sub-tasks of argument mining.
Dialo-AP achieves significant improvements across all argument mining tasks compared to existing state-of-the-art baselines, as validated by rigorous human evaluation.
MuSeCLIR is a new evaluation dataset for cross-lingual information retrieval that focuses on polysemous common nouns with multiple possible translations.
MuSeCLIR is constructed from multilingual Wikipedia and supports searches on documents written in French, German, Italian, Chinese, and Japanese.
Baseline statistical and neural models evaluated on MuSeCLIR reveal that the dataset imposes a higher requirement on systems to disambiguate query terms.
Contrastive Cross-Channel Data Augmentation (C3DA) is a training framework that leverages an in-domain generator to construct multi-aspect samples for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
C3DA boosts the robustness of aspect-based sentiment analysis models via contrastive learning on generated multi-aspect data.
C3DA generates synthetic sentences through an Aspect Augmentation Channel that conditions on a given aspect.
C3DA generates synthetic sentences through a Polarity Augmentation Channel that conditions on a given polarity.
C3DA performs cross-channel sentence generation to produce additional training samples.
C3DA includes an Entropy-Minimization Filter to remove low-quality generated samples.
C3DA improves aspect-based sentiment analysis performance compared to baselines without data augmentation.
HINT constructs coding trees from dependency parsing graphs by minimizing structural entropy.
HINT obtains graph representations for text classification by updating non-leaf node representations layer by layer in the coding tree.
HINT leverages hierarchical information in text classification.
HINT outperforms state-of-the-art methods on popular benchmarks.
HINT has a simple structure and uses few parameters.
A fact-checking framework augments verification with cross-lingual retrieval and aggregates evidence retrieved from multiple languages.
The Cross-lingual Inverse Cloze Task (X-ICT) is a self-supervised algorithm that creates training instances by translating passage titles to train a cross-lingual retriever.
The framework achieves higher zero-shot cross-lingual fact-checking performance on the X-Fact dataset than prior systems.
Plugin-tuning introduces a unified classifier that enables parameter-efficient tuning for text classification tasks.
Plugin-tuning reformulates token and sentence classification tasks as a unified language modeling task.
Plugin-tuning maps label spaces of different classification tasks into a shared vocabulary space.
Plugin-tuning reuses the language modeling heads of pre-trained language models, eliminating the need for task-specific output layers.
Plugin-tuning reduces parameter usage compared to other parameter-efficient methods while maintaining comparable performance to fully fine-tuned language models.
RAGthoven enables automatic evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation pipelines.
RAGthoven provides an abstraction that allows starting evaluation with a single configuration file.
RAGthoven’s usefulness is demonstrated through case studies in text classification, question answering, and code generation.
DAMT is a distance-aware multi-task framework that combines transition-based and graph-based paradigms for conversational discourse parsing.
Encoding Interactive Module (EIM) enhances semantic information flow between transition-based and graph-based paradigms during encoding.
Distance-Aware Graph Convolutional Network (DAGCN) incorporates dependency links predicted by the transition-based paradigm to facilitate decoding in the graph-based paradigm.
The proposed framework significantly improves performance of the state-of-the-art graph-based paradigm on long-distance dependency links in conversational discourse parsing.
Experiments on the STAC and Molweni datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework.
A definition of the reasoning steps expected from a "slow reading" system is proposed.
The behavior of five BERT-family reading comprehension models is analyzed with respect to coreference resolution and comparison using saliency scores and counterfactual explanations.
Larger BERT encoders are more likely to rely on the appropriate information for comparison tasks than smaller encoders.
Even larger BERT encoders struggle with generalization on comparison, indicating reliance on specific lexical patterns rather than general comparison principles.
Two probing models—a binary classifier and a sequence labeler—are introduced to assess the discourse-new versus discourse-old status of entities in pre-trained language models.
Pre-trained language models encode information indicating whether an entity has been previously introduced in the discourse.
The encoded information about an entity's discourse status is insufficient for locating the entity within the discourse.
Initializing node features with large language model-generated descriptions enhances node representations for link prediction in graph neural networks.
Large language model-generated node features reduce the need for manual feature creation and lower associated costs.
The proposed method outperforms traditional node embedding techniques on a quantum computing semantic network link prediction task.
The latent codebook of a pretrained wav2vec2.0 model can be transferred as weights for the latent bottleneck of a Keyword Spotting Perceiver, providing phonetic embeddings for initialization.
Cross-attention between transferred phonetic embeddings and input audio in a Perceiver architecture generates improved representations for keyword spotting.
Initializing a Keyword Spotting Perceiver with phonetic embeddings from a wav2vec2.0 codebook yields accuracy gains over random initialization without increasing latency.
Downsampling phonetic embeddings with k-means clustering reduces inference time substantially while causing only slight accuracy loss.
LUCE provides a dynamic framework with an interactive dashboard for analyzing opinionated text.
LUCE includes computational modules for classifying and extracting sentiment or emotion, suggestion, figurative language, hate or toxic speech, and topics from opinionated text.
LUCE is built with a modular architecture that enables scalability and extensibility to support additional NLP tasks.
LUCE comprises trained models, Python-based APIs, and a user-friendly dashboard.
LUCE’s capabilities and performance have been demonstrated through prototypes and pilot studies in a relevant environment.
BioSpere is a multi-stage framework that maps bilingual word embeddings onto a shared vector space using adversarial initialization, refinement, and point set registration.
BioSpere achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised performance on parallel dictionary induction.
BioSpere achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised performance on word similarity tasks across diverse languages.
BioSpere demonstrates robustness to variability in adversarial performance.
PAAM-HiA-T5 is a hierarchy-aware T5 model that incorporates a path-adaptive attention mechanism for hierarchical text classification.
PAAM-HiA-T5 generates a multi-level sequential label structure using breadth-first search and a T5 model to exploit hierarchical dependencies across label levels.
The path-adaptive attention mechanism adaptively focuses on the specific path of the currently generated label, shielding noise from other paths.
PAAM-HiA-T5 outperforms existing state-of-the-art hierarchical text classification approaches, especially in Macro-F1 performance.
ConnPrompt transforms implicit discourse relation prediction into a connective-cloze task.
Insert-cloze Prompt and Prefix-cloze Prompt are template styles used in ConnPrompt for implicit discourse relation recognition.
An answer space mapping relation senses to implicit connectives based on hierarchical sense tags is constructed for ConnPrompt.
A multi-prompt ensemble fuses predictions from different prompting results in ConnPrompt.
ConnPrompt outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms for implicit discourse relation recognition, even with fewer training data.
Evalica is an open-source toolkit that enables the creation of reliable and reproducible model leaderboards.
Evalica provides a web interface, a command-line interface, and a Python API for constructing model leaderboards.
Evalica’s design supports modern evaluation protocols that incorporate both human and machine feedback.

A two-phase framework for data augmentation integrates text-to-text language models, with a fine-tuning phase that employs two novel schemes and a generation phase that creates new samples using the fine-tuned models.
The generation phase leverages fine-tuned models to synthesize new training samples for performance improvement.
The framework can be easily extended to other tasks.
Evaluation on two public classification datasets demonstrates that the framework improves performance.
A benchmark for evaluating attribute injection models is introduced, comprising eight datasets across diverse tasks and domains and six synthetically sparsified datasets.
A lightweight and memory-efficient method injects metadata attributes into pretrained language models by extending adapters with attribute-aware feed-forward modules.
Approximation techniques are employed to efficiently parameterize the model for domains with large attribute vocabularies.
Training mechanisms are introduced to handle multi-labeled and sparse attributes in attribute injection.
The method achieves state-of-the-art performance on all evaluated datasets, outperforming previous attribute injection methods.
Equivalence Constraint theory is applied to generate realistic code-switching text for language modeling in ASR.
Part-of-speech labeling is applied to generate realistic code-switching text for language modeling in ASR.
Generated realistic code-switching text improves ASR performance.

Mutual identifier masking, dedentation, and syntax-aligned target selection are used to prevent leakage between contexts and targets in self-supervised contrastive training for contextualized code retrieval.
Source code is split randomly into contexts and targets to facilitate large-scale self-supervised contrastive training for contextualized code retrieval.
A new dataset for direct evaluation of contextualized code retrieval is introduced, comprising manually aligned subpassages of code clones.
The proposed approach improves retrieval performance for contextualized code retrieval.
The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art results on code clone detection and defect detection.
PolyMinder is an automated support system that extracts and annotates polymer-related entities and their relationships from scientific documents.
PolyMinder utilizes recent advanced named entity recognition and relation extraction models tailored to the polymer domain.
PolyMinder provides a web-based interface that allows users to visualize, verify, and refine extracted information before finalizing annotations.
sDPO partitions preference datasets and applies them incrementally during Direct Preference Optimization training.
sDPO enables integration of progressively more aligned reference models within the DPO training framework.
sDPO enhances alignment precision of reference models.
sDPO improves overall performance of the final model, surpassing larger-parameter language models.
A case study with industry practitioners was conducted to formulate key research questions about leveraging LLMs across organizations of different sizes.
Existing industrial publications were examined to address research questions concerning LLM adaptation challenges varying with company size.
A practical guide for industries to utilize LLMs more efficiently was provided.
A GitHub repository containing recent papers on LLM industrial use was released.
A human-like embodied AI interviewer integrates android robots with advanced conversational capabilities such as attentive listening, conversational repairs, and user fluency adaptation.
The system analyzes and presents interview results after each interview.

A real-world case study at an international conference demonstrated the system’s effectiveness in conducting interviews comparable to a human, with participants reporting predominantly positive experiences.
The system constitutes the first deployment of an embodied AI interviewer at an international conference.
EDAR is a pipeline for emotion and dialogue act recognition designed for the debt collection process in France.
EDAR demonstrates that smaller machine learning and deep learning models with fewer parameters can achieve high performance on emotion and dialogue act recognition.
EDAR provides an efficient alternative to large language models for emotion and dialogue act recognition.
QFCL is a question focus-driven contrastive learning framework for medical question summarization.
QFCL generates hard negative samples based on question focus using an easy and effective approach.
QFCL applies contrastive learning at both encoder and decoder to obtain improved sentence-level representations.
QFCL achieves state-of-the-art performance on three medical benchmark datasets.
Human evaluation shows that QFCL learns sentence representations that distinguish different meanings and generates high-quality summaries by capturing question focus.
GECTurk WEB is a web-based system that detects and corrects common Turkish writing errors such as misuse of diacritics, compound and foreign words, pronouns, light verbs, and spelling mistakes.
GECTurk WEB provides explanations of the violated grammatical rules for each detected error.
GECTurk WEB offers a feedback mechanism that enables users to learn from their mistakes.
GECTurk WEB achieves high system usability.
GECTurk WEB helps users learn and remember grammatical rules, as confirmed by participant feedback.
STAND-Guard is a small task-adaptive content moderation model.
STAND-Guard uses instruction tuning on multiple content moderation tasks to enable adaptation to unseen content moderation tasks.
STAND-Guard's performance is comparable to GPT-3.5-Turbo across many public and proprietary content moderation datasets.
STAND-Guard achieves nearly equivalent performance to GPT-4-Turbo on unseen English binary classification tasks.
The impact of training task selection and model size on the effectiveness of cross-task fine-tuning for content moderation is studied.
Community Topic mines communities from term co-occurrence networks for topic modelling.
Community Topic operates without hyperparameters.
Community Topic runs faster than Latent Dirichlet Allocation and top2vec.
Community Topic generates topics with higher coherence than Latent Dirichlet Allocation and top2vec.
Community Topic's network representation yields a topic hierarchy that allows on-demand discovery of sub- and super-topics.
RecStream optimizes stream configurations for concurrent recommendation model online serving based on model characteristics.
RecStream employs a hybrid Graph Neural Network architecture to select configurations for recommendation models.
RecStream reduces serving latency in high-concurrency scenarios.
A three-stage multi-task learning framework for large language models is introduced, comprising task filtering, fine-tuning on high-resource tasks, and fine-tuning on all tasks.
Comprehensive experiments evaluate the framework in both single-task and multi-task settings across multiple benchmarks.
The framework achieves performance comparable to single-task methods while substantially reducing computational overhead.
A method predicts correctness of retrieval-augmented generation answers by modeling model uncertainty on quantified input perturbations.
The method quantifies RAG robustness by aligning its predictions with ground-truth correctness.
The method provides flexibility to accommodate diverse qualitative metrics.
An integrated retrieval-summarization framework captures global document relationships through graph-based clustering and uses the clusters to re-rank retrieved documents.
Cluster-level thematic information from graph-based clustering guides large language models to refine retrieved documents and generate more accurate, coherent summaries.
The framework improves retrieval accuracy in open-domain multi-document summarization.
The framework produces summaries that surpass those generated from oracle documents.
A method estimates the performance of pretrained models fine-tuned on larger datasets using results from fine-tuning on smaller datasets.
Classification performance of a fine-tuned pretrained model increases at a consistent overall rate across different original dataset sizes as the number of epochs increases.
An approximate formula based on the consistent performance increase trend can predict performance when training with ten times or more data, even from a limited initial dataset.
The performance prediction approach enables resource-limited companies to develop machine-learning models.
A text rewrite approach transforms transcribed speech into a cleaner style aligned with written-text translation models.
The rewrite model can be distilled into a standalone translation model.
Adding a rewrite model to a traditional speech translation pipeline provides a cost-effective means to address speech irregularities.
Adding a rewrite model to a traditional speech translation pipeline improves speech translation quality across multiple language directions and domains.
AMAN is a companion chatbot that provides real-time guidance to novice gamers in MMORPGs.
A multi-stage learning approach that combines continual pre-training on online resources with instruction tuning on curated dialogues is used to train the chatbot.
