A domain-specific dataset of user-oriented topics from online communities of a widely played MMORPG is constructed.
Multi-turn dialogue data is created to enable dynamic conversations with users.
Evaluation shows that the conversational assistant can help novice gamers in online games.
CASE provides a tool for exploiting topic information for semantic analysis of large corpora.
CASE uses a Solr engine with a customized indexing strategy to represent information from Bayesian and neural topic models for efficient topic-enriched searches.
CASE enables topic inference on new texts via ad-hoc plug-ins.
CASE supports semantic search over topic-enriched representations via ad-hoc plug-ins.
CASE demonstrates scalability and versatility in calculating aggregated Science, Technology, and Innovation (STI) indicators.
CASE implements a web service to evaluate research projects.
SeBe-VQA uses a multi-modal encoder to extract joint embeddings from all modalities and aligns queries with retrieved knowledge via contrastive learning.
SeBe-VQA adds an MLLM re-selection step that chooses the best matching knowledge from the top‑k retrieved results of the alignment model.
Evaluation on the Encyclopedic VQA dataset shows that the multi‑modal retrieval framework improves VQA performance compared with prior methods and without knowledge augmentation.
CompUGE is a benchmark for evaluating comparative question answering systems.
CompUGE defines four core tasks: comparative question identification, object and aspect identification, stance classification, and answer generation.
CompUGE unifies multiple existing datasets into a single benchmark.
CompUGE provides an evaluation platform for comparing models across comparative QA sub-tasks.
CompUGE includes additional datasets created by filtering and merging existing datasets.
CompUGE is offered as a web application on HuggingFace Spaces.
Continued pre-training of large language models with queries as input and clicked titles and summaries as output enhances their ranking capabilities.
Supervised fine-tuning of large language models using a rank loss that treats the final token as a representation of the entire sentence is introduced.
A hybrid point-wise and margin mean squared error loss is introduced to transfer ranking knowledge from large language models to smaller BERT-like models.
The distillation method enables deployment of compact BERT-like models for web search ranking in resource-constrained environments.
Offline and online evaluations confirm the efficacy of the distillation approach, and the resulting model has been integrated into a commercial web search engine.
OKG is an LLM agent-based method that dynamically monitors KPI changes and adapts keyword generation in real time for sponsored search advertising.
A publicly accessible dataset of real keyword data with associated KPI metrics across diverse domains is released.
OKG achieves significant improvements across multiple advertising performance metrics.
Ablation studies demonstrate that each component of OKG contributes to its overall effectiveness.
LAW orchestrates domain-specific tools and text agents to answer user queries about custody and fund services contracts.
LAW outperforms baseline models on tasks involving custody and fund services contracts.
LAW achieves superior performance on calculating contract termination dates compared to baseline models.
LAW provides a cost-effective alternative to fine-tuned legal language models by leveraging reusable domain-specific tools.
ViSoLex provides a Non-Standard Word lookup service that retrieves standard forms of informal Vietnamese social media language.
ViSoLex provides a lexical normalization service that standardizes Vietnamese social media text containing non-standard words.
ViSoLex integrates pre-trained language models with weakly supervised learning techniques to achieve accurate and efficient lexical normalization.
ViSoLex offers a flexible, customizable framework that can be adapted to various Vietnamese datasets and research requirements.
ViSoLex is released as an open-source repository for Vietnamese social media lexical normalization.
Query-LIFE uses query-based multimodal fusion to incorporate product images and titles according to product types.
Query-LIFE employs query-aware modal alignment to enhance the accuracy of comprehensive product representations.
GenFilt utilizes the generation capability of large models to filter false negative samples, improving contrastive learning performance.
Query-LIFE outperforms existing baselines on e-commerce relevance tasks.
Ablation studies and human evaluations validate the effectiveness of each module within Query-LIFE.
Deployment of Query-LIFE on Miravia Search improves relevance and conversion efficiency.
RAG datasets are characterized through a label-based taxonomy.
Label-targeted data generation strategies are proposed for evaluating RAG systems.
Fine-tuned small language models can efficiently generate question-and-answer datasets for RAG evaluation.
Assessing retrieval performance with public question-and-answer datasets can lead to suboptimal RAG system design.
Common tools for RAG dataset generation can produce unbalanced data.
Sibyl is a framework that acquires commonsense knowledge focused on the immediately succeeding dialogue turn.
Sibyl equips large language models with the ability to uncover implicit conversational requirements to generate more empathetic responses.
Incorporating Sibyl into large language models improves the overall quality of their dialogue responses.
Clustering-based retrieval selects semantically similar in-context examples for eBPF code summarization prompts.
The clustering-based retrieval approach improves eBPF code summarization accuracy.
A simple prompt design yields superior-quality eBPF code summary generation.
BackMATH-Data provides thousands of backward reasoning problems and hundreds of thousands of reasoning steps for mathematical problem solving.
Backward-reasoning Process-supervision Reward Model (BackPRM) evaluates the quality of generated backward reasoning problems.
BackMATH-LLM is fine‑tuned with reinforcement learning that supervises backward reasoning problem quality and provides feedback on reasoning steps.
BackMATH-LLM improves mathematical reasoning capabilities of large language models.
BackMATH-LLM surpasses state‑of‑the‑art performance on the GSM8K and MATH benchmark datasets.
RED-CT is a systems design methodology for using LLM-labeled data to train and deploy edge linguistic classifiers.
The methodology introduces system intervention measures that improve classification performance when LLMs are used as imperfect annotators.
The methodology achieves higher classification performance than using raw LLM-generated labels and than base classifiers across multiple supervised learning tests.
A logistic regression model using 28 linguistically motivated features classifies paragraph-level text into fiction and non-fiction genre.
The linguistically motivated feature set comprises lexical, part-of-speech ratio, syntactic, and raw features.
The selected feature set improves classification accuracy over a baseline model that uses only part-of-speech ratio features.
The feature-based logistic regression model generalizes to the Baby BNC corpus.
A 1D convolutional neural network model achieves higher classification accuracy than the logistic regression classifier on both corpora.
A BERT-base-uncased model attains the highest classification accuracy on the Brown and Baby BNC corpora.
Regression model coefficients reveal that fiction texts exhibit greater character-level diversity and lower lexical density than non-fiction texts.
Analysis indicates that in fiction texts verbs tend to precede adverbs, reflecting subtle word order differences.
AutoProteinEngine is an agent framework that leverages large language models for multimodal automated machine learning in protein engineering.
AutoProteinEngine provides a conversational interface that enables biologists without deep learning expertise to interact with deep learning models using natural language.
AutoProteinEngine integrates large language models with AutoML to process protein sequence and graph modalities, automate hyperparameter optimization, and retrieve data from protein databases.
Evaluation on two real-world protein engineering tasks shows that AutoProteinEngine achieves higher model performance than traditional zero-shot and manual fine-tuning approaches.
DaCoM is a methodology for collecting low-resource language pairs from industrial domains.
DaCoM is a hybrid translation framework that combines a large language model with neural machine translation for effective data collection.
Existing translation models perform inadequately on datasets created by DaCoM, exhibiting large BLEURT score differences that vary with domain inclusion.
DaCoM supplies domain-specific low-resource language translation datasets that are easily pluggable into future state-of-the-art models while maintaining an industrial domain-agnostic approach.
REVerSum is a multi-staged retrieval-augmented generation method that enhances Wikipedia B and C category biography articles using personal narratives such as autobiographies and biographies.
Personal narratives can significantly improve the quality of Wikipedia articles.
REVerSum outperforms the best performing baseline in integrability to the original Wikipedia article and in informativeness.
The framework extracts relevant information from external English resources and adapts it to Wikipedia’s neutral point of view using in-context learning of large language models.
The adapted English content is machine-translated into Hindi for integration into corresponding Hindi Wikipedia articles.
When an English Wikipedia page is comprehensive and up-to-date, the framework directly transfers its content to the Hindi version.
The framework generates new content for Hindi Wikipedia sections, enhancing article quality as confirmed by automatic and human evaluations.
A long-term memory architecture for voice assistants structures user preferences into predefined categories and uses large language models to extract, store, and retrieve preferences.
The architecture provides personalization and transparency in preference handling.
CarMem is a synthetic multi-turn, multi-session conversation dataset for in-car voice assistants, grounded in real industry data.
The architecture achieves high accuracy in preference extraction across category granularities.
A maintenance strategy reduces redundant and contradictory preferences and enables accurate retrieval of optimal preferences.
A method generates retrieval queries using large language models, embeds the queries, and retrieves tools via nearest-neighbor search.
Zero-shot prompting, supervised fine-tuning on tool descriptions, and alignment learning are three approaches for generating retrieval queries with large language models.
Alignment learning iteratively optimizes a reward metric that measures retrieval performance for query generation.
Leveraging large language models for query generation improves tool retrieval for both seen and unseen tools.
Vocabulary reduction compresses the embedding layer and reduces model size of small language models with minimal performance loss.
Fine-tuning recovers most performance lost due to vocabulary reduction in small language models.
Vocabulary-reduced small language models outperform baseline models on truthfulness and summarization tasks.
Vocabulary reduction enables effective domain adaptation in the medical domain for small language models.
Vocabulary reduction improves cross-lingual robustness and task efficiency in multilingual adaptation for small language models.
Vocabulary reduction reduces GPU memory footprint and increases inference speed of small language models.
Semantic sentence matching is formalized as a graph matching problem where each sentence is represented as a directed syntactic graph.
Interacted Syntax Graphs (ISG) represents syntactic alignments and semantic matching signals of two sentences within a single association graph.
Neural quadratic assignment programming is adapted to extract syntactic matching patterns from the association graph in ISG.
ISG outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on three public semantic sentence matching datasets.
ISG provides interpretable sentence matching by aligning sub-graph structures.
A data generation workflow uses large language models to extract query rewrites from product text.
A Seq2Seq model is trained to generate query rewrites for unseen queries.
Query rewriting improves precision@30 for product search queries containing negations.
LENS is an automatic entity recognition system for skin cancer narratives from Reddit forums.
LENS defines a comprehensive set of 24 entity labels covering clinical, demographic, and psychosocial aspects of skin cancer.
LENS is provided as a PyPI package and a web application that enables interactive entity prediction for users with minimal programming experience.
Annotation guidelines for spontaneous skin cancer narratives are released to facilitate development of corpora and systems for similar diseases.
LENS demonstrates high entity-level performance on key entity types such as cancer type, stage, place of birth, gender, anatomy/gender, and people.
LLM ContextBridge integrates pretrained language model-based intent classification with large language models for in-vehicle speech recognition.
LLM ContextBridge combines precise intent recognition of conventional natural language understanding with contextual handling and ambiguity resolution of large language models.
LLM ContextBridge enhances command recognition and dialogue management in in-vehicle speech recognition systems.
LLM ContextBridge improves recognition accuracy and user experience in complex multi-turn dialogues.
Experimental evaluation shows that LLM ContextBridge increases task success rates and user satisfaction in in-vehicle speech recognition systems.
A system integrates state-of-the-art domain-specific large language models with advanced information retrieval techniques to deliver comprehensive, context-aware responses.
The system enables cross-validation of outputs to produce accurate, high-quality responses enriched with relevant data, images, tables, and other modalities.
Leveraging a robust question-answering model, the system enhances response precision and improves dialogue generation quality.

The system provides a real-time, high-fidelity interaction platform that supports efficient human-computer interaction, precise retrieval, and simultaneous access to a wide range of literature and data.
The system dramatically improves research efficiency for biomedical and pharmaceutical professionals and accelerates decision-making throughout the R&D process.
A unified encoder-decoder architecture enables the encoder representation to be used for retrieval and the decoder for re-ranking within a single model.
The architecture incorporates XTR-style retrieval on top of a trained MonoT5 reranker to create a lightweight model that addresses practical constraints.
The unified architecture achieves superior performance on the BIER benchmark with a highly optimized index and parameters.
The unified model outperforms ColBERT, XTR, and the MonoT5 reranker as a re-ranker.
Performance gains of the unified system in re-ranking increase with model capacity, especially compared to rerankers built on traditional first-stage retrievers such as BM25.
Global planning on a commonsense knowledge graph is employed for target-oriented dialog.
Global reinforcement learning with planned paths adjusts a local response generation model toward a global target.
A knowledge-graph-based method automatically collects target-oriented samples from chit-chat corpora for model training.
The proposed method achieves a higher success rate in reaching dialog targets.
The proposed method reduces the number of dialog turns and generates more coherent responses.
SCV performs multi-vector retrieval using sequence compressive vectors.
SCV applies span compressive vectors with a non-linear operation to generate span-level representations for scoring.
SCV employs a coarse-to-fine vector search that uses a coarse single-vector search at inference followed by fine-grained multi-vector search end-to-end.
SCV reduces the computational cost of search compared to approaches that rely on Approximate Nearest Neighbor retrieval of document subsets.
SCV achieves the fastest latency among state-of-the-art retrieval models while maintaining competitive performance on both in-domain and out-of-domain benchmark datasets.
PARROT-360V comprises 2,487 visual puzzles designed to test complex visual reasoning in Vision Language Models.
PARROT-360V evaluates Vision Language Models on their ability to integrate visual clues with language to solve multi-step problems akin to human problem solving.

Evaluation of leading Vision Language Models on PARROT-360V reveals a substantial performance gap compared to their scores on popular benchmarks, indicating limited capability for complex multi-step reasoning.
An end-to-end architecture based on the M2M100 multilingual transformer provides a unified grammatical error correction system for low-resource languages.
A synthetic data generation pipeline tailored to language-specific error categories is introduced for grammatical error correction.
The system is implemented for Spanish and receives promising evaluations from expert linguists.
A user analysis tracking interactions indicates a high acceptance rate of the grammatical error correction system.
SubRegWeigh uses subword regularization to simulate multiple error detection models for annotation weighting.
SubRegWeigh performs annotation weighting faster than existing methods.
SubRegWeigh improves performance on document classification and named entity recognition tasks.
SubRegWeigh identifies pseudo-incorrect labels as annotation errors.
Luna is a DeBERTA-large encoder fine-tuned for hallucination detection in Retriever-Augmented Generation (RAG) settings.
Luna detects hallucinations in RAG systems with higher accuracy than GPT-3.5 and commercial evaluation frameworks.
Luna reduces evaluation cost and latency compared to existing hallucination detection techniques.
Luna generalizes across multiple industry verticals and out-of-domain data for hallucination detection.
A method generates a synthetic general instruction-following dataset with safety data for real estate chatbot development.
Fine-tuning llama-3-8B-instruct produces a real estate chatbot that matches the performance of large closed-source models while improving safety and compliance.
Extensive evaluations and benchmarks assess the chatbot’s performance and compliance.
The model, dataset, and code are released as open-source resources for community use.
PO-HRL is an end-to-end partial order-centered hyperbolic representation learning framework for few-shot relation extraction.
PO-HRL imposes constraints of relations on instances by modeling partial order in hyperbolic space.
PO-HRL employs hyperbolic supervised contrastive learning based on Lorentzian cosine similarity to align relation and instance representations.
PO-HRL constrains instances to reside within the Lorentzian entailment cone of their respective relation to model partial order.
PO-HRL outperforms strong baselines in few-shot relation extraction, especially in 1-shot settings lacking relation descriptions.
QABISAR provides a framework for statutory article retrieval that models query-article pairs jointly rather than in isolation.
QABISAR captures diverse aspects of queries and articles through bipartite interactions.

QABISAR employs knowledge distillation to transfer enriched query representations from a graph network into a query bi-encoder, enabling rich semantic capture without graph supervision for unseen queries.
Experiments on a real-world expert-annotated dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of QABISAR for statutory article retrieval.
ProRBP integrates world knowledge stored in LLM parameters with domain knowledge from user behavior data for relevance modeling.
ProRBP includes a user-driven behavior neighbor retrieval module that learns domain-specific knowledge over time.
ProRBP includes a progressive prompting and aggregation module that considers diverse aspects of relevance and prediction stability.
An industrial implementation of ProRBP deploys LLMs to handle full-scale search traffic of Alipay with acceptable cost and latency.
Experiments on real-world industry data and online A/B testing demonstrate the superiority of ProRBP and the effectiveness of its main modules.
ZEUS selects effective demonstrations for zero-shot chain-of-thought prompting using uncertainty estimates without requiring model parameters.
ZEUS exhibits high sensitivity in distinguishing helpful from ineffective questions for chain-of-thought prompting.
ZEUS consistently outperforms existing chain-of-thought prompting strategies across multiple challenging reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating robustness and scalability.
Progressive Dataset Construction (PDC) builds a SQL bug-fixing dataset from scratch using breadth-first and depth-first data expansion methods.
Dynamic Mask Supervised Fine-tuning (DM-SFT) provides an efficient supervised learning approach for SQL bug-fixing that reduces total training steps.
Dynamic Mask Supervised Fine-tuning (DM-SFT) mitigates disorientation during SQL code bug-fixing training.
Combining Progressive Dataset Construction and Dynamic Mask Supervised Fine-tuning enables code large language models to surpass the performance of larger existing models on SQL bug-fixing tasks.
UCTG provides a unified controllable text generation framework that integrates a control module, a prompt module, and a generation module for query auto-completion.
The control module of UCTG uses a fine-tuned model to distill user preference features and behavioral patterns from historical data and incorporates human feedback into loss functions.
The prompt module of UCTG transforms distilled user preference features into vectors that guide the generation module.
UCTG enables flexible control of text generation without modifying task settings.
UCTG improves query accuracy and coherence across tasks with different objectives and constraints, as demonstrated on Meituan and AOL real-world datasets.
MupPCQA generates user-centric responses for programming-community question answering.
MupPCQA incorporates Preference Standardization to control content quality.
MupPCQA incorporates Preference Integration to consider diverse user tendencies.
MupPCQA incorporates Preference Timeliness Mitigation to alleviate outdated answers.
MupPCQA achieves higher BLEU, BERTScore, and CodeBERTScore than its base model on a real-world PCQA dataset.
A novel multimodal framework addresses the modality gap in fashion-related social media sentiment analysis by generating pseudo data through a two-stage process.
The framework employs a multimodal fusion approach that efficiently integrates textual and visual information for sentiment classification of fashion posts.
Experiments demonstrate that the framework outperforms existing unimodal and multimodal baselines in sentiment classification of fashion-related social media posts.
Sparse Fine-Tuning Masks and adapters enable more lightweight and effective zero-shot transfer for cross-lingual neural reranking.
Language adapters are trained via masked language modeling and combined with retrieval adapters trained on relevance data while keeping all other parameters fixed.
The modular design composes a reranker by applying a source-language-trained reranking adapter together with a target-language language adapter.
The CLEF-2003 benchmark is extended with queries in Kyrgyz, Uyghur, and Turkish.
The parameter-efficient methods outperform full multilingual Transformer fine-tuning and machine translation-based rankers, especially for low-resource languages, while reducing training time.
A manually constructed Korean-English discourse-level corpus of 600 instances covering lexical ambiguity, zero anaphora, slang, idiom, figurative language, and implicature is introduced.
State-of-the-art large language model GPT-4o struggles with specific linguistic phenomena in Korean-to-English discourse-level translation.
Step-by-step prompting, such as Chain-of-Thought prompting, significantly improves translation performance of large language models compared to zero-shot prompting.
LionGuard is a Singapore-contextualized moderation classifier that guards against unsafe LLM usage.
LionGuard outperforms existing widely-used moderation APIs on Singlish data.
LionGuard demonstrates a practical and scalable approach for moderation in low-resource English-based creole languages.
Localization improves moderation classifier performance for Singapore-context content.
Multimodal user profiling generates comprehensive user profiles by integrating review texts and accompanying images.
A unified joint training framework with multimodal training strategies incorporates users' historical review texts and images for user profile generation.
Incorporating multimodal data enhances the quality of generated user profiles compared to using textual data alone.
The unified joint training approach improves user profile generation performance.
Fine-tuned Sentence-BERT serves as a lightweight safety guardrail for large language models.
Fine-tuned Sentence-BERT reduces model size from billions of parameters to approximately 67 million parameters while preserving performance.
Fine-tuned Sentence-BERT achieves comparable performance to LlamaGuard on the AEGIS safety benchmark.
A small backward language model can be trained and its token representations concatenated with those of an existing language model for token-classification tasks.
Concatenating representations from a backward language model with those of an existing language model improves benchmark performance on token-classification tasks.
The concatenation method is especially effective for token-classification in rare domains.
The concatenation method is especially effective in few-shot learning settings for token-classification.
CorXFact simulates human fact-checkers' reasoning by assessing and weighing correlations between a claim and each piece of evidence.
CorXFact enables efficient claim verification and transparent explanation generation.
CorFEVER test set evaluates claim-evidence correlation identification and claim verification in both closed-domain and real-world fact-checking scenarios.
CorXFact outperforms four strong fact-checking baselines in claim authenticity prediction and verdict explanation.

Adaptive mixup image augmentation generates augmented images for multimodal named entity recognition by determining the mixing ratio from a text-image matching score using a triplet loss-based Gaussian Mixture Model.
Adaptive mixup image augmentation can be seamlessly integrated into existing multimodal named entity recognition models.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that adaptive mixup image augmentation consistently improves the performance of multimodal named entity recognition models.
Ablation studies and case studies confirm the effectiveness of adaptive mixup image augmentation for multimodal named entity recognition.
The method sequentially adds adapters for each new language and incorporates a fusion layer above language-specific adapters.
The fusion layer distills attention scores from the previously trained fusion layer, focusing on older adapters.
Translating a portion of new language data into older languages supports backward knowledge transfer.
Attention distillation reduces the number of trainable parameters, enhancing computational efficiency and enabling frequent updates.
Attention distillation improves ROC-AUC compared to single marketplace baselines and outperforms state-of-the-art continual learning methods on proprietary and external datasets.
Automatic data annotation with slot induction is introduced for zero-shot slot filling in conversational dialogue.
Black-box knowledge distillation from a teacher LLM to a smaller model is introduced for zero-shot slot filling in conversational dialogue.
The combination of automatic data annotation with slot induction and black-box knowledge distillation yields higher zero-shot slot filling performance than vanilla LLMs.

An efficient system architecture for call center product settings achieves higher accuracy than off-the-shelf extractive models while maintaining low latency and supporting near real-time inference on dialogue streams.
FS-DAG is a scalable and efficient model architecture for visually rich document understanding in few-shot settings.
FS-DAG leverages domain-specific and language/vision-specific backbones within a modular framework to adapt to diverse document types with minimal data.
FS-DAG is robust to OCR errors, misspellings, and domain shifts.
FS-DAG contains fewer than 90 million parameters, making it suitable for information extraction tasks with limited computational resources.
Experiments show that FS-DAG converges faster and achieves higher performance on information extraction tasks compared to state-of-the-art methods.
PCKE compresses prompts for text classification annotation by extracting keywords, reducing prompt token count with minimal accuracy loss.
A BERT-based multi-task model can simultaneously perform classification and keyword extraction using labels and keywords generated by an LLM from a small unannotated dataset.
Extracted keywords can replace full texts for LLM annotation, substantially reducing annotation cost.
The compression method achieves a higher compression rate than existing general-purpose compression techniques while maintaining high classification accuracy.
The approach provides a cost-efficient solution for large-scale text classification annotation with LLMs in industrial settings.
DDPE integrates external knowledge from large language models for multi-party dialogue discourse parsing.
DDPE includes explanation generation, structural parsing, and contrastive learning components.
DDPE uses large language models to generate explanatory and contrastive information about discourse structure, providing additional reasoning cues.
DDPE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on the STAC and Molweni datasets.
Concatenation of LoRAs (CAT) merges LoRA modules by optimally weighting them to combine multiple skills.
CAT outperforms existing model-merging techniques on compositional tasks.
CAT outperforms existing data-merging techniques on compositional tasks.
Model merging is demonstrated as an efficient solution for compositional tasks when target-task training data is scarce.

Practical use-cases for skill composition are identified, including solving hard math-word problems with code, building bots for proprietary manuals, and answering questions about domain-specialized corpora.
RETURN is a dataset of 2,492 Wikipedia individuals with associated question‑answer pairs for evaluating machine unlearning methods in realistic personal data protection scenarios.
Name‑Aware Unlearning Framework (NAUF) enables selective protection of specified individuals' personal information in large language models without degrading performance on unrelated queries.
NAUF achieves state‑of‑the‑art average unlearning performance while preserving the model’s general capabilities.
MICL integrates multimodal incongruities via contrastive learning for multimodal sarcasm detection.
MICL drives multi-view learning from token-patch, entity-object, and sentiment views using incongruity.
MICL employs extensive data augmentation to reduce biased learning of the textual modality.
SPMSD test set comprises potential spurious correlations to assess model generalizability in multimodal sarcasm detection.
MICL achieves superior performance on benchmark multimodal sarcasm detection datasets and better mitigates spurious correlation effects.
Two primary causes of spurious correlation reliance in multimodal sarcasm detection are identified.
MLD-EA identifies narrative gaps in stories using large language models.
MLD-EA generates coherent sentences that align with a story’s emotional and logical flow.
MLD-EA enhances narrative understanding.
MLD-EA improves story generation by increasing logical coherence and emotional consistency.
Large language models can serve as effective logic checkers in story writing.
Aurora-M is a 15B parameter multilingual open-source model trained on English, Finnish, Hindi, Japanese, Vietnamese, and code.
Aurora-M is continually pretrained from StarCoderPlus on 435 billion additional tokens, exceeding a total of two trillion training tokens.
Aurora-M is the first open-source multilingual model fine-tuned on human-reviewed safety instructions.
Aurora-M exhibits robustness against catastrophic forgetting during continual pretraining.
Aurora-M achieves superior performance in multilingual tasks, particularly in safety evaluations.
An evaluation method tailored to the German extractive question answering task is defined.
Perturbations originally used for English QA models are assessed for applicability to German QA.
A comprehensive experimental evaluation is conducted on eight German QA models using the defined evaluation method.
All evaluated models are vulnerable to character-level perturbations.
Monolingual German QA models are less affected by character and word-level perturbations than multilingual models.
Factualusion is a novel corpus with hallucination features for document-level event factuality identification and hallucination evaluation of large language models.
Trucidator is a graph-based framework that constructs intra-document and cross-document graphs for event factuality identification.
Trucidator employs a multi-task learning paradigm to acquire robust node embeddings.
Trucidator leverages cross-document inference to improve event factuality identification accuracy.
Experiments demonstrate that Trucidator outperforms several baselines on document-level event factuality identification.
TypeDA uses large language models as annotators for type-aware data augmentation in grammatical error correction.
TypeDA prevents in-distribution corruption during data augmentation for grammatical error correction.
TypeDA can generate sentences with multi-granularity error types for grammatical error correction.
TypeDA improves grammatical error correction performance of various backbone models when using a small amount of augmented data.
Pseudo data generated by TypeDA exhibits high consistency and diversity.

A simple instruction augmentation method improves zero-shot sentiment classification performance across multiple large language models on numerous benchmark datasets without using labeled sentiment instances.
The instruction augmentation method outperforms more complex instruction tuning methods for zero-shot sentiment classification.
The instruction augmentation method outperforms instruction tuning that uses actual domain-specific instruction instances.
Analysis shows that the method induces a probability shift toward positive and negative classes, which drives its performance improvement.
The method achieves performance gains using only 240 pseudo instruction instances.
MLLM-I2W is an image-text mapping network that enables zero-shot composed image retrieval by converting description-related image information into pseudo-word markers.
The image and text encoding enhancement module within MLLM-I2W selects subject headings and generates text descriptions.
Uncertainty modeling is used to reduce the modality gap between images and text in MLLM-I2W.
An adaptive weighting module and a prototype in MLLM-I2W adjust and learn deep fusion features before mapping them to pseudo-word markers via a mixture-of-experts based mapping network.
MLLM-I2W achieves consistent improvements on composed image retrieval benchmarks such as COCO, CIRR, and Fashion-IQ.
A prompting approach first asks large language models to predict motivational interviewing strategies as reasoning and then uses the predicted strategies to guide psychotherapy dialogue generation.
Automatic and human evaluations are conducted to assess the controllability and explainability of psychotherapy dialogues generated with motivational interviewing strategy guidance.
Guiding generation with motivational interviewing strategies produces psychotherapy dialogues that are more controllably aligned with therapeutic objectives and provide explainable reasoning.
TEEMIL-H and TEEMIL-K are datasets of multiple-choice questions in Hindi and Kannada with manually annotated difficulty labels.
State-of-the-art multilingual models are benchmarked on TEEMIL-H and TEEMIL-K for difficulty estimation.
Ablation studies analyze the effect of context on difficulty estimation.
Ablation studies analyze the impact of answer options on difficulty estimation.
Ablation studies analyze the influence of a "None of the Above" option on difficulty estimation.
Baseline performance for difficulty estimation in Hindi and Kannada is established.
MIDLM is a bidirectional LLM framework for multi-intent detection that incorporates intent number detection and multi-intent selection.
MIDLM enables autoregressive LLMs to leverage bidirectional information through post-training, eliminating the need to train the models from scratch.
MIDLM consistently outperforms existing vanilla models and pretrained baselines on multi-intent detection across multiple datasets.
Commonsense Subgraph Meta-Learning (CSML) performs inductive relation reasoning using commonsense subgraphs.
CSML extracts concepts from entities to provide high-level semantic information.
CSML constructs subgraphs using concepts instead of nodes.
CSML combines concept-based subgraphs with structural subgraphs to leverage both structural and semantic information for predictions.
CSML treats concepts as meta-information and applies meta-learning to enable rapid knowledge transfer in few-shot inductive reasoning.
CSML achieves superior performance in standard inductive reasoning tasks.
CSML achieves superior performance in few-shot inductive reasoning scenarios.
ToolEyes provides a fine-grained evaluation system for large language models' tool learning capabilities in authentic scenarios.
ToolEyes examines seven real-world scenarios and analyzes five dimensions: format alignment, intent comprehension, behavior planning, tool selection, and answer organization.
ToolEyes incorporates a tool library of approximately 600 tools that act as an intermediary between large language models and the physical world.
Evaluation of ten large language models across three categories with ToolEyes reveals a preference for specific scenarios and limited cognitive abilities in tool learning.
Increasing model size further hinders tool learning abilities according to evaluations conducted with ToolEyes.
MPPO leverages the average likelihood of model responses to fit the reward function and maximizes utilization of preference data.
MPPO optimizes preferences using multiple replies, addressing data waste in single-question two-reply scenarios.
The pairwise implementation of MPPO achieves the best performance among pointwise, pairwise, and listwise approaches.
MPPO outperforms DPO, ORPO, and SimPO on the MT-Bench benchmark.
MPPO surpasses DPO and ORPO on the Arena-Hard benchmark by substantial margins.
A method detects anomalies in texts and arbitrary data sequences using language models without supervision.
Oddballness is a metric that measures how strange a token is according to a language model.
Oddballness outperforms low-likelihood token detection for grammatical error detection in an unsupervised setting.

RoLargeSum is a large-scale Romanian news dataset for summary, headline, and keyword generation that includes over 600,000 articles with associated summaries, headlines, keywords, dialect information, and metadata.
RoLargeSum was constructed by crawling publicly available news websites from Romania and the Republic of Moldova and applying thorough cleaning to ensure high-quality data.
Several BART variants and open-source large language models were benchmarked on RoLargeSum.
Manual evaluation of the best-performing system on RoLargeSum revealed potential pitfalls of the dataset and informed directions for future development.
An iterative differential generation framework with contrastive feedback generates high-quality pseudo samples for fine-grained intent detection.
Differential guidelines that incorporate potential ambiguous labels reduce confusion among similar intent classes.
Rubric-driven refinement ensures validity and diversity of generated pseudo samples.
Iterative generation with contrastive feedback enables accurate identification and distillation of target knowledge for fine-grained intent detection.
Experiments across zero-shot, few-shot, and full-shot settings on three datasets demonstrate the method’s effectiveness.
A multi-layer annotation system linking meaning to text enables comprehensive description of the relations between morphological properties, syntactic function, and expressed meaning for particles.
The multi-layer system contributes to greater accuracy in describing polyfunctional uninflected words (particles).
The multi-layer system enhances overall consistency of annotated data for particles.
These benefits are demonstrated within the Prague Dependency Treebank annotation scheme.
MambaForGCN enhances long-range dependencies between aspect and opinion words in aspect-based sentiment analysis.
MambaForGCN incorporates a syntax-based Graph Convolutional Network (SynGCN) to encode input with dependency relations.
MambaForGCN incorporates a MambaFormer (Mamba-Transformer) module that uses Multihead Attention and Selective State Space model blocks to capture short and long-range dependencies.
MambaForGCN uses Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks gated fusion to adaptively integrate SynGCN and MambaFormer representations and capture non-linear complex dependencies.
MambaForGCN outperforms state-of-the-art baseline models on three benchmark aspect-based sentiment analysis datasets.
A multi-LLM correction approach performs meeting summarization through a two-phase process of mistake identification and summary refinement.
QMSum Mistake is a dataset of 200 automatically generated meeting summaries annotated by humans with nine error types, including structural, omission, and irrelevance errors.
An LLM can identify the nine annotated error types in meeting summaries with high accuracy.
Identified mistakes are transformed into actionable feedback that improves summary relevance, informativeness, conciseness, and coherence.
Post-hoc refinement using multiple LLMs improves the overall quality of meeting summaries.
In-context learning (ICL)-based debiasing methods exhibit a higher correlation between intrinsic and extrinsic bias scores than fine-tuning (FT)-based debiasing methods.
ICL-based debiasing methods cause less performance degradation on downstream tasks than FT-based debiasing methods.
GL-GAN captures and integrates global and local handwriting styles.
Hybrid Style Encoder combines a state space model and convolution to capture multilevel style features through various receptive fields.
Dynamic Feature Enhancement Module integrates multilevel style features by adaptively modeling their relationships and removing redundant details.
GL-GAN outperforms state-of-the-art handwritten text generation models on two widely used handwriting datasets.
Adaptive Language Agent Mechanism Activation Learning with Self-Exploration (ALAMA) optimizes mechanism activation adaptability without reliance on expert models.
UniAct unifies different mechanisms via Actions within a harmonized agent framework.
A training-efficient self-exploration optimization method enables UniAct to adaptively activate appropriate mechanisms based on task characteristics.
The approach yields significant improvements in downstream agent tasks, demonstrating more dynamic and context-sensitive mechanism activation.
Persona-DB introduces a hierarchical construction process that improves generalization across task contexts.
Persona-DB employs collaborative refinement to bridge knowledge gaps among users.
Persona-DB maintains response prediction accuracy while using a significantly reduced retrieval size.
Persona-DB improves response prediction performance in cold-start scenarios with extremely sparse user data.
Analysis indicates that collaborative knowledge becomes increasingly important as retrieval capacity expands.
UniEA unifies dual-space embedding by simultaneously learning graph structure embeddings in Euclidean and hyperbolic spaces to preserve intrinsic knowledge graph structure.
UniEA employs contrastive learning to mitigate misalignment caused by similar neighboring entities.
UniEA achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets for structure-based entity alignment.
A framework for finetuning large language models for comparative assessment aligns model outputs with target comparative probability distributions.
Training on soft probabilities improves state-of-the-art performance in comparative assessment.
Finetuned models maintain high performance while using an efficient subset of pairwise comparisons.

A compliance checking framework based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines a static factual knowledge layer, a dynamic regulatory and business process layer, and a computational retrieval and reasoning layer.
The framework employs an eventic graph to structurally describe regulatory information centered on actions and states.
Experiments on Chinese and English compliance checking datasets show that the framework consistently achieves state-of-the-art results across various scenarios.
Ethical consideration guidelines are provided to mitigate harm in developing suicide-related resources.
An existing dictionary of suicidal ideation is translated into 200 languages.
Human evaluations are performed on a subset of the translated dictionaries.
A public website is released to host the resources and enable community participation.
Fine-grained attribution reasoning distillation (FARD) incorporates grounded citations to consolidate relationships between reasoning steps.
FARD distills attribution reasoning rationales from large language models to replace chain-of-thought reasonings, thereby clarifying dependencies among reasoning steps.
FARD regularizes model attention patterns by leveraging causal dependencies between reasoning steps to enhance reasoning consistency.
Grounded attribution reasoning in FARD improves interpretability and verifiability of model reasoning.
FARD outperforms chain-of-thought distillation methods on mathematical reasoning benchmarks.
Small models trained with FARD achieve strong out-of-distribution reasoning performance, indicating strong generalization capabilities.
FET is a discrete optimization attack that directly searches for text sequences whose gradients match known gradients, unlike prior methods that optimize continuous embedding vectors.
FET infers the total number of tokens and the set of unique tokens in target text data from gradients of the embedding layer.
FET employs a discrete optimization algorithm that combines global exploration and precise local refinement to recover text from gradients.
Gradients of the fully connected layer dominate the optimization process and provide sufficient guidance for reconstructing text.
FET substantially improves exact‑match reconstruction rates across multiple BERT model sizes, demonstrating that smaller models do not effectively preserve text privacy.
Reasoning-Oriented Locating and Editing (ROLE) locates and edits key modules of language models to reason about event relations.
ROLE improves interpretability and reasoning performance while reducing computational cost.
Analogy-Based Locating and Editing (ABLE) exploits similarities and differences between tasks to optimize zero-shot event-relational reasoning capability.
ABLE achieves state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot event-relational reasoning.
Topology-of-Question-Decomposition (ToQD) is a framework that activates retrieval only when necessary during large language model reasoning.
ToQD guides large language models to construct a topology graph from an input question, with each node representing a sub-question.
ToQD employs self-verify inference to determine for each sub-question whether to retrieve relevant documents, further decompose the sub-question, or provide an answer directly.
ToQD achieves superior performance and robustness on complex, knowledge-intensive tasks.
ToQD significantly enhances system response efficiency for complex, knowledge-intensive tasks.
A controllable framework generates commonsense reasoning questions with controllable complexity by iteratively composing sub-questions derived from contextual and commonsense clues.
A voting verifier evaluates generated questions for answer consistency, reasoning difficulty, and context correlation.
An optimal question generation model produces thought‑provoking commonsense reasoning questions.
Experiments on two typical datasets validate the method.
GPT-4, Llama-2, and BLOOM are evaluated for generating motivational interviewing reflections.
The effect of dialogue context size on the quality of LLM-generated motivational interviewing reflections is examined.
Incorporating motivational interviewing strategies into LLM reflection generation is examined.
Both automatic metrics and human judgments on appropriateness, relevance, engagement, and naturalness are employed to assess LLM-generated motivational interviewing reflections.
LLMs are shown to generate motivational interviewing reflections comparable to those produced by human therapists.
Content analysis identifies significant challenges remaining in LLM-generated motivational interviewing reflections regarding empathy and contextual appropriateness.
Forgettting-Subdued Learning mitigates catastrophic forgetting of old entity types within each client by employing prototype-guided adaptive pseudo labeling.
Forgettting-Subdued Learning mitigates catastrophic forgetting of old entity types within each client by employing a prototypical relation distillation loss.

Forgettting-Subdued Learning mitigates catastrophic forgetting of old entity types across clients by employing a task transfer detector that identifies the arrival of new entity types and stores the latest global model for relation distillation.
Qualitative experiments show that Forgettting-Subdued Learning achieves significant improvements over several baseline methods.
Retrieval Augmented Instruction Tuning (RA-IT) incorporates semantically similar retrieved examples as context for instruction tuning in open named entity recognition.
A Chinese instruction-tuning dataset for open named entity recognition is constructed.
RA-IT demonstrates effectiveness across various data sizes in both English and Chinese open named entity recognition scenarios.
Analyses reveal the impact of different retrieval strategies within the RA-IT framework.

TermDiffuSum incorporates legal terminology into a diffusion model via a multifactor fusion noise weighting schedule that allocates higher attention weight to sentences containing a higher concentration of legal terms.
TermDiffuSum employs a re‑ranking loss function that refines summary selection by leveraging the relationship between candidate summaries generated by the diffusion process and reference summaries.
TermDiffuSum outperforms existing diffusion‑based extractive summarization models on a legal summarization dataset.
TermDiffuSum demonstrates scalability by achieving strong performance on public news and social‑media summarization datasets.
Four types of similarity metrics based on sentence representations are defined to assess crosslingual alignment.
Multiple models are trained under varying setups on Polish‑Ukrainian and Hindi‑Urdu language pairs to evaluate transliteration‑based alignment.
Adding transliterations alone improves overall similarity between sentence representations, including for random sentence pairs.
Auxiliary transliteration‑based alignment objectives, especially a contrastive objective, enable distinction between matched and random sentence pairs, leading to better crosslingual alignment.
Improved crosslingual alignment does not necessarily translate into better downstream task performance.
Multi-modal distribution alignment is important for effective knowledge distillation of large language models.
Existing knowledge distillation approaches are inefficient at learning multi-modal probability distributions from teacher large language models.
Ranking Loss based Knowledge Distillation (RLKD) aligns the ranking of peak predictions between teacher and student models.
RLKD incorporates a word-level ranking loss that is compatible with existing distillation objectives and leverages fine-grained information between categories in distribution peaks.
RLKD enables student models to better learn the multi-modal distributions of teacher models, leading to improved performance on various downstream tasks.
Edit-wise Preference Optimization (EPO) distinguishes token importance and assigns higher reward weights to edit tokens during preference optimization for grammatical error correction.
EPO captures fine-grained distinctions in grammatical error correction that traditional preference learning overlooks.
EPO consistently outperforms strong baselines on English and Chinese grammatical error correction datasets, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
EPO demonstrates the advantages of large language models in grammatical error correction.
Dual-Process Theory is applied to dialogue topic shift detection.
Dual-Module Framework (DMF) includes an intuition module that extracts and stores the global topic structure of historical dialogue using large language models.

DMF includes a reasoning module that generates reasoning samples linking the response to the most recent historical topic using a large language model, providing local detail explanations for topic shift.
The dual-module framework is distilled into a small generative model to enable more precise reasoning.
DMF outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on three public dialogue topic shift detection datasets.
MBA-RAG dynamically selects the most suitable retrieval strategy based on query complexity using a multi-armed bandit algorithm.
MBA-RAG incorporates a dynamic reward function that balances accuracy against the number of retrieval steps.
MBA-RAG achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple single-hop and multi-hop datasets while reducing retrieval costs.
Task-sensitive neurons in large language models are detected via gradient attribution on task-specific data.
Deactivation and fine-tuning experiments show that the detected neurons are highly correlated with their respective tasks, defining them as task-specific neurons.
Overlap of task-specific neurons across tasks is strongly associated with generalization and specialization.
Parameter similarity among task-specific neurons at certain layers is highly correlated with generalization performance.
A neuron-level continuous fine-tuning method fine-tunes only the current task-specific neurons during continuous learning.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that the neuron-level continuous fine-tuning method improves continual learning outcomes.
MiMoTable is a table reasoning benchmark that uses real-world spreadsheet tables spanning seven domains.
MiMoTable defines a difficulty criterion composed of six categories of meta operations for measuring question difficulty.
The meta-operation difficulty criterion can be applied to existing table reasoning benchmarks to assess their difficulty.
Experiments show that large language models achieve moderate accuracy on MiMoTable, indicating substantial room for improvement.
Experiments reveal that large language model performance declines as difficulty, measured by the meta-operation criterion, increases, validating the criterion.
An evaluation framework assesses the sensitivity of large language models to key medical information in clinical diagnosis.
Perturbation strategies are introduced to modify key medical information for testing model sensitivity.
A dataset for evaluating large language model sensitivity to key medical information in diagnostic decision-making is released.
Empirical evaluation of GPT-3.5, GPT-4, Gemini, Claude3, and LLaMA2-7b demonstrates that current models often lack sensitivity to key medical information in diagnostic reasoning.
Spoken dialogue systems that incorporate incremental response generation were developed.
Spoken dialogue systems that incorporate Voice Activity Projection (VAP) based turn-taking were developed.
Incremental response generation combined with VAP-based turn-taking improves task success and dialogue satisfaction according to user assessments.
A framework for implicit discourse relation classification in Nigerian Pidgin is introduced.
English implicit discourse relation classification tools are applied to Nigerian Pidgin text and its English translations with back-projection of labels.
A synthetic Nigerian Pidgin discourse corpus is generated by automatically translating the English PDTB and projecting its discourse relation labels.
A native Nigerian Pidgin implicit discourse relation classifier trained on the synthetic corpus outperforms a baseline approach.
The framework can be used for implicit discourse relation classification in other English-lexified languages.
Entropy-Consistency Word Selection (ECWS) selects words for vocabulary augmentation using integrated semantic and frequency metrics.
Entropy-Consistency Word Selection (ECWS) addresses the mismatch between model representation distributions and frequency distributions in vocabulary augmentation.
Entropy-Consistency Word Selection (ECWS) improves performance of multilingual language models on low-resource languages.
ProSparse introduces intrinsic activation sparsity to large language models that previously used activation functions without such sparsity.
ProSparse applies progressive sparsity regularization with a smoothly increasing factor across multiple stages to enhance activation sparsity while mitigating performance degradation.

ProSparse achieves high activation sparsity in LLaMA2-7B, LLaMA2-13B, and MiniCPM-1B with performance comparable to their original Swish-activated versions, establishing the most sparsely activated open-source LLaMA models.
Models sparsified with ProSparse demonstrate significant inference acceleration, achieving multiple-fold speedup.
Peer-review effectiveness is framed as a forecasting task for future citation impact.
A predictive model using venue information and early citation counts estimates whether papers will become highly cited.
Early citation returns are more predictive of future high citation impact than venue.
A constructive suggestion to simplify the reviewing process is proposed.
CMMaTH is a Chinese multi-modal math skill evaluation benchmark for foundation models.

CMMaTH comprises 23,856 multimodal K12 math questions spanning elementary to high school levels with diverse problem types, solution goals, visual elements, knowledge points, and standard solution annotations.
CMMaTH is the largest Chinese multimodal mathematical problem benchmark to date.
GradeGPT is an open-source tool integrated with CMMaTH that provides stable, fast, and cost-free evaluation of multimodal large models.
RGR-KBQA is a semantic parsing approach that uses a Retrieve-Generate-Retrieve framework for knowledge base question answering.
The first retrieval step introduces factual knowledge from a knowledge graph to enhance the semantic understanding of large language models for logical form generation.
A fine-tuned model is used in the generation step to produce logical forms.
The final step performs unsupervised relation and entity retrieval to further improve logical form generation accuracy.
The Retrieve-Generate-Retrieve framework mitigates hallucination problems in large language models.
RGR-KBQA achieves promising performance on the CWQ and WebQSP datasets.
ReVrDU is a variant of visually-rich document understanding that does not use manually annotated semantic groups.
ReLayout learns to capture semantic grouping by arranging words and pulling together representations of words that potentially belong to the same semantic group.
Existing methods' performance deteriorates on the ReVrDU task.
ReLayout achieves superior performance on the ReVrDU task compared to existing methods.
RBCoSum incorporates a generated Chain Of Comments as auxiliary intermediate information to bridge the heterogeneity gap between code and summaries.
A rethinking process uses a learned ranker trained on a constructed ranking dataset to score the match between generated summaries and code and selects the highest-scoring summary.
A ranking dataset is constructed to train the ranker for evaluating summary-code matching.
RBCoSum outperforms existing automatic code summarization models and code large language models.
Human evaluation indicates that summaries generated by RBCoSum are more natural, informative, useful, and truthful than those from baseline methods.
A subset of the BELEBELE multilingual dataset is repurposed to enable extractive question answering.
Annotation guidelines for converting multiple-choice QA to extractive QA are provided.
A parallel extractive QA dataset for English and Modern Standard Arabic is released.
QA evaluation results are reported for monolingual and cross-lingual QA pairs involving English, Modern Standard Arabic, and five Arabic dialects.
An analysis of challenges and opportunities in NLP task reformulation is presented.
MCS-SQL leverages multiple prompts to robustly refine database schema linking.
MCS-SQL generates diverse candidate SQL queries from the refined schema using multiple prompts.
MCS-SQL filters candidate queries with confidence scores and selects the optimal query via a multiple-choice selection presented to the LLM.
MCS-SQL achieves higher execution accuracy on the BIRD benchmark than previous in-context learning-based methods.
MCS-SQL achieves higher execution accuracy on the Spider benchmark than previous in-context learning-based methods.
Alympics provides a systematic framework that utilizes large language model agents for empirical game theory research.
Alympics creates a versatile platform for studying complex game theory problems by simulating human-like strategic interactions with large language model agents.
The Water Allocation Challenge case study demonstrates that the framework enables qualitative and quantitative analysis of game determinants, strategies, and outcomes.
Human assessment evaluates large language model agents' performance in rational strategic decision-making scenarios.
Large language model agents can emulate human strategic behavior and contribute to advancing game theory knowledge.
SKIntern internalizes symbolic knowledge and few-shot examples into small language models through progressive fine-tuning guided by a linear decay schedule under curriculum learning.
SKIntern enables inference that relies solely on the question, removing the need for external symbolic knowledge during generation.
SKIntern reduces computational overhead and inference cost of small language models while preserving or improving reasoning performance.
SKIntern achieves higher reasoning performance than state-of-the-art baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain tasks.
A weighted propagation tree with edge weights representing time intervals between posts is constructed to incorporate temporal information in rumor detection.
Structural entropy theory is used to transform the weighted propagation tree into a coding tree that preserves essential propagation structure while reducing noise.
A recursive neural network is applied to the coding tree to predict rumor veracity.
The proposed approach achieves superior performance on two common rumor detection datasets compared to prior methods.
APEX adaptively warms up experts using SQL syntax guidance to improve model initialization for continual semantic parsing.
APEX maintains a dynamically expanding expert pool that stores knowledge and models relationships between experts and instances.
APEX employs a selection/fusion inference strategy based on sample historical visibility to promote expert collaboration.
APEX achieves superior performance on continual semantic parsing benchmarks without data replay or ideal settings, handling cold start scenarios and generalizing to unseen tasks.
APEX surpasses established performance upper bounds on continual semantic parsing benchmarks.
FineSumFact is a large-scale dataset containing fine-grained factual feedback on summaries.
FineSumFact is built from summaries generated by 10 distinct large language models and factual feedback produced by Llama-3-70B-Instruct.
Fine-tuning Llama-3-8B-Instruct on FineSumFact yields high performance with optimized resource efficiency.
A model trained on extensive LLM-generated datasets surpasses a model trained on smaller human-annotated datasets on human-generated test sets.
Fine-tuning fact verification models with LLM feedback is more effective and cost-efficient than using human feedback.
COF is a model-unaware adaptive chain-of-feedback method for post-processing comparative opinion quintuple extraction.
COF comprises dynamic example selection, self-critique, and self-revision modules.
COF enables dynamic iterative self-optimization of extraction results, making it applicable across different baselines.
COF improves exact-matching performance compared to a frozen-parameter few-shot learning baseline on multiple comparative opinion quintuple extraction benchmarks.
COF surpasses current state-of-the-art supervised fine-tuned models on comparative opinion quintuple extraction benchmarks.
Zero-to-strong generalization elicits strong capabilities of large language models using only unlabeled data.
Iterative prompting of large language models with filtering produces high-quality labels from unlabeled data.
The iterative process gradually unlocks large language models’ potential on downstream classification and reasoning tasks.
The framework is effective for both in-context learning and fine-tuning across various model sizes.
Gen-SQL introduces a generate‑ground‑regenerate framework for Text‑to‑SQL that bridges natural language questions and database schemas via a pseudo‑schema.
The framework leverages prior knowledge from large language models to enhance embedding‑based retrievers and reduce retrieval cost.
Experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate the method’s effectiveness and scalability.
A methodology leverages the vision component of multimodal large language models to improve manga translation quality.
An evaluation studies the impact of translation unit size and context length on manga translation performance.
A token-efficient approach for manga translation is proposed.
A new parallel Japanese-Polish manga translation dataset is introduced as a benchmark for future research.
An open-source software suite is released for benchmarking multimodal large language models on manga translation.
The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance on Japanese-English manga translation.
The proposed methods set a new standard for Japanese-Polish manga translation.
Bias Vector mitigates language model biases without requiring manually created debiasing data.
Bias Vector constructs a bias vector as the difference between the weights of biased fine‑tuned language models and the original pre‑trained language models.
Bias Vector debiases language models by subtracting the bias vector from the pre‑trained weights.
Bias Vector improves SEAT bias evaluation scores for multiple language models.
Bias Vector maintains downstream task performance on the GLUE benchmark after debiasing.
Chain-of-Specificity emphasizes specific constraints in input instructions.
Chain-of-Specificity unlocks knowledge within large language models.
Chain-of-Specificity improves specificity of generated content compared to existing methods.
Chain-of-Specificity maintains performance as the number of specific constraints increases, while other baselines degrade.
Distilling responses generated by Chain-of-Specificity enhances smaller models' ability to follow constrained instructions.
Alternate Preference Optimization (AltPO) combines negative feedback with in-domain positive feedback on the forget set.
Alternate Preference Optimization (AltPO) enables effective unlearning of factual knowledge in large language models.
Alternate Preference Optimization (AltPO) avoids undesirable model behaviors.
Alternate Preference Optimization (AltPO) maintains overall model performance.
New evaluation metrics assess the quality of responses related to the forget set.
A dataset of 320 synthetic and 360 natural Mandarin sentences is created to evaluate long-distance binding of the reflexive ziji.
Twenty-one language models are evaluated on the ziji binding dataset and compared to native Mandarin speaker judgments.
No language model consistently replicates human-like judgments on the long-distance binding of Chinese reflexive ziji.
Existing language models rely heavily on sequential cues rather than subtle syntactic and semantic constraints when resolving ziji binding.
Language models show greater sensitivity to noun-related semantics than to verb-related semantics in the ziji binding task.
A semantic evaluation approach based on a sliding window mechanism is introduced for assessing claim clarification.
A reinforcement learning-based clarification method is proposed that balances content preservation with claim clarity and enhances LLM performance on claim clarification.
LLama2, Mistral, and Qwen2 are evaluated on the claim clarification task using zero-shot prompting, few-shot prompting, and supervised fine-tuning.
A first analysis of factual knowledge boundaries of large language models and the impact of retrieval augmentation on open-domain question answering is presented.
Large language models maintain unwavering confidence in their knowledge and struggle to resolve conflicts between internal and external knowledge.
Retrieval augmentation enhances large language models' awareness of knowledge boundaries.
A simple method dynamically utilizes supporting documents based on a judgement strategy.
Relevance between supporting documents and questions significantly impacts large language models' question answering and judgement capabilities.
A method automatically clusters sign languages into families based on language distribution in the multilingual sign language translation network.
Leveraging sign language family information improves multilingual sign language translation performance.
Dedicated translation models are trained for each automatically identified language family in a many-to-one translation scenario.
Experimental evaluation on the SP-10 dataset shows that regulating the number of language families balances translation accuracy and computational cost.
The automatically derived language families are compared with analyses by sign language linguists.
Systems for semantic role labeling of English partitive nouns in the NomBank corpus are built using traditional machine learning, transformer-based models, and ensembling.
The highest-scoring system attains high F1 performance when using gold parses from the Penn Treebank and when using the Berkeley Neural parser.
Both classroom and experimental settings are employed for system development and evaluation.
Hybrid Uncertainty and Diversity Sampling (HUDS) is an active learning strategy for domain adaptation in neural machine translation that combines uncertainty and diversity for sentence selection.
HUDS computes uncertainty scores for unlabeled sentences, stratifies them, clusters sentence embeddings within each stratum, and computes diversity scores as distance to the centroid.
HUDS uses a weighted hybrid score that combines uncertainty and diversity to select top instances for annotation in each active learning iteration.
HUDS achieves better performance than strong active learning baselines on multi-domain German-English and French-English neural machine translation datasets.
HUDS prioritizes diverse instances with high model uncertainty for annotation in early active learning iterations.

MQM-Chat defines a multidimensional quality metric for chat translation comprising seven error types, including three chat-specific types: ambiguity and disambiguation, buzzword or loanword issues, and dialogue inconsistency.
Human annotations were performed on translations of chat data produced by five translation models using MQM-Chat.
MQM-Chat effectively classifies translation errors and explicitly highlights chat-specific issues.
MQM-Chat can assess both lexical accuracy and semantic accuracy of translation models in chat translation tasks.
A novel metric based on semantic similarity is introduced for predicting N400 and P600 components.
Surprisal, entropy, and the semantic similarity metric are evaluated for predicting N400 and P600 amplitudes using Mandarin Chinese reading materials.
Expectancy, as captured by surprisal, primarily predicts N400 amplitude.
P600 amplitude reflects the cognitive effort required to evaluate linguistic input semantically.
Information uncertainty, as captured by entropy, most influences language processing during the target time window.
AHVE-CNER integrates multi-source visual and phonetic information of Hanzi for Chinese named entity recognition.
AHVE-CNER explicitly aligns pictographic features of Hanzi with their corresponding radicals.
Gated Pangu-π Cross Transformer facilitates integration of multi-modal representations for Chinese named entity recognition.
A multi-source glyph alignment strategy enables AHVE-CNER to capture visual and semantic nuances of Hanzi for named entity recognition.
AHVE-CNER achieves superior performance compared to existing multi-modal Chinese named entity recognition methods on benchmark datasets.
Ablation studies confirm the effectiveness of the visual alignment module and the fusion approach in AHVE-CNER.
A fake news detection method predicts claim veracity using relevant factual evidence when available and large language model inference mechanisms otherwise.
The method generates a synthesized prediction accompanied by well-founded facts or reasoning.
The method achieves state-of-the-art detection performance on several large COVID-19 fake news datasets.
The method achieves state-of-the-art evidence explanation performance on several large COVID-19 fake news datasets.
Generation-Augmented and Embedding Fusion creates role‑relationship embeddings by applying predefined templates to generative language models.
Generation-Augmented and Embedding Fusion combines generative‑model embeddings with classification‑model embeddings through a novel embedding fusion mechanism.
Generation-Augmented and Embedding Fusion outperforms baseline methods on the RAMS and WikiEvents document‑level event argument extraction datasets.
Generation-Augmented and Embedding Fusion remains effective in low‑resource scenarios, demonstrating data efficiency.
Several multimodal large language models exhibit miscalibration in performance across various scenarios.
Calibration of multimodal large language models shows no significant differences before and after visual fine-tuning or multimodal training of the base language models.
The IDK dataset evaluates multimodal large language models' ability to handle unknown inputs.
Multimodal large language models preferentially provide answers rather than express uncertainty, but their self-assessment improves with prompt adjustments.
Temperature scaling and iterative prompt optimization calibrate multimodal large language models and enhance their reliability.
A quaternion knowledge graph embedding model combines semantic matching with entity geometric distance to measure triplet plausibility.
The model performs a right rotation on head entities and a reverse rotation on tail entities in quaternion space to learn semantic features.
The model utilizes distance-adaptive translations to learn geometric distance between entities.
Mathematical proofs demonstrate that the model can handle complex logical relationships.
Experimental results show that the model outperforms previous models on standard knowledge graph completion benchmark datasets.
Classical textual data augmentation methods function as a form of improved fine-tuning rather than providing distinct benefits.
Extending fine-tuning duration before applying data augmentation eliminates the effect of data augmentation.
All data augmentation techniques achieve comparable performance when the generated data closely matches the training distribution.
Zero- and few-shot data augmentation using conversational agents such as ChatGPT or Llama2 improves performance and is preferable to classical data augmentation methods.
C3LRSO provides a high-quality Chinese sentence ordering dataset with genuinely unordered sentences and no artificial segmentation cues.
A parameter-free approach outperforms existing methods on Chinese sentence ordering.
Large language models achieve strong performance when evaluated on C3LRSO.
Experiments demonstrate that C3LRSO is challenging for sentence ordering models.
CodeAlignKGQA aligns logical form generation for knowledge graph question answering with code generation that incorporates logical-form-specific constraints.
CodeAlignKGQA extracts question-specific subgraph information to enable knowledge-aware code generation.
CodeAlignKGQA introduces a dynamic self-code-correction mechanism applied as required during generation.
Experiments on complex KGQA benchmarks demonstrate that CodeAlignKGQA outperforms few-shot baselines and achieves a new state-of-the-art on KQA Pro.

Syntax-Opinion-Sentiment Reasoning Chain (Syn-Chain) is a multi-step reasoning framework that sequentially analyzes syntactic dependencies, extracts opinions, and classifies sentiment for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Syn-Chain can be incorporated into large language models through zero-shot prompting.
Syn-Chain significantly enhances aspect-based sentiment analysis performance of large language models.
Distilling Syn-Chain responses generated by GPT-3.5 into smaller language models yields state-of-the-art aspect-based sentiment analysis performance for those smaller models.
Smaller language models exhibit weaker aspect-based sentiment analysis performance when using Syn-Chain without distillation.
MSOF is a multi-strategy optimization framework for positive text reframing.
Positive sentiment reward and content preservation reward are designed to transform negative expressions while preserving semantic integrity.
Decoding optimization approaches are introduced to improve the quality of generated reframed text.
A multi-dimensional re-ranking method selects candidate sentences based on strategy consistency, text similarity, and fluency.
The framework achieves improvements on both unconstrained and controlled positive reframing tasks with BART and T5.
DefVerify encodes a user-specified definition of hate speech.
DefVerify quantifies the extent to which a hate‑speech model reflects the intended definition.
DefVerify identifies the point of failure in the workflow of dataset construction and model training for hate‑speech detection.
