MCA utilizes an autoregressive Transformer to model dialogue trajectories, enabling more accurate simulation of the environment.
MCA includes a causal chains module that outputs latent preference distributions for intention-action pairs, elucidating the relationship between user intentions and agent actions.
MCA achieves state-of-the-art performance on three dialogue datasets, demonstrating effectiveness and robustness.
Propulsion selectively rescales specific dimensions of a pre-trained model to guide output predictions toward task objectives without modifying the model’s parameters.
Propulsion introduces lightweight trainable parameters at pre-trained layers to minimize the number of parameters updated during fine-tuning.
Theoretical analysis based on Neural Tangent Kernel theory shows that Propulsion approximates the performance of full fine-tuning with far fewer trainable parameters.
Propulsion reduces the number of trainable parameters required for fine-tuning by an order of magnitude compared to standard parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods.
Propulsion maintains competitive performance across benchmarks despite the substantial reduction in trainable parameters.
COMEDY eliminates traditional retrieval modules and memory databases in long-term conversation systems.
COMEDY uses a single language model to perform memory generation, compression, and response generation.
COMEDY introduces compressive memory that integrates session-specific summaries, user-bot dynamics, and past events into a concise format.
Dolphin is the largest Chinese long-term conversation dataset collected from real user-chatbot interactions.
COMEDY outperforms traditional retrieval-based methods in producing more nuanced and human-like conversational experiences.
DevEval evaluates large language models across software design, environment setup, implementation, acceptance testing, and unit testing stages of the software development lifecycle.
DevEval includes four programming languages, multiple domains, high-quality data collection, and verified metrics for each task.
Empirical evaluation shows that current large language models, including GPT-4, are unable to solve the challenges presented in DevEval.
The study provides actionable insights for developing large language models toward real-world programming applications.
The task of automatic section titling is defined by assigning generic and specific headings to each section of court opinions.
A dataset of sections with generic or specific headings covering the U.S. Supreme Court and two lower courts is compiled.
A supplementary set of U.S. Supreme Court sections is manually annotated with generic and specific titles.
Fine-tuned LegalBERT predicts generic headings with high F1 performance.
BART and T5 generate specific headings with comparable performance.
Human assessment reveals a quasi-linear correlation between human agreement scores and conventional automatic measures such as ROUGE and BERTScore.
A benchmark evaluating different systems for generic heading prediction and specific heading generation is established.
DEGAP introduces dual learnable prompt vectors—an instance-oriented prefix and a template-oriented prefix—that capture information from event instances and templates for event argument extraction.
DEGAP incorporates an event-guided adaptive gating mechanism that dynamically leverages connections between different events to select relevant prefix information.
The event-guided prefixes enable the event argument extraction model to obtain relevant cues without requiring external retrieval.
DEGAP attains state-of-the-art performance on multiple event argument extraction benchmarks, including ACE05, RAMS, WikiEvents, and MLEE.
Ablation analysis demonstrates the contribution of each DEGAP component to overall performance.
A retrieval-augmented generation framework backed by a large language model corrects the output of a smaller model for morphological glossing.
The framework leverages linguistic information from written descriptive grammars interpreted through a large language model to compensate for limited data and parameters.
Combining linguistic inputs, large language model interpretive power, and trainable token classification networks yields significant performance and efficiency gains for morphological glossing.
A compact model supported by retrieval-augmented generation achieves state-of-the-art performance on morphological glossing in low-resource languages.
The tool provides well-reasoned explanations and confidence scores for each morphological glossing output, enhancing reliability and usability for documentary linguists.
An entailment-based zero-shot text classification approach is introduced for annotating biomedical text passages with the Homeostasis Imbalance Process ontology.
The approach reformulates biomedical annotation as a multi-class, multi-label classification problem using natural language inference.
The approach demonstrates promising performance for annotating processes that are not explicitly mentioned in the text.
Mathematical priors are used to compute aggregated digit embeddings for numbers.
Aggregated digit embeddings are incorporated into transformer models by adding a special token to the input embeddings.
Aggregated digit embeddings are incorporated into transformer models by introducing an additional loss function to enhance correct predictions.
The explicit aggregation of digit embeddings is evaluated for effectiveness, with analysis of its strengths and shortcomings.
The proposed methods are compatible with any pretrained model and are easy to implement.
LKCER constructs a conceptual-level heterogeneous event graph by leveraging local contextual information of related event mentions.
The constructed graph enables generation of a comprehensive global semantic representation of event concepts.
COMET-generated knowledge is filtered and enriched using a large language model to strengthen associations between event pairs and knowledge.
Joint event conceptual representation and knowledge-enhanced event representation are used to uncover potential causal relationships between events.
The method achieves superior performance on the EventStoryLine and Causal-TimeBank benchmarks.

SLARD is a Chinese superior legal article retrieval dataset containing 2,627 manually annotated queries and 9,184 candidate articles drawn from over 4.3 million regulations across 32 categories, with candidates including provincial and national superior articles.
Experiments on SLARD demonstrate that existing retrieval methods achieve limited performance, with the best method attaining low recall at rank 1.
Analysis of SLARD indicates that existing large language models lack prior knowledge of the content of superior legal articles.
A formal task definition for measuring contextual informativeness in children’s stories is provided.
A dataset for evaluating contextual informativeness in children’s stories is introduced.
A method using a large language model automates the measurement of contextual informativeness in children’s stories.
The LLM-based method achieves higher correlation with human judgments of informativeness than prior baselines.
The LLM-based method generalizes to measuring contextual informativeness in adult-directed text and outperforms baselines.
TOP-Training is a pretraining technique that adapts large language models to better solve target extractive medical question answering problems.
TOP-Training does not require a large set of unlabeled instances from the target domain.
Pretraining on synthetic texts containing entities extracted from a target Medical-EQA dataset improves performance on Medical-EQA benchmarks.
TOP-Training moves beyond domain-oriented fine-tuning by both approaching the target domain and familiarizing with the target dataset.

Experiments demonstrate that autoregressive large language models have limitations for extractive medical question answering, and that TOP-Training enables bidirectional large language models to achieve higher performance.
LOLA is a massively multilingual large language model trained on over 160 languages using a sparse Mixture-of-Experts Transformer architecture.
Architectural and implementation choices in LOLA enable efficient handling of linguistic diversity while avoiding common multilinguality pitfalls.
LOLA achieves competitive performance on natural language generation and understanding tasks.
The expert-routing mechanism in LOLA exploits implicit phylogenetic linguistic patterns to potentially mitigate the curse of multilinguality.
An in-depth analysis of LOLA's training process and datasets is provided.
LOLA is released as an open-source model that promotes reproducibility and serves as a robust foundation for future research.
High-level guidelines enable large language models to implicitly adjust their decision strategies for interactive feature collection within a single prompt.
Large language models guided by these guidelines ask fewer and more strategic questions during interactive problem solving.
Large language models guided by these guidelines achieve improved problem‑solving efficiency in synthetic 20‑Questions games and real‑world business and medical diagnosis scenarios.
EERPD uses emotion regulation, a psychological concept, to predict personality traits.
EERPD combines emotion regulation, emotion features, and few-shot example retrieval for personality detection.
EERPD provides chain-of-thought processes for inferring personality labels from text.
EERPD improves the accuracy and robustness of personality detection relative to previous state-of-the-art methods.
Evolver uses Chain-of-Evolution Prompting to integrate evolution attributes and in-context information of memes into large multimodal models.

Evolver simulates the evolving and expressing process of memes and reasons step-by-step through large multimodal models using an evolutionary pair mining module, an evolutionary information extractor, and a contextual relevance amplifier.
Chain-of-Evolution Prompting can be incorporated into existing large multimodal models to improve performance on hateful meme detection.
Chain-of-Evolution Prompting functions as an interpretive tool that promotes understanding of meme evolution.
A novel dataset and task are introduced for independently summarizing each political perspective in opinionated news article passages.
A framework for evaluating multiple dimensions of perspective summary performance is proposed.
Eleven summarization models and large language models of varying sizes and architectures are benchmarked on the perspective summarization task using both automatic and human evaluation.
All evaluated models struggle to generate summaries that are faithful to the intended political perspective.
Extraction behavior of generated summaries is shown to be impacted by features of the input documents.
SAGA22 is a compact dataset for analyzing mathematics tutoring discourse using talk moves.
Supplementary pretraining on classroom teaching data enhances model performance for talk move analysis in tutoring settings.
Incorporating longer dialogue context and speaker information improves model performance for talk move analysis in tutoring settings.
Multiple modeling strategies—including dialogue context, speaker information, pretraining datasets, and fine-tuning—are evaluated for talk move analysis in mathematics tutoring.
Extensive ablation studies reveal challenges in modeling talk moves.
NüshuRescue is an AI-driven framework that trains large language models on endangered languages using minimal data.
NüshuRescue automates evaluation and expands target corpora to accelerate linguistic revitalization.
NCGold is a 500-sentence Nüshu-Chinese parallel corpus, the first publicly available dataset of this kind.
Using GPT-4-Turbo with only 35 short examples from NCGold, NüshuRescue achieves translation accuracy on withheld Nüshu sentences.
NüshuRescue generates NCSilver, a set of newly translated modern Chinese sentences of varying lengths.
FastText-based and Seq2Seq models for Nüshu are developed to support research on the language.
Parallel corpora for text detoxification are created for German, Chinese, Arabic, Hindi, and Amharic.
An automated, explainable analysis of descriptive features of toxic and non-toxic sentences is performed across nine languages.
A novel text detoxification method that incorporates Chain-of-Thoughts reasoning and clustering on descriptive attributes is introduced.
Multilingual text style transfer baselines are evaluated in an extensive multilingual setup covering nine languages.
TriFine provides a large-scale vision-audio-subtitle tri-modal VMT dataset with seven fine-grained annotation tags derived from visual and audio modalities.
FIAT is a fine-grained information-enhanced translation approach that leverages the annotated fine-grained tags.
FIAT achieves superior translation performance and lower computational overhead compared to traditional coarse-grained methods and text-only models.
FinDABench is a benchmark that evaluates the financial data analysis capabilities of large language models.
FinDABench contains 15,200 training instances and 8,900 test instances that were crafted by human experts.
FinDABench assesses large language models on Core Ability, which includes financial indicator calculation and corporate sentiment risk assessment.
FinDABench assesses large language models on Analytical Ability, which includes rapid comprehension of textual information and analysis of abnormal financial reports.
FinDABench assesses large language models on Technical Ability, which includes using technical knowledge to generate analyses and visualize charts from multiple perspectives.
Evaluation scripts for FinDABench are provided.
An annotation-free cross-lingual evaluation protocol for multilingual text generation is proposed.
The protocol generates English references by translating non-English inputs and using an LLM that excels in the equivalent English generation task.
The protocol assesses candidate LLMs by comparing their non-English outputs to the generated English references with a cross-lingual evaluation metric.
The protocol exhibits high correlation with reference-based ROUGE metric on news summarization across multiple languages.
A comprehensive evaluation of diverse LLMs across many languages with varying prompting strategies is conducted to study multilingual generative abilities.
PToco enables effective collaboration among less capable large language models independent of tokenizer differences.
PToco uses a prefix-grouping method to extract consensus among tokens at varying granularity, ensuring coherent and robust token generation across multiple models.
PToco improves reasoning task performance compared to individual models.
PToco generalizes across different numbers and sizes of participating models, providing a flexible and efficient solution for multi-LLM ensembles.
SA-DETR leverages instance-related span anchors generated from video-text pairs and supervised with ground-truth labels for moment retrieval.
SA-DETR enhances content embedding with textual feature guidance and uses a Gaussian mask to modulate interaction between content embedding and fused multimodal features.
SA-DETR incorporates feature alignment across multiple stages and granularities and applies denoise learning to improve span awareness.
SA-DETR demonstrates effective moment retrieval performance on QVHighlights, Charades-STA, and TACoS.
A benchmark dataset for SQL-to-text semantic captioning is released.
An iterative in-context learning prompt using GPT-4o generates multiple additional utterances for SQL-to-text captioning.
A graph-aware few-shot in-context learning sample selection method leverages SQL graph properties for captioning.
Graph-aware sample selection significantly outperforms random selection and alternative approaches for SQL-to-text captioning.
The Harmful Essay Detection (HED) benchmark provides essays on sensitive topics such as racism and gender bias for evaluating models' ability to detect harmful content.
Large language models are insufficiently able to distinguish harmful essays from argumentative essays.
Current automatic essay scoring models and large language models fail to consider ethical dimensions of essay content when assigning scores.
A systematic review of the language adaptation process for large language models is presented, covering vocabulary expansion, continued pre-training, and instruction fine-tuning.
Empirical studies on LLaMA2 are conducted to examine language adaptation steps such as vocabulary expansion, continued pre-training, and instruction fine-tuning.
The review highlights compatibility and interactions between different language adaptation steps for large language models.
A practical guidebook for facilitating effective adaptation of large language models across languages is offered.
APTY is a dataset of 800 sentence-level and word-level annotations of atomic paraphrase types created by 15 annotators.
APTY provides a human preference ranking of paraphrases across ten atomic paraphrase types and five prompting techniques for fine-tuning language models with RLHF and DPO.
Human preference evaluation indicates that ChatGPT can generate simple atomic paraphrase types such as additions and deletions.
Human preference evaluation indicates that ChatGPT struggles with complex atomic paraphrase types such as subordination changes.
Human preference evaluation indicates that a DPO-trained Llama 7B model can generate simple atomic paraphrase types such as additions and deletions.
Human preference evaluation indicates that a DPO-trained Llama 7B model struggles with complex atomic paraphrase types such as subordination changes.
The curated datasets can be used to develop language models with specific linguistic capabilities.
An experimental study compares word predictability in turn-taking across video, audio, and transcript modalities of face-to-face conversations.
Higher word predictability leads to more accurate predictions regardless of communication modality.
Richer multimodal cues increase response accuracy but also increase response time in word prediction tasks.
Participants with more positive and less negative self-reported emotions achieve higher prediction accuracy.
Semantic relatedness between omitted words and context does not influence predictability.
Prediction plays a key role in language processing and conversation smoothness in computer-mediated communication.
DeTriever learns a weighted combination of LLM hidden states to encode rich semantic information for demonstration retrieval.
DeTriever uses a proxy score that estimates the relative benefits of examples based on similarities between output queries.
DeTriever outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on NL2SQL benchmarks.
ParaPLUIE is a measure based on a log likelihood ratio from a large language model for assessing paraphrase quality.
ParaPLUIE is compared with traditional lexical and embedding-based measures on two established paraphrase datasets.
Three new small datasets are created to enable metric comparison across different scenarios and to avoid data contamination bias.
ParaPLUIE sorts sentence pairs by semantic proximity more effectively than traditional measures.
ParaPLUIE exhibits greater independence from lexical distance than traditional measures.
ParaPLUIE provides an interpretable classification threshold distinguishing paraphrases from non-paraphrases.
CoFactSum mitigates language bias and irrelevancy bias in abstractive summarization through counterfactual estimation.
Explicit Counterfactual Masking employs a dynamic masking approach for counterfactual estimation in summarization.
Implicit Counterfactual Training utilizes a discriminative cross-attention mechanism for counterfactual estimation in summarization.
Debiasing Degree Adjustment dynamically calibrates the level of debiasing at each decoding step.
CoFactSum enhances factual consistency of generated summaries compared to baseline methods.
Causal graphs are constructed to analyze abstractive summarization and identify language bias and irrelevancy bias as intrinsic causes of factual inconsistency.
A framework leverages Visual Question Answering models to automate evaluation of large language model-generated data visualizations.
The framework assesses data representation quality and communicative clarity of charts.
Experiments using the ChartQA and PlotQA benchmarks evaluate visualizations generated by GPT-3.5 Turbo and Llama 3.1 70B-Instruct.
LLM-generated charts achieve lower accuracy than original non-LLM-generated charts according to VQA performance measures.
Few-shot prompting significantly increases the accuracy of chart generation by large language models.
The framework enables rapid iteration of visualization evaluation without human annotation.
A dataset of 73,500 prompts constructed with a taxonomy of real-world occupations is released.
A multi-step verification framework for evaluating language models' behavior regarding gender stereotypes is introduced.
Gender stereotypes in language models are quantified via multi-round question answering.
All tested language models (RoBERTa, GPT, and Llama families) exhibit gender stereotypes analogous to human biases, with distinct preferences across model families.

GPT-4o-mini shows a preference for female subjects, suggesting that current alignment methods may be insufficient for debiasing and could introduce new biases contrary to traditional gender stereotypes.
A BERT encoder fine-tuned via contrastive learning achieves semantic alignment between randomly generated texts from the same model.

Machine-Generated Text Detection with Rewritten Texts uses prompt refactoring to obtain rewritten texts from LLMs and leverages semantic and statistical relationships between original and rewritten texts for detection and traceability.
A text dataset expanded with multi-parameter random sampling is used to evaluate detection methods.
MAGRET outperforms baseline methods in detecting machine-generated text from closed-source models.
MAGRET demonstrates stable detection and tracing performance across various randomly sampled texts.

Multi-hop multimodal claim verification requires models to reason over multiple pieces of evidence from text, images, and tables to determine whether the combined multimodal evidence supports or refutes a claim.
MMCV is a large-scale dataset of multi-hop claims paired with multimodal evidence, generated and refined using large language models and human feedback.
State-of-the-art multimodal large language models find MMCV challenging, with difficulty increasing as the number of reasoning hops grows.
A human performance benchmark is established on a subset of MMCV.
SAug-LLM leverages large language models to autonomously identify shortcuts in training data and generate anti-shortcut counterparts.
Dual validation is used in SAug-LLM to mitigate confirmation bias during model retraining.
SAug-LLM optimizes the data augmentation process to rectify model biases while minimizing data consumption.
SAug-LLM mitigates shortcut learning in pre-trained language models, improving robustness and generalization.
SAug-LLM demonstrates performance improvements across multiple natural language processing tasks.
DPC-FT includes a lightweight relation encoder per task to mitigate negative knowledge transfer across tasks.
DPC-FT includes a dynamic prototype module that allocates less memory for easier relations and more memory for harder relations.
DPC-FT introduces None-Of-The-Above (NOTA) detection for continual few-shot relation extraction.
DPC-FT proposes a threshold criterion to identify relations that have never been learned.
Experiments demonstrate that DPC-FT is effective and efficient for continual few-shot relation extraction.
CaDRL learns document-specific logical rules for document-level relation extraction via differentiable rule learning.
CaDRL uses Transformer-based relation attention to encode document and relation information and capture contextual relation information.
CaDRL employs a sequence-generated differentiable rule decoder to generate relational probabilistic logic rules at each reasoning step.
CaDRL introduces a parameter sharing training mechanism that integrates the DocRE model with the rule learning module.
CaDRL improves document-level relation extraction performance relative to existing rule-based frameworks.
CaDRL produces more interpretable and logical predictions for document-level relation extraction.
TEF employs a structural causal model with the front-door criterion to eliminate bias from unobserved confounding factors in taxonomy expansion.
TEF decomposes the taxonomy expansion process into a parser module and a connector guided by the front-door criterion.
TEF isolates confounding effects and reveals the true causal relationship between a query and its anchor node in taxonomy expansion.
TEF achieves higher accuracy than prior state-of-the-art methods on the SemEval16-Environment taxonomy expansion benchmark.
SweetSpan generates candidate spans from each model based on a shared prefix and selects spans by calculating perplexity scores for mutual evaluation, filtering out unfaithful scores.
SweetSpan balances real-time adjustment and information richness to enable accurate ensemble decisions at the span level.
A challenging evaluation setting that ensembles models with significant performance gaps is introduced to assess ensemble methods in realistic scenarios.
SweetSpan outperforms previous ensemble methods across various language generation tasks in both standard and challenging settings.
PARAPHRASUS provides a multi-dimensional benchmark for evaluating paraphrase detection models.
PARAPHRASUS includes three challenges that span over ten datasets, comprising eight repurposed and two newly annotated datasets.
PARAPHRASUS enables prompt calibration to tailor large language models to specific strictness levels for paraphrase detection.
Fine-grained evaluation with PARAPHRASUS reveals trade-offs in paraphrase detection models that are not captured by a single classification dataset.
A novel analysis leverages linguistic minimal pairs to probe internal linguistic representations of large language models.
The analysis quantifies linguistic similarity by measuring similarity between activation differences across minimal pairs.

Large-scale experiments across many large language models and hundreds of thousands of minimal pairs in three languages reveal that linguistic similarity is significantly influenced by training data exposure, leading to higher cross-model agreement for higher-resource languages.
Linguistic similarity strongly aligns with fine-grained theoretical linguistic categories but weakly with broader categories.
Linguistic similarity shows a weak correlation with semantic similarity, indicating a context-dependent nature.
Large language models exhibit limited cross-lingual alignment in their understanding of relevant linguistic phenomena.
Minimal pairs serve as a probing tool to investigate neural representations of language in large language models, linking model behavior to linguistic theory.
Many-Shot with Reference (MSwR) prompt template uses in-context examples with model-generated rationales as references for LLM evaluators.
Many-Shot without Reference (MSoR) prompt template uses in-context examples without references for LLM evaluators.
Increasing the number of in-context examples improves the consistency and quality of LLM evaluation results.
Advanced LLMs such as GPT-4 achieve higher evaluation performance in many-shot regimes compared to zero-shot regimes.
Many-Shot with Reference (MSwR) yields better evaluation performance than Many-Shot without Reference (MSoR) in most cases.
CharMoral provides annotations of character actions, contextual information, and morality labels for characters in long-form narratives.
A four-stage framework using large language models automatically classifies character actions as moral or immoral based on context.
Human evaluations demonstrate that the four-stage framework is effective for moral reasoning tasks across multiple genres.
TempBench is a benchmark that evaluates the temporal-relative ability of large language models.
TempBench comprises four scenarios: Physiology, Psychology, Cognition, and Mixture.
Extensive experiments on GPT-4, Llama series, and other popular large language models are performed using TempBench.
Experiments with TempBench reveal a significant performance gap between large language models and humans in temporal-relative capability.
A taxonomy of error types for temporal-relative ability in large language models is proposed to analyze multiple aspects and challenges.
Rule distillation encodes rule-based knowledge into large language model parameters by learning from in-context signals generated from textual rules.
Rule distillation leverages the strong in-context abilities of large language models to extract knowledge from textual rules.
Learning from rules via rule distillation is more sample-efficient than example-based learning.
Learning from rules via rule distillation yields better generalization ability than example-based learning.
Complex visual reasoning instructions are particularly effective at improving zero-shot generalization of multi-modal large language models, and performance correlates with instruction complexity.
A systematic approach using a synthesize‑complicate‑reformulate paradigm automatically creates high‑quality complex visual reasoning instructions.
The ComVint dataset contains 32,000 automatically generated complex visual reasoning instruction examples.
Fine‑tuning multi‑modal large language models on the ComVint dataset consistently enhances their performance across evaluated benchmarks.
FedCSR is a federated cross-domain sequential recommendation framework that operates without user alignment between platforms.
FedCSR utilizes cross-domain knowledge to mitigate inter- and intra-platform data heterogeneity.
Model Contrastive Learning (MCL) reduces the gap between local and global models in federated recommendation.
Sequence Contrastive Learning (SCL) employs tailored sequence augmentation to address heterogeneity of user preferences across domains within a platform.
FedCSR achieves superior performance compared to existing baseline methods on multiple real-world datasets.
Cultural Alignment Test (Hoftede’s CAT) quantifies cultural alignment of large language models using Hofstede’s cultural dimension framework.
Cultural Alignment Test provides explanatory cross-cultural comparison through latent variable analysis.
Cultural Alignment Test quantifies the cultural alignment of Llama 2, GPT‑3.5, and GPT‑4 for regions including the United States, China, and Arab countries.
GPT‑4 uniquely adapts to Chinese cultural nuances compared to other evaluated large language models.
GPT‑4 exhibits challenges aligning with American and Arab cultural values.
Fine‑tuning Llama 2 with different languages changes its responses to cultural questions.
Two novel datasets for evaluating conversational recommendation systems with out-of-the-box LLMs are introduced.
Increasing the number of user preference questions answered in a conversational recommendation setting with out-of-the-box LLMs leads to degraded recommendation quality.
An evaluation strategy is identified that reveals round-over-round performance loss missed by earlier benchmarks.
Preference elicitation and recommendation strategies are proposed that mitigate performance degradation and achieve state-of-the-art results.
GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet exhibit different effects on the proposed preference elicitation and recommendation strategies.
A comprehensive framework for systematic extraction, detection, and generation of research questions from scientific articles is presented.
The extraction component uses regular expressions to identify articles containing well-defined research questions.
The detection component identifies complex research questions that are not captured by rule-based extraction.
The generation component creates research questions for articles that lack them.
An integrated pipeline can extract research questions or generate them based on the full text of articles.
The pipeline reliably detects research questions and generates high-quality research questions as demonstrated by evaluation metrics.
Intent-Aware Prompting detects mental manipulation in conversations using large language models.
Intent-Aware Prompting captures underlying participant intents to provide a deeper understanding of manipulative tactics.
Intent-Aware Prompting achieves superior effectiveness compared to other advanced prompting strategies on the MentalManip dataset.
Intent-Aware Prompting reduces false negatives, enabling detection of more mental manipulation instances while minimally misjudging positive cases.

An extensive evaluation of 18 combinations of large language models (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, Mistral-7B) and prompting strategies (zero-shot, few-shot, with and without personas, with engagement feedback) for rewriting Dutch news tweets in formal, casual, and factual styles is conducted.
The evaluation measures ROUGE-L, semantic similarity, and predicted audience engagement of the rewritten tweets.
GPT-4 combined with engagement-feedback prompting and persona conditioning achieves the highest predicted audience engagement across formal, casual, and factual rewriting styles.
CmEAA connects a vision encoder with a frozen large language model for radiology report generation.
Cross-modal Feature Enhancement (CFE) extracts observation-related contextual features to enhance visual features of lesions and abnormal regions via a cross-modal enhancement transformer.
Neural Mutual Information Aligner (NMIA) maximizes neural mutual information between visual and textual representations within a low-dimensional alignment embedding space during training.
Neural Mutual Information Aligner (NMIA) provides potential global alignment visual representations during inference.
A weights generator enables dynamic adaptation of cross-modal enhanced features and vanilla visual features.
The proposed model outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods on the IU X-Ray and MIMIC-CXR datasets.
LOG discovers beneficial information for multi-hop question answering by optimizing retrieval from local to global.
LOG employs pointwise conditional v-information based local information modeling to cover usable documents with reasoning knowledge.
LOG improves tuplet objective loss to enable multi-examples-aware global optimization that models relationships between scattered documents.
LOG outperforms prior state-of-the-art models on multi-hop question answering benchmarks.
LOG significantly improves multi-hop reasoning, particularly for long-chain reasoning.
A multimodal empathetic response generation system incorporates user personality traits to produce responses.
The system analyzes multimodal inputs, including images and text, to infer user emotions and situational context.
The response generator models correlations among personality, emotion, and multimodal data to generate empathetic responses.
Experiments on the MELD and MEDIC datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the personality-aware multimodal approach.
KEEM is a generation-based dataset designed to enhance memory updates in long-term conversational systems.
KEEM dynamically generates integrative memories that preserve essential factual information while incorporating emotional context and causal relationships.
Updating system memory with the emotional and essential data from KEEM promotes deeper empathy and improves meaningful response generation in open-domain conversations.
PoemToPixel framework generates images that visually represent the inherent meanings of poems.
PoemToPixel incorporates prompt tuning to align generated images with poetic content.
PoeKey algorithm extracts emotions, visual elements, and themes from poems to create instructions for a diffusion model.
MiniPo is a multimodal dataset of 1001 children’s poems and images covering diverse genres and ages.
Quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of PoemToPixel for image generation from literary sources.
A novel approach enables instruction-guided image editing without any task-specific labeling, masking, or training.
Eliminating task-specific supervision provides better potential for improvement in instruction-guided image editing.
The approach achieves highly effective, very competitive performance on instruction-guided image editing.
Domain- and task-specific adaptive pre-training enhances the model’s ability to capture varying Chinese text difficulties.

Adaptive Task-guided Corpus Filtering (ATCF) selects training samples via embedding-based nearest-neighbor search and a sample balancing mechanism to ensure comprehensive learning across difficulty levels.
Interactive Attention-Driven Feature Fusion integrates linguistic features with deep representations through an interactive attention mechanism to provide rich difficulty information.
The combined method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a Chinese textbook readability assessment dataset.
Transfer learning experiments demonstrate that the method generalizes effectively to extracurricular reading and Chinese as a Foreign Language readability assessment tasks.
Instruction Fusion combines two distinct prompts through a hybridization process to enhance the evolution of training prompts for code generation LLMs.
Instruction Fusion addresses shortcomings of prior prompt evolution methods for code generation LLMs.
Instruction Fusion improves code generation performance of LLMs across five benchmarks (HumanEval, HumanEval+, MBPP, MBPP+, MultiPL-E).
The constraints of existing prompt evolution techniques for code generation LLMs are analyzed.
KG-TRICK is a sequence-to-sequence framework that jointly performs textual and relational information completion for multilingual knowledge graphs.
KG-TRICK demonstrates that unifying knowledge graph completion and knowledge graph enhancement into a single framework is feasible.
KG-TRICK demonstrates that incorporating textual information from multiple languages improves the completeness of multilingual knowledge graphs.
WikiKGE10++ is a manually-curated benchmark for textual information completion of knowledge graphs that spans ten languages and includes a large number of entities.
EICR detects sarcasm by performing commonsense-augmented incongruity reasoning.
Retrieval-augmented large language models are used to supplement missing commonsense background knowledge for sarcasm detection.
A dependency graph is constructed and its topology is optimized via graph refinement to capture complex contextual associations in sarcasm detection.
An adaptive reasoning skeleton integrates prior rules to explicitly extract sentiment-inconsistent subgraphs for sarcasm detection.
Adversarial contrastive learning is employed to eliminate spurious word‑label relations and enhance the robustness of the sarcasm detector.
EICR demonstrates effectiveness on five sarcasm detection datasets.
LLMs are trained to implicitly infer unspoken personalized preferences of a user through multi‑turn conversations and to dynamically align their responses to those inferred preferences.
A collection of 3,310 distinct user personas is constructed by creating seed examples and expanding them through iterative self‑generation and filtering.
A multi‑turn preference dataset containing over 3,000 conversations organized in tree structures is generated via multi‑LLM collaboration guided by the distinct user personas.
Supervised fine‑tuning and reinforcement learning are applied to the multi‑turn preference dataset to improve LLMs’ ability to perform personalized alignment.
The ALOE benchmark, comprising 100 carefully selected examples and dedicated metrics, evaluates customized alignment performance of LLMs during conversations.
Experimental results demonstrate that the trained LLMs achieve dynamic, personalized alignment through interaction.
Representation-based sparse upcycling converts dense language models into sparsely activated models with similar computational costs.
Representation-based sparse upcycling initializes router weights using intermediate representations from language models.
Initializing router weights with intermediate representations addresses the mismatch between randomly initialized and well‑trained parameters and guides expert specialization during training.
Representation-based sparse upcycling improves model capabilities compared to existing sparse upcycling methods.
Representation-based sparse upcycling enhances routing consistency compared to existing approaches.
CFSP performs structured pruning of large language models using both interblock and intrablock activation information as an importance criterion.
CFSP computes feature activations with a single forward pass to guide pruning.
CFSP allocates the sparsity budget across blocks based on block importance and retains important weights within each block.
CFSP includes a recovery fine-tuning strategy that adaptively allocates training overhead based on coarse-grained importance to improve performance.
CFSP outperforms existing pruning methods on diverse models across various sparsity budgets.
CoSTA is an end-to-end model architecture for code-switched speech translation that scaffolds pretrained automatic speech recognition and machine translation modules.
CoSTA fuses speech and ASR text representations using an aligned interleaving scheme before feeding them to a pretrained machine translation module.
CoSTA is trained end-to-end for spoken translation using synthetically created speech translation data.
A new evaluation benchmark for code-switched Bengali-English, Hindi-English, Marathi-English, and Telugu-English speech-to-English text is released.
CoSTA achieves higher translation quality than competitive cascaded and end-to-end multimodal baselines.
Rule-KBQA uses learned rules to guide generation of logical forms for knowledge base question answering.
The induction phase of Rule-KBQA extracts rules from existing data and employs a Rule-Following Fine-Tuned LLM to generate additional rules, forming a comprehensive rule library.
The deduction phase of Rule-KBQA employs a symbolic agent, guided by the learned rule library, to explore the knowledge base and incrementally construct executable logical forms.
LLMs are used in Rule-KBQA to evaluate the plausibility of candidate decisions during logical form construction.
Experiments demonstrate that Rule-KBQA attains competitive results on standard knowledge base question answering datasets.
LIMN introduces an inference module pretrained with plain QA articles as external knowledge to generate logical inference-aware representations for multiparty dialogues.
LIMN employs a key-utterance-based interaction method to model complex interactions among logical dialogue contexts, questions, and key-utterance information.
LIMN adopts a multitask learning strategy to improve robustness in machine reading comprehension.
LIMN achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Molweni and FriendsQA benchmarks for extractive question answering in multiparty dialogues.
AraTrust is a comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark for Arabic large language models.
AraTrust contains hundreds of human-written multiple-choice questions covering truthfulness, ethics, privacy, illegal activities, mental health, physical health, unfairness, and offensive language.
Evaluation of LLMs on AraTrust shows that GPT-4 attains the highest trustworthiness among the tested models.
Evaluation on AraTrust shows that open-source models such as AceGPT 7B and Jais 13B achieve low trustworthiness scores.
The AraTrust dataset is publicly released for use by the research community.
ME2-BERT is a holistic framework for fine-tuning BERT on moral foundation prediction.
ME2-BERT integrates events and emotions to learn domain-invariant morality-relevant text representations.
ME2-BERT outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods for moral foundation prediction, particularly in out-of-domain scenarios.
ALIS is an instruction tuning paradigm that decomposes user inputs into irreducible atomic instructions and organizes them into instruction streams to guide response generation.
ALIS treats user inputs as user-mode instructions and system prompts as kernel-mode instructions within a hierarchical structure.
ALIS enables a model to maintain security constraints by ignoring or rejecting user-mode instructions that conflict with kernel-mode instructions.
An automatic instruction generation method is developed for training ALIS.
An instruction decomposition task and associated datasets are introduced for training ALIS.
The ALIS framework with a small model improves resilience of large language models to attacks without loss of general capabilities.
Dual-granularity adversarial training models news pieces and their entities separately to generate domain-independent representations for cross-domain fake news detection.
A dual-granularity soft prompt consisting of two independent learnable segments trained on source domains facilitates adaptation of the fake news detector to unseen target domains.
New-MultiFC is a reconstructed, more domain-discriminative dataset for evaluating cross-domain fake news detection.
The proposed method attains state-of-the-art performance on unseen domains when evaluated on New-MultiFC and FND3.
A novel dataset of user-generated metadata for music entity detection is introduced.
A benchmark and robustness study of in-context learning with large language models for music entity detection is conducted.
Large language models using in-context learning achieve higher performance than smaller language models on music entity detection.
Entity exposure during pre-training has a large impact on the performance of the best-performing large language model in music entity detection.
PaSIG is a graph neural network that leverages pre-trained semantic interaction for text classification.
PaSIG constructs a text-word heterogeneity graph with an asymmetric structure that enforces one-way message passing from words to test texts.
PaSIG uses a pre-trained language model to generate node features that embed classification semantic information.
PaSIG employs adaptive aggregation with a gated fusion mechanism.
PaSIG incorporates subgraph sampling and intermediate state preservation to enable fast inference.
PaSIG achieves state-of-the-art performance on five text classification datasets, surpassing baseline accuracy.
LLMTreeRec structures all items into an item tree to improve the efficiency of large language model item retrieval.
LLMTreeRec models recommendation tasks as language analysis tasks and provides zero-shot recommendations using large language models' open-world knowledge.
LLMTreeRec achieves state-of-the-art performance under the system cold-start setting on two widely used datasets.
LLMTreeRec is competitive with conventional deep recommendation systems that rely on substantial training data.
LLMTreeRec outperforms a baseline model in an A/B test on a Huawei industrial system.
LLMTreeRec has been successfully deployed online as an industry-friendly solution.
AIDER is a robust and topic-independent framework for detecting AI-generated text.
AIDER leverages the ALBERT model to disentangle topic content, enhancing transferability to unseen topics.
AIDER incorporates an augmentor that generates adversarial data for training.
AIDER applies contrastive learning techniques to improve resilience against adversarial attacks.
AIDER demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art detectors while maintaining robustness with minimal performance degradation under adversarial attacks.
AIDER achieves high detection accuracy in non-augmented scenarios.
AIDER generalizes well to unseen topics.

A multi-stage parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach adds Persian to Llama by sequentially pretraining on monolingual Persian data, aligning representations through bilingual pretraining and instruction datasets, and instruction-tuning with task-specific datasets.
Evaluation of the extended Llama model at each stage on generation and classification tasks demonstrates the impact of each training phase.
Incorporating Persian via bilingual data alignment improves classification accuracy on Persian tasks without degrading performance on English tasks.
The initial strength of the base model is a critical factor when training data is limited, and cross-lingual alignment provides minimal benefit for low-resource languages.
Knowledge transfer from English to Persian yields marginal improvements, primarily for simple classification tasks.
Term definitions serve as a useful source of information for translating scientific neologisms.
In-context learning with co-hyponyms and terms sharing the same derivation paradigm improves translation performance of large language models for scientific neologisms.
Large language models exhibit sensitivity to superficial and morphological similarity between source and target terms in scientific neologism translation.
Subword tokenization influences predictions of large language models for prefixed scientific neologisms.
Fact-aware Multilingual Selective Synergy selects an optimal subset of languages based on language bias and transfer contributions for cross-lingual truthfulness transfer.
Fact-aware Multilingual Selective Synergy uses translation instruction tuning to transfer truthfulness across languages.
Fact-aware Multilingual Selective Synergy reduces multilingual representation disparity in large language models.
Fact-aware Multilingual Selective Synergy boosts cross-lingual truthfulness transfer in large language models.
Truthfulness evaluation is extended to multilingual contexts.

Narrative-Integrated Metapath Graph Auto-Encoder (NIMGA) dynamically reconstructs propagation structures via metapath-based heterogeneous graph reconstruction to capture complex interactions and hidden pathways in social networks.

Narrative-Integrated Metapath Graph Auto-Encoder (NIMGA) employs a dual-agent mechanism that uses large language models for viewpoint distillation and comment narrative reordering to refine diverse perspectives and semantic evolution.
Narrative-Integrated Metapath Graph Auto-Encoder (NIMGA) enhances rumor detection accuracy and robustness.
Narrative-Integrated Metapath Graph Auto-Encoder (NIMGA) reveals patterns of information propagation and latent semantic correlations among comments.
Narrative-Integrated Metapath Graph Auto-Encoder (NIMGA) outperforms existing rumor detection methods.
A speech-based visual question answering system is implemented.
The TM-VQA dataset provides speech-based questions in English, German, Spanish, and French paired with answers for a large collection of images.
A cross-attention-based unified multi-modal framework is proposed for evaluating speech-based visual question answering.
The unified cross-attention framework outperforms a cascaded framework for both text and speech-based visual question answering.
RUIE is a retrieval-based framework for unified information extraction that enables efficient task generalization via in-context learning.
RUIE introduces a demonstration selection mechanism that combines large language model preferences with a keyword-enhanced reward model.
RUIE employs a bi-encoder retriever trained through contrastive learning and knowledge distillation.
RUIE is the first trainable retrieval framework for unified information extraction and serves as a universal plugin for various large language models.
RUIE achieves higher average F1 scores than instruction-tuning methods and other retrievers on eight held-out datasets.
Frequencies extracted from carefully processed YouTube subtitles approximate spoken vocabulary comparably to, and often better than, existing resources such as film subtitle frequencies.
Frequency norms for Chinese, English, Indonesian, Japanese, and Spanish are constructed from YouTube subtitles.
The constructed YouTube-based frequency norms show strong correlation with lexical decision time, word familiarity, and lexical complexity.

A simple linear regression using the new YouTube-based frequencies achieves a new high score on lexical complexity prediction in English and Japanese, surpassing models trained on film subtitle frequencies and GPT-4.
Code, frequency lists, fastText word embeddings, and statistical language models derived from YouTube subtitles are publicly released.
A unified taxonomy for LLM-based agent frameworks is introduced.
The taxonomy defines environments and tasks for LLM-based agents.
The taxonomy defines common LLM-profiled roles, including policy models, evaluators, and dynamic models.
The taxonomy defines universally applicable workflows found in prior LLM-based agent work.
The taxonomy enables comparison of key perspectives on LMPR implementations and workflow usage across different agent paradigms.
Zero-shot and few-shot learning approaches are explored for claim matching.
Instruction-following large language models are evaluated on claim matching using various prompt templates.
ClaimMatch, a new claim matching dataset, is introduced.
A pipeline for claim matching is proposed and evaluated on texts of different lengths.
Claim matching can be tackled by leveraging natural language inference or paraphrase detection tasks.
A methodology creates nine Twitter datasets for evaluating the generative capabilities of Llama 3, Mistral, Qwen2, and GPT4o.
The Twitter datasets include four censored and five uncensored model configurations, covering 7B and 8B parameter base-instruction models of three open-source LLMs.

A data quality analysis compares textual outputs from humans, censored models, and uncensored models using semantic meaning, lexical richness, structural patterns, content characteristics, and detector performance metrics.
Uncensored models substantially reduce the effectiveness of automated detection methods for machine-generated text.
Domain adaptation and content moderation strategies affect both the detectability and structural characteristics of machine-generated tweets.
CE-DA introduces a Custom Embedding module that generates customized sentence embeddings using unsupervised contrastive learning and entity emphasis prompts.
CE-DA introduces a Dynamic Aggregation module that learns to weight different types of relation side information via a learnable network.
The Dynamic Aggregation module enhances the quality of relation prototype representations by strengthening interaction among side information types.
CE-DA achieves competitive performance on two zero-shot relation extraction datasets.
Time-Perception Enhanced Video Grounding via Boundary Perception and Temporal Reasoning mitigates discrepancies between video and text temporal sequences for large language models.
Boundary-perception tasks are designed to enable large language models to capture accurate video temporality.
Specialized tasks for boundary perception and temporal relationship reasoning deepen large language models' perception of video temporality.
The proposed method yields notable improvements on standard video grounding benchmarks such as ActivityNet, Charades, and DiDeMo.
Con-ReCall leverages asymmetric distributional shifts induced by member and non-member contexts through contrastive decoding to enhance membership inference.
Subtle distributional shifts caused by member contexts can be effectively leveraged for membership inference when contrasted with non-member contexts.
Con-ReCall achieves state-of-the-art performance on the WikiMIA benchmark.
Con-ReCall is robust against various text manipulation techniques.
HyperIDP is a hypergraph-based model that performs both macroscopic and microscopic information diffusion prediction.
HyperIDP captures macro-level cascade interactions and dynamics using hypergraph neural networks.
HyperIDP integrates social homophily information to predict the next user likely to be influenced in microscopic diffusion tasks.
HyperIDP employs a differentiable architecture search over a diffusion hypergraph search space to select optimal HGNN architectures.
HyperIDP introduces a cooperative-adversarial loss that enables shared representation across macro and micro diffusion tasks while mitigating task conflicts.
HyperIDP achieves superior performance compared to baseline methods on information diffusion prediction benchmarks.

A four-stage process consisting of monolingual data collection and preprocessing, continuous pre-training on extensive monolingual data, fine-tuning with limited parallel corpora using translation supervision, and direct preference optimization based on translation self-evolution (DPOSE) expands low-resource languages for large language models.
Direct preference optimization based on translation self-evolution (DPOSE) improves the translation ability of large language models.
The described strategy expands the set of low-resource languages supported by large language models.
The described strategy enhances translation ability for Uyghur using limited parallel data.
The study provides detailed guidance for expanding other low-resource languages into large language models.
Dr.ECI is a multi-agent decomposed reasoning framework for event causality identification.
Dr.ECI incorporates specialized agents named Causal Explorer and Mediator Detector that capture implicit causality and indirect causality more effectively.
Dr.ECI includes agents Direct Reasoner and Indirect Reasoner that leverage knowledge of a generalized causal structure specific to event causality identification.
Evaluations show that Dr.ECI achieves state-of-the-art performance compared with baselines based on large language models and supervised training.
Large language models exhibit inadequate causal reasoning capability when evaluated on the event causality identification task.
A novel Chinese nursing dataset is introduced for training large language models in nursing and elderly care.
Incremental pre-training and supervised fine-tuning techniques are applied to improve large language model performance on specialized nursing tasks.
An interactable nursing assistant built with LangChain provides real-time care and personalized interventions.
The combined pre-training, fine-tuning, and nursing assistant demonstrate improved performance of large language models for nursing and elderly care tasks.
RGER selects exemplars using a graph kernel that considers both semantic and structural similarity.
RGER generates an initial response from a large language model.
RGER represents intermediate problem-solving steps as a graph structure.
Structural relationships between queries and candidate exemplars improve alignment in in-context learning.
RGER outperforms state-of-the-art retrieval-based approaches on mathematics and logical reasoning tasks.
AgriCLIP is a vision-language foundational model dedicated to agriculture and livestock.
ALive is a large-scale image-text dataset for crops, livestock, and fishery created using a customized prompt generation strategy.
A training pipeline that integrates contrastive and self-supervised learning is used to learn both global semantic and local fine-grained domain-specialized features in AgriCLIP.
AgriCLIP achieves higher average zero-shot classification accuracy than standard CLIP adaptation on a diverse set of downstream tasks.
CUTE provides parallel and non-parallel corpora for Chinese, Uyghur, Tibetan, and English.
Human assessment indicates that machine translation quality for Chinese-Uyghur and Chinese-Tibetan approaches that of Chinese-English translation.
CUTE is the largest publicly available corpus for Uyghur and Tibetan languages.
CUTE enhances large language models' ability to process low-resource languages.
CUTE enables investigation of the impact of corpus parallelism on cross-lingual transfer learning.
NesTools is a dataset for evaluating nested tool learning abilities of large language models.
NesTools includes a novel automatic data generation method that constructs large-scale nested tool calls with varied nesting structures.
NesTools contains manually reviewed and refined instances that are high quality and closely aligned with real-world scenarios.
NesTools serves as a benchmark for assessing nested tool learning capabilities of large language models.
Experiments on multiple large language models using NesTools reveal that current models still struggle with complex nested tool learning tasks.
Disentangle to Decay (D2D) is a positional encoding for linear attention that disentangles the decay factor into two parts.
Disentangle to Decay (D2D) enables stable training of the decay factor in linear attention.
Disentangle to Decay (D2D) can be transformed into a recurrent form for efficient inference.
Disentangle to Decay (D2D) enhances performance of linear attention for normal context lengths and for length extrapolation scenarios.
A benchmark dataset of numerical error detection with automatically generated errors is created.
Numerical errors are classified based on their properties.
Model abilities are investigated across error class, error size, and passage domain.
GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and Llama-3-Instruct achieve strong performance on numerical error detection but remain less accurate than humans.
LLMs misidentify correct numbers as errors more frequently than humans.
Current LLMs struggle with detecting numerical errors that require calculations or extensive prior knowledge.

Language-sensitive intervention (LSI) is a lightweight, label-free approach that intervenes on a language vector in the residual stream of a large language model during inference to generate responses in a specified language.
Causal analysis shows that the language distribution of the training corpus acts as a confounder that biases multilingual language models against underrepresented languages.

A language-sensitive dimension, called the language vector, is identified in the residual stream of large language models, enabling estimation of the average causal effect of prompts on language generation.
A benchmark for detecting language confusion and assessing content quality is introduced.
Experimental evaluation demonstrates that intervening on the language vector mitigates language confusion without requiring additional complex mechanisms.
Two new Persian evaluation sets for simile and idiom interpretation were built, extending existing multilingual datasets.
Large language models were evaluated on simile and idiom interpretation using two multilingual datasets across multiple languages.
The effectiveness of chain-of-thought, few-shot, and English translation prompts for figurative language interpretation was assessed.
Closed-source models (GPT-3.5, GPT-4o mini, Gemini 1.5) and open-source models (Llama 3.1, Qwen2) exhibit significant performance differences across languages and figurative types.
Open-source models perform poorly on simile interpretation in low-resource languages.
Idiom interpretation performance is approaching saturation for many languages, indicating a need for more challenging evaluations.
ConCoder is a contrastive learning-based code generation model that incorporates execution feedback.
ConCoder enables generation of accurate code on the first attempt while also rectifying and optimizing incorrect code.
ConCoder's training emphasizes learning from error causes to understand and avoid mistakes.
Experiments demonstrate that ConCoder improves generation of accurate code and error correction capabilities.
An in-context reinforcement learning framework refines synthetic question generation for Text-to-SQL, enabling the production of more intricate queries.
The framework generates diverse and complex synthetic SQL queries for training Text-to-SQL models.
The framework integrates retrieval-augmented generation with in-context learning to address schema identification in Text-to-SQL.
Experiments demonstrate that the framework achieves substantially higher recall in database and table retrieval compared to state-of-the-art models.
Experiments demonstrate that the framework attains higher execution accuracy for SQL generation compared to state-of-the-art models.
GAProtoNet is a white-box multi-head graph attention-based prototypical network for interpretable text classification using language model encoders.

GAProtoNet treats input vectors and prototypes as graph nodes and applies multi-head graph attention to construct edges between the input node and prototype nodes, learning an interpretable prototypical representation.
During inference, GAProtoNet makes classification decisions via a linear combination of activated prototypes weighted by attention scores, enabling transparent explanation of predictions.
GAProtoNet achieves superior classification performance compared to original black-box language models while preserving accuracy.
GAProtoNet outperforms four alternative prototypical network variations in accuracy and F1.
Case studies and prototype cluster visualizations demonstrate GAProtoNet's efficiency in explaining decisions of black-box language models.
Self-Evolution KD integrates teacher distribution and ground-truth one-hot distribution into the student model's distribution as prior knowledge.
Self-Evolution KD dynamically adjusts the weighting of prior knowledge according to token learning difficulty.
Self-Evolution KD enhances knowledge transfer from teacher to student models in machine translation.
Self-Evolution KD yields higher translation quality as measured by SacreBLEU across multiple language directions.
A black-box evaluation approach enables assessment of abstention ability in large language models without requiring token prediction probabilities.
The Abstain-QA dataset evaluates abstention ability across answerable and unanswerable questions, well-represented and under-represented domains, and fact-centric and reasoning tasks.
The Answerable-Unanswerable Confusion Matrix (AUCM) provides a structured metric for evaluating abstention ability.
Strict prompting improves abstention ability in large language models.
Chain-of-Thought prompting improves abstention ability in large language models.
A comprehensive survey of Chain-of-X methods for large language models is presented.
Chain-of-X methods are categorized by the type of node (the X) and by application tasks.
Findings and implications of existing Chain-of-X methods are analyzed.
A tuning-free multi-agent collaboration framework normalizes biomedical terminology in social media using large language models.

A retrieval-augmented generation knowledge card module expands short biomedical mentions and terms into accurate, harmonized, and informative descriptions using a search engine and a domain knowledge base.
LLM agents perform recall, ranking, and re‑ranking processes for biomedical terminology normalization.
The framework achieves competitive performance across multiple datasets.
Self-Consistency of LLM evaluators is evaluated across different scoring scales and criterion granularity using open-source and proprietary models.
Inter-scale Consistency of LLM evaluators is evaluated across different scoring scales and criterion granularity using open-source and proprietary models.
Strong proprietary models are shown to be not necessarily consistent evaluators.
CDQA is a Chinese Dynamic QA benchmark containing question-answer pairs about the latest news on the Chinese Internet.
CDQA data are collected using a pipeline that combines humans and models.
CDQA samples are classified by the frequency of answer changes to enable fine-grained observation of LLM capabilities.
Mainstream and advanced Chinese LLMs are evaluated on CDQA.
A dataset of over 1,600 sentences from three foundational International Relations articles annotated for argumentation and domain content types is released.
An annotation scheme for labeling argumentation and domain content types in scholarly International Relations articles is introduced.
A discourse analysis of argumentation and domain content types in scholarly International Relations articles is conducted.
State-of-the-art classifiers achieve high performance in distinguishing basic argumentative elements such as claims and support/attack relations.
Domain-specific training and fine-tuning are required for state-of-the-art classifiers to accurately predict fine-grained relation and content type labels.
The annotation scheme provides educationally relevant insight into scholarly International Relations discourse.
A collaborative reasoning framework combines reinforcement learning and large language models to answer complex knowledge graph questions.
The framework leverages large language models for common-sense priors while using reinforcement learning to learn from the environment.

A hierarchical agent is employed, where a high-level component identifies reasoning constraints and a low-level component selects promising relations for efficient path reasoning in the knowledge graph.
Experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that the framework surpasses state-of-the-art approaches for complex knowledge graph question answering.
The recovery of valid words from BPE tokens is interpreted as a ranking problem.
A model-agnostic, training-free recovery method maps topic-token distributions onto a selected vocabulary space.
Existing topic coherence evaluation measures can be applied to topics recovered from BPE vocabulary space.
Topic sets recovered from BPE vocabulary space are coherent.
CodeJudge-Eval is a benchmark that assesses large language models' code understanding by judging the correctness of provided code solutions.
CodeJudge-Eval challenges models with various error types and compilation issues.
CodeJudge-Eval uses a diverse set of problems and a fine-grained judging system to address limitations of traditional language-to-code benchmarks, including solution memorization.
Evaluation of multiple well-known large language models on CodeJudge-Eval reveals that even state-of-the-art models struggle with code understanding.

LeNS-Align is a cross-lingual mapping technique that improves word alignment quality for parallel English–low-resource language text by integrating lexical alignment, named entity recognition, and semantic alignment.
LeNS-Align enables cross-lingual projection of knowledge-base triples from English to low-resource languages.
Projected knowledge-base triples are used to augment multilingual language models with graph neural network embeddings of the projected knowledge for question answering.
Comprehensive low-resource language knowledge bases for four South African languages are constructed by projecting triples from ConceptNet and DBpedia.
The combined approach increases zero-shot question answering accuracy for low-resource languages relative to baselines without knowledge-base access.
MoKA combines Kronecker product with Mixture-of-Experts for parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
MoKA replaces low-rank decomposition of weight update matrices with Kronecker products.
MoKA utilizes a sparse Mixture-of-Experts architecture.
MoKA includes an efficient routing module that further compresses parameter size.
MoKA outperforms existing parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods on multiple benchmarks including GLUE, E2E NLG Challenge, and instruction tuning tasks.
A black-box zero-shot detection approach calculates a Grammar Error Correction Score to differentiate human-written from LLM-generated text.
The approach outperforms current state-of-the-art zero-shot and supervised detection methods.
The approach achieves high AUROC across XSum and Writing Prompts datasets.
The approach demonstrates strong reliability in the wild, showing robust generalization and resistance to paraphrasing attacks.
Human-written texts typically contain more grammatical errors than LLM-generated texts, as observed from the perspective of LLMs.
An automated, scalable pipeline creates false premise questions from knowledge graphs by modifying true KG triplets and using GPTs to generate semantically rich questions.
The KG-FPQ benchmark provides a large collection of false premise questions spanning three knowledge domains, six confusability levels, and two task formats.
Evaluations using KG-FPQ reveal factuality hallucination behavior across several representative large language models.
VoxpopuliTTS provides a large-scale multilingual text-to-speech dataset with 30,000 hours of high-quality speech across three languages, multiple speakers, and styles, suitable for TTS and ASR tasks.
The dataset is created by refining the Voxpopuli corpus through an improved processing pipeline.
The processing pipeline filters out low-quality speech-text pairs using automatic speech recognition confidence scores.
The processing pipeline concatenates short transcripts by checking semantic information completeness to generate longer transcripts.
Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the VoxpopuliTTS dataset and the enhanced processing pipeline are effective for speech generation and recognition tasks.
ZoomDIT is an end-to-end network that jointly performs word positioning, sentence recognition and translation, and document organization for document image translation.
ZoomDIT employs a fine-to-coarse zoom-out framework to progressively transform chaotic OCR words into a coherent translated document.
A new large-scale document image translation dataset with multi-level fine-grained labels is introduced.
Experiments on public benchmarks and the new dataset demonstrate that ZoomDIT improves translation quality for complex-layout document images.
Decompose-ToM recursively simulates user perspectives and decomposes Theory of Mind tasks into subject identification, question-reframing, world model updating, and knowledge availability subtasks.
Decompose-ToM yields significant performance improvements on higher-order Theory of Mind tasks across multiple models compared to baseline methods.
Decompose-ToM yields significant performance improvements on a conversational Theory of Mind task compared to baseline methods.
Decompose-ToM achieves performance gains without additional model training.
Decompose-ToM requires only minimal prompt tuning across tasks.
AI Hospital provides a multi-agent simulation of doctor-patient-examiner interactions for evaluating large language models.
Multi-View Medical Evaluation benchmark measures LLM-driven Doctor agents on symptom collection, examination recommendation, and diagnosis using Chinese medical records.
Dispute resolution collaborative mechanism facilitates iterative discussion to improve medical interaction capabilities of LLM agents.
Large language models, including GPT-4, exhibit substantial performance deficits in multi-turn interactive medical scenarios relative to static question answering settings.
AI Hospital enables practical assessment of LLMs in simulated clinical scenarios.
CTGL framework combines text-learning and graph-learning models in parallel to capture both text attributes and graph structure.
CTGL introduces coupled text-graph augmentation that enables coupled contrastive learning and information exchange between text and graph modalities.
CTGL demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art text-learning and graph-learning baselines on diverse datasets.
News-triggered opinion expressing timing identification is introduced as a new task.
A novel dataset of professional stock analyst actions for news-triggered opinion expressing timing is created.
Generator-Assisted Decision-Focused Approach (GADFA) leverages text generation models to steer a classification model for opinion timing identification.
GADFA enhances overall performance on the opinion timing identification task.
Text generated by GADFA provides fresh insights from various angles that aid in identifying the optimal timing for opinion expression.
LogiGraph employs lightweight graph networks for logical reasoning in machine reading comprehension.
LogiGraph constructs a dual graph using conjunction and punctuation marks as edge types.
LogiGraph integrates contrastive learning with graph reasoning by using altered logical expressions as negative samples to capture negative logical relationships.
LogiGraph achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ReClor dataset.
LogiGraph achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LogiQA dataset.
A high-quality human-generated cross-lingual parallel dataset for Luxembourgish is compiled.
LuxEmbedder provides enhanced Luxembourgish sentence embeddings with strong cross-lingual capabilities.
Including low-resource languages in parallel training datasets benefits other low-resource languages more than using only high-resource language pairs.
A paraphrase detection benchmark for Luxembourgish is created.
Output embedding vectors encode output token probabilities in an approximate common log-linear manner.
The log-linear encoding of output token probabilities in output embeddings is both accurate and sparse.
Manipulating the encoding in output embeddings can accurately modify the output probability distribution.
A substantial proportion of output embedding dimensions can be removed without significantly affecting output distribution or sequence generation.
Output embeddings capture corpus token frequency information during early pretraining steps before parameter convergence.
FG-Bench evaluates video OCR performance of multi-modal models in videos.
FG-Bench comprises 1,028 videos and 2,961 question-answer pairs.
FG-Bench defines six sub-tasks covering text recognition, visual attribute extraction, semantic and spatial comprehension of OCR objects, dynamic motion detection, and temporal localization.
FG-Bench was constructed using a semi-automated pipeline that integrates OCR capabilities of image LLMs with manual refinement to balance efficiency, cost, and data quality.
Neuron activation patterns in multilingual LLMs exhibit similarity when processing the same language, revealing key linguistic regions.
Neuron activation patterns in multilingual LLMs are similar for sentences with the same semantic meaning across different languages, indicating a common semantic latent space.
Semantic alignment across languages in multilingual LLMs becomes more pronounced with increased training and model size, leading to more language-agnostic activation patterns.
Key linguistic neurons in multilingual LLMs are concentrated in the first and last layers, with increasing density in the first layers as training progresses.
Experiments on BLOOM and LLaMA2 confirm the identified structural evolution of multilingual LLMs during training and scaling.
FarExStance is a dataset for explainable stance detection in Farsi that provides a claim, a stance label, and an extractive explanation for each instance.
A fine-tuned multilingual RoBERTa model achieves the highest stance detection accuracy among evaluated models on FarExStance.
Aya-23-8B fine-tuned with parameter-efficient tuning attains high stance detection accuracy on FarExStance.
Few-shot Claude-3.5-Sonnet attains high stance detection accuracy on FarExStance.
Few-shot GPT-4o generates the most coherent explanations according to automatic evaluation metrics on FarExStance.
Few-shot Claude-3.5-Sonnet receives the highest Overall Explanation Score in human evaluation on FarExStance.
A fine-tuned Aya-32-8B model produces explanations that most closely align with reference explanations on FarExStance.
