An evaluation framework for interactive dialogue assessment of English as a Second Language conversations is introduced.
The framework collects dialogue-level interactivity labels and micro-level span features.
Machine learning models are constructed to analyze how micro-level features influence interactivity quality in ESL dialogues.

Reference words and other micro-level features are found to strongly correlate with interactivity quality, revealing new insights into the relationship between higher-level dialogue quality and lower-level linguistic signals.
The framework provides a means to assess ESL communication for language assessment.
Case2Code is an inductive inference task that infers code implementations from input-output examples or program behaviors.

A data synthesis pipeline uses LLMs to generate program inputs, executes the programs to obtain outputs, and creates diverse high-quality Case2Code data at scale for training and evaluating code LLMs.
Current representative LLMs find case-to-code induction challenging when they are untrained.
Training code LLMs with Case2Code data improves performance on case-to-code induction tasks.
Training code LLMs with Case2Code data improves performance on various code generation tasks.
Fine-grained alignment between LLM evaluators and human annotators is examined across conventional and alignment NLP tasks.
LLM evaluators sometimes generate unnecessary evaluation criteria or omit crucial criteria, leading to slight deviation from expert judgments.
LLM evaluators excel at assessing general criteria such as fluency.
LLM evaluators face challenges when evaluating complex criteria such as numerical reasoning.
Pre-drafting evaluations with LLMs before human assessment reduces human subjectivity and minimizes annotation outliers, yielding more objective evaluations.
FineRAG is a fine-grained model for retrieval-augmented text-to-image generation.
FineRAG decomposes the retrieval-augmented image generation task into four stages: query decomposition, candidate selection, retrieval-augmented diffusion, and self-reflection.
FineRAG reduces noise associated with retrieval-augmented image generation.
FineRAG performs better in complex, open-world scenarios.
Chain-of-Discussion framework leverages synergy among multiple open-source LLMs to produce more correct and comprehensive answers for open-ended question answering.
Discussions among multiple LLMs improve the quality of answers in open-ended question answering.
A context filtering approach removes non-essential details and summarizes crucial content using reward modeling for question answering.
A framework enables development of efficient question answering models by discerning useful information from dataset pairs without requiring human evaluation.
The context filtering approach significantly outperforms baseline question answering performance in token efficiency.
EM Per Token (EPT) is introduced as a metric for measuring token efficiency in question answering.
FtG introduces a filter-then-generate paradigm for knowledge graph completion.
FtG formulates knowledge graph completion as a multiple-choice question format.
FtG employs a flexible ego-graph serialization prompt to incorporate graph structure.
FtG uses a structure-text adapter to couple structural and textual information in a contextualized manner.
FtG achieves substantial performance gains over existing state-of-the-art methods on knowledge graph completion.
Bias within large language models and its impact on media bias detection are investigated.
Bias in large language models is examined for political bias prediction tasks.
Bias in large language models is examined for text continuation tasks.
Bias across diverse topics is explored to uncover nuanced variations in bias expression within large language models.
Prompt engineering and model fine-tuning are proposed as debiasing strategies for large language model–based bias detection.
Bias tendencies across different large language models are extensively analyzed to illuminate bias propagation in language models.
Analysis of voice synthesis in English and Polish corpora reveals prediction differences in speaker verification systems.
Biometric similarity between synthetic and target voices is shown to affect the success of speaker impersonation attacks.
Both Text-to-Speech and Voice Conversion synthetic voices can preserve personal traits sufficient to compromise speaker verification systems.
Comparative evaluation indicates that Text-to-Speech and Voice Conversion paradigms differ in their impact on speaker verification vulnerability.
B2NERD is a compact dataset that guides large language models' generalization in open named entity recognition under a universal entity taxonomy.
B2NERD constructs a universal taxonomy of over 400 entity types by detecting inconsistent entity definitions across datasets and clarifying them with distinguishable label names.
B2NERD reduces redundancy through a data pruning strategy that selects fewer samples with greater category and semantic diversity.
B2NER models trained on B2NERD achieve higher F1 scores than GPT-4 on open named entity recognition tasks.
B2NER models trained on B2NERD surpass previous methods on out-of-domain benchmarks across multiple datasets and languages.
Large language models can enrich excerpts from small-group recorded conversations with socially relevant context.
Effective contextualization approaches improve comprehension of excerpts from small-group recorded conversations.
Effective contextualization approaches improve readability of excerpts from small-group recorded conversations.
Effective contextualization approaches improve empathy conveyed by excerpts from small-group recorded conversations.
Subjective and objective evaluations demonstrate significant improvements in understanding of context-enriched excerpts.
The Human-annotated Salient Excerpts (HSE) dataset provides human-annotated salient excerpts for research.
Context-enriched excerpts enable more focused and comprehensive conversation summaries.
Lightweight large language model generation is used to produce target-domain phrase-structure subtrees guided by grammar rules and lexical head information.
Tree hybridization merges LLM-generated target-domain subtrees with source-domain subtrees to create structurally diverse training instances.
The data augmentation method improves cross-domain constituency parsing performance on multiple target domains.
The data augmentation method incurs a low generation cost for the lightweight large language model.
Trait-Aware Mix-of-Experts Representation Learning provides a multi-trait essay scoring method.
The method obtains trait-specific essay representations via a Mix-of-Experts scoring architecture.
A diversified trait-expert component learns distinguishable expert weights within the scoring architecture.
Two trait correlation learning strategies enable learning of correlations among traits for multi-trait scoring.
The method achieves improved computational efficiency compared to existing essay scoring approaches.
BinarySelect combines binary search with attack selection methods to reduce the number of queries needed to find a token for black-box text attacks.
BinarySelect requires approximately log2(n) × 2 queries to locate the first token, compared to n queries in standard token‑removal attacks.
BinarySelect achieves a substantial reduction in query count while maintaining comparable attack effectiveness across multiple classifiers and datasets.
BinarySelect demonstrates a viable tradeoff between query savings and attack effectiveness in experiments on several classifiers and datasets.
RagID combines retrieval-augmented generation with an iterative discriminator for few-shot Chinese spelling checking.
RagID uses semantic-based similarity search to select prompt samples for LLM-based Chinese spelling checking.
RagID’s iterative discriminator mechanism reduces over-correction in LLM-based Chinese spelling checking.
RagID achieves state-of-the-art performance on multi-domain Chinese spelling checking datasets and on datasets containing modal conversion spelling errors in few-shot scenarios.
InstructGEC integrates grammatical detection knowledge into grammatical error correction models via instruction tuning.
An instruction format with a consistent masking strategy applied to both erroneous sentences and their instructions mitigates the copy phenomenon in synthetic GEC data.
InstructGEC outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised grammatical error correction methods on English benchmarks.
InstructGEC outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised grammatical error correction methods on Chinese benchmarks.
PrahokBART is a compact pre-trained sequence-to-sequence model for Khmer trained from scratch on curated Khmer and English corpora.
PrahokBART incorporates linguistic components such as word segmentation and normalization to address Khmer linguistic issues.
PrahokBART outperforms mBART50 on Khmer machine translation, text summarization, and headline generation tasks.
Analysis of PrahokBART reveals the impact of each linguistic module on performance.
PrahokBART effectively handles spacing during Khmer text generation, contributing to naturalness.
Entity-driven answer set expansion expands the gold answer set to include diverse surface forms according to entity type patterns.
Soft exact match with entity-driven answer set expansion is used for evaluating QA models.
The method outperforms traditional QA evaluation methods.
The method achieves reliability comparable to LLM-based evaluation methods.
The method provides higher interpretability and lower environmental impact than LLM-based evaluation methods.
RLEE introduces a two-layer representation of temporal knowledge graphs comprising an entity layer and a relation layer.
RLEE extracts relation paths to mine logical correlations between relations and learns relation embeddings through relation logical reasoning.
RLEE employs a relation-aware attention mechanism to learn entity embeddings specific to predicted query relations.
RLEE uses the learned relation and entity embeddings to predict future facts in temporal knowledge graphs.
RLEE achieves superior performance compared to prior baselines on five commonly used public datasets.

Open-weight (LLaMA-3) and closed-source (GPT-4) large language models are compared with fine-tuned encoder-decoder models on Bengali translation, summarization, paraphrasing, question answering, and natural language inference tasks.
Large language models generally excel in reasoning tasks but exhibit inconsistent performance on tasks that require generation of Bengali script.
Existing large language models inefficiently tokenize Bengali script, leading to increased computational costs and potential performance degradation.
Machine-translated datasets commonly used for Bengali NLP tasks contain notable biases.

A significant need for a Bengali-oriented large language model is identified, while the field currently lacks high-quality pretraining and instruction-tuning datasets required for effective model development.
A method models uncertainty in under-represented languages using deep Bayesian Gaussian Processes.
The framework integrates prior knowledge and leverages kernel functions.
The framework enables quantification of uncertainty in predictions for under-represented languages.
Experiments validate the approach and benchmark it against existing methods, showing enhanced prediction accuracy.
Experiments benchmark the approach against existing methods, showing improved measurement of uncertainty.
Comet is a dialog context fusion mechanism for end-to-end task-oriented dialog.
Comet uses dialog summarization to achieve a more comprehensive understanding of important dialog context.
Comet employs domain prediction to focus on domain-specific information and reduce interference from irrelevant information.
Comet incorporates slot detection to accurately identify essential dialog context information.
Comet includes a data refinement strategy that enhances the comprehensiveness and recommendability of generated responses.
Comet achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MultiWOZ and CrossWOZ datasets.
ImPoster generates a target image of a source subject performing a driving action using a single source image, a driving image, and their text descriptions.
ImPoster operates without any additional annotations such as keypoints or pose.
ImPoster fine‑tunes a pretrained text‑to‑image latent diffusion model for a small number of iterations to learn characteristics of the source and driving images.
ImPoster performs step‑wise text prompting at inference, denoising first toward the driving image manifold and then toward the text description manifold.

ImPoster introduces image frequency guidance, a diffusion guidance formulation derived from frequency‑domain properties, to steer generation toward the source subject and driving action manifolds at each denoising step.
ImPoster demonstrates improvements over baseline methods on a diverse set of source‑driving image pairs.
ImPoster is the first approach to achieve both subject‑driven and action‑driven image personalization.
Compressive Memory-based Retrieval (CMR) introduces a dynamic matrix that caches retrieved information and supports continuous updates for Event Argument Extraction.
CMR overcomes input length constraints in retrieval-augmented Event Argument Extraction by storing information in a compressive memory.
CMR retrieves and filters relevant information from the compressive memory based on the input query, bridging the gap between the retriever and the inference model.
CMR achieves state-of-the-art performance on three public Event Argument Extraction datasets, surpassing existing retrieval-based methods.

CounterFactual Multi-Agent Debate (CFMAD) framework presets the stances of LLMs to override their inherent biases by compelling them to generate justifications for a predetermined answer’s correctness.
In CFMAD, LLMs with different predetermined stances are engaged with a skeptical critic for counterfactual debate on the rationality of generated justifications.
A third-party judge evaluates the debate process in CFMAD to determine the final answer.
CFMAD demonstrates superiority over existing methods for hallucination elimination on four datasets spanning three tasks.
CPsyExam is a benchmark that evaluates psychological knowledge and case analysis capabilities of large language models.
CPsyExam is constructed from Chinese examination questions covering a wide range of psychology subjects and multiple exam systems.
CPsyExam provides balanced subject coverage and incorporates diverse case analysis techniques.
CPsyExam enables comparison of large language models across various granularities of psychological understanding.
Experiments demonstrate that CPsyExam is an effective benchmark for assessing and improving large language models' understanding of psychology.
A dataset of approximately 240 million papers spanning 1980–2023 across 20 fields is compiled for citation analysis.
Citation age trends across 20 fields over 43 years are analyzed, revealing that many fields have markedly declined in citing older works.
NLP and machine learning research exhibit the strongest decline in citation age among the studied fields.
Controlling for the increase in publication volume shows that the shift toward citing more recent works is not directly driven by growth in publication rates.
A publicly available demo showcases the citation analysis results.
LLM-Personalize personalizes large language model planners for household robotics.
LLM-Personalize performs iterative planning in multi-room partially-observable household environments using a dynamically built scene graph from local observations.
LLM-Personalize integrates imitation learning and reinforced self-training to align planner behavior with user preferences.
LLM-Personalize achieves higher success rates on the Housekeep benchmark than existing large language model planners, demonstrating improved alignment with human preferences.
A multi-agent framework with specialized agents is introduced for generating analytical reports from earnings calls.
The specialized agents inject diverse viewpoints and desired analysis topics into the report generation process.
Adding additional agents to the framework produces reports that are more insightful than those generated with fewer agents.
Human experts' reports are preferred over generated reports in the majority of cases.
Large language models are evaluated as assessors of report quality, demonstrating a significant correlation with human expert evaluations across multiple dimensions.
Model uncertainty metrics exhibit weak correlations with student response distributions for multiple-choice questions.
Model behavior differs between correct and incorrect answers when using generative large models for multiple-choice questions.
Correlation between model uncertainty and student response distribution varies substantially across different question types.
A fine-grained dataset of 451 multiple-choice questions from a Biopsychology course is introduced.
Model uncertainty is investigated as a proxy for multiple-choice item difficulty.
GIFR is a graph interaction framework for multimodal named entity recognition with multiple images.
Reinforcement learning based on human preference is used to model human ability to determine relevance of image-text pairs for multimodal named entity recognition.
A heterogeneous graph is constructed and processed with a graph transformer to enable information interaction in multimodal named entity recognition.
The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark multimodal named entity recognition datasets.
Methodologies for incorporating domain-specific chemistry knowledge and multi-modal information into large language models are outlined.
Chemistry large language models are conceptualized as agents that use chemistry tools.
Existing benchmarks for evaluating the chemistry ability of large language models are consolidated.
Current challenges in chemistry-oriented large language models are critically examined.
Promising future research directions for chemistry large language models are identified.
LMCrowd is a hierarchical framework that leverages multiple LLMs for efficient data labeling under budget constraints.
LMCrowd aggregates labels from multiple freely available LLMs before employing a paid MaaS LLM to relabel selected instances.
LMCrowd formulates data labeling as an optimization problem that selects the optimal set of instances for relabeling given the current belief state.
LMCrowd outperforms human labelers in accuracy and efficiency.
LMCrowd outperforms GPT-4 in accuracy and efficiency.
An annotated dataset of euphemistic and literal references to death in Danish and Norwegian novels from the late 19th century is introduced.
State-of-the-art pre-trained language models fine-tuned for euphemism detection are evaluated on the death euphemism dataset.
Fixed literal expressions of death became less frequent over time while metaphorical euphemisms grew in prevalence.
Euphemistic language is more common in historical novels than in contemporary novels, reflecting a shift toward more literal references in contemporary works.
ITERATE retrieves and optimizes images to improve alignment between text and images for question-answering tasks.
ITERATE employs a multi-generational evolutionary process with filtering, optimizing, and retrieving actions to acquire higher-quality image clusters.
ITERATE leverages large language models and multimodal models to enhance visual modality for pure text-based question answering.
ITERATE demonstrates performance improvements on the ScienceQA, ARC-Easy, and OpenDataEval datasets.
BiLD loss filters out long-tail noise by utilizing only the top teacher and student logits.
BiLD loss leverages internal logits ranking information by constructing logits differences.
Fine-tuned large language model logits exhibit a more extreme long-tail distribution than vision model logits, and hidden noise in the long tail harms distillation performance.
BiLD loss outperforms supervised fine-tuning, vanilla KL loss, and several other distillation methods for large language model distillation.
Comprehensive experiments on multiple datasets with two types of large language models demonstrate the effectiveness of BiLD loss.
Multi-Graph Co-Training model (MGCOT) integrates the current session graph, similar session graphs, and a global item relation graph to capture user intent in session-based recommendation.
MGCOT applies multi-head attention mechanisms to capture relevant session intent.
MGCOT utilizes contrastive learning to produce accurate and robust session representations.
MGCOT achieves significant performance improvements on session-based recommendation benchmarks, including notable gains on the Diginetica dataset.
Identifying manipulation of news on social media detects manipulation in social media posts and identifies manipulated or inserted information.
A data collection schema is introduced for assembling tweet-article pairs for the manipulation detection task.
ManiTweet consists of several thousand pairs of tweets and corresponding articles for the manipulation detection task.
A basic model outperforms large language models on the ManiTweet dataset.
Exploratory analysis of human-written tweets shows a connection between manipulation and the domain and factuality of news articles.
Exploratory analysis shows manipulated sentences are more likely to encapsulate the main story or consequences of a news outlet.
A method determines whether a large language model possesses knowledge about a query by inspecting its contextualized pre-trained token embeddings.
The method enables adaptive retrieval-augmented generation that retrieves external information only when the model lacks the required knowledge.
The method avoids accessing the pre-training corpus or prompting with additional model inferences.
The method achieves superior performance across various benchmarks compared to prior adaptive retrieval-augmented generation approaches.
RichRAG is a retrieval-augmented generation framework that produces rich, long-form answers covering multiple aspects of broad, open-ended queries.
RichRAG includes a sub-aspect explorer that identifies potential sub-aspects of input questions.
RichRAG includes a multi-faceted retriever that builds a candidate pool of diverse external documents related to identified sub-aspects.

RichRAG includes a generative list-wise ranker that selects the top-k most valuable documents for the generator, ensuring coverage of various query aspects and awareness of the generator’s preferences.
The ranker is trained via supervised fine-tuning for document coverage and reinforcement learning to align large language model preferences with document ranking.
Experiments on two publicly available datasets demonstrate that the framework provides comprehensive and satisfying responses efficiently.
Identifying representative points in the latent space enables interpretation of learned authorship embeddings.
Large language models generate natural language descriptions of writing style for representative latent points.
The interpretable space aligns with the latent space and achieves higher prediction agreement than baseline methods.
Human evaluation confirms that the generated style descriptions are of high quality and useful for explaining the latent space.
Explanations generated by the technique improve human performance on the authorship attribution task.
RoBGuard enhances large language models to assess risk of bias in randomized controlled trial documents.
RoBGuard uses medical knowledge-enhanced question reformulation.
RoBGuard integrates multimodal document parsing and multi-expert collaboration.
RoB-Item and RoB-Domain datasets are introduced for risk-of-bias assessment in clinical trial documents.
RoBGuard outperforms existing methods on the RoB-Item dataset.
CEHA is a benchmark dataset of conflict events in the Horn of Africa.
CEHA contains 500 English event descriptions with fine-grained event-type definitions that emphasize the cause of conflict.
CEHA categorizes key types of conflict risk according to stakeholder-specified areas in the Humanitarian-Peace-Development Nexus.
A new task is defined for identifying violent conflict events using online resources with the CEHA dataset.
Baseline models demonstrate that event-relevance classification and event-type classification using CEHA are challenging and useful for evaluating models in low-resource settings.

A comparative analysis of pre-trained and fine-tuned sense-aware contextualised word embeddings under Principal Component Analysis and Independent Component Analysis transformations is conducted on contextual and temporal semantic change benchmarks.
Pre-trained sense-aware contextualised word embeddings contain a limited set of dimensions that are specific to semantic changes of words.
Fine-tuning sense-aware contextualised word embeddings distributes semantic change information across all dimensions.
Principal Component Analysis better represents semantic changes than Independent Component Analysis among the leading dimensions of the embedding space.
The findings suggest that semantic change detection can be performed efficiently using a small number of dimensions that are aware of semantic change.
NyayaAnumana is the largest and most diverse corpus of Indian legal cases for legal judgment prediction, containing hundreds of thousands of preprocessed cases from multiple court levels.
NyayaAnumana surpasses existing datasets such as PredEx and ILDC in size and diversity, offering a comprehensive foundation for AI research in Indian law.

INLegalLlama is a domain-specific generative large language model for the Indian legal system, trained via a two-phase approach of continual pretraining on Indian legal documents followed by task-specific supervised finetuning.
Incorporating diverse court data significantly boosts model accuracy in legal judgment prediction.
INLegalLlama generates comprehensible explanations for its predictions, enhancing explainability in AI-assisted legal decisions.
